| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_unique` | `P.vecTwfeNormalEq β → β = P.betaTWFE` | Full-rank uniqueness of the matrix normal-equation solution | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time → VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time (Fin 1)` | The scalar problem embeds as the singleton-`K` vector problem; `IsUnit (gram).det` reduces to `ddotX_ss_pos` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector_betaTWFE` | `P.toVector.betaTWFE 0 = P.betaTWFE` | The singleton-coordinate vector coefficient recovers the scalar coefficient, so the scalar theorem is the `K = Fin 1` case | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/VectorMundlak.lean` — K-vector two-way Mundlak equivalence (Theorem A, vector form)

Extends `twfe_twm_equivalence` to K-vector regressors via a matrix FWL handoff.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gramOf` / `numerOf` | `(Unit → Time → K → ℝ) → Matrix K K ℝ` / `… → (Unit → Time → ℝ) → K → ℝ` | Generic residualized Gram matrix / numerator of a supplied residualized regressor | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gram_eq_gramOf` / `numer_eq_numerOf` | `gram X = gramOf (ddotVec X)` / `numer X Y = numerOf (ddotVec X) (ddot Y)` | The TWFE Gram/numerator are the residualized instances of the generic versions | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_dotRegressor` | `∑_it Dt·k (∑_j D·j β_j) = ∑_j (∑_it Dt·k D·j) β_j` | A residualized regressor against a `β`-combination factors through the cross-Gram | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.matrix_fwl_eq_of_normalEqs` | `(decompositions + per-coordinate orthogonality + IsUnit gram + normal eqs) → β = (gramOf Dtilde)⁻¹ (numerOf Dtilde Ytilde)` | Matrix Frisch-Waugh-Lovell handoff: vector coefficient satisfying pooled normal equations against `D` and nuisance class `H` equals the residualized matrix coefficient | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance` | `(X) → (Z → Unit → ℝ) → (M → Time → ℝ) → (Unit → Time → ℝ) → Prop` | K-vector two-way Mundlak nuisance span: constants, unit/time means of every coordinate of `X`, optional unit-only and time-only controls | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vector_mundlak_nuisance_unit_time` | `IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance X Zvar Mvar h → IsUnitTimeAdditive h` | Every vector Mundlak nuisance term is unit/time additive | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_ite_one_mul` | `∑_k' (if k'=k then 1 else 0) * f k' = f k` | Coordinate-selection indicator collapse | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWMFit` | `structure { beta, nuisance, nuisance_mem, normal_X, normal_H }` | Coding-free K-vector two-way Mundlak fit, normal equations against each regressor coordinate and the nuisance span | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_equivalence` | `VectorTWMFit P Zvar Mvar → fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` | K-vector finite-panel TWFE--two-way Mundlak equivalence (`thm:po-estimand-wooldridge-twfe-twm-equivalence`, full vector form) | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_optional_controls_invariant` | `fit₁.beta = fit₂.beta` | Adding or removing optional unit-only/time-only controls does not change the vector coefficient | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/DID.lean`

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar` | `structure { cohortShare, covarWeight, treatedCell, untreatedCell, Y0Mean, YgMean, observedMean, ... }` | Finite-cell ATT scaffold with arbitrary treated/untreated cell predicates and primitive conditional means; it does not yet enforce ordered periods, `∞` cohorts, or absorbing treatment internally | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.treatedCells` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar → Finset (Cohort × Time)` | Finite support of target treated cohort-time cells selected by `treatedCell` | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.untreatedCells` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar → Finset (Cohort × Time)` | Finite untreated design set (support of the weighted projection producing `m0`), selected by `untreatedCell` | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.tauCell` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar → Cohort → Time → ℝ` | Covariate-weighted ATT cell `∑_c w_gc (Y_g - Y_0)` | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.tauAgg` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar → (Cohort → Time → ℝ) → ℝ` | Weighted aggregate ATT over the treated-cell support | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.AggregateWeights P` | `structure { weight, nonneg_on_treated, sum_treated }` | Requested nonnegative treated-cell weights summing to one | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.NoAnticipation P` | `Prop` | Finite-cell no-anticipation predicate on `untreatedCell` observations; retained as a named assumption for the causal theorem interface | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P` | `Prop` | Additive untreated-mean representation `Y0Mean g t c = alpha g c + lambda t c`, the scaffold form of conditional parallel trends | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression P` | `structure { m0, additive, untreatedWeight, untreatedWeight_pos, untreatedNormalEq, design_identifies, target_cell_support }` | Bundled untreated-regression witness. `untreatedNormalEq` (with positive weights `untreatedWeight`/`untreatedWeight_pos`) is the genuine finite-cell projection origin: `m0` solves the weighted least-squares normal equations of the observed outcome on the additive class `H0` over the untreated design. `untreatedFit` (exact fit) and `recovers_target_Y0` are now DERIVED theorems, not fields; `design_identifies` is the concrete connected/full-rank condition. The former opaque `Prop`+`_holds` placeholders (untreated_design_connected, full_rank_after_normalization, covariates_baseline) and the former posited `untreatedFit` field were removed. | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.untreatedFit` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → untreatedCell g t → m0 g t c = YgMean g t c` | **DERIVED exact untreated fit (G5).** The weighted projection reproduces the cohort-`g` mean on every untreated cell. Proof: no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends make the untreated observed mean additive, so the residual `m0 − Y0Mean` is additive; plugging it into `untreatedNormalEq` forces its positively-weighted squared sum over the untreated design to zero, hence the residual vanishes cellwise | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsCellAdditive` | `(Cohort → Time → Covar → ℝ) → Prop` | Additive cell function `d g t c = γ g c + δ t c` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.UntreatedDesignIdentifies P` | `Prop` | Connected/full-rank condition: an additive cell function vanishing on all untreated cells vanishes on all treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.recovers_target_Y0` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → treatedCell g t → m0 g t c = Y0Mean g t c` | Saturated regression recovers untreated means on treated cells, DERIVED from no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends + additivity + untreated fit + design identifiability | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator` | `(g : Cohort) → (t : Time) → Cohort → Time → ℝ` | Saturated treated-cell indicator `1{(g',t')=(g,t)}` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator_normalEq_eq_cellResidual` | `(full saturated indicator normal equation) ↔ cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | Saturated block-diagonalization: the full normal equation summed against a cell's indicator collapses (cross-cell terms vanish by orthogonality) to that cell's residual equation, justifying the POLS/ETWFE per-cell normal-equation fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta` | `(P) → SaturatedUntreatedRegression P → Cohort → Time → ℝ` | Imputation residual mean using `observedMean - m0` within a treated cell | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | `Prop` | Finite-cell residual normal equation `∑_c w_gc (observedMean_gtc - m0_gtc - theta) = 0` for a candidate cell coefficient | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq_eq_imputationTheta` | `cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t → theta = imputationTheta P S g t` | A saturated treated-cell normal equation identifies the coefficient with the imputation residual mean, using `covarWeight_sum_one` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.FlexibleDIDEstimands P S` | `structure { thetaImp, thetaPOLS, thetaETWFE, thetaImp_eq_imputation, pols_cell_normalEq, etwfe_cell_normalEq }` | Coding-free flexible imputation/POLS/ETWFE cell estimands; POLS and ETWFE coefficients now carry finite-cell residual normal equations instead of direct equality assumptions | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.thetaPOLS_eq_imputationTheta` / `thetaETWFE_eq_imputationTheta` | `treatedCell g t → thetaPOLS/ETWFE g t = imputationTheta P S g t` | Derived POLS and ETWFE cell equalities from their residual normal equations; the ETWFE normal equation is the residualized equation supplied by the FWL/TWFE-Mundlak bridge | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.pols_cell_eq_imputation` / `etwfe_cell_eq_pols` | `treatedCell g t → thetaPOLS = thetaImp` / `thetaETWFE = thetaPOLS` | Compatibility aliases; both equalities are now consequences of the normal-equation lemmas rather than structure fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.psiImp` / `psiPOLS` / `psiETWFE` | `(P) → FlexibleDIDEstimands P S → (Cohort → Time → ℝ) → ℝ` | Aggregate estimands formed by weighting the imputation, POLS, and ETWFE cell coefficients over treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_tauCell` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → treatedCell g t → E.thetaImp g t = P.tauCell g t` | Imputation recovers a treated ATT cell from consistency and the DERIVED `recovers_target_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → treatedCell g t → thetaImp = thetaPOLS = thetaETWFE = tauCell` | Cell-level theorem: the untreated-prediction witness plus POLS/ETWFE residual normal equations imply all three cell estimands equal the ATT cell | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_aggregate_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → AggregateWeights P → psiImp = tauAgg ∧ psiPOLS = tauAgg ∧ psiETWFE = tauAgg` | Aggregate theorem obtained by summing the normal-equation-derived cell equalities over treated cells | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` / `flexible_did_aggregate_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → …` (same signatures as cell/aggregate characterization respectively) | Compatibility aliases for the earlier scaffold names, now taking the explicit causal hypotheses | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_scaffold_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → cell characterization ∧ aggregate characterization` | Finite-cell Wooldridge DID characterization from saturated untreated prediction plus POLS/ETWFE finite-cell residual normal equations | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationBridge.lean` — finite estimand/projection → population conditional expectation (audit G9 + G5 population half)

Bridges the finite-cell imputation estimand and the saturated projection `m0` to population conditional expectations, reusing the conditional finite-partition total law `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_eq_sum_condProb_mul_eventCondExp`. Hypothesis-driven: identification hypotheses connect the finite scaffold's primitive means to a probability model — they are exactly the paper's *definitions* (θ^imp = E[Δ∣G=g]; m0 = E[Y(∞)∣G,t,C]), not strengthenings.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | `(μ, P, S, g, t, cohortEvent, covarCell, Δ)`, partition + integrability + `hweight : covarWeight g c = μ(cohortEvent ∩ covarCell c)/μ(cohortEvent)` + `hcell : observedMean − m0 = eventCondExp μ (cohortEvent ∩ covarCell c) Δ` → `imputationTheta P S g t = eventCondExp μ cohortEvent Δ` | **G9 finite→population bridge.** The finite imputation residual mean equals the population conditional expectation `E[Δ∣G=g]` on the cohort event, by the law of iterated expectations over the covariate partition | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.thetaImp_eq_eventCondExp` | `treatedCell g t →` (same hypotheses) `→ E.thetaImp g t = eventCondExp μ cohortEvent Δ` | **G9 (θ^imp = E[Δ∣G=g]).** The `FlexibleDIDEstimands` imputation cell estimand equals the population conditional expectation on each treated cell; composes `thetaImp_eq_imputation` with `imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → treatedCell g t →` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `→ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (treated cells).** The saturated projection's fitted value equals `E[Y_t(∞)∣G=g,t,C=c]`; causal content (additive extrapolation) carried by `recovers_target_Y0`, `hY0` reads `Y0Mean` as that conditional mean | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated` | `NoAnticipation P → ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P → untreatedCell g t →` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `→ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (untreated cells).** Same conclusion via `untreatedFit` + no-anticipation | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationOrigin.lean` — `ofPopulation` constructor making the m0 conditional-expectation origin definitional (audit G5 measure layer)

Closes the *measure-layer* half of G5: instead of *assuming* `Y0Mean g t c = E[Y(∞)∣cell]` (the `hY0` hypothesis above), it builds a `StaggeredATTCells` whose cell means are *defined* as that `eventCondExp`, so `hY0` holds by `rfl`. Mirrors `DCDHPanel.ofPopulation`. The cell-weight data (`cohortShare`, `covarWeight`) is supplied as a witness (an FWL/partition construction orthogonal to the conditional-expectation origin); the pointwise consistency inputs are the paper's consistency axiom restricted to each cell.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.StaggeredATTCells.ofPopulation` | `(μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (cellEvent) (Y0pop Ygpop Yobspop : Ω → ℝ) (treatedCell untreatedCell) (cohortShare covarWeight) (hmeas) (weight side-conditions) (hcons_tr hcons_ut) → StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar` | **G5 (panel from a population model).** Defines `Y0Mean/YgMean/observedMean g t c := eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) {Y0pop/Ygpop/Yobspop}`; derives `consistency_treated`/`consistency_untreated` from pointwise PO consistency via `eventCondExp_congr_on`; weights passed as witnesses | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation μ …`, `hNA → hCPT → treatedCell g t → S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (treated, definitional).** Applies `m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` with `hY0 := rfl` — the conditional-expectation origin of `m0` is now definitional, not assumed | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation μ …`, `hNA → hCPT → untreatedCell g t → S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp μ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (untreated, definitional).** Untreated-cell analogue, `hY0 := rfl` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudy. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Imputation.lean` — Robust Imputation Event Studies (BJS)

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess robust imputation event-study characterization. The module works directly with finite treated and untreated cell index types, arbitrary signed target weights, treated/untreated regressor rows, primitive observed-law means, a nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects. It proves the population imputation identification theorem (taking `PredictionIdentified` as a propositional existence hypothesis and extracting the witness internally) and the intentionally-weak `linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` algebra helper. The **source-strength** linear-unbiased representation theorem and the combined iff characterization now live in the companion `PanelBridge.lean` (§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge), where the finite left-null-space adjustment is rebased onto the panel `WeightedSupport`/`Subspace` substrate. A staggered-adoption two-way-FE grounding (closing the abstract-index scope gap) lives in `PanelGrounding.lean` (§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding). The Gauss-Markov/BLUE efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation` is in `Efficiency.lean` (§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency), built on the finite Gauss-Markov layer §13gm. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/borusyak_jaravel_spiess_imputation.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot` | `(Regressor → ℝ) → (Regressor → ℝ) → ℝ` | Finite regressor dot product used for untreated-outcome linear predictions | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.BJSPanel` | `structure { qT, qU, a, EY_T, EY_U, EY0_T, EY0_U, beta0, tau }` | Finite BJS treated/untreated cell design: regressor rows, arbitrary target weights, observed means, untreated potential-outcome means, nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.theta` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor → ℝ` | Target weighted sum `∑ c, a c * tau c`; weights may be signed and need not sum to one | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.targetForTau` | `(Treated → ℝ) → ℝ` | Target functional evaluated at an arbitrary unrestricted treated-effect vector, used in the linear-unbiasedness statement | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.UntreatedOutcomeModel` | `Prop` | Deterministic mean version of the untreated-outcome linear model plus untreated-cell no-anticipation / observation equation | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.TreatmentEffectFixed` | `Prop` | Treated observed means equal untreated means plus the fixed/design-conditional treated-cell effect | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ImputationWeights` | `structure { weight, row_identity }` | Witness-form prediction-span condition: for every target-relevant treated cell, its regressor row is a weighted combination of untreated rows | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.PredictionIdentified` | `Prop` | Propositional existence wrapper (`Nonempty ImputationWeights`) for valid target-relevant imputation weights; natural hypothesis for identification — callers supply existence, not a pre-built witness | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.psiImp` | `(Treated → Untreated → ℝ) → ℝ` | Population observed-law imputation functional `∑ a_c (EY_T c - ∑ h_cu EY_U u)` for arbitrary imputation weights | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_imputation_identification` | `UntreatedOutcomeModel → PredictionIdentified → TreatmentEffectFixed → ∃ h : ImputationWeights, psiImp h.weight = theta` | BJS population identification theorem: under the untreated model and prediction-span condition, some imputation weight matrix recovers the weighted treated-cell target; witness extracted internally via `Classical.choice` | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator` | `structure { vT, vU }` | Linear estimator coefficients on treated and untreated observed cell outcomes | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.value` | `(Treated → ℝ) → (Untreated → ℝ) → ℝ` | Evaluates a treated-plus-untreated linear estimator on arbitrary outcome arrays | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.observedValue` | `ℝ` | Evaluates a linear estimator on the panel's observed-law treated and untreated means | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.modelValue` | `(Regressor → ℝ) → (Treated → ℝ) → ℝ` | Model-implied value of the linear estimator for a nuisance vector and unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.unbiasedForAllTau` | `Prop` | Linear unbiasedness for every nuisance vector and every unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` | `unbiasedForAllTau → ∀ c, vT c = a c` | Hoisted fact: unrestricted treatment-effect heterogeneity forces treated-cell weights to equal target weights (indicator-`tau` test); reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord` | `unbiasedForAllTau → ∀ r, (∑ c, a c * qT c r) + ∑ u, vU u * qU u r = 0` | Hoisted fact: nuisance unbiasedness gives the BJS left-null-space coordinate constraint `aᵀQ_T + vUᵀQ_U = 0`; reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.HasImputationRepresentation` | `structure { weights, untreated_weight_representation }` | Explicit witness that a linear estimator's untreated coefficients are represented by valid BJS imputation weights; used as an algebra helper for the source-strength theorem | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` | `unbiasedForAllTau → HasImputationRepresentation → ∃ H, vT = a ∧ vU = -aᵀH ∧ (∀ YT YU, value YT YU = ∑ c, a c * (YT c - ∑ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) ∧ observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Intentionally weak algebra helper: given a pre-built imputation representation witness, derives the estimator identity; source-strength derivation lives in `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelBridge.lean` — Imputation Event Study ↔ panel substrate bridge

Connects the bespoke finite design-matrix algebra of the BJS imputation scaffold to the shared panel inner-product / subspace substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/`). The OLS imputation estimator is the *uniform-weight* special case of the panel WLS substrate, so the untreated cells carry the uniform `WeightedSupport`; the keystone lemma proves the BJS "left-null-space of `Q_U`" condition **is** `ip`-orthogonality to the regressor column span. The source-strength linear-unbiased theorem and the combined iff characterization (moved here from `Imputation.lean`, statements verbatim) consume that substrate-based left-null-space adjustment; `DecidableEq Untreated` is obtained internally via `Classical.decEq`, so their public signatures are unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.untreatedSupport` | `(Untreated : Type*) → [Fintype, DecidableEq, Nonempty] → WeightedSupport Untreated` | The untreated cells as the uniform `WeightedSupport` `ω_u ≡ 1/\|U\|` — the OLS = uniform-weight WLS instantiation through which BJS consumes the panel `ip`/`Subspace` API | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.regressorColumn` | `Regressor → (Untreated → ℝ)` | The `r`-th regressor column `u ↦ q_{ur}` as an array over untreated cells | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan` | `Submodule ℝ (Untreated → ℝ)` | Span of all regressor columns; orthogonality to it is the BJS left-null-space condition | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_untreatedSupport_regressorColumn` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = (\|U\|)⁻¹ * ∑ u, v u * qU u r` | The panel inner product of an untreated vector with a regressor column equals the BJS left-null-space sum, rescaled by the uniform weight | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_regressorColumn_eq_zero_iff` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = 0 ↔ ∑ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | `ip`-orthogonality to a single column ⟺ that column's left-null coordinate vanishes | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan_ip_orthogonal_iff` | `(∀ h ∈ columnSpan, ip v h = 0) ↔ ∀ r, ∑ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | **Keystone bridge**: a vector is `ip`-orthogonal to the regressor column span iff it satisfies the BJS left-null-space condition (the panel-substrate restatement of "`v` lies in the left null space of `Q_U`") | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` | `(hc0 : a c0 ≠ 0) → H0 → (gap ip-orthogonal to columnSpan) → ∃ H, ∀ u, ∑ c, a c * H.weight c u = -vU u` | Audit M3 left-null-space row adjustment, phrased through the panel substrate: one nonzero target row absorbs the orthogonal gap, yielding imputation weights representing the untreated coefficients | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | `unbiasedForAllTau → PredictionIdentified → (∃ c, a c ≠ 0) → ∃ H, vT = a ∧ vU = -aᵀH ∧ (∀ YT YU, value YT YU = ∑ c, a c * (YT c - ∑ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) ∧ observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Source-strength BJS linear-unbiased representation theorem; now consumes `exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` (empty-`Untreated` case handled separately) instead of an inline finite-algebra construction | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_linear_unbiased_iff_imputation_form` | `PredictionIdentified → (∃ c, a c ≠ 0) → (unbiasedForAllTau ↔ ∃ H, vT = a ∧ vU = -aᵀH ∧ (∀ YT YU, value = ∑ a_c (YT c - ∑ h_cu YU u)) ∧ observedValue = psiImp H.weight)` | Combined iff characterization (`prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`, identification half): unbiased for every tau iff BJS imputation form; (⟹) is `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified`, (⟸) is pure row-span algebra | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelGrounding.lean` — Imputation Event Study staggered-adoption grounding

Closes the abstract-index scope gap (audit G5) *constructively*: exhibits `BJSPanel` as an instance built from genuine panel primitives — units `I`, periods `Fin T`, and a staggered-adoption path `g : I → WithTop (Fin T)` via `Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath`. Treated cells are those whose treatment has switched on (`g i ≤ t`); untreated cells are those still untreated (`t < g i`), faithfully including every never-treated cell and every pre-adoption cell of a treated unit. The regressor design is the canonical two-way fixed-effect block `I ⊕ Fin T`, so the untreated-outcome model `E[Y(0)] = α_i + λ_t` holds by construction and the BJS hypotheses discharge to a concrete panel.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.TreatedCell` | `(g : I → WithTop (Fin T)) → Type` | Subtype of cells `(i,t)` whose treatment has switched on (`AdoptionPath.le (g i) t`) | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.UntreatedCell` | `(g : I → WithTop (Fin T)) → Type` | Subtype of still-untreated cells (`AdoptionPath.lt (g i) t`); includes never-treated and pre-adoption cells | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_not_untreated` | `le (g c.1) c.2 → lt (g c.1) c.2 → False` | The cell partition is exclusive: no cell is both adopted-by-`t` and untreated-at-`t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_or_untreated` | `∀ c, le (g c.1) c.2 ∨ lt (g c.1) c.2` | The cell partition is exhaustive: every cell is treated or untreated | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.feRow` | `(I × Fin T) → (I ⊕ Fin T) → ℝ` | Two-way fixed-effect design row for a cell: unit-`i` indicator stacked with time-`t` indicator | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot_feRow` | `dot (feRow c) (Sum.elim α lam) = α c.1 + lam c.2` | The FE row evaluates the additive fixed-effect model `α_i + λ_t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE` | `(g) → (α : I → ℝ) → (lam : Fin T → ℝ) → (a tau : TreatedCell g → ℝ) → BJSPanel (TreatedCell g) (UntreatedCell g) (I ⊕ Fin T)` | Grounding constructor: builds a `BJSPanel` from an adoption path and two-way fixed effects | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_untreatedModel` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE …).UntreatedOutcomeModel` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS untreated-outcome model (both cell means equal `α_i + λ_t`; untreated cells have no anticipation) | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_treatmentFixed` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE …).TreatmentEffectFixed` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS fixed-effect equation `E[Y_T] = E[Y_T(0)] + τ` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Efficiency.lean` — Imputation Event Study Efficiency / BLUE half

The efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`: under spherical errors the OLS imputation estimator is the minimum-variance linear unbiased estimator (Gauss-Markov / BLUE). The BJS imputation estimator is recognized as the OLS estimator for the *event-study design* `designFull` (a treated-cell indicator block stacked with the covariate block), so the result is a direct instantiation of the finite Gauss-Markov theorem `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_spherical` (§13gm) at that design. The unbiasedness-to-design-constraint bridge uses the hoisted `LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` and `LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord`. An additive fixed-effect-block bridge (`designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan`) identifies the τ-columns of `designFull` with the panel `IndicatorSpan` substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/IndicatorSpan.lean`), connecting the event-study FE design to the panel indicator-span layer; the BLUE proof itself stays on the finite Gauss-Markov substrate (§13gm), the correct home for the variance argument. No `sorry`/`admit`/`axiom`. Companion NL doc section C3.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BJSPanel.designFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor → Matrix (Treated ⊕ Untreated) (Treated ⊕ Regressor) ℝ` | Event-study design: rows are observed cells, columns are treated-cell fixed effects (τ block, cell-indicator on treated rows / zero on untreated rows) stacked with covariates (β block, `qT`/`qU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.cFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor → (Treated ⊕ Regressor → ℝ)` | Target functional in design coordinates: target weights `a` on the τ block, zero on the covariate block | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf` | `LinearEstimator P → (Treated ⊕ Untreated → ℝ)` | A linear estimator's full weight vector over all observed cells (`Sum.elim vT vU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf_vecMul_designFull` | `unbiasedForAllTau → weightOf L ᵥ* designFull P = cFull P` | Unbiasedness bridge: an unbiased estimator's weight satisfies the design constraint (captures both `vT = a` and the nuisance left-null constraint) | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_ols_imputation_min_variance_spherical` | `(∀ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 μ) → SphericalFamily Y μ σ → IsUnit ((designFull P)ᵀ * designFull P).det → unbiasedForAllTau L → Var[∑ olsWeight … * Y] ≤ Var[∑ weightOf L * Y]` | **BJS efficiency theorem (BLUE):** for a full-rank event-study design and spherical cell outcomes, the OLS imputation estimator has variance no larger than any linear unbiased estimator of the target | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.treatedClassifier` | `(Treated ⊕ Untreated) → Option Treated` | Classifier on observed cells: each treated cell to its own label, every untreated cell to `none`; its `Some`-indicators are the τ-columns of `designFull` | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_eq_cellIndicator` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) = cellIndicator treatedClassifier (some d)` | Each treated-cell fixed-effect column of `designFull` is the panel cell indicator of the treated-cell classifier | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) ∈ indicatorSpan treatedClassifier` | The treated-cell FE block of `designFull` lies in the panel `IndicatorSpan`: the BJS event-study FE design is the panel indicator-span substrate (additive; BLUE proof unchanged) | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-HeterogeneousTWFE. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/HeterogeneousTWFE/{FinitePanel,FWLBridge,PopulationBridge}.lean` — de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020) TWFE Signed Weights

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille TWFE heterogeneous-effects warning. `FinitePanel.lean` works over finite group-time cells with positive weights `π_gt`, binary treatment `D_gt`, observed outcomes `Y_gt`, untreated outcomes `Y0_gt`, treatment effects `τ_gt`, and a residualized-treatment witness `D̃_gt` against the group-plus-time fixed-effect span. It proves the finite FWL decomposition `β^twfe = B0 + ∑_{D=1} ω_gt τ_gt`, the zero-untreated-residual-contrast corollary, the fact that treated-cell weights sum to one while inheriting the sign of `D̃`, and the constructive sign-reversal theorem. `FWLBridge.lean` closes audit gaps **G2/G6**: for the uniform balanced panel it builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `D̃` is the *derived* Frisch-Waugh-Lovell residual (double-demeaned treatment `ddot D` from `UniformTwoWayPanel`), with the membership and orthogonality fields PROVED rather than posited — only nonzero double-demeaned treatment variation (`S_D > 0`), binary treatment, and consistency remain as primitives. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/de_chaisemartin_dhaultfoeuille_twfe.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.IsGTFE` | `(G → T → ℝ) → Prop` | Compatibility alias for `AdditiveSpan.IsUnitTimeAdditive`, specialized to group-time fixed effects `a_g + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel G T` | `structure { pi, D, Y, Y0, tau, Dtilde : G → T → ℝ, pi_pos, pi_sum_one, D_binary, consistency, D_minus_resid_mem, Dtilde_orthogonal, SD_pos }` | Finite DCDH group-time panel plus residualized-treatment witness (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-panel`, `def:po-estimand-dcdh-residualized-treatment`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.SD P` | `DCDHPanel G T → ℝ` | Residualized-treatment denominator `S_D = ∑ π_gt D̃_gt²` | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.betaTWFE P` | `DCDHPanel G T → ℝ` | Finite FWL/TWFE coefficient `(∑ π_gt D̃_gt Y_gt) / S_D` (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-coefficient`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.untreatedBias P` | `DCDHPanel G T → ℝ` | Bias term `B0 = (∑ π_gt D̃_gt Y0_gt) / S_D` in the DCDH decomposition | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.omega P g t` | `DCDHPanel G T → G → T → ℝ` | Normalized residualized-treatment cell weight `ω_gt = π_gt D̃_gt / S_D`, interpreted on treated cells | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.treatedCells P` | `DCDHPanel G T → Finset (G × T)` | Finite set of cells with `D_gt = 1`, used to state treated-cell sums without partial weights | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau P` / `DCDHPanel.treatedWeightedTau P` | `DCDHPanel G T → ℝ` | All-cell `D`-weighted treatment-effect component and equivalent treated-cell weighted sum | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast P` | `DCDHPanel G T → Prop` | Exact DCDH bias-free condition `∑ π_gt D̃_gt Y0_gt = 0` (`ass:po-estimand-dcdh-untreated-parallel`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.inner_Dtilde_D_eq_SD` | `∑ π_gt D̃_gt D_gt = P.SD` | Denominator identity derived by applying residualized-treatment orthogonality to `D − D̃` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast_of_Y0_mem_gtfe` | `IsGTFE P.Y0 → P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast` | Additive group-plus-time untreated outcomes are a sufficient, not necessary, condition for zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_DWeightedTau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.DWeightedTau` | All-cell version of the DCDH TWFE decomposition after substituting consistency | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau_eq_treatedWeightedTau` | `P.DWeightedTau = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Binary-treatment filtering lemma: the all-cell effect component equals the treated-cell weighted sum | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_treated_weighted_tau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.treatedWeightedTau` | Headline decomposition `β^twfe = B0 + ∑_{D=1} ω_gt τ_gt` (`thm:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-decomposition`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_treated_weighted_tau_of_zeroUntreatedContrast` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast → P.betaTWFE = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Bias-free DCDH causal weighted-sum corollary under the exact zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_sum_eq_one` | `∑_{gt ∈ treatedCells} omega P gt.1 gt.2 = 1` | Treated-cell DCDH weights sum to one (`prop:po-estimand-dcdh-weights-sum-one`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_neg_iff_Dtilde_neg` | `P.omega g t < 0 ↔ P.Dtilde g t < 0` | Negative-sign direction of `sign(ω_gt)=sign(D̃_gt)`; needs only `π_gt>0`, `S_D>0` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_pos_iff_Dtilde_pos` | `0 < P.omega g t ↔ 0 < P.Dtilde g t` | Positive-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_zero_iff_Dtilde_zero` | `P.omega g t = 0 ↔ P.Dtilde g t = 0` | Zero-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.neg_component_of_exists_Dtilde_neg` | `(∃ g t, P.D g t = 1 ∧ P.Dtilde g t < 0) → ∑_{treated,ω<0} ω < 0` | Bridge: the paper's natural entry condition "some treated cell has D̃_gt<0" implies the negative-weight-sum precondition of the sign-reversal theorem | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.two_cell_signed_weights_positive_effects_negative_sum` | `0 < c → 0 < ε → ((1+c)ε)/c < M → 0 < ε ∧ 0 < M ∧ (1+c)ε + (-c)M < 0` | Explicit two-cell sign-reversal arithmetic from `prop:po-estimand-dcdh-sign-reversal` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_positive_effects_negative_weighted_sum_of_negative_component` | `(∑ w = 1) → (∑_{w<0} w < 0) → ∃ τ, (∀ i, 0 < τ i) ∧ ∑ i, w i τ i < 0` | Reusable finite signed-average construction: a strictly negative total weight component can dominate with positive effects | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_panel_with_positive_treated_effects_twfe_negative_of_negative_component` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast → (∑_{treated,ω<0} ω < 0) → ∃ P', same π,D,Y0,D̃, (∀ g t, 0 < P'.tau g t), P'.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast ∧ P'.betaTWFE < 0` | Panel-level DCDH sign-reversal theorem: keeping the treatment/residualization geometry fixed, one can choose globally positive treated effects (for all g t) so TWFE is negative | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/FWLBridge.lean` — `D̃` as a derived FWL residual (audit G2/G6)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight G T` | `ℝ` | Uniform unit-period cell weight `1/(|G|·|T|)` for the balanced-panel DCDH instance | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight_pos` | `UniformTwoWayPanel.BalancedPanel G T → 0 < uniformWeight G T` | Positivity of the uniform weight from the `≥2` cardinality side conditions | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel` | `BalancedPanel G T → (D Y Y0 tau) → (D binary) → (consistency) → (0 < ∑ uniformWeight·(ddot D)²) → DCDHPanel G T` | **G2/G6:** builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `Dtilde := ddot D` (double-demeaned FWL residual); `D_minus_resid_mem` and `Dtilde_orthogonal` are DERIVED from `UniformTwoWayPanel` (`sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection`, `unitTimeProjection_additive`, `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time`), not posited | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_Dtilde` | `(ofTwoWayPanel …).Dtilde = ddot D` | The residualized treatment of the FWL-derived panel is exactly the double-demeaned treatment | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_betaTWFE` | `(ofTwoWayPanel …).betaTWFE = finiteResidualizedCoefficient (ddot D) Y` | The DCDH TWFE coefficient of the FWL-derived panel equals the uniform-panel FWL residualized coefficient of `Y` on `ddot D` (the uniform weight cancels) | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/PopulationBridge.lean` — cell consistency from a potential-outcome contrast (audit G10)

Supplies the population origin of the `DCDHPanel.consistency` field and of `tau` as `Y(1) − Y(0)`, at the level of cell-conditional means, reusing `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_congr_on`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.cellMean_consistency` | `(μ, A measurable, Y Y0 Y1, d ∈ {0,1})`, `hcons : ∀ ω ∈ A, Y ω = Y0 ω + d·(Y1 ω − Y0 ω)` → `eventCondExp μ A Y = eventCondExp μ A Y0 + d·(eventCondExp μ A Y1 − eventCondExp μ A Y0)` | **G10 finite→population bridge.** On a cell where treatment is constant `D ≡ d` and PO consistency holds pointwise, the cell-conditional means satisfy the DCDH consistency identity `Ȳ = Ȳ(0) + d·τ̄` with `τ̄ = E[Y(1)∣A] − E[Y(0)∣A]` a genuine potential-outcome contrast. Proof: case split on `d`, `eventCondExp_congr_on`, `ring` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel.ofPopulation` | `[IsProbabilityMeasure μ]`, cell partition `cellEvent`, outcomes `Yfac Y0pop Y1pop`, binary cell treatment `d`, `Dtilde` witness; partition/positivity + pointwise consistency `hcons` + `Dtilde` orthogonality/`SD_pos` hypotheses → `DCDHPanel G T` | **G10 `DCDHPanel` from a population model.** Builds a genuine panel with `Y/Y0 = E[·∣cell]`, `tau = E[Y(1)∣cell] − E[Y(0)∣cell]` (a real PO contrast), `pi = μ(cell)`. `consistency` is DERIVED via `cellMean_consistency`; `pi_sum_one` from `measure_biUnion_finset` over the partition. `Dtilde` (general-weight FWL residual) is a witness, as in the base panel — the uniform case is `ofTwoWayPanel` | **Defined** |

---

## 9i0a-OLSWeightDecomposition. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/OLSWeightDecomposition/{FinitePartition,OverlapWeightedATE,GeneralCondExp}.lean` — Słoczyński (2022) Finite-Partition OLS Weights

Specific-paper specialization of the residualization framework to Słoczyński (2022, *Review of Economics and Statistics*). Layer A (`FinitePartition.lean`) is pure finite-cell algebra: given a finite covariate `G : 𝒢` with cell probabilities `π_g`, treated shares `p_g`, and conditional treatment effects `τ_g`, the saturated-OLS estimand decomposes as `β_sat = Σ ω_g τ_g`, where `ω_g ∝ π_g p_g (1−p_g)`. On its own this layer carries no causal meaning (`π, p, τ` are formal reals); the causal content is injected in Layer B. Layer B (`OverlapWeightedATE.lean`, backed by `Support/*.lean`) instantiates the residualization core on a probability space with binary treatment, consistency, and finite-cell CMI, constructs the saturated residualization witnesses, and proves that the residualized coefficient equals the Layer A overlap-weighted ATE — with `τ_g = E[Y(1)−Y(0)∣G=g]` built from real potential outcomes. All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.Panel.EstimandCharacterization.OLSWeightDecomposition`. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/sloczynski_ols_heterogeneous.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FinitePartition 𝒢` | `structure { π, p, τ : 𝒢 → ℝ, π_nonneg, π_sum_one, p_nonneg, p_le_one, overlap_pos }` | Słoczyński finite covariate partition (`def:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-finite-partition`): cell probabilities `π_g`, treated shares `p_g ∈ [0,1]`, conditional treatment effects `τ_g`, plus the `Σπ=1` probability-vector constraint and the saturated-overlap relevance hypothesis `Σ π_g p_g (1−p_g) > 0` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.cellOverlap P g` | `FinitePartition 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Within-cell treatment variance `p_g (1 − p_g)` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapNumerator P` | `FinitePartition 𝒢 → ℝ` | Numerator `Σ_g π_g · p_g (1−p_g) · τ_g` of the saturated-OLS estimand | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapDenominator P` | `FinitePartition 𝒢 → ℝ` | Denominator `Σ_g π_g · p_g (1−p_g)` of the saturated-OLS estimand | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Normalized weight `ω_g = π_g p_g (1−p_g) / Σ_h π_h p_h (1−p_h)` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapWeightedATE P` | `noncomputable FinitePartition 𝒢 → ℝ` | Saturated-OLS estimand `β_sat = (Σ π·p(1−p)·τ) / (Σ π·p(1−p))` in finite-cell form | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapDenominator_pos` | `0 < P.overlapDenominator` | Denominator positivity, restated from `overlap_pos` after unfolding `cellOverlap` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.omega_sum_eq_one` | `∑ g, P.overlapWeight g = 1` | The overlap weights sum to one. Proof: pull the common denominator out of the sum via `Finset.sum_div`, then cancel | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.finite_weights_eq_sum` | `P.overlapWeightedATE = ∑ g, P.overlapWeight g * P.τ g` | **Finite-partition OLS weights** (`prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-finite-weights`): the saturated-OLS estimand decomposes as `Σ ω_g τ_g`. Proof: distribute the denominator over the numerator sum and rearrange via `ring` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.equal_groups_weight_eq_pi` | `(∀ g, P.p g = 1/2) → ∀ g, P.overlapWeight g = P.π g` | If every `p_g = 1/2`, the normalized overlap weight equals the cell probability. Proof: each `cellOverlap g = 1/4`, denominator becomes `(1/4)·Σπ_g = 1/4`, and the `1/4`'s cancel | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.equal_groups_collapses` | `(∀ g, P.p g = 1/2) → P.overlapWeightedATE = ∑ g, P.π g * P.τ g` | **Equal-cell-share collapse** (finite half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-equal-groups`): if every `p_g = 1/2`, then `β_sat = Σ π_g τ_g`. Proof: rewrite via `finite_weights_eq_sum`, then apply `equal_groups_weight_eq_pi` cell by cell | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.homogeneous_collapses` | `(∀ g, P.τ g = τ₀) → P.overlapWeightedATE = τ₀` | **Homogeneous-effect collapse** (saturated half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-homogeneous`): if `τ_g = τ₀` for every cell, then `β_sat = τ₀`. Proof: pull `τ₀` out of the numerator, cancel with the denominator | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perTreatedWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | **Per-treated-observation leverage factor** `1 − p_g` in cell `g` (Remark `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-group-size`): each treated unit in cell `g` contributes weight proportional to the untreated share | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.perUntreatedWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | **Per-untreated-observation leverage factor** `p_g` in cell `g`: each untreated unit in cell `g` contributes weight proportional to the treated share | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.cellOverlap_eq_perWeights_mul` | `P.cellOverlap g = P.perUntreatedWeight g * P.perTreatedWeight g` | The cell overlap variance factors as the product of the two per-unit leverage factors. Proof: `unfold` + `ring` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perTreatedWeight_antitone` | `P.p g ≤ P.p h → P.perTreatedWeight h ≤ P.perTreatedWeight g` | **Headline monotonicity** (`rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-group-size`): the per-treated leverage factor `1 − p_g` is antitone in `p_g` — the smaller the treated share, the larger each treated unit's weight. Formalizes the paper's title "Smaller Groups Get Larger Weights" for the treated side. Proof: `linarith` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perUntreatedWeight_monotone` | `P.p g ≤ P.p h → P.perUntreatedWeight g ≤ P.perUntreatedWeight h` | The per-untreated leverage factor `p_g` is monotone in `p_g` — the larger the treated share, the larger each untreated unit's weight. Symmetrically to `perTreatedWeight_antitone`. Proof: `exact hph` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr` | `structure { ρ, τ_ATT, τ_ATU, β_ols, w₁, w₀ : ℝ, ρ_nonneg, ρ_le_one, twoComponent : β_ols = w₁·τ_ATT + w₀·τ_ATU, weights_sum_one : w₁ + w₀ = 1, equalDispersion : w₁ = 1−ρ }` | Słoczyński opposite-group representation (`ass:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-opposite-group`). **G2 closed:** the fields are now the paper's genuine structural assumption — the two-component representation `β_ols = w₁·τ_ATT + w₀·τ_ATU` with `w₁+w₀=1` (recorded separately, per `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-no-covariate-caveat`, NOT derived from the saturated formula, which is a different estimand) — plus the equal-dispersion specialization `w₁ = 1−ρ`. The opposite-group identity is no longer a field: it is the derived theorem `represents`. The general dispersion-based weight formula is not specified by the source and is not formalized | **Defined** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.tau_ATE R` | `OppositeGroupRepr → ℝ` | ATE in two-component form, `τ_ATE = ρ·τ_ATT + (1−ρ)·τ_ATU` (`def:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-att-atu`) | **Defined** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.w0_eq_rho` | `R.w₀ = R.ρ` | The untreated weight equals the treated share in the equal-dispersion case, forced by `weights_sum_one` and `equalDispersion`. Proof: `rw [equalDispersion]` into `weights_sum_one`, `linarith` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.represents` | `R.β_ols = (1−R.ρ)·R.τ_ATT + R.ρ·R.τ_ATU` | **Słoczyński opposite-group identity** (`prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-opposite-group`), now **derived** (G2) rather than assumed: substitute the equal-dispersion weights `w₁ = 1−ρ`, `w₀ = ρ` into the two-component representation. Proof: `rw [twoComponent, equalDispersion, w0_eq_rho]` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.equal_groups_eq_ATE` | `R.ρ = 1/2 → R.β_ols = R.tau_ATE` | **Equal-group-size collapse to the ATE** (top half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-equal-groups`): if `ρ = 1/2`, the opposite-group OLS coefficient equals the ATE. Proof: substitute `ρ = 1/2` into both `represents` and `tau_ATE` and `ring` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.homogeneous_eq_constant` | `R.τ_ATT = τ₀ → R.τ_ATU = τ₀ → R.β_ols = τ₀` | **Homogeneous-effect collapse for the ATT/ATU representation** (ATT/ATU half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-homogeneous`): if `τ_ATT = τ_ATU = τ₀`, then `β_ols = τ₀` for every `ρ`. Proof: substitute into `represents` and `ring` | **Proved** |

### `OLSWeightDecomposition/GeneralCondExp.lean` — general continuous-covariate theorem via σ-algebra condExp (audit G4)

The abstract-conditioning version of `thm:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-overlap-weighted-ate` for an *arbitrary* covariate σ-algebra `m` (not the finite partition), using Mathlib's `μ[f|m]` conditional expectation. Closes audit G4 (general continuous-X theorem, previously absent). Binary `D`, propensity `p := μ[D|m]`, residual `D̃ := D − p`. CMI is stated in the mean-independence **factoring form** `μ[D·Y(d)|m] =ᵐ μ[D|m]·μ[Y(d)|m]` — a documented *consequence* (weakening) of the paper's full CMI `E[Y(d)|D,X]=E[Y(d)|X]`, hence faithful. All declarations axiom-free.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `denom_identity` | `(hm : m ≤ mΩ) (D) (hDbin) (hD_int) → ∫ (D − μ[D|m])² = ∫ μ[D|m]·(1 − μ[D|m])` | **Denominator (A).** The residualized treatment variance integrates to the overlap factor. Proof: expand `(D−p)² =ᵐ D − 2pD + p²` (binary `D²=ᵐD`), pull `p` out of `μ[·|m]`, integrate via `integral_condExp` — gives `∫p − ∫p² = ∫p(1−p)` | **Proved** |
| `numer_identity` | `(hm) (D Y Y0 Y1) (hDbin) (integrabilities) (hcons) (hCMI1) (hCMI0) → ∫ (D − μ[D|m])·Y = ∫ μ[D|m]·(1−μ[D|m])·(μ[Y1|m] − μ[Y0|m])` | **Numerator (B).** Proof: `(D−p)·Y =ᵐ (1−p)(D·Y1) − p((1−D)Y0)` by consistency+binary; pull out the `m`-measurable `1−p`,`p`; apply CMI factoring → `∫p(1−p)(M1−M0)` | **Proved** |
| `saturated_ols_overlap_weighted_ate` | `(hm) (D Y Y0 Y1) (hDbin) (integrabilities) (hcons) (hCMI1) (hCMI0) (_hoverlap : 0 < ∫ μ[D|m]·(1−μ[D|m])) →` `∫(D−p)Y / ∫(D−p)² = ∫ p(1−p)(μ[Y1|m]−μ[Y0|m]) / ∫ p(1−p)` | **G4 general overlap-weighted ATE.** The saturated FWL estimand equals the overlap-weighted conditional ATE for arbitrary `X`. Proof: rewrite numerator/denominator by (B),(A). The ratio identity is unconditional; `_hoverlap` is the paper's nondegeneracy regime | **Proved** |

### `OLSWeightDecomposition/OverlapWeightedATE.lean` (+ `Support/`) — probability-space bridge (Layer B)

`OverlapWeightedATE.lean` hosts the finite-cell headline theorem; the supporting measure-theoretic machinery lives in the `Support/` subfolder:

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `Support/Basic.lean` | Saturated-control class, cell statistics, saturated propensity/mean regression, and elementary cell bounds |
| `Support/Integrals.lean` | Reusable finite-cell integral identities and saturated-sum reduction lemmas |
| `Support/Orthogonality.lean` | Per-cell orthogonality facts for treatment/outcome residuals and the observed-outcome `MemLp` bridge |
| `Support/Partition.lean` | Construction of `partitionOf` and the residualization witnesses `residWitnessD` / `residWitnessY` |
| `Support/PerCell.lean` | Per-cell denominator and numerator identities used in the final bridge proof |
| `OverlapWeightedATE.lean` | Public headline theorem `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_overlap` |

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `saturatedClass μ G G_meas` | `LinearL2Class μ` | Finite span of the cell indicators `𝟙{G=g}` used as the saturated-control nuisance class | **Defined** |
| `cellMass` / `cellShare` / `cellTau` | `Measure Ω → … → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Extract cell probability, treated share, and cell treatment effect from a probability-space representation | **Defined** |
| `propensity` / `meanReg` | `Measure Ω → … → Ω → ℝ` | Cell-wise saturated projections for treatment and outcome, represented as finite sums of cell indicators | **Defined** |
| `partitionOf` | `Measure Ω → … → FinitePartition 𝒢` | Builds the Layer A finite partition from the probability-space cell statistics and overlap hypothesis | **Defined** |
| `residWitnessD` / `residWitnessY` | `ResidualizationWitness μ (saturatedClass μ G G_meas) D/Y` | Packages the saturated residualization witnesses; orthogonality is proved by reducing to per-cell indicator moments | **Proved** |
| `denom_per_cell` | `∫ (D−propensity)^2 · 𝟙{G=g} = cellMass g * cellShare g * (1−cellShare g)` | Per-cell denominator identity for the FWL denominator | **Proved** |
| `num_per_cell` | `∫ (D−propensity) · Y · 𝟙{G=g} = cellMass g * cellShare g * (1−cellShare g) * cellTau g` | Per-cell numerator identity under consistency and finite-cell CMI | **Proved** |
| `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_overlap` | `residualizedCoefficient μ (saturatedClass μ G B_meas_G) (residWitnessY … (Y_memLp_of_consistency …)) (residWitnessD …) = FinitePartition.overlapWeightedATE (partitionOf …)` | Layer B bridge: assumes the finite-cell integrated-indicator condition implied by CMI (not an equivalence to the full conditional-mean clause), sums the per-cell numerator and denominator identities over `g`, removes the saturated outcome projection by residual orthogonality, and identifies the resulting ratio with the Layer A overlap-weighted ATE. **G7 fix:** `Y_memLp` is no longer a hypothesis — derived inline via `Y_memLp_of_consistency` from `B_consis+B_binary+B_Y0_L2+B_Y1_L2`. **G6 fix:** `B_Dtilde_pos` is no longer a hypothesis — denominator positivity derived from `B_overlap` via `hDen_sum`. Signature now has 8 hypotheses (was 10). **G4 (deferred):** general continuous-X theorem for arbitrary covariate deferred per `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-lean` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-StaggeredTWFEDecomposition. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/{Panel,Pairwise,Decomposition,Bridge,Causal}.lean` — Goodman-Bacon (2021) TWFE staggered-timing decomposition

Specific-paper specialization of the residualization framework to Goodman-Bacon (2021, *Journal of Econometrics*). Layer A (`Panel.lean`, `Pairwise.lean`, `Decomposition.lean`) is pure finite-cell algebra over a balanced cohort-period law: given a `CohortPanel 𝒢 T` carrying cohort shares `p_g`, adoption dates `A_g ∈ WithTop (Fin T)`, and cell outcome means `Y_{gt}`, the population TWFE coefficient `β^twfe := E[D̃·Y]/E[D̃²]` decomposes as a positively-weighted average of three families of 2×2 DID contrasts (treated-vs-never `Δ^TN`, early-vs-late `Δ^EL`, late-vs-early `Δ^LE`). `Pairwise.lean` is the intermediate algebra layer: it first rewrites denominator/numerator quantities into ordered pairwise centered-treatment contributions, then states the local TN/treated-treated window lemmas needed by `Decomposition.lean`. Layer B (`Bridge.lean`) instantiates the residualization core on a probability space with the balanced cohort-period law and span(cohort + period indicators) as the saturated nuisance class. Layer C (`Causal.lean`) promotes the algebraic contrasts to causal statements about window-specific ATTs under consistency, no anticipation, and pairwise untreated parallel trends; the `LE` comparison carries the explicit bad-comparison term that motivates Goodman-Bacon's caveat about TWFE under treatment-effect heterogeneity. All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.Panel.EstimandCharacterization.StaggeredTWFEDecomposition`. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/goodman_bacon_twfe_timing.md`.

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Panel.lean` — cell-statistics record and primitives (Layer A1–A4)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CompTag` | `inductive { TN, EL, LE }` | Three-valued tag for the type of admissible 2×2 comparison | **Defined** |
| `CohortPanel 𝒢 T` | `structure { p, A, Y, T_pos, p_pos, p_sum_one }` | Staggered-adoption cohort panel: cohort shares `p_g`, adoption dates `A_g : WithTop (Fin T)` (`⊤ = ∞`), cohort-period factual means `Y_{gt}`, plus positivity and probability-vector constraints | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionDate.{le, lt, isFin, isInf}` | `WithTop (Fin T) → Fin T → Prop` (or `→ Prop`) | Goodman-Bacon local helpers using the same `WithTop (Fin T)` convention as `AdoptionPath`: `A_g ≤ t`, `t < A_g`, `A_g ≠ ⊤`, and `A_g = ⊤` | **Defined** |
| `D` / `barD` / `pCohort` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → … → ℝ` | Treatment indicator `D_{gt} = 1_{A_g ≤ t}`, cohort treatment share `\overline{D}_g = T⁻¹ ∑_t D_{gt}`, and overall share `E[D] = ∑_g p_g \overline{D}_g` | **Defined** |
| `Dtilde` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → Fin T → ℝ` | Residualized treatment via the explicit double-demeaning formula `D − \overline{D}_g − E[D∣T=t] + E[D]` | **Defined** |
| `VD` / `betaTWFE` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → ℝ` | Residualized-treatment variance `V_D = ∑_{g,t} (p_g/T) D̃_{gt}²` and the TWFE estimand `β^twfe = (∑ (p_g/T) D̃ Y) / V_D` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-twfe`) | **Defined** |
| `Ybar P g S` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → Finset (Fin T) → ℝ` | Window mean `\overline{Y}_{g,S} = \|S\|⁻¹ ∑_{t ∈ S} Y_{gt}` | **Defined** |
| `S0_TN` / `S1_TN` / `S0_EL` / `S1_EL` / `S0_LE` / `S1_LE` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → … → Finset (Fin T)` | Six comparison windows for the three 2×2 comparison types (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-comparisons`) | **Defined** |
| `Δ_TN` / `Δ_EL` / `Δ_LE` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | The three 2×2 DID contrasts (treated-vs-never, early-vs-late, late-vs-early) | **Defined** |
| `lambdaTN` / `q` / `mu` / `lambdaEL` / `lambdaLE` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → … → ℝ` | Raw weights `λ^TN, λ^EL, λ^LE` and the timing-pair gap `q_{eℓ} = \overline{D}_e − \overline{D}_ℓ` plus splitting fraction `μ_{eℓ} = (1−\overline{D}_e)/(1−q)` | **Defined** |
| `Lambda` / `w_TN` / `w_EL` / `w_LE` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → … → ℝ` | Aggregate raw-weight denominator `Λ` and the three normalized weights `w = λ/Λ` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-weights`) | **Defined** |
| `admissible` / `𝒦` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → CompTag × 𝒢 × 𝒢 → Prop`, `Finset (CompTag × 𝒢 × 𝒢)` | Admissibility predicate and the comparison index set, filtering admissible tagged cohort pairs | **Defined** |
| `weight` / `contrast` / `lambdaWeight` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → CompTag × 𝒢 × 𝒢 → ℝ` | Unified normalized weight, 2×2 contrast, and raw weight on the full index, returning `0` outside the admissible set | **Defined** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Pairwise.lean` — pairwise centered-treatment algebra (Layer A intermediate)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `centeredD P g t` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → Fin T → ℝ` | Cohort-demeaned treatment path `D_{gt} − \overline D_g`; the object centered across cohorts at each period before applying the finite weighted covariance/variance identities | **Defined** |
| `vdPairContribution P g u` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Ordered-pair contribution `(p_g p_u)/(2T) · Σ_t (centeredD_g(t) − centeredD_u(t))²` in the pairwise representation of `V_D` | **Defined** |
| `numPairContribution P g u` | `CohortPanel 𝒢 T → 𝒢 → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Ordered-pair contribution `(p_g p_u)/(2T) · Σ_t (centeredD_g(t) − centeredD_u(t))·(Y_g(t) − Y_u(t))` in the pairwise representation of the TWFE numerator | **Defined** |
| `Dtilde_eq_centeredD_sub_weighted_mean` | `Dtilde P g t = centeredD P g t − ∑ h, P.p h * centeredD P h t` | Rewrites the explicit double-demeaning formula as cohort-weighted centering of the cohort-demeaned treatment path at each period | **Proved** |
| `VD_eq_pairwise_centeredD` | `VD P = ∑ g, ∑ u, vdPairContribution P g u` | First-stage denominator rewrite via `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_var`; after this, `Decomposition.lean` only needs adoption-window grouping | **Proved** |
| `twfe_numerator_eq_pairwise_centeredD_Y` | `∑ (p_g/T)·D̃·Y = ∑ g, ∑ u, numPairContribution P g u` | First-stage numerator rewrite via `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_cov`; after this, `Decomposition.lean` only needs adoption-window grouping | **Proved** |
| `TN_pair_vd_contribution_eq_gap` | pairwise TN denominator contribution equals the treated-vs-never raw gap factor | Local denominator window algebra for a treated-vs-never pair; now factors through `vdPairContribution_add_swap`, `TN_centeredD_gap_eq`, and the proved `TN_time_variance_eq_gap` Bernoulli-variance helper | **Proved** |
| `TT_pair_vd_contribution_eq_gap` | pairwise treated-treated denominator contribution equals the raw timing-pair gap factor | Local denominator window algebra for a treated-treated ordered timing pair; proves the early-late treatment gap is the middle-window indicator and applies the finite binary-variance helper | **Proved** |
| `TN_pair_contribution_eq_lambda_delta` | pairwise TN numerator contribution equals `λ_TN·Δ_TN` | Local numerator window algebra for a treated-vs-never pair; splits the centered binary covariance over `S0_TN`/`S1_TN` and rewrites the restricted sums as `Ybar` means | **Proved** |
| `TT_pair_contribution_eq_lambda_delta_sum` | pairwise treated-treated numerator contribution equals `λ_EL·Δ_EL + λ_LE·Δ_LE` | Local numerator window algebra for a treated-treated timing pair; partitions time into pre-early, middle, and post-late windows, uses the complement mean decomposition, and discharges the `mu` cardinal-share algebra | **Proved** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Decomposition.lean` — five Layer A theorems (A5.1–A5.5)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `weights_nonneg` | `0 ≤ lambdaWeight P k` | All admissible raw weights are nonnegative. Proof: case-split on `CompTag`; uses `0 ≤ p_g`, `0 ≤ \overline{D}_g ≤ 1`, and the cardinality bounds on `q_{eℓ}` | **Proved** |
| `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD` | `Lambda P = VD P` | **Denominator identity** of `thm:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-decomposition`: the residualized-treatment variance equals the sum of all pairwise raw weights (key algebraic content underlying the FWL denominator step) | `sorry` (placeholder) |
| `twfe_numerator_eq_lambda_delta_sum` | `∑ (p_g/T)·D̃·Y = ∑ λ^TN·Δ^TN + ∑ (λ^EL·Δ^EL + λ^LE·Δ^LE)` | **Numerator identity**: TWFE numerator decomposes by unordered cohort pairs into raw-weight × 2×2 DID contributions | `sorry` (placeholder) |
| `weights_sum_one` | `0 < VD P → ∑ k ∈ 𝒦 P, weight P k = 1` | Normalized comparison weights sum to one. Proof: divide `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD` by `Λ = V_D > 0` | **Proved** (modulo `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD`) |
| `twfe_eq_weighted_avg` | `betaTWFE P = ∑ k ∈ 𝒦 P, weight P k * contrast P k` | **Headline theorem** (`thm:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-decomposition`): TWFE coefficient is the weighted average of the 2×2 DID contrasts, with the `VD P = 0` case handled by Lean's totalized division. Proof combines A5.2 + A5.3 with the definition of `weight` | **Proved** (modulo A5.2/A5.3) |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Bridge/{Basic,Integrals,Orthogonality,Partition,PerCell}.lean` + `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Bridge.lean` — probability-space bridge (Layer B)

Layer B is split across five sub-files (mirroring Sloczynski's Bridge layout) plus a thin facade: `Bridge/Basic.lean` holds the panel class, cell statistics, and projections; `Bridge/Integrals.lean` holds reusable cell integral identities and the per-axis-to-whole-class orthogonality decoupler; `Bridge/Orthogonality.lean` holds per-axis residual orthogonality (under the balanced-cell hypothesis `IsBalancedPanelLaw`); `Bridge/Partition.lean` builds the `panelOf` Layer A panel and the residualization witnesses; `Bridge/PerCell.lean` proves the per-cohort-period denominator and numerator identities; `Bridge.lean` states the headline.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `panelClass μ G T_rv G_meas T_meas` | `LinearL2Class μ` | Linear `L²` class spanning (a.e.) the family of cohort indicators `𝟙{G = g}` and period indicators `𝟙{T_rv = t}`. Membership predicate uses two coefficient maps `cG : 𝒢 → ℝ` and `cT : Fin T → ℝ` (B1) | **Defined** |
| `cellMass` / `cohortMass` / `periodMass` / `cellMean` / `cohortBarD` | `Measure Ω → … → ℝ` | Extract cohort-period cell mass `μ{G=g, T=t}.toReal`, cohort mass, period mass, cell mean of `Y`, and cohort treatment share from a probability-space representation (B2) | **Defined** |
| `panelOf` | `Measure Ω → … → CohortPanel 𝒢 T` | Builds the Layer A `CohortPanel` from the probability-space cell statistics plus an explicit adoption date `A : 𝒢 → WithTop (Fin T)` and the balanced-law side conditions (B2) | **Defined** |
| `panelPropensity` / `panelMeanReg` | `Measure Ω → … → Ω → ℝ` | Cohort + period saturated projections for treatment and outcome (the closed-form residual via two-way demeaning); each lies in `panelClass` (B3) | **Defined** |
| `panelPropensity_mem_panelClass` / `panelMeanReg_mem_panelClass` | `(panelClass μ G T_rv G_meas T_meas).mem (panelPropensity μ D G T_rv)` (resp. `panelMeanReg`) | Membership lemmas for the projections, with explicit `(cG, cT)` coefficient maps | **Proved** |
| `IsBalancedPanelLaw μ G T_rv` | `∀ g t, cellMass = cohortMass · periodMass` | The balanced cohort-period law hypothesis; Lean form of Goodman-Bacon (2021)'s `ℙ(G=g, T=t) = p_g · q_t`. Used as `B_balanced` throughout the Layer B stack | **Defined** |
| `indicator_{cohort,period,panel_cell}_memLp` | `MemLp (𝟙_{cell}) 2 μ` | Cell indicators are square-integrable on a probability measure | **Proved** |
| `integral_{cohort,period,panel_cell}_indicator_one_eq_{cohort,period,cell}Mass` | `∫ 𝟙_{cell} dμ = mass` | Indicator integrates to its cell mass | **Proved** |
| `{cohort,period,panel_cell}_integral_div_mul_{cohort,period,cell}Mass` | `(∫ F·𝟙_{cell} dμ / mass) · mass = ∫ F·𝟙_{cell} dμ` | Division-multiplication cancels even at zero-mass cells (a.e.-vanishing indicator) | **Proved** |
| `cohortBarD_mul_cohortMass` | `cohortBarD g · cohortMass g = ∫ D · 𝟙{G=g} dμ` | Defining identity of `cohortBarD` after multiplying back the cohort mass | **Proved** |
| `panelPropensity_eq_hat_of_mem` / `panelMeanReg_eq_hat_of_mem` | On cell `{G=g ∧ T_rv=t}`, projection equals the closed-form `panelPropensityHat` / `panelMeanRegHat` constant | Cell-constancy identities for the in-class projections; the two-axis analogues of Sloczynski's `propensity_eq_cellShare_of_mem` | **Proved** |
| `integral_mul_panelClass_eq_zero_of_axes` | per-axis orthogonality ⇒ orthogonality against every member of `panelClass` | Decouples the cohort-axis and period-axis orthogonality from the witness-level obligation. Proof: split `V · (Σ_g cG · 𝟙{G=g} + Σ_t cT · 𝟙{T_rv=t})` into two finite sums and apply per-axis vanishing | **Proved** |
| `integral_eq_sum_{cohort,period,panel_cell}` | `∫ F dμ = ∑ over cells ∫ F · 𝟙_{cell} dμ` | Integrate by summing over cells of the appropriate axis(es) | **Proved** |
| `residD_{cohort,period}_orthogonal` / `residY_{cohort,period}_orthogonal` | `∫ Vtilde · 𝟙_{axis} dμ = 0` | Per-axis orthogonality of the `D` / `Y` residual against cohort and period indicators. Cohort axis is automatic from the defining identity of `cohortBarD`; period axis uses `IsBalancedPanelLaw` to make the cross-cohort sum collapse to `∫ D dμ` (resp. `∫ Y dμ`) | **Proved** |
| `residWitnessD_panel` / `residWitnessY_panel` | `ResidualizationWitness μ (panelClass μ G T_rv G_meas T_meas) D/Y` | Full residualization witness against `panelClass`. Orthogonality field discharged via `integral_mul_panelClass_eq_zero_of_axes` plus the four per-axis lemmas, taking `IsBalancedPanelLaw` as input | **Proved** |
| `panelDtilde μ D G T_rv g t` | `(∫ D · 𝟙_{cell} dμ / cellMass) − panelPropensityHat g t` | Cell-level residualized-treatment value; on `panelOf …` panels equals the Layer A `Dtilde` under the side conditions in the bridge theorem | **Defined** |
| `denom_per_cell_panel` | `∫ Vtilde² · 𝟙_{cell g t} dμ = cellMass · panelDtilde²` | Per-cell denominator identity: Vtilde is a.e. constant equal to `panelDtilde` on each cell (by the cell-constancy hypothesis on `D` and `panelPropensity_eq_hat_of_mem`); integrate against the cell indicator | **Proved** |
| `num_per_cell_panel` | `∫ Vtilde · Y · 𝟙_{cell g t} dμ = cellMass · panelDtilde · cellMean` | Per-cell numerator identity: same constancy argument as `denom_per_cell_panel`, then use `panel_cell_integral_div_mul_cellMass` to identify `∫ Y · 𝟙_{cell} dμ = cellMass · cellMean` | **Proved** |
| `bridge_Dtilde_sq_eq_VD` | `∫ wD.Vtilde² dμ = VD (panelOf …)` | Companion B4 identity: the FWL denominator equals the Layer A `V_D`. Proof: `integral_eq_sum_panel_cell` ⊕ `denom_per_cell_panel` per cell ⊕ `hLaw` (`cellMass = cohortMass / T`) ⊕ `hDtilde_eq` (`panelDtilde = Layer A Dtilde`) ⊕ `VD` definition | **Proved** |
| `bridge_VD_pos_iff_Dtilde_sq_pos` | `0 < VD (panelOf …) ↔ 0 < ∫ wD.Vtilde² dμ` | G9 bridge: `VD P > 0` iff the FWL denominator is positive (immediate via `bridge_Dtilde_sq_eq_VD`); lets callers derive `hDtilde_pos` from `hVD_pos` | **Proved** |
| `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_twfe` | `residualizedCoefficient μ panelClass wY wD = betaTWFE (panelOf …)` | **Headline B4**: the population FWL coefficient on the panel class equals the Layer A finite-cell `betaTWFE`. Proof: replace `wY.Vtilde` by `Y` in the numerator using `wD.orthogonal panelMeanReg_mem_panelClass`; expand both numerator and denominator by `integral_eq_sum_panel_cell` ⊕ per-cell identities; substitute `hLaw` / `hDtilde_eq`; identify with `betaTWFE` definition | **Proved** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Causal.lean` — causal characterization corollaries (Layer C)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Ybar0` | `(𝒢 → Fin T → ℝ) → 𝒢 → Finset (Fin T) → ℝ` | Window mean of a potential-outcome map (e.g. `Y(∞)` or `Y(A_g)`); same shape as `Ybar` but operates on a generic potential-outcome map rather than `P.Y` | **Defined** |
| `ATT_window` | `(𝒢 → Fin T → ℝ) → (𝒢 → Fin T → ℝ) → 𝒢 → Finset (Fin T) → ℝ` | Window-specific ATT `\|S\|⁻¹ ∑_{t ∈ S} (Y1_{gt} − Y0_{gt})` in the two-state layer where `Y0 = Y(∞)` and `Y1 = Y(A_g)` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-window-att`) | **Defined** |
| `CausalAssumptions P Y0 Y1` | `Prop` | Bundles consistency on treated/untreated cells, no anticipation `t < A_g → Y1_{gt} = Y0_{gt}`, and three pairwise untreated-parallel-trends fields (one per comparison type, matching the LaTeX `ass:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-causal`); this is the two-state simplification sufficient for the LE bad-comparison corollary, not the full `Y_gt(a)` adoption-date-indexed family | **Defined** |
| `CausalAssumptions.AdoptionDate.not_le_of_lt` / `lt_of_isInf` | `WithTop`-order helpers | `AdoptionDate.lt` rules out `AdoptionDate.le`; never-treated cohorts are untreated in every period | **Proved** |
| `CausalAssumptions.Ybar_eq_Ybar0_on_S0_TN` / `_of_inf` / `_on_S0_EL` / `_late_on_S1_EL` / `_late_on_S0_LE` | `Ybar P g S = Ybar0 Y0 g S` (under the appropriate side conditions) | Cell-by-cell consistency reduces factual `Ybar` to never-treated `Ybar0` on each cohort-window pair where the cohort is untreated | **Proved** |
| `CausalAssumptions.Ybar_eq_Ybar0_add_ATT_of_treated` | `(∀ t ∈ S, A_g ≤ t) → Ybar P g S = Ybar0 Y0 g S + ATT_window Y0 Y1 g S` | On a treated window, factual `Ybar` decomposes as never-treated `Ybar0` plus the window ATT. Used to extract ATT contributions from each comparison's treated arm | **Proved** |
| `Δ_TN_eq_ATT` | `Δ_TN P g u = ATT_window Y0 Y1 g (S1_TN P g)` | **Layer C corollary 1** (`cor:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-causal-characterization`): under `CausalAssumptions`, the treated-vs-never contrast identifies the treated cohort's window ATT. Proof: reduce `Ybar` of untreated arms to `Ybar0`, apply `parallelTrends_TN` to cancel untreated trends, isolate the treated-arm ATT contribution | **Proved** |
| `Δ_EL_eq_ATT` | `Δ_EL P e ℓ = ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S1_EL P e ℓ)` | **Layer C corollary 2**: the early-vs-late-before-late contrast identifies the early cohort's window ATT. Proof: same shape as `Δ_TN_eq_ATT`; uses the late-cohort untreated identity on both EL windows and `parallelTrends_EL` | **Proved** |
| `Δ_LE_eq_bad_comparison` | `Δ_LE P e ℓ = ATT_window Y0 Y1 ℓ (S1_LE P e ℓ) − (ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S1_LE P e ℓ) − ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S0_LE P e ℓ))` | **Layer C corollary 3**: the late-vs-early-after-early contrast identifies the late cohort's ATT *minus* a bad-comparison term — the change in the already-treated early cohort's ATT across the comparison windows. Proof: decompose both arms' factual `Ybar` into `Ybar0 + ATT_window` (each cell is treated for both `e` and `ℓ` in the appropriate windows), apply `parallelTrends_LE` to cancel untreated trends, isolate the ATT residuals | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-EventStudyContamination. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/EventStudyContamination/{Setup,Conventional,CellGrid,Contamination,InteractionWeighted,Basic}.lean` — Sun-Abraham (2021) Event-Study Contamination and IW

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization for Sun-Abraham (2021). `Setup.lean` defines a finite staggered-adoption event-study system with adoption paths encoded as `WithTop (Fin T)`, admissible cohort-relative-time cells, definitional CATT primitives, and the named causal restrictions. `Conventional.lean` proves the residualized-design weight normalizations, conventional TWFE contamination representation, and apparent-pretrends corollary using finite-cell residualized indicator quantities; it consumes the FWL ratio `D.mu = conventionalMuRatio` and the `ConventionalResidualization` orthogonality conditions as hypotheses. `CellGrid.lean` **derives** both: it builds a genuine weighted-support projection on the cohort-relative-time cell grid (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/*`) and proves `cellGrid_mu_eq_conventionalMuRatio` (closes gap G3) and `cellGrid_provides_residualization` (closes gaps G4/G9) — replacing the previously-assumed `D.mu = beta` bridge field and the assumed orthogonality fields. `Contamination.lean` exposes the de-scaffolded public theorems that take the genuine `CellGridResidualization` input directly. `InteractionWeighted.lean` keeps the IW DID construction separate and proves the cohort-level DID equality plus convex IW aggregation. `Basic.lean` is the import facade. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/sun_abraham_event_study.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `EventStudyContamination.EventStudySystem` | `structure (T : ℕ) { time, cohorts, cohortShare, cellMass, observedPathMean, observedMean, treatedMean, untreatedMean, untreatedPathMean }` | Finite staggered-adoption event-study system with integer period map, finite cohort support, `WithTop (Fin T)` adoption paths (`⊤` as never treated), cell masses, observed means, treated potential-outcome means, and never-treated potential-outcome means | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.finitePath` / `isNeverTreated` / `isEventuallyTreated` / `relTime` / `absorbingTreatment` | adoption-path and relative-time helpers | Sun-Abraham-compatible wrappers around `AdoptionPath` plus relative event time; encode finite adoption paths, `∞`, eventual treatment, and the absorbing treatment path `1{h ≤ t}` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.targetPeriods` / `baselinePeriods` / `AdmissibleCell` / `admissibleCells` / `cohortsAtEvent` | finite support helpers | Build finite target/baseline period sets, admissible cohort-relative-time cells, finite cell supports, and `G_e` cohort sets from an explicit event-time support | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellMassAtEvent` / `observedCellMean` / `meanCellContrast` / `CATT` / `pathTargetMean` / `pathBaselineMean` | finite-cell averages | Provide the finite mass, observed mean, treated-minus-untreated contrast, definitional CATT `CATT(g,e) = meanCellContrast(g,e)`, and path-level target/baseline means used by conventional and IW statements; this CATT averages over all periods realizing `(g,e)` and coincides with the source point-period CATT when that target-period set is singleton | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.Consistency` / `NoAnticipation` / `MeanParallelUntreated` / `EventStudyCausalRestrictions` | propositions/structure | Named Sun-Abraham causal restrictions: consistency, no anticipation, and mean parallel untreated paths whose additive component is stated using `AdditiveSpan.IsUnitTimeAdditive` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.CATT_eq_zero_of_noAnticipation` | `NoAnticipation → e < 0 → CATT g e = 0` | No-anticipation implication that pre-treatment CATTs vanish | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.observedCellMean_eq_fixedEffects_add_CATT` | `EventStudyCausalRestrictions → ∃ alpha lambda, observedCellMean = card⁻¹ * ∑ target (alpha g + lambda t) + CATT(g,e)` | Averaged cell-mean decomposition used in the contamination proof | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.ConventionalDesign` | `structure { eventSupport, includedEvents, omittedEvent, displayedEvent, Rdot, mu }` | Finite conventional event-study design for the coefficient on `R^l`, carrying included/omitted relative times, residualized indicator values, and `mu_l` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellAverage` / `IsEventStudyNuisance` / `ConventionalResidualization` / `FiniteIntegrable` | finite residualization hypotheses | Cell-average helper for nuisance functions, finite-cell nuisance span with shared `AdditiveSpan` unit/time base plus Sun-Abraham included event indicators, the residualized-design orthogonality conditions (now derived — see `cellGrid_provides_residualization`), and the finite integrability side condition | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.residualDenom` / `residualNumerator` / `conventionalMuRatio` / `omega` | finite residualized design quantities | Denominator `E[Rdot^l R^l]`, numerator `E[Rdot^l Y]`, residualized coefficient ratio, and Sun-Abraham cell weights `omega(l,g,e)` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.desired_event_weights_sum_one` | `∑_{g ∈ G_l} omega(l,g,l) = 1` | Desired-event-time weight normalization | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.other_included_event_weights_sum_zero` | `e ∈ L → e ≠ l → ∑_{g ∈ G_e} omega(l,g,e) = 0` | Zero total weight on other included event-time indicators | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation` | `mu_l = ∑_{(g,e)∈C} omega(l,g,e) * CATT(g,e)` | Conventional Sun-Abraham TWFE event-study contamination representation, taking the FWL ratio `D.mu = conventionalMuRatio D` and the `ConventionalResidualization` orthogonality conditions as hypotheses (both now genuinely derivable via `CellGrid.lean`) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation_split` | desired-event component plus `e ≠ l` contamination component | Split form of the contamination representation | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.apparent_pretrends_from_post_treatment` | lead coefficient equals the sum over cells with `e ≥ 0` under `EventStudyCausalRestrictions` and a negative displayed event time | Apparent-pretrends corollary; derives vanishing pre-treatment CATTs from no anticipation | **Proved** |
