| `EventStudySystem.CellIndex` / `cellTotalMass` / `cellSupport` (`CellGrid.lean`) | cell-grid empirical population | The admissible-cell subtype index, total admissible cell mass `Z`, and the `WeightedSupport` over the cell grid with weight `cellMassAtEvent / Z` (requires positive cell masses + nonempty admissible set) | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.ip_cellSupport` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `cellSupport.ip A∘val B∘val = (∑ cellMassAtEvent·A·B)/Z` | The cell-grid weighted inner product reproduces the finite-cell sum (divided by total mass) — the bridge turning weighted-projection facts into the finite-cell orthogonality conditions | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellNuisance` / `cellAverage_mem_cellNuisance` / `eventIndicator_mem_cellNuisance` (`CellGrid.lean`) | cell-nuisance subspace + memberships | Span of cell-evaluated event-study nuisance functions; every cell-evaluated nuisance and every non-displayed included relative-time indicator lies in it | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.sum_admissible_mul_eventIndicator` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `∑ admissible f·1{e=e'} = ∑_{G_{e'}} f g e'` | Cell-grid filtering identity; the engine behind `hDisplayedExpansion` and `hOtherIncludedOrthogonal` | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellRegressor` / `cellOutcome` / `CellGridResidualization` (`CellGrid.lean`) | genuine residualization input | Displayed-event indicator and observed-cell-mean as cell functions, and the honest hypothesis bundle: positive cell masses, `Rdot` **is** the weighted residual of the regressor against the nuisance span, and `D.mu` solves the weighted normal equations | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellGrid_mu_eq_conventionalMuRatio` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `CellGridResidualization → D.mu = conventionalMuRatio D` | **Closes gap G3**: derives the FWL ratio from the weighted normal equations via `WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` (no assumed `D.mu = beta`) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellGrid_provides_residualization` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `CellGridResidualization → ConventionalResidualization D` | **Closes gaps G4/G9**: derives the three finite-cell orthogonality conditions from `residualize_in_orthogonal` + the filtering identity (no assumed orthogonality fields) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation_of_cellGrid` / `contamination_representation_split_of_cellGrid` / `apparent_pretrends_from_post_treatment_of_cellGrid` (`Contamination.lean`) | genuine public entry points taking `CellGridResidualization` | De-scaffolded contamination theorems: take the genuine cell-grid projection input and derive `ConventionalResidualization` + the FWL ratio internally before applying the finite-cell contamination algebra | **Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.PathConsistency` / `pathConsistency_observed_eq_untreated` | `absorbingTreatment h t = 0 → observedPathMean h t = untreatedPathMean h t` | Path-level consistency primitive + lemma converting an untreated comparison path's observed mean to its never-treated potential-outcome mean; now DERIVES the IW observed/untreated bridge (replacing two assumed `IWSupport` fields) | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.targetPeriods_subsingleton_of_injective` / `CATT_eq_sourceCATT_of_injective` | `Function.Injective P.time → CATT g e = treatedMean g t − untreatedMean g t` at the unique `t∈targetPeriods g e` | Under injective `time`, the cell-averaged CATT collapses to the source point value CATT_{g,e} at period g+e | **Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.sum_convex_mem_Icc` | nonneg weights summing to one keep a weighted average in `[lo,hi]` | Finite convex-combination bound helper | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.IWDesign` / `IWSupport` / `IWComparisonParallelTrends` / `IWFiniteIntegrable` | IW DID data and assumptions | Interaction-weighted cohort set, comparison groups, aggregation weights, support restrictions, comparison-group parallel trends, and finite integrability side condition. NOTE: the former observed/untreated bridge fields `hComparisonObservedBaseline`/`hComparisonObservedTarget` were dropped from `IWSupport` — now derived via `PathConsistency` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.observedTargetMean` / `observedBaselineMean` / `comparisonMass` / `comparisonMeanChange` / `DIDContrast` / `Delta` / `nuIW` | IW estimand primitives | Finite treated-cohort target/baseline means, comparison-group population mass, population-share weighted comparison-group mean change, cohort-specific DID contrast, definitional `Delta(g,l) = DIDContrast(g,l)`, and interaction-weighted event-study estimand `nu_l^IW` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.IW_Delta_eq_CATT` | `PathConsistency → Delta(g,l) = CATT(g,l)` | Cohort-specific IW DID identification under consistency, no anticipation, IW parallel trends, support, path consistency, and finite integrability | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.IW_convex_characterization` | `PathConsistency → hLo → hHi → (∀ g ∈ G_l^IW, Delta(g,l)=CATT(g,l)) ∧ nu_l^IW = ∑ rho(g,l)*CATT(g,l) ∧ lo ≤ nuIW ∧ nuIW ≤ hi` | Convex IW characterization with nonnegative weights summing to one, no contamination from `e ≠ l`, and a 4-way conjunction: now also takes `PathConsistency` and value bounds `hLo`/`hHi` and concludes `lo ≤ nuIW ∧ nuIW ≤ hi` (IW is genuinely a CONVEX combination of target CATTs — the paper's selling point, using the nonneg/sum-one weight hypotheses) | **Proved** |

## 9i0b. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/RDDLimits.lean` — One-sided Limit Identification Engine

Generic topology + measure-theory engine shared by SharpRDD and FuzzyRDD.
All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.PO.RDDLimits`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `oneSidedLimit_eq_right` | `f =ᵐ[π.restrict (Ici c)] g → ContinuousAt g c → support_right → Tendsto f (𝓝[>] c) (𝓝 L) → L = g c` | If `f` agrees a.e. with a continuous `g` on `Ici c` and there is positive mass to the right of `c`, any right-limit `L` of `f` at `c` equals `g c` | **Proved** |
| `oneSidedLimit_eq_left` | symmetric form for `Iio c` / `𝓝[<] c` | Left-side analogue of `oneSidedLimit_eq_right` | **Proved** |
| `value_eq_of_aeEq_right` | `f =ᵐ[π.restrict (Ici c)] g → ContinuousAt f c → ContinuousAt g c → support_right → f c = g c` | If both functions are continuous at `c` and agree a.e. on `Ici c` with positive right support, they are pointwise equal at `c` | **Proved** |
| `value_eq_of_aeEq_left` | symmetric form | Left-side analogue | **Proved** |

---

## 9i'. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/SharpRDD.lean` — Sharp RDD Identification (PO framework)

Formalizes the sharp regression discontinuity example from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(`def:po-sharp-rdd-system`, `def:po-sharp-rdd-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-sharp-rdd`) in the bare potential-outcome framework.  This is the
first exact-ID example in the project to expose pointwise regression-function
representatives and one-sided topological limits at a cutoff.  The proof
faithfully derives `ν =ᵐ[π.restrict] μ_d` on each half-line from sharp cutoff +
consistency + the integral characterisation of regression representatives,
then upgrades a.e. agreement to limit equality via two-sided local support.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsRegressionFunction` | imported from `PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` | Lightweight primitive for a regression representative `f(x) = E[g \| X=x]`; see `## 9i0. PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` for the full declaration and derived lemmas | **Imported** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem P` | `structure { Xvar, Dvar, Yvar, c, hYD }` | Sharp RDD subsystem: real running variable `X`, binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, cutoff `c`, and distinctness of `Y` from the treatment node (for consistency rewrites) | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{factualX, factualD, factualY}` | `P.Ω → ℝ` / `P.Ω → Bool` / `P.Ω → ℝ` | Empty-regime factual running variable, treatment, and outcome | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.YofD` | `Bool → P.Ω → ℝ` | Treatment-specific potential outcome `Y(d) := Yvar.cfUnder Dvar d` | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{measurable_factualX, measurable_factualD, measurable_factualY, measurable_YofD}` | `Measurable …` | Basic measurability lemmas for factual and counterfactual maps | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.dEvent` | `Bool → Set P.Ω` | Factual treatment event `{D = d}` | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, sharpCutoff, mu, nu, mu_isReg, nu_isReg, mu_continuousAt, support_right, support_left, nu_right_limit_exists, nu_left_limit_exists }` | Sharp RDD assumptions: consistency, a.s. sharp cutoff `D = 1{X ≥ c}`, potential-outcome regressions `μ_d` and observable regression `ν` (each a witnessed `IsRegressionFunction`), continuity of each `μ_d` at `c`, per-`ε > 0` positive `(P.μ.map X)`-mass on every one-sided open neighborhood of `c` (two-sided local support), and existence of both one-sided limits of `ν` at `c` (matching the classical `lim` notation in the textbook statement) | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.tau_RDD` | `S.Assumptions → ℝ` | Cutoff-local estimand `μ_1(c) - μ_0(c)`, by definition of the chosen treatment-specific regression representatives | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{nu_right_limit, nu_left_limit}` | `S.Assumptions → ℝ` | Chosen witnesses of `lim_{x↓c} ν(x)` and `lim_{x↑c} ν(x)` via `Classical.choose` of the existence fields | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{tendsto_nu_right_limit, tendsto_nu_left_limit}` | `Tendsto ν (𝓝[>] c) (𝓝 (nu_right_limit hA))` etc. | Spec lemmas: the chosen witnesses are genuine one-sided limits | **Proved** |
| private slice-bridge lemmas | — | Sharp cutoff + consistency: on every measurable slice `X⁻¹ A` with `A ⊆ Ici c` (resp. `A ⊆ Iio c`), `factualY` agrees a.e. with `Y(1)` (resp. `Y(0)`), so the corresponding integrals — and hence the integral characterisations of `ν` and `μ_d` — coincide on these slices | **Proved** |
| private pushforward bridges | `setIntegral_pushforward_eq_right/left` | Translate the slice-level integral identity to the pushforward `(P.μ.map X)` via `setIntegral_map`: integrals of `ν` and `μ_d` agree on every measurable subset of the corresponding half-line | **Proved** |
| private a.e. agreement | `nu_eq_mu_true_ae_restrict_Ici`, `nu_eq_mu_false_ae_restrict_Iio` | Apply `Integrable.ae_eq_of_forall_setIntegral_eq` on the restricted pushforward to upgrade the integral identity to `ν =ᵐ[(P.μ.map X).restrict (Ici c)] μ_1` and the symmetric left version | **Proved** |
| private extraction lemmas | `exists_eq_in_Ioo_right/left` | For every `ε > 0`, the support assumption combined with a.e. agreement yields a point in `(c, c+ε)` (resp. `(c-ε, c)`) where `ν = μ_d`, via `Measure.exists_mem_of_measure_ne_zero_of_ae` | **Proved** |
| private NeBot lemmas | `neBot_right/left` | The agreement-restricted right (resp. left) neighborhood filter `𝓝[>] c ⊓ 𝓟 {ν = μ_d}` is non-trivial | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.nu_right_limit_eq` | `Tendsto ν (𝓝[>] c) (𝓝 L) → L = μ_1 c` | Any right-limit of the observable regression at the cutoff equals `μ_1 c`; proved by uniqueness of limits along the agreement subfilter, using continuity of `μ_1` | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.nu_left_limit_eq` | `Tendsto ν (𝓝[<] c) (𝓝 L) → L = μ_0 c` | Symmetric left-limit identification | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.rdd_identification` | `tau_RDD hA = nu_right_limit hA - nu_left_limit hA` | **Sharp RDD identification at the cutoff** (textbook form of `prop:po-sharp-rdd`): the cutoff-local effect equals the difference of the one-sided observable regression limits, `τ_RDD(c) = lim_{x↓c} ν(x) - lim_{x↑c} ν(x)` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i''. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/FuzzyRDD.lean` — Fuzzy RDD Identification (PO framework)

Formalizes the fuzzy regression discontinuity example from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(`def:po-fuzzy-rdd-system`, `def:po-fuzzy-rdd-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`) in the bare potential-outcome framework.  The Lean theorem
uses the regression-representative Wald form described in the implementation
plan: `μ_D(z, ·)` represents `E[D(z) | X=·]`, `μ_Y(z, ·)` represents
`E[Y(D(z)) | X=·]`, and the observable regressions `ν_D`, `ν_Y` are identified
with the corresponding right/left latent regressions at the cutoff.  Local
exclusion is encoded by the `Y(d)` interface; monotonicity is kept in the
assumption bundle to match the classical fuzzy RDD assumption list, though the
representative-level Wald identity itself only uses the bridge from consistency,
cutoff eligibility, continuity, support, and one-sided limit existence.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem P` | `structure { Xvar, Zvar, Dvar, Yvar, c, hYD, hZD, hDY }` | Fuzzy RDD subsystem: real running variable `X`, binary cutoff-eligibility instrument `Z`, binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, cutoff `c`, and distinctness witnesses for consistency rewrites | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{factualX, factualZ, factualD, factualY}` | `P.Ω → ℝ` / `P.Ω → Bool` / `P.Ω → Bool` / `P.Ω → ℝ` | Empty-regime factual running variable, eligibility instrument, treatment, and outcome | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.DofZ` | `Bool → P.Ω → Bool` | Instrument-specific potential treatment `D(z) := Dvar.cfUnder Zvar z` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofD` | `Bool → P.Ω → ℝ` | Treatment-specific potential outcome `Y(d) := Yvar.cfUnder Dvar d` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofDofZ` | `Bool → P.Ω → ℝ` | Composed potential outcome `Y(D(z))`, implemented as an if-then-else over the Boolean `D(z)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{measurable_factualX, measurable_factualZ, measurable_factualD, measurable_factualY, measurable_DofZ, measurable_YofD, measurable_YofDofZ}` | `Measurable …` | Basic measurability lemmas for factual variables and counterfactual/composed maps | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.zEvent` | `Bool → Set P.Ω` | Factual eligibility event `{Z = z}` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, cutoffEligibility, monotonicity, muD, muY, nuD, nuY, *_isReg, *_continuousAt, support_right, support_left, one-sided limit witnesses, firstStageJump }` | Fuzzy RDD assumptions: consistency, a.s. cutoff rule `Z = 1{X ≥ c}`, monotonicity `D(1) ≥ D(0)`, latent and observable regression representatives with integral characterisations, continuity of latent regressions at `c`, two-sided local support for the law of `X`, existence of observable one-sided limits, and a nonzero latent first-stage jump at the cutoff | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_FRD` | `S.Assumptions → ℝ` | Regression-representative cutoff Wald estimand `(μ_Y(1,c)-μ_Y(0,c))/(μ_D(1,c)-μ_D(0,c))`, the formal counterpart used for `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit, nuD_left_limit, nuY_right_limit, nuY_left_limit}` | `S.Assumptions → ℝ` | Chosen witnesses of the four one-sided observable limits via `Classical.choose` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{tendsto_nuD_right_limit, tendsto_nuD_left_limit, tendsto_nuY_right_limit, tendsto_nuY_left_limit}` | `Tendsto …` | Spec lemmas: the chosen witnesses are genuine one-sided limits of `ν_D` and `ν_Y` | **Proved** |
| private D-side bridge lemmas | — | Cutoff eligibility + consistency: on right/left measurable slices of `X`, factual treatment agrees a.e. with `D(1)`/`D(0)`, yielding integral and pushforward identities between `ν_D` and `μ_D` | **Proved** |
| private Y-side bridge lemmas | — | Consistency rewrites `Y` to `Y(factualD)` and then, on `{Z=z}`, to `Y(D(z))`; combined with the regression representative identities this gives the analogous bridges between `ν_Y` and `μ_Y` | **Proved** |
| private a.e. agreement and extraction lemmas | — | Apply `Integrable.ae_eq_of_forall_setIntegral_eq`, local support, and `Measure.exists_mem_of_measure_ne_zero_of_ae` to obtain agreement points arbitrarily close to `c` on both sides for treatment and outcome regressions | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit_eq, nuD_left_limit_eq, nuY_right_limit_eq, nuY_left_limit_eq}` | `Tendsto ν_* (𝓝[>/<] c) (𝓝 L) → L = μ_*(z,c)` | Any one-sided observable regression limit equals the corresponding latent regression representative at the cutoff, by uniqueness of limits along the agreement subfilter | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.frd_identification` | `tau_FRD hA = (nuY_right_limit hA - nuY_left_limit hA) / (nuD_right_limit hA - nuD_left_limit hA)` | **Fuzzy RDD identification at the cutoff** (regression-representative form of `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): the cutoff Wald estimand equals the Wald ratio of one-sided observable regression limits | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YdiffComplier` | `P.Ω → ℝ` | `(Y(1) - Y(0)) · 1_{D(1)=1 ∧ D(0)=0}`: the Y-difference weighted by the complier indicator; equals `Y(D(1)) - Y(D(0))` a.e. under monotonicity | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions.{mu_Ydiff_complier, mu_Ydiff_complier_isReg, mu_Ydiff_complier_continuousAt}` | `ℝ → ℝ`, `IsRegressionFunction`, `ContinuousAt` | Regression representative for `E[(Y(1)-Y(0))·1_C \| X=·]`, its `IsRegressionFunction` witness, and continuity at `c`; together they support the LATE bridge | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_LATE` | `S.Assumptions → ℝ` | Cutoff-local LATE `E[Y(1)-Y(0) \| X=c, C_c]` in regression-representative form: `mu_Ydiff_complier c / (muD true c - muD false c)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_late_identification` | `tau_LATE hA = tau_FRD hA` | **Fuzzy RDD identifies cutoff-local LATE** (`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): under all fuzzy RDD assumptions including monotonicity, the Wald ratio `τ_FRD` equals the cutoff-local LATE `τ_LATE = E[Y(1)-Y(0)\|X=c, C_c]`; proved via `IsRegressionFunction.sub` + monotonicity case analysis + `aeEq_of_aeEq_response` + continuity at `c` | **Proved** |

---

## 9j. `PO/ID/Partial/Manski.lean` — Manski ATE Bounds under a Discrete IV (PO framework)

Formalizes the **Manski bound family** for the ATE under a discrete IV,
from `Basic Concepts.tex` §`subsec:po-iv-manski`, §`subsec:po-iv-manski-shape`,
and `rem:manski-bound-family` (lines ~1561–2010).  Split into one file per
shape restriction.  Instrument value space `α` is arbitrary with
`MeasurableSingletonClass`; everything lives on the event-level
`eventCondExp` layer plus `POVar` consistency (no `CondIndepFun` stubs).

Layout:

- `Manski/Setup.lean` — data layer (`POManskiIVSystem`) + bound functionals.
- `Manski/Assumptions.lean` — `BaseAssumptions` + shape restrictions.
- `Manski/Helpers.lean` — shared utilities + conditional stratum bounds.
- `Manski/NonAsp.lean` — prop:po-iv-manski (baseline / mean-independence).
- `Manski/MTR.lean` — prop:po-iv-mtr (monotone treatment response).
- `Manski/MTS.lean` — prop:po-iv-mts (monotone treatment selection).
- `Manski/MIV.lean` — prop:po-iv-miv (monotone IV; integrated forms under `[Fintype α]`).
- `Manski/Combined.lean` — cor:po-iv-mtr-mts, cor:po-iv-mtr-miv.
- `Manski.lean` — thin re-export.

### Setup (`Manski/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POManskiIVSystem P α` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZ, hDbool, hYreal, hZD, hZY, hDY }` | Discrete-IV subsystem over a PO system: instrument `Z : α`, binary `D`, real `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `zVar` / `dVar` / `yVar` | `POVar P α` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ℝ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z`, `D`, `Y` | **Defined** |
| `YofD S d` | `P.Ω → ℝ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d) := yVar.cfUnder dVar d` | **Defined** |
| `factualZ` / `factualD` / `factualY` | `P.Ω → α / Bool / ℝ` | Factual instrument / treatment / outcome | **Defined** |
| `zEvent S z` / `dEvent S d` | `Set P.Ω` | Factual events `{Z = z}` / `{D = d}` | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurable_{YofD, factualZ, factualD, factualY}`, `measurableSet_{zEvent, dEvent}` | One-line delegations to `POVar` | **Proved** |
| `ATE` | `ℝ := ∫ (YofD true - YofD false) ∂P.μ` | Target parameter, average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `support S` | `Set α := {z \| P.μ (zEvent z) ≠ 0}` | Support of the instrument | **Defined** |
| `boundArm d c z` | `ℝ` | Unified arm bound functional `eventCondExp μ (zEvent z) (factualY · dVar.indicator d + c · dVar.indicator (!d))`; specialises to the four named bounds below | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound1 lo z` | `ℝ := boundArm true lo z` | Observable `L_{1,z}`; now a one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound1 hi z` | `ℝ := boundArm true hi z` | Observable `U_{1,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound0 lo z` | `ℝ := boundArm false lo z` | Observable `L_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound0 hi z` | `ℝ := boundArm false hi z` | Observable `U_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`Manski/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, lo, hi, hle, bounded_one, bounded_zero, integrable_{Y1,Y0} }` | Shared hypotheses: consistency, outcome bounds `lo ≤ Y(d) ≤ hi` a.s., integrability of `Y(1)` and `Y(0)`. Factual-`Y` integrability is *derived*, not bundled (see `integrable_factualY`) | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.bounded hA d` | `∀ᵐ ω ∂P.μ, hA.lo ≤ S.YofD d ω ∧ S.YofD d ω ≤ hA.hi` | Binary-folded form of `bounded_one` / `bounded_zero`: `Y(d)` is a.s. in `[lo, hi]` uniformly in `d : Bool`. Derived by `cases d` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_YofD hA d` | `Integrable (S.YofD d) P.μ` | Binary-folded form of `integrable_Y1` / `integrable_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_factualY hA` | `Integrable S.factualY P.μ` | Factual outcome integrability, *derived* from arm integrability via consistency (`factualY = Σ_d Y(d)·1{D=d}` a.e.); no longer an assumed field | **Proved** |
| `MeanIndep S` | `Prop { meanIndep_one, meanIndep_zero }` | Mean independence `E[Y(d) \| Z=z] = E[Y(d)]` on `eventCondExp`, for `d ∈ {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MTR S` | `Prop { monotone }` | Monotone treatment response: `Y(0) ≤ Y(1)` a.s. | **Defined** |
| `MTS S` | `Prop { mts_one }` | Monotone treatment selection: `eventCondExp μ (dEvent false) (YofD d) ≤ eventCondExp μ (dEvent true) (YofD d)` for `d ∈ {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MIV S` | `structure { inst, monotone }` | Monotone instrumental variable: bundled `LinearOrder ↑S.support` and monotonicity of `u ↦ eventCondExp μ (zEvent u) (YofD d)` on the support | **Defined** |

### Helpers (`Manski/Helpers.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| integrability lemmas | `factualY_mul_indD_integrable`, `YofD_mul_indD_integrable`, `const_mul_indD_integrable` | Integrability side conditions under `[IsFiniteMeasure P.μ]` + `BaseAssumptions`; now consume `POVar.indicator` + `integrable_indicator` / `indicator_eq_one_or_zero` | **Proved** |
| pointwise fill-in lemmas | `dVar_inds_at_ω` (private), `YofD_indD_plus_lo_le_YofD`, `YofD_le_YofD_indD_plus_hi` | a.e. "floor/ceiling fill-in" bounds; `dVar_inds_at_ω` packages the binary case split for `(indicator d, indicator (!d))` | **Proved** |
| `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD` | `[IsFiniteMeasure], BaseAssumptions, d : Bool, z ∈ support → boundArm d hA.lo z ≤ eventCondExp μ (zEvent z) (YofD d)` | Parametric conditional stratum lower bound; the mean-indep-free core used by `NonAsp` and `MIV` | **Proved** |
| `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi` | analogous upper, parametric in `d` | Parametric conditional stratum upper bound | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound1_le_cond_Y1` | — | Legacy corollary: `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD hA true` | **Proved** |
| `cond_Y1_le_upperBound1` | — | Legacy corollary: `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi hA true` | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound0_le_cond_Y0` | — | Legacy corollary: `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD hA false` | **Proved** |
| `cond_Y0_le_upperBound0` | — | Legacy corollary: `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi hA false` | **Proved** |

### NonAsp (`Manski/NonAsp.lean`) — prop:po-iv-manski

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lowerBound1_le_integral_Y1` | `[IsFiniteMeasure], BaseAssumptions, MeanIndep, z ∈ support → lowerBound1 hA.lo z ≤ ∫ YofD true ∂μ` | Stratum-level lower bound for `E[Y(1)]` via `lowerBound1_le_cond_Y1` + mean-indep collapse | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y1_le_upperBound1` | analogous upper | Stratum-level upper bound for `E[Y(1)]` | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound0_le_integral_Y0` | — | Stratum-level lower bound for `E[Y(0)]` | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y0_le_upperBound0` | — | Stratum-level upper bound for `E[Y(0)]` | **Proved** |
| `manski_bounds_ATE` | per-stratum-pair sandwich `L_{1,z₁} - U_{0,z₀} ≤ ATE ≤ U_{1,z₁} - L_{0,z₀}` | Manski bounds, per-pair form; strictly stronger than sup/inf form | **Proved** |
| `ciSup_lowerBound1_le_integral_Y1` | `support.Nonempty → ⨆ z, L_{1,z} ≤ ∫ Y(1)` | sup aggregation over support | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y1_le_ciInf_upperBound1` | inf form | inf aggregation of `U_{1,·}` | **Proved** |
| `ciSup_lowerBound0_le_integral_Y0` / `integral_Y0_le_ciInf_upperBound0` | — | sup/inf aggregations for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup` | `support.Nonempty → (⨆ L_{1}) - (⨅ U_{0}) ≤ ATE ≤ (⨅ U_{1}) - (⨆ L_{0})` | Manski bounds in tex form (sup/inf over support) | **Proved** |

### MTR (`Manski/MTR.lean`) — prop:po-iv-mtr

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mtr_E_Y_le_E_Y1` | Step 1: `∫ Y ≤ ∫ Y(1)` via a.s. inequality `Y ≤ Y(1)` from consistency + MTR | **Proved** |
| `mtr_E_Y0_le_E_Y` | Step 4: `∫ Y(0) ≤ ∫ Y` symmetrically | **Proved** |
| `mtr_E_Y1_le_upper` | Step 2: `∫ Y(1) ≤ p·E[Y\|D=1] + (1-p)·hi` via total-law decomposition + consistency + ceiling bound | **Proved** |
| `mtr_lower_le_E_Y0` | Step 3: `(1-p)·E[Y\|D=0] + p·lo ≤ ∫ Y(0)` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_nonneg_ATE` | `0 ≤ ATE` from MTR a.s. inequality | **Proved** |
| `mtr_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided ATE sandwich: `0 ≤ ATE ≤ (p·E[Y\|D=1]+(1-p)·hi) - ((1-p)·E[Y\|D=0]+p·lo)` | **Proved** |

### MTS (`Manski/MTS.lean`) — prop:po-iv-mts

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mts_lower_le_E_Y1` | `p·E[Y\|D=1] + (1-p)·lo ≤ ∫ Y(1)` (MTS lower on arm 1) | **Proved** |
| `mts_E_Y1_le_condY1` | `∫ Y(1) ≤ E[Y\|D=1]` (MTS upper on arm 1) | **Proved** |
| `mts_condY0_le_E_Y0` | `E[Y\|D=0] ≤ ∫ Y(0)` (MTS lower on arm 0) | **Proved** |
| `mts_E_Y0_le_upper` | `∫ Y(0) ≤ p·hi + (1-p)·E[Y\|D=0]` (MTS upper on arm 0) | **Proved** |
| `mts_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided MTS ATE sandwich, obtained by subtracting the four arm bounds | **Proved** |

### MIV (`Manski/MIV.lean`) — prop:po-iv-miv

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mLower1`, `mUpper1`, `mLower0`, `mUpper0` | Envelope functions `sSup { L_{d,u} \| u ≤ z }` and `sInf { U_{d,u} \| z ≤ u }` on the MIV linear order | **Defined** |
| `miv_mLower1_le_cond_Y1` | `mLower1 z ≤ eventCondExp μ (zEvent z) (YofD true)` on support; chains `Helpers` conditional bound with MIV monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y1_le_mUpper1` | analogous upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_mLower0_le_cond_Y0` | lower for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y0_le_mUpper0` | upper for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower1_le_integral_Y1` | `[Fintype α]`: `∫ mLower1(Z) ≤ ∫ Y(1)`; decomposes each side via `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` and compares stratum-wise, exploiting that off-support strata have measure `0` | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y1_le_integral_mUpper1` | `[Fintype α]`: `∫ Y(1) ≤ ∫ mUpper1(Z)`; symmetric to the lower bound | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower0_le_integral_Y0` | `[Fintype α]`: arm 0 integrated lower | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y0_le_integral_mUpper0` | `[Fintype α]`: arm 0 integrated upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype α]`: `∫ (mLower1(Z) - mUpper0(Z)) ≤ ATE ≤ ∫ (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))`; follows by linearity from the four integrated bounds | **Proved** |

### Combined (`Manski/Combined.lean`)

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mtr_mts_bounds_ATE` | cor:po-iv-mtr-mts: `0 ≤ ATE ≤ E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]` under MTR + MTS; combines `mtr_nonneg_ATE` with `mts_E_Y1_le_condY1 ∩ mts_condY0_le_E_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype α]`: cor:po-iv-mtr-miv: `0 ≤ ATE` (MTR) and `ATE ≤ ∫ (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))` (MIV); forwards from `miv_bounds_ATE` | **Proved** |

### IntervalForm (`Manski/IntervalForm.lean`) — `Set.Icc` restatements

Pure restatements of the ATE sandwiches as `S.ATE ∈ Set.Icc lo hi`, obtained by
feeding each `L ≤ ATE ∧ ATE ≤ U` pair through the
`Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge (`Basic.lean`). No new
mathematical content; same hypotheses/instances as each source theorem.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the Manski no-assumption per-stratum-pair ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE`), via the `mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge | **Proved** |
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc_ciSup` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the sup/inf-aggregated Manski no-assumption ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup`) | **Proved** |
| `mtr_mts_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MTS ATE sandwich (`mtr_mts_bounds_ATE`): `ATE ∈ [0, E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]]` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | `[Fintype α]`: closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MIV ATE sandwich (`mtr_miv_bounds_ATE`): `ATE ∈ [0, ∫ (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))]` | **Proved** |

**Scope note.** Two baseline aggregation forms are exposed in `NonAsp`: the
per-stratum-pair `manski_bounds_ATE` is strictly stronger (pick any
`z₁, z₀ ∈ support`), while `manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup` matches the tex's
sup/inf form but requires `support.Nonempty` (Lean `⨆`/`⨅` fall back to `0`
on empty index sets). The MIV integrated bounds require a `[Fintype α]`
hypothesis on the instrument value space (standard Manski setting with a
discrete IV) and are discharged via the `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp`
total law in `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean`; no outstanding `sorry` in the folder.

---

## 9j2. `PO/ID/Partial/` — Abstract Partial-Identification Bounds Engine

Reusable, PO-framework-free partial-identification primitives. Two files, both in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`:

- `Basic.lean` — the sharp identified interval + a sharp-interval API, including the
  two scalar-sandwich bridge helpers (`mem_Icc_of_sandwich`, `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup`)
  that turn concrete `L ≤ θ ≤ U` bounds into the engine's `Set.Icc` vocabulary.
- `Frechet.lean` — Fréchet–Hoeffding copula bounds + the easy Makarov lower bound.

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | `Set ℝ` | Sharp identified interval: range of `obj` over all `{x // feasible x}`; the set of all attainable objective values | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedInterval hx` | `obj x ∈ IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | If `x` is feasible then `obj x` is in the identified interval | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_subset_Icc hL hU` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible ⊆ Set.Icc L U` | Outer bound: uniform two-sided bounds `L ≤ obj ≤ U` on the feasible set imprison the identified set in `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_Icc hL hU hLmem hUmem hconn` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Sharp bound (order-connected form): outer bound + both endpoints attained + identified set order-connected ⇒ identified set is exactly `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_param_Icc hfeas hcont hL hU hbound` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Mixing-pattern constructor: feasible set = image of `[0,1]` under `γ`, `obj∘γ` continuous, endpoint values `L,U` bracketing `obj` along the path ⇒ sharp interval `[L,U]` (continuous image of a connected set is order-connected) | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_of_sandwich hlo hhi` | `θ ∈ Set.Icc L U` | Scalar-sandwich bridge: the literal reading of a two-sided bound `L ≤ θ ≤ U` as `θ ∈ [L, U]`; names the inequality-pair → `Set.Icc` step every concrete bound ends in. Consumers: `Manski`/`Proxy` `IntervalForm` | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup hb ha hy` | `y ∈ Set.Icc (sInf s) (sSup s)` | Worst/best case over a nuisance: every member of a bounded set `s ⊆ ℝ` lies between its infimum and supremum (the `csInf_le`/`le_csSup` step of a sharp bound). Consumers: `Lee/TrimBound`, `BalkePearl/IntervalForm` | **Proved** |

### `Frechet.lean`

Self-contained measure theory over a probability space `P` with two real random
variables `X, Y`; CDF/joint-CDF values written as `.toReal` of event measures.
Only the *easy* (fixed-joint-law) directions are asserted — no sharpness claim.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `frechet_upper u v` | `(P{X≤u ∧ Y≤v}).toReal ≤ min (P{X≤u}).toReal (P{Y≤v}).toReal` | Fréchet–Hoeffding upper bound on the joint CDF (joint event ⊆ each marginal event); needs no measurability — pure measure monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `frechet_lower hY u v` | `max ((P{X≤u}).toReal + (P{Y≤v}).toReal - 1) 0 ≤ (P{X≤u ∧ Y≤v}).toReal` | Fréchet–Hoeffding lower bound on the joint CDF (inclusion–exclusion `P A + P B - 1 ≤ P(A∩B)`); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_param hY s a` | `max ((P{X≤a}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 ≤ (P{X-Y≤s}).toReal` | Makarov lower bound on the CDF of the difference `X-Y`, per reference point `a` (from `{X≤a}∩{a-s≤Y} ⊆ {X-Y≤s}` + Fréchet lower); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_iSup hY s` | `⨆ a, max ((P{X≤a}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 ≤ (P{X-Y≤s}).toReal` | Sup-convolution envelope form: the supremum over reference points still lower-bounds the CDF of `X-Y` | **Proved** |

### `RandomSet/Interval.lean`

Tier-0 random-set substrate for partial identification (the measure-theoretic
upgrade of `IdentifiedInterval`). An interval random set is a pair of measurable
endpoints `L, U : Ω → ℝ` with `L ≤ U`, viewed as `ω ↦ [L ω, U ω]`. Provides the
two facts a sharpness proof needs — measurable selection and the selection
(Aumann) expectation — both proved *elementarily* (no Kuratowski–Ryll-Nardzewski,
no atomlessness/Lyapunov). Endpoints are reported via `sInf`/`sSup` to match the
`SandwichInterval` convention and keep the support-function generalisation a
drop-in. Namespace `Causalean.PartialID.RandomSet`; all sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `Ω → Set ℝ` | The interval-valued random closed set `ω ↦ [L ω, U ω]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection: `Measurable f ∧ ∀ ω, f ω ∈ [L ω, U ω]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_left hL hLU` | `IsSelection L U L` | The lower endpoint is always a selection ⇒ the random set has a measurable selection | **Proved** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param hL hU hLU f` | `IsSelection L U f ↔ ∃ t, Measurable t ∧ (∀ ω, t ω ∈ [0,1]) ∧ ∀ ω, f ω = L ω + t ω·(U ω−L ω)` | Measurable selection of an interval random set: every selection is `L + t·(U−L)` for a measurable `t : Ω → [0,1]`, and conversely (divide-by-width construction) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U μ` | `Set ℝ` | The selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable measurable selections, `{∫f \| f a selection}` | **Defined** |
| `integral_le_integral_of_le hLint hUint hLU` | `(∫L) ≤ ∫U` | Endpoint integrals are ordered (monotonicity of the Bochner integral) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc hL hU hLint hUint hLU` | `selectionExpectation L U μ = Set.Icc (∫L) (∫U)` | **Tier-0 core:** the selection expectation is exactly `[∫L, ∫U]` (⊆ by integral monotonicity; ⊇ realised by a *constant* `t ∈ [0,1]`, so no atomlessness needed) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation …` | `sInf (selectionExpectation L U μ) = ∫L` | Sharp lower endpoint as the infimum over selections | **Proved** |
| `sSup_selectionExpectation …` | `sSup (selectionExpectation L U μ) = ∫U` | Sharp upper endpoint as the supremum over selections | **Proved** |

*Out of scope (flagged for later):* conditional selection expectation
`E[X∣𝒢] = [E[L∣𝒢], E[U∣𝒢]]` (Tier-0.5, for regression envelopes such as
Manski–Tamer interval regression), and the support-function / Artstein /
CLR-inference layers.

### `LP/ConicDuality.lean`

Abstract conic linear-programming duality over a pair of **real Hilbert spaces**,
the value-form backbone for sharp partial-ID bounds posed as
`min ⟪c,x⟫ s.t. A x = b, x ∈ K`. Built on Mathlib's `ProperCone` / `innerDual`.
Hilbert generality is already infinite-dimensional (e.g. `L²(μ)` for proxy/bridge
functions). The *measure*-cone case (weak-∗ duality of measures against `C(X)`)
is **out of scope**: Mathlib gives `FiniteMeasure` only a `Module ℝ≥0` (no signed
`ℝ`-module), no ordered-cone instance, and a non-order weak-∗ topology — so the
nonnegative-measure `ProperCone` needs new infrastructure (a module note in the
file records this). Namespace `Causalean.PartialID`; weak duality + Farkas are
sorry-free, strong duality is a CQ-gated statement awaiting a modelling decision.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ConicProgram E F` | structure | Primal data `(K, A, b, c)`: cone `K : ProperCone ℝ E`, operator `A : E →L[ℝ] F`, rhs `b : F`, objective `c : E` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.PrimalFeasible x` | `Prop` | `A x = b ∧ x ∈ K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.DualFeasible y` | `Prop` | reduced cost in the dual cone: `c − Aᵀ y ∈ innerDual K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.primalValue` | `ℝ` | `sInf { ⟪c,x⟫ : x primal feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue` | `ℝ` | `sSup { ⟪b,y⟫ : y dual feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.weak_duality hx hy` | `⟪b,y⟫ ≤ ⟪c,x⟫` | Pointwise weak duality: any dual-feasible value lower-bounds any primal-feasible value (via `mem_innerDual` + adjoint) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue_le_primalValue hP hD` | `dualValue ≤ primalValue` | Value form of weak duality (both feasible; no boundedness needed) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.farkas` | `b ∈ ProperCone.map A K ↔ ∀ y, Aᵀ y ∈ innerDual K → 0 ≤ ⟪b,y⟫` | Feasibility alternative / strong-duality engine; restatement of `ProperCone.relative_hyperplane_separation`. `map` bakes in the closure of `A(K)` — exactly the CQ gap | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.augmentedImage` | `Set (F × ℝ)` | The augmented image cone `{(A x, ⟪c,x⟫) : x ∈ K}`; its closedness is the primal-attainment CQ | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_primal_attained hP hbdd hCQ` | `∃ x, PrimalFeasible x ∧ ⟪c,x⟫ = primalValue` | Strong duality I (closedness CQ): the primal optimum is attained — an extremal feasible point exists. Via the closed value-slice + `IsClosed.csInf_mem` | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_zero_gap hP hbdd hCQ` | `primalValue = dualValue` | Strong duality II (closedness CQ — same as I): no duality gap. Closedness suffices (no Slater needed). Proof: for each `ε>0`, separate `(b, primalValue−ε)` from the augmented cone `map Ã K` (`Ã x = (A x, ⟪c,x⟫)`) via `ProperCone.hyperplane_separation_point` on the normed product `F × ℝ`; Riesz-decompose the functional as `⟪y₀,v⟫ + s·t`, rule out `s ≤ 0`, and read off the dual-feasible `−(1/s)•y₀`. | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2inf. `PO/ID/Partial/Inference/` — Confidence intervals for interval-identified parameters

First statistical-inference layer over the partial-identification *geometry*. Where the rest of `PartialID/` produces population sandwiches `L ≤ θ₀ ≤ U`, this folder turns them into finite-sample confidence intervals, formalizing the two coverage notions of the literature (Horowitz–Manski 2000 set coverage vs. Imbens–Manski 2004 parameter coverage). Built on `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval` (Hoeffding/Bernstein miss bounds) + a union bound.

### `Inference/Basic.lean` — coverage vocabulary + abstract honest-CI lemma (pure measure theory)

| Declaration | Type / statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `RandomCoversPoint A B θ` | `Set Ω` | Event that the random interval `[A ω, B ω]` contains the fixed value `θ` (Imbens–Manski parameter-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `RandomCoversIcc A B L U` | `Set Ω` | Event that `[A ω, B ω]` contains the whole population interval `[L, U]` (Horowitz–Manski set-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `randomCoversIcc_subset_randomCoversPoint` | `θ ∈ [L,U] → RandomCoversIcc A B L U ⊆ RandomCoversPoint A B θ` | Set coverage ⟹ point coverage; the easy half of Imbens–Manski (2004, Lemma 1): parameter-coverage CIs are weakly smaller than set-coverage CIs | **Proved** |
| `randomCoversIcc_compl_subset` | `(RandomCoversIcc (lo−wL) (hi+wU) L U)ᶜ ⊆ {wL ≤ |lo−L|} ∪ {wU ≤ |hi−U|}` | Non-coverage is contained in the two one-sided endpoint-failure events; geometric heart of the union bound | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_set_cover` | `1 − δL − δU ≤ μ.real (RandomCoversIcc (lo−wL) (hi+wU) L U)` | **Abstract honest CI.** Given one-sided miss bounds `μ{wL≤|lo−L|}≤δL`, `μ{wU≤|hi−U|}≤δU`, the widened interval covers `[L,U]` w.p. `≥1−δL−δU` (single union bound; stated in miss probabilities so any concentration inequality plugs in) | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_point_cover` | `θ ∈ [L,U] → 1 − δL − δU ≤ μ.real (RandomCoversPoint (lo−wL) (hi+wU) θ)` | Parameter-coverage corollary: covering the set suffices to cover any point of it (conservative Horowitz–Manski parameter CI) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/IntervalCI.lean` — finite-sample plug-in instantiations (Hoeffding / Bernstein)

Plug-in Manski sandwich: bounding statistics `f_L, f_U` with population means `L = ∫ f_L`, `U = ∫ f_U`; estimate each endpoint by its sample mean and widen by a concentration half-width.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_set_cover` | `1−δL−δU ≤ μ.real (RandomCoversIcc [X̄ₙ(f_L)−wL, X̄ₙ(f_U)+wU] L U)` | Honest set CI with Hoeffding half-widths for bounded statistics `f_L∈[aL,bL]`, `f_U∈[aU,bU]`; valid at every `n` | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_point_cover` | `θ₀∈[∫f_L,∫f_U] → 1−δL−δU ≤ μ.real (RandomCoversPoint … θ₀)` | Parameter-coverage version (Hoeffding) | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_honest_ci_point_cover` | as above with `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | Variance-adaptive version; far tighter when the bounding statistics have small variance (leading term `2σ√(log(2/δ)/n)`) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/ImbensManski.lean` — asymptotic one-sided sharp interval (Imbens–Manski 2004)

When the identified set has positive width `Δ = U−L > 0`, covering the single true `θ₀` (not the whole set) needs protection on only **one** side: the `O(n^{-1/2})` sampling error is asymptotically negligible vs the fixed `Δ`, so non-coverage is one-sided and a *one-sided* critical value `c = z_{1−α}` already gives `1−α` coverage (vs the two-sided `z_{1−α/2}` the set-coverage interval needs). Normalized endpoint statistics `Sₗ,ₙ = √n(θ̂ₗ−L)/σₗ`, `Sᵤ,ₙ = √n(θ̂ᵤ−U)/σᵤ ⇒ N(0,1)`; offsets `aₙ=√n(θ₀−L)/σₗ≥0`, `bₙ=√n(U−θ₀)/σᵤ≥0`. Sources: Imbens–Manski 2004 (Lemma 1), Stoye 2009, Molinari 2020 §Pointwise-vs-Uniform.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Iic c` | `gaussianMeasure 0 1 (frontier (Iic c)) = 0` | Standard normal gives zero mass to the boundary `{c}` of a half-line; continuity-point fact for one-sided portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `farEnd_vanishes_of_tendsto_atBot` | `Sᵤ ⇒ N(0,1) → t ⟶ atBot → (μ{Sᵤ,ₙ < tₙ}).toReal ⟶ 0` | The `Δ>0` mechanism: as the far threshold drifts to `−∞` (`bₙ→∞`), the far-endpoint failure probability vanishes. Bounds the moving tail by a fixed Gaussian tail (`gaussian_tail_small`) + portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `imbensManski_pointwise_coverage` | `… → ∀ε>0, ∀ᶠ n, Φ(c) − ε ≤ (μ{Sₗ,ₙ≤c+aₙ ∧ −(c+bₙ)≤Sᵤ,ₙ}).toReal` | **Core asymptotic guarantee.** With `Sₗ⇒N(0,1)`, `aₙ≥0`, and the far end vanishing, IM coverage of `θ₀` eventually exceeds `Φ(c)−ε` where `Φ(c)=N(0,1)((-∞,c])`. One-sided `c=z_{1−α}` ⟹ `≥1−α`. Pointwise (not uniform; Stoye's superefficiency caveat not addressed) | **Proved** |

Supporting lemma `Causalean.Stat.gaussian_tail_small` (Gaussian half-line tails `<ε` for large cutoff) was factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof into `Causalean/Stat/CLT/GaussianTail.lean` for reuse here.

---

## 9j2sf. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/` — Support-Function Identified Sets (convex/Hilbert spine)

The multivariate generalization of the scalar `Basic.lean` engine: when the
feasible set `C` is **convex** in a real inner-product space and the target is the
**linear functional** `x ↦ ⟪d, x⟫`, the sharp identified interval is governed by
the *support function* `supportFn C d = sup { ⟪d,x⟫ : x ∈ C }`. Three files, all in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`. Boundedness/nonemptiness side conditions are carried
explicitly (the real `sSup` convention), matching `SandwichInterval`/`RandomSet`.
This is the abstraction the proximal-L2-bridge, sensitivity-model, and reweighting
partial-ID problems bottom out in.

- `Basic.lean` — `supportFn` definition + the two monotone spine lemmas.
- `Calculus.lean` — the support-function algebra (direction + set operations).
- `Interval.lean` — sharp identified interval `[-h(-d), h(d)]`, width, point-identification, scalar-engine bridge.
- `AffineBall.lean` — closed-form endpoints over a Hilbert affine fiber `{A h = b}` intersected with a ball `‖h‖ ≤ B` (the proximal-L2-bridge identified set).

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn C d` | `ℝ := sSup ((fun x => ⟪d,x⟫) '' C)` | Support function: best-case value of the linear target `⟪d,·⟫` over the feasible set `C` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_eq_iSup_image C d` | `supportFn C d = sSup (…)` | Unfolding lemma exposing the `sSup`-of-image form | **Proved** |
| `le_supportFn hx hbdd` | `⟪d,x⟫ ≤ supportFn C d` | Lower sandwich: a feasible point's value is `≤` the support value (needs the image bounded above) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_le hne hb` | `supportFn C d ≤ b` | Upper sandwich: a uniform upper bound `b` on the functional over nonempty `C` bounds the support value | **Proved** |

### `Calculus.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn_singleton d x₀` | `supportFn {x₀} d = ⟪d,x₀⟫` | Support function of a point is the functional value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_nonneg_of_zero_mem h0 hbdd` | `0 ≤ supportFn C d` | `0 ∈ C` forces a nonnegative support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_dir ht hne hbdd` | `supportFn C (t•d) = t * supportFn C d` | Positive homogeneity in the direction (`t ≥ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_add_dir_le hne hb₁ hb₂` | `supportFn C (d₁+d₂) ≤ supportFn C d₁ + supportFn C d₂` | Subadditivity / sublinearity in the direction | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_mono hCD hne hbdd` | `supportFn C d ≤ supportFn D d` | Monotone in the set (`C ⊆ D`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_union hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C∪D) d = max (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Union rule | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_inter_le hne hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C∩D) d ≤ min (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Intersection rule (one-sided) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_translate x₀ hne hbdd` | `supportFn ((x₀+·) '' C) d = ⟪d,x₀⟫ + supportFn C d` | Translation shifts the support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_set ha hne hbdd` | `supportFn (a•C) d = a * supportFn C d` | Positive set-scaling (`a ≥ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_minkowski hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C+D) d = supportFn C d + supportFn D d` | Additivity over Minkowski sums | **Proved** |

### `Interval.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `neg_supportFn_neg_le hx hbdd'` | `-supportFn C (-d) ≤ ⟪d,x⟫` | Lower endpoint `-h(-d)` bounds the functional from below (worst case) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_subset_Icc hbdd hbdd'` | `(⟪d,·⟫) '' C ⊆ Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | Outer bound: identified set sits inside the support interval | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_ordConnected hC` | `((⟪d,·⟫) '' C).OrdConnected` | Convex `C` ⇒ identified set has no gaps (linear image of convex is convex in `ℝ`) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc hC hbdd hbdd' hUmem hLmem` | `(⟪d,·⟫) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (attainment form): convex + both endpoints attained ⇒ identified set is exactly `[-h(-d), h(d)]` | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc_of_isCompact hcomp hC hne` | `(⟪d,·⟫) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (compact convex form): the workhorse — continuity gives bounded + attained automatically | **Proved** |
| `width C d` | `ℝ := supportFn C d + supportFn C (-d)` | Width of the identified set (upper minus lower endpoint) | **Defined** |
| `width_nonneg hne hbdd hbdd'` | `0 ≤ width C d` | The identified width is nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `width_eq_zero_iff hne hbdd hbdd'` | `width C d = 0 ↔ ∀ x ∈ C, ⟪d,x⟫ = supportFn C d` | **Point identification**: zero width iff the target is constant on `C` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_image obj C` | `IdentifiedInterval obj (· ∈ C) = obj '' C` | Bridge: the scalar `Basic.lean` engine's identified set is the set-image of the objective (so concrete theorems consume either vocabulary) | **Proved** |

### `AffineBall.lean`

Closed-form endpoints over the **bridge fiber** `affineBall A b B = {h | A h = b ∧ ‖h‖ ≤ B}` in a real Hilbert space `H` (operator `A : H →L[ℝ] F`). Decomposing `h = h₀ + v` (min-norm solution `h₀ ⊥ ker A`, free direction `v ∈ ker A`), the Pythagorean split + Cauchy–Schwarz give the sharp value/width in terms of `P c = orthogonalProjection (ker A) c`. This is the `pid_proximal_l2_bridge` identified set.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `opKer A` | `Submodule ℝ H := LinearMap.ker ↑A` | Free-direction subspace `ker A` of the operator (closed; has an orthogonal projection — instance provided) | **Defined** |
| `affineBall A b B` | `Set H := {h | A h = b ∧ ‖h‖ ≤ B}` | The affine-ball feasible fiber: equation solutions inside the norm ball | **Defined** |
| `mem_affineBall` | `h ∈ affineBall A b B ↔ A h = b ∧ ‖h‖ ≤ B` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `supportFn (affineBall A b B) c = ⟪c,h₀⟫ + √(B²−‖h₀‖²)·‖P c‖` | Closed-form support value (best case), given min-norm solution `h₀ ⊥ ker A` fitting the ball | **Proved** |
| `width_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 2·√(B²−‖h₀‖²)·‖P c‖` | Closed-form identified width | **Proved** |
| `affineBall_point_identified_iff A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 0 ↔ P c = 0` | **Point identification** (strict ball slack `‖h₀‖<B`): collapse iff `c ⊥ ker A`, i.e. `c` in the closed row space of `A` | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2rs. `PO/ID/Partial/RandomSet/` — Random sets, Hausdorff distance & the support-function bridge (Beresteanu–Molinari 2008)

The estimation/inference layer for set-identified parameters. Beresteanu & Molinari (2008) show the whole inference theory rests on one identity (Hörmander's embedding, their eq. (A.1)): for compact convex `A,B`, `H(A,B) = sup_{‖p‖=1} |s(p,A) − s(p,B)|`. This folder builds that bridge, its `d = 1` (interval) specialization (the scalar interval-data CLT, Theorems 3.1/3.2, in `IntervalCLT.lean`), and the general-`d` finite-grid support-process CLT on genuine random sets (`SetValued.lean` + `SupportProcess.lean`, Theorem A.2). Plan: `doc/beresteanu_molinari_plan.md`. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

### `Interval.lean` — interval random sets & Aumann (selection) expectation (Tier-0, pre-existing)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `Ω → Set ℝ := fun ω => Icc (L ω) (U ω)` | The interval-valued random closed set `ω ↦ [L ω, U ω]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection of `[L,U]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param` | selection ↔ `L + t·(U−L)`, `t : Ω→[0,1]` measurable | Elementary measurable selection (no Kuratowski–Ryll-Nardzewski) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U μ` | `Set ℝ` | Selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable selections | **Defined** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc` | `= Icc (∫L) (∫U)` | **Aumann expectation `= [∫L, ∫U]`** (`⊇` uses a constant `t`, no atomlessness) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation` / `sSup_selectionExpectation` | `= ∫L` / `= ∫U` | Sharp endpoints as inf/sup over selections | **Proved** |

### `Hausdorff.lean` — directed/symmetric Hausdorff distance + the geometric keystone

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `directedHausdorff A B` | `ℝ := sSup ((fun a => infDist a B) '' A)` | One-sided (directed) Hausdorff distance `sup_{a∈A} infDist a B` | **Defined** |
| `hausdorffDist A B` | `ℝ := max (dᴴ A B) (dᴴ B A)` | Symmetric Hausdorff distance | **Defined** |
| `infDist_Icc hcd x` | `infDist x [c,d] = max 0 (max (c−x) (x−d))` | Distance from a real point to an interval (zero inside; signed gap outside) | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_Icc hab hcd` | `dᴴ([a,b],[c,d]) = max 0 (max (c−a) (b−d))` | Directed Hausdorff between intervals (worst over-reach, attained at endpoints) | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_Icc hab hcd` | `H([a,b],[c,d]) = max \|a−c\| \|b−d\|` | **The geometric keystone** (Beresteanu–Molinari eq. (A.1), `d=1`): set distance = larger endpoint gap | **Proved** |

### `Aumann.lean` — support function of an interval & the d=1 Artstein bridge

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn_Icc_one hab` | `supportFn [a,b] (1:ℝ) = b` | Support function of a real interval at `+1`: the upper endpoint | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_Icc_neg_one hab` | `supportFn [a,b] (-1:ℝ) = -a` | Support function at `−1`: the negated lower endpoint | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_Icc_eq_supportFn hab hcd` | `H = max \|Δs(−1)\| \|Δs(1)\|` | **The `d=1` Hörmander identity**: Hausdorff distance = sup over `{±1}` of support-function differences (bridges `Hausdorff` to the `SupportFunction` engine) | **Proved** |
| `artstein_supportFn_one` | `s(+1, E[F]) = ∫ s(+1, F ω) ∂μ` | **Artstein identity** (`d=1`, dir `+1`): support of the Aumann expectation = expectation of the support (`= ∫U`) | **Proved** |
| `artstein_supportFn_neg_one` | `s(−1, E[F]) = ∫ s(−1, F ω) ∂μ` | **Artstein identity** (`d=1`, dir `−1`): `= −∫L` | **Proved** |

### `IntervalCLT.lean` — scalar interval-data CLT (Beresteanu–Molinari Theorem 3.2)

The Tier-1 capstone: `√n · H(Ȳₙ, E[Y]) ⇒ max(|z_L|, |z_U|)`, a continuous-mapping image of the multivariate CLT (`Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit`) through the Hausdorff keystone.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eucl₂ v` | `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)` | Pack a pair `Fin 2 → ℝ` into `ℝ²` (the `PiLp 2` synonym) | **Defined** |
| `maxAbs w` | `ℝ := max \|w 0\| \|w 1\|` | The max-abs functional on `ℝ²` (the Hausdorff statistic in coordinates) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_maxAbs` / `measurable_maxAbs` | `Continuous`/`Measurable maxAbs` | `maxAbs` is continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `normalizedSum_maxAbs_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbs∘normalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbs) μ _` | **Abstract continuous-mapping CLT**: `maxAbs` of the vector normalised sum ⇒ pushforward of the Gaussian limit by `maxAbs` (via `clt_normalizedSum_vec` + `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous`) | **Proved** |
| `sqrt_inv_centered` | `(√n)⁻¹·(s − n·c) = √n·(s/n − c)` | Centered-sum / `√n` algebra (normalised centered sum = `√n × (sample mean − pop mean)`) | **Proved** |
| `sampleMean S y n ω` | `ℝ := (∑_{i<n} y (Zᵢ ω)) / n` | Sample mean of `y` over the first `n` draws | **Defined** |
| `intervalIFVec yL yU P` | `X → ℝ²` | Centered endpoint influence function `ψ(z) = (y_L(z) − E y_L, y_U(z) − E y_U)` | **Defined** |
| `sampleMean_le` | `y_L ≤ y_U ⇒ sampleMean yL ≤ sampleMean yU` | Sample-mean interval is well-ordered | **Proved** |
| `maxAbs_normalizedSum_eq` | `maxAbs (normalizedSum) = √n · H(Ȳₙ, E[Y])` | **The Hausdorff bridge**: the statistic is the scaled Hausdorff distance between the sample-mean interval `Ȳₙ` and `E[Y] = [E y_L, E y_U]` | **Proved** |
| `interval_data_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (fun n ω => √n·H(Ȳₙ,E[Y])) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbs) μ _` | **Beresteanu–Molinari Theorem 3.2**: `√n · H(Ȳₙ, E[Y]) ⇒ max(\|z_L\|, \|z_U\|)` (CLT ∘ bridge via `congr_ae`). Takes the centered-`ψ` multivariate-CLT hypotheses + statistic measurability | **Proved** |

### `SetValued.lean` — compact-convex set-valued RVs & the Minkowski-mean support bridge (Tier-2 substrate, general `d`)

The general-`d` substrate for the support-process CLT. A set-valued random variable takes **body** values (nonempty compact convex sets, the class `𝒦ₖ(E)`); on a compact set the linear functional `⟪d,·⟫` attains its sup so `supportFn (F ω) d` is well-defined. The one load-bearing fact is the **Minkowski-mean support bridge**, which turns the support value of an empirical Minkowski average into an ordinary sample mean. Pure convex geometry — no probability, no atomlessness.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsBody C` | `structure` (`nonempty`, `isCompact`, `convex`) | A body: nonempty compact convex subset of `E` — the value type of an SVRV | **Defined** |
| `bddAbove_inner_image hC d` | `BddAbove ((⟪d,·⟫) '' C)` | On a compact set the linear functional is bounded above (Cauchy–Schwarz on the bounded set) | **Proved** |
| `IsBody.bddAbove h d` | `BddAbove ((⟪d,·⟫) '' C)` | A body's support function is well-defined | **Proved** |
| `isBody_finsetSum s F h` | `IsBody (∑ i ∈ s, F i)` | A finite Minkowski sum of bodies is a body | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_finsetSum s F d h` | `supportFn (∑ᵢ Fᵢ) d = ∑ᵢ supportFn (Fᵢ) d` | Support function commutes with finite Minkowski sums | **Proved** |
| `minkowskiMean s F` | `Set E := (\|s\|)⁻¹ • (∑ i ∈ s, F i)` | The empirical Minkowski average `(1/n) • ∑ᵢ Fᵢ` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_minkowskiMean s F d h` | `supportFn (minkowskiMean s F) d = (\|s\|)⁻¹ · ∑ᵢ supportFn (Fᵢ) d` | **The keystone**: support of a Minkowski average = average of supports (makes `s(p,F̄ₙ)` an iid sample mean) | **Proved** |

### `SupportProcess.lean` — Tier-2 finite-direction support-process CLT (general `d`)

The general-`d` analogue of `IntervalCLT`, the honest finite-dimensional projection of Beresteanu–Molinari Theorem A.2 (the full `C(Sᵈ⁻¹)` Banach CLT is the deferred Tier-3 gap). Over `k` fixed directions, the centered support process `ψ(z)_j = s(pⱼ,F(z)) − E s(pⱼ,F)` ⇒ a `k`-variate Gaussian; the Hausdorff-over-grid `ℓ^∞` statistic converges accordingly. The `SetValued` section instantiates the abstract shell on genuine random sets.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `maxAbsK w` | `ℝ := Finset.univ.sup' _ (fun j => \|w j\|)` | The `ℓ^∞`/Hausdorff-over-grid functional `supⱼ \|wⱼ\|` on `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` (`[NeZero k]`); generalizes `maxAbs` (`Fin 2`) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_maxAbsK` / `measurable_maxAbsK` | `Continuous`/`Measurable maxAbsK` | The grid functional is continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_sup_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK ∘ normalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbsK) μ _` | **BM Theorem A.2, finite-grid form (abstract shell)**: `maxAbsK` of the vector normalised process ⇒ `(gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbsK` = law of `supⱼ \|z(pⱼ)\|`. General-`d` analogue of `normalizedSum_maxAbs_clt` | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess F EF p` | `X → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` | The centered **support process** `ψ(x)_j = s(pⱼ, F x) − s(pⱼ, EF)` over a grid of `k` directions; `∫ψ=0` is the carried general-`d` Artstein condition | **Defined** |
| `supportProcess_ofLp` | `(supportProcess F EF p x).ofLp j = s(pⱼ,F x) − s(pⱼ,EF)` | Coordinate access (`rfl`) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_normalizedSum_apply` | `normalizedSum (supportProcess ..) n ω j = √n · (s(pⱼ, F̄ₙ) − s(pⱼ, EF))` | **The Minkowski-mean bridge in coordinates**: the `j`-th coordinate of the normalised process is the rescaled support deviation of the empirical Minkowski average `F̄ₙ = minkowskiMean (range n) (F∘Z)`; via `supportFn_minkowskiMean` | **Proved** |
| `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK ∘ normalizedSum (supportProcess ..)) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbsK) μ _` | **BM Theorem A.2 on genuine random sets**: the `ℓ^∞`-over-grid statistic of normalised support deviations ⇒ `maxⱼ\|z(pⱼ)\|`; coordinates given by the bridge above (literal form `√n·maxⱼ\|s(pⱼ,F̄ₙ)−s(pⱼ,EF)\|`) | **Proved** |

### `GridTest.lean` — finite-grid specification test (Beresteanu–Molinari Theorem 2.2)

The two-sided test of `H₀ : E[F] = EF` built on `setValued_supportProcess_clt`, rejecting when the rescaled Hausdorff-over-grid statistic exceeds a critical value.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gridTestStat S F EF p` | `ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | The test statistic `Tₙ = maxAbsK(normalizedSum(supportProcess ..))` = `maxⱼ √n·\|s(pⱼ,F̄ₙ)−s(pⱼ,EF)\|` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestReject S F EF p n c` | `Set Ω := {ω \| c < Tₙ ω}` | Rejection region at critical value `c` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestStat_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (gridTestStat ..) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map maxAbsK) μ _` | Under `H₀`, `Tₙ ⇒ maxⱼ\|z(pⱼ)\|`; thin restatement of `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | **Proved** |
| `gridTest_asymptotic_level` | `μ(gridTestReject .. c) → L(Ioi c)` (`L{c}=0`) | **BM Theorem 2.2 asymptotic level**: at a continuity point `c` of the limit law `L`, the rejection probability → the limit-law tail `L(c,∞)`; at the `(1−α)`-quantile this is level `α`. Via portmanteau (`tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'`) on `Ioi c` (frontier `{c}` null) | **Proved** |

Additional results landed in `IntervalCLT.lean` (moment discharge): `measurable_intervalIFVec`, `intervalIFVec_var_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_mean_zero`, `intervalIFVec_sum_aemeasurable`, `intervalIFVec_hHmeas` discharge the six analytic side-conditions of `interval_data_clt` from clean hypotheses, yielding the self-contained headline **`interval_data_clt_of_memLp`** (`√n·H(Ȳₙ,E[Y]) ⇒ max(|z_L|,|z_U|)` requiring only `Measurable yL/yU`, `MemLp yL/yU 2 P`, `yL ≤ yU`). All proved, axiom-clean.

### `IntervalInference.lean` — confidence regions & coverage (Beresteanu–Molinari §2–3, Prop 2.7)

Confidence-region geometry for the interval-identified mean + the directed-Hausdorff CLT and its asymptotic coverage.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dilate A r` | `Set ℝ := A ⊕ [−r,r]` | Minkowski dilation; the BM region `Uₙ = Ȳₙ ⊕ B(0, c/√n)` | **Defined** |
| `dilate_Icc hab hr` | `[a,b] ⊕ [−r,r] = [a−r, b+r]` | Dilation of an interval (`a≤b`, `r≥0`) | **Proved** |
| `subset_dilate_iff_directedHausdorff_le` | `[μL,μU] ⊆ [yl,yu]⊕[−r,r] ↔ dᴴ ≤ r` | **BM Prop 2.7 core**: one-sided coverage ↔ directed Hausdorff within radius | **Proved** |
| `subset_dilate_iff_hausdorff_le` | mutual containment `↔ H ≤ r` | Symmetric (two-sided) coverage characterization | **Proved** |
| `coverage_event_eq` | `{E[Y]⊆Uₙ} = {√n·dᴴ(E[Y],Ȳₙ) ≤ c}` | Coverage event = directed-statistic event (`n≥1`, `c≥0`) | **Proved** |
| `dirStat w` | `ℝ := max 0 (max w₀ (−w₁))` | Directed-Hausdorff functional on `ℝ²` (the `dᴴ` analogue of `maxAbs`) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_dirStat` / `measurable_dirStat` | — | `dirStat` continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `normalizedSum_dirStat_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (dirStat∘normalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ψ).map dirStat) μ _` | Directed continuous-mapping CLT | **Proved** |
| `dirStat_normalizedSum_eq` | `dirStat(normalizedSum) = √n · dᴴ(E[Y], Ȳₙ)` | Directed Hausdorff bridge | **Proved** |
| `directedRegion_coverage` | `μ{E[Y] ⊆ Uₙ} → L(−∞,c]` | **BM Proposition 2.7**: coverage of the identified set converges to the limit-law mass of `Iic c` (`L = (gaussianLimit ψ).map dirStat`); at the `(1−α)`-quantile (`L{c}=0`) this is asymptotic `1−α` coverage. Via the directed CLT + portmanteau `tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'` (frontier `{c}` null) on `coverage_event_eq` | **Proved** |

**Frontier (Tier 3, deferred):** the full `C(Sᵈ⁻¹)` Banach CLT (Jain–Marcus) + BLP (Thm 4.3) — same Mathlib gap class as the bracketing-entropy chaining frontier (§12ep.d).

---

## 9j2cs. `PO/ID/Partial/CriterionSet/` — Criterion-function set inference (Chernozhukov–Hong–Tamer 2007)

The second pillar of partial-ID inference, complementary to the support-function/random-set program (§9j2rs): a partially identified parameter is the **argmin set** of a population criterion `Q`, estimated by a **level set** of the sample criterion `Q̂ₙ`. Consistency is phrased in the *same* metric Hausdorff distance (`RandomSet.directedHausdorff`/`hausdorffDist`) that drives Beresteanu–Molinari, tying the two pillars to one geometry. All declarations **proved, sorry-free**.

### `Basic.lean` — criterion, identified set, level-set estimator

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `identifiedSet Q` | `Set Θ := {θ \| Q θ = 0}` | The identified set = zero set (argmin of a nonnegative criterion normalised to inf 0) | **Defined** |
| `levelSet Qn c` | `Set Θ := {θ \| Qn θ ≤ c}` | The level-set estimator (sample analogue, relaxed by cutoff `c`) | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedSet` / `mem_levelSet` | membership `Iff.rfl` (`@[simp]`) | Unfolding lemmas | **Proved** |
| `levelSet_mono h` | `c₁ ≤ c₂ → levelSet Qn c₁ ⊆ levelSet Qn c₂` | Level set monotone in the cutoff | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_eq_levelSet_zero_of_nonneg hQ` | `identifiedSet Q = levelSet Q 0` | For `Q ≥ 0` the identified set is the level-`0` set | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_subset_levelSet_self hc` | `identifiedSet Q ⊆ levelSet Q c` (`c≥0`) | Identified set sits inside any nonneg-cutoff level set of the same criterion | **Proved** |

### `Consistency.lean` — Hausdorff consistency of the level-set estimator

The mathematical core is a deterministic rate bound under a linear identifiability minorant; consistency is then a squeeze. Reuses the metric Hausdorff machinery of `RandomSet/Hausdorff.lean`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `LinearMinorant Q δ` | `Prop := ∀ θ, δ · d(θ, Θ_I) ≤ Q θ` | The CHT polynomial-minorant identifiability condition (degree `γ=1`): `Q` grows ≥ linearly away from `Θ_I` | **Defined** |
| `directedHausdorff_nonneg` / `hausdorffDist_nonneg` | `0 ≤ ·` | Metric Hausdorff distances are nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_le_of_forall hM h` | `(∀ a∈A, infDist a B ≤ M) → dᴴ A B ≤ M` | Directed Hausdorff bound from a uniform point-to-set bound | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_eq_zero_of_subset h` | `A ⊆ B → dᴴ A B = 0` | Directed distance vanishes on a subset | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_levelSet_le` | `H(levelSet Qn c, identifiedSet Q) ≤ (c+ε)/δ` | **Deterministic CHT rate bound** under `LinearMinorant Q δ` (`δ>0`), sup-norm error `\|Qn−Q\|≤ε`, cutoff `c≥ε`: estimator within `(c+ε)/δ` of `Θ_I` (minorant pins `Θ̂ₙ`; `Θ_I ⊆ Θ̂ₙ` outright) | **Proved** |
| `tendsto_hausdorffDist_levelSet` | `Tendsto (fun n => H(levelSet (Qn n) (c n), Θ_I)) atTop (𝓝 0)` | **Hausdorff consistency**: with `εₙ→0` (ULLN input) and `εₙ ≤ cₙ → 0`, the level-set estimator is consistent for the identified set (squeeze on the rate bound) | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2sg. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/Sensitivity.lean` — Reweighting / Sensitivity Models (worst-case mean under a budget)

Sensitivity analysis replaces an unidentified reweighting `w` (the weights that would recover the estimand under unconfoundedness) by an **ambiguity set** `W` and reports the sharp range of the reweighted mean `⟪c, w⟫` over `W`. This is the **applications layer of `SupportFunction/`**: the support-function picture instantiated for sensitivity budgets (general robust-bound vocabulary + the χ²/L² ball), all in one file.

### Reweighting layer — sensitivity vocabulary + structural facts

The general ambiguity-set layer: the robust bounds are the support-interval endpoints, monotone in the budget and collapsing under no ambiguity.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `robustUpper W c` | `ℝ := supportFn W c` | Upper robust bound (worst case from above) of the reweighted mean over ambiguity set `W` | **Defined** |
| `robustLower W c` | `ℝ := -supportFn W (-c)` | Lower robust bound (worst case from below) | **Defined** |
| `robustLower_le_robustUpper` | `robustLower W c ≤ robustUpper W c` | The robust interval is well-ordered (width `≥ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `le_robustUpper` / `robustLower_le` | `⟪c,w⟫ ≤ robustUpper W c` / `robustLower W c ≤ ⟪c,w⟫` | A feasible weight's reweighted mean lies in the robust interval | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_mono` | `W ⊆ W' → robustUpper W c ≤ robustUpper W' c` | **Budget monotonicity** (upper): a larger ambiguity set raises the upper bound | **Proved** |
| `robustLower_antitone` | `W ⊆ W' → robustLower W' c ≤ robustLower W c` | **Budget monotonicity** (lower): a larger ambiguity set lowers the lower bound (interval widens) | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_singleton` / `robustLower_singleton` | `robust* {w₀} c = ⟪c,w₀⟫` | **No ambiguity ⇒ point identification** at `⟪c,w₀⟫` (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `robustInterval_eq_image` | `(⟪c,·⟫)''W = Icc (robustLower W c) (robustUpper W c)` | For compact convex `W` the robust bounds are sharp (the interval is the identified set) | **Proved** |

### χ²/L² divergence-ball model — closed form via the affine-ball engine

The χ²/L² ambiguity set `l2Ball e ρ = {w | ⟪e,w⟫ = 1 ∧ ‖w − e‖ ≤ ρ}` (unit base direction `e`, signed-reweighting relaxation of the χ²-ball). Substituting `v = w − e` reduces it to the affine-ball fiber of `A = innerSL ℝ e`, giving the classic **mean ± `ρ·SD`** band. Reading `e` as the constant and `c = Y` in `L²(P)`: `⟪e,c⟫ = 𝔼[Y]`, `‖c‖²−⟪e,c⟫² = Var(Y)`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `l2Ball e ρ` | `Set H := {w | ⟪e,w⟫ = 1 ∧ ‖w−e‖ ≤ ρ}` | The χ²/L² ambiguity set: weights normalized against `e`, within an L²-ball of radius `ρ` | **Defined** |
| `mem_l2Ball` | `w ∈ l2Ball e ρ ↔ ⟪e,w⟫ = 1 ∧ ‖w−e‖ ≤ ρ` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `opKer_innerSL_eq` | `opKer (innerSL ℝ e) = (ℝ ∙ e)ᗮ` | The free-direction kernel is the orthogonal complement of the base direction | **Proved** |
| `norm_orthogonalProjection_opKer_innerSL` | `‖P_{ker} c‖ = √(‖c‖²−⟪e,c⟫²)` | Free-direction projection norm equals the standard deviation (under `‖e‖=1`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_eq_translate` | `l2Ball e ρ = (e + ·)''(affineBall (innerSL ℝ e) 0 ρ)` | Reduction to the affine-ball engine (substitution `v = w−e`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_l2Ball_eq` | `supportFn (l2Ball e ρ) c = ⟪e,c⟫ + ρ·√(‖c‖²−⟪e,c⟫²)` | **Closed-form worst case** (`mean + ρ·SD`), `‖e‖=1`, `ρ≥0` | **Proved** |
| `width_l2Ball_eq` | `width (l2Ball e ρ) c = 2ρ·√(‖c‖²−⟪e,c⟫²)` | Closed-form identified width (`2ρ·SD`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_point_identified_iff` | `width (l2Ball e ρ) c = 0 ↔ ‖c‖² = ⟪e,c⟫²` | **Point identification** (`ρ>0`): collapse iff `c` collinear with `e` (outcome constant; equality in Cauchy–Schwarz) | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2msm. `PO/ID/Partial/Sensitivity/MSM/Setup.lean` — Marginal Sensitivity Model for `E[Y(1)]` (Tan / Zhao–Small–Bhattacharya)

The **marginal sensitivity model** in the measure-theoretic backdoor PO framework (`POBackdoorSystem`): partial identification of `E[Y(1)]` when no-unmeasured-confounding is relaxed by a bounded odds ratio `Λ`. The complete propensity `e₀(X,Y(1)) = P[D=1 | σ(X,Y(1))]` (conditioning on the covariate *and* the treated potential outcome) is allowed to differ from the observed propensity `e(X)`, with the discrepancy controlled by `Λ`. Builds on `PO/ID/Exact/ATE.lean` (`propScore`, the condExp machinery); the IPW bridge is the genuine measure-theoretic content.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `OR p₁ p₂` | `ℝ := (p₁/(1−p₁))/(p₂/(1−p₂))` | The odds ratio of two probabilities | **Defined** |
| `completeProp` | `Ω → ℝ := μ[1_{D=1} \| σ(X,Y(1))]` | The complete propensity `e₀(X,Y(1))` — conditions on covariate + treated potential outcome | **Defined** |
| `candMean ẽ` | `ℝ := ∫ 1_{D=1}·Y/ẽ` | Candidate IPW mean `E[A·Y/ẽ]` for a candidate complete propensity `ẽ` | **Defined** |
| `Y1mean` | `ℝ := ∫ Y(1)` | The estimand `μ = E[Y(1)]` | **Defined** |
| `MSMSet Λ` | `Set (Ω→ℝ)` | The MSM ambiguity set: candidates a.e. in `(0,1)` with `1/Λ ≤ OR(ẽ, e(X)) ≤ Λ` a.e. | **Defined** |
| `msmUpper Λ` / `msmLower Λ` | `ℝ := sSup/sInf (candMean '' MSMSet Λ)` | The MSM interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `candMean_completeProp_eq_Y1mean` | `candMean e₀ = E[Y(1)]` | **IPW / tower bridge** — the candidate mean at the *true* complete propensity recovers the estimand (condExp pull-out + cancellation; needs consistency, `e₀>0` a.e., IPW-integrability of `A·Y/e₀`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y1mean_mem_Icc` | `E[Y(1)] ∈ Icc (msmLower Λ) (msmUpper Λ)` | **The bound** — the estimand lies in the MSM interval (truth is a feasible candidate) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet_mono` | `1≤Λ → Λ≤Λ' → MSMSet Λ ⊆ MSMSet Λ'` | The ambiguity set grows with the sensitivity parameter | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpper_mono` / `msmLower_anti` | interval widens in `Λ` | **Budget monotonicity** of the MSM interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet_one_eq` | `ẽ ∈ MSMSet 1 → ẽ =ᵐ e(X)` | **Λ=1 collapse**: no slack forces `OR=1 ⇒ ẽ = e(X)` a.e. (point identification = the NUC/IPW value) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Closed form (`Bounds.lean`), with `wMin = 1+(1−e)/(Λe)`, `wMax = 1+Λ(1−e)/e` the box endpoints:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `wMin Λ` / `wMax Λ` | `Ω → ℝ` | The smallest/largest admissible inverse-propensity weight (`OR = 1/Λ` / `OR = Λ` endpoints) | **Defined** |
| `msmUpperForm Λ` / `msmLowerForm Λ` | `ℝ := ∫ A·Y·(wMax/wMin if Y≥0 else wMin/wMax)` | The closed-form upper/lower integrals | **Defined** |
| `msmUpper_eq` / `msmLower_eq` | `msmUpper Λ = msmUpperForm Λ` / `msmLower Λ = msmLowerForm Λ` | **Closed form of the MSM bounds** — the box optimization is pointwise-separable (worst-case weight `wMax` where `Y≥0`, `wMin` where `Y<0`), attained by a feasible boundary `ẽ*`. Needs `1≤Λ`, overlap, envelope-integrability, and a.e.-measurability of candidates | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Sharp (calibrated) set (`Sharp.lean`) — the Dorn–Guo refinement: data-compatibility adds the calibration constraint `E[Z/ẽ | σ(X)] = 1`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Calibrated ẽ` / `MSMSetCalib Λ` | `Prop` / `Set (Ω→ℝ)` | calibration `E[Z/ẽ\|σ(X)] =ᵐ 1`; the box members that are calibrated (data-compatible) | **Defined** |
| `msmUpperCalib Λ` / `msmLowerCalib Λ` | `ℝ := sSup/sInf (candMean '' MSMSetCalib Λ)` | the **sharp** interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `completeProp_calibrated` | `Calibrated e₀` | **the truth is calibrated** — `E[Z/e₀\|σ(X)]=1` by the tower property (`e₀` conditions on `Y(1)`); same condExp pull-out as the IPW bridge | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y1mean_mem_Icc_calib` | `E[Y(1)] ∈ Icc (msmLowerCalib Λ) (msmUpperCalib Λ)` | **the sharp interval is valid** (contains the truth) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_le_msmUpper` / `msmLower_le_msmLowerCalib` | `msmUpperCalib Λ ≤ msmUpper Λ`, `msmLower Λ ≤ msmLowerCalib Λ` | **the sharp interval is tighter than the ZSB interval** (`MSMSetCalib ⊆ MSMSet`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Quantile balancing (`QuantileBalance.lean`) — the Dorn–Guo sharp closed form, via the Neyman–Pearson exchange argument:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cutoffProp Λ c` | `Ω → ℝ := 1/(wMax if Y > c(X) else wMin)` | the quantile-cutoff complete propensity at `σ(X)`-measurable cutoff `c` | **Defined** |
| `cutoff_optimal` | `ẽ ∈ MSMSetCalib Λ → candMean ẽ ≤ candMean (cutoffProp Λ c)` | **optimality of the cutoff weight** — Neyman–Pearson/Dantzig–Wald exchange: `candMean ẽ − candMean(1/w_c) = ∫A·(Y−c)·(w_ẽ−w_c) ≤ 0` (the `c`-term cancels by calibration, the rest is `≤0` pointwise) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_eq_cutoff` | `msmUpperCalib Λ = candMean (cutoffProp Λ c)` | **the sharp upper bound has the quantile-balancing closed form** (given a calibrating cutoff `c`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Cutoff existence — calibration brackets (`CutoffExists.lean`): `wMin_mul_propScore_le_one` (`wMin·e = e+(1−e)/Λ ≤ 1`), `one_le_wMax_mul_propScore` (`1 ≤ e+Λ(1−e) = wMax·e`), and the condExp pull-outs `condExp_treat_wMin_eq` / `condExp_treat_wMax_eq` (`E[Z·wMin\|σ(X)] = wMin·e`, resp. `wMax`). Together: the conditional calibration value at the extreme cutoffs is `E[Z·wMin\|σ(X)] = wMin·e ≤ 1 ≤ wMax·e = E[Z·wMax\|σ(X)]`, so `1` is bracketed and a calibrating cutoff exists. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

Cutoff calibration → survival equation (`CutoffSelection.lean`): `cutoff_calibValue_eq` decomposes `E[Z/cutoffProp Λ c\|σ(X)] = wMin·e + (wMax−wMin)·G(c)` (`G(c) = E[Z·1{Y>c}\|σ(X)]` the conditional treated-survival); `cutoffProp_calibrated_of_survival` ⇒ the cutoff is calibrated iff `G(c) = survTarget := (1−wMin·e)/(wMax−wMin)`; `cutoffProp_mem_MSMSet` (the cutoff is always in the box); and `cutoffProp_mem_MSMSetCalib_of_survival` — **the Phase-4 `hcut_mem` holds as soon as `c` solves `G(c) = survTarget`**. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

Cutoff construction — the sharp bound closed unconditionally (`CutoffConstruct.lean`): constructs the calibrating cutoff and discharges `hcut_mem`. `treatedXYLaw` (treated push-forward of `(X,Y)`), `treatedCondCDF` (its Mathlib-`condCDF`), `calibLevel Λ = 1 − survTarget/e`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `treatedSurv_const_eq` | `E[Z·1{Y>t}\|σ(X)] =ᵐ e(X)·(1 − F(t\|X))` | **the core bridge** — the weighted conditional survival equals the treated conditional CDF scaled by the propensity (condExp-uniqueness on σ(X)-sets: both sides `= μ(s∩{D=1}∩{Y≤t})`; `setLIntegral_condCDF` + `e=E[Z\|σX]` pull-out + σ(X)-factorization) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `treatedSurv_eq` | `E[Z·1{Y>c(X)}\|σ(X)] =ᵐ e(X)·(1 − F(c(X)\|X))` | the variable-cutoff bridge (frozen σ(X)-measurable `c`; variable-cutoff `condCDF` set-integral via the `condKernel` disintegration) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `exists_calibrating_cutoff` | `∃ c, Measurable[σX] c ∧ treatedSurv c =ᵐ survTarget Λ` | **the calibrating cutoff exists** — the conditional quantile `Q_{calibLevel}(X)` (via `measurable_condQuantile_and_attains`); needs overlap, `1<Λ`, atomless treated outcome law, interior level | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_eq_cutoff_unconditional` | `∃ c, … ∧ msmUpperCalib Λ = candMean (cutoffProp Λ c)` | **the Dorn–Guo sharp upper bound, UNCONDITIONALLY** — the quantile-balancing closed form with `hcut_mem` discharged (only overlap / `1<Λ` / atomless / interior-level / envelope-integrability regularity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Lower bound (`LowerBound.lean`) — the `sInf` mirror: the lower cutoff `lowerCutoffProp Λ c = 1/(wMin if Y>c else wMax)` (opposite weights) *minimizes* the candidate mean; `survTargetLower = (wMax·e−1)/(wMax−wMin) = e − survTarget`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lowerCutoffProp` / `survTargetLower` / `calibLevelLower` | defs | the minimizing cutoff weight, its calibration target, and quantile level | **Defined** |
| `cutoff_optimal_lower` | `candMean (lowerCutoffProp Λ c) ≤ candMean ẽ` for calibrated `ẽ` | the lower cutoff is the Neyman–Pearson minimizer | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmLowerCalib_eq_cutoff` / `lowerCutoffProp_mem_MSMSetCalib_of_survival` | closed form given a calibrating cutoff; calibration ⟺ `treatedSurv c = survTargetLower` | mirror of the upper reductions | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `exists_calibrating_cutoff_lower` | `∃ c, … ∧ treatedSurv c =ᵐ survTargetLower Λ` | the lower calibrating cutoff exists (`Q_{calibLevelLower}(X)`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
