| `msmLowerCalib_eq_cutoff_unconditional` | `∃ c, … ∧ msmLowerCalib Λ = candMean (lowerCutoffProp Λ c)` | **the Dorn–Guo sharp LOWER bound, unconditionally** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Control-arm cutoff stack (`ControlCutoff.lean`, `ControlQuantileBalance.lean`, `ControlCutoffConstruct.lean`, `ControlLowerBound.lean`) — the exact `D=0` mirror of the treated cutoff development (`wMin0`/`wMax0`/`cutoffProp0`/`lowerCutoffProp0`/`controlSurv`/`survTarget0`/`controlXYLaw`, the control condExp↔condCDF bridge `controlSurv_const_eq`/`controlSurv_eq`, optimality, calibration→survival reduction, construction). Capstones: `msmUpperCalib0_eq_cutoff_unconditional` and `msmLowerCalib0_eq_cutoff_unconditional` — the control sharp bounds in unconditional closed form. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

ATE closed form (`ATEClosedForm.lean`) — combines the four arm closed forms into the sharp ATE interval:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ate_endpoints_eq_cutoff` | `ateUpperCalib = candMean(cutoffProp cTU) − candMean0(lowerCutoffProp0 cCL)`, `ateLowerCalib = candMean(lowerCutoffProp cTL) − candMean0(cutoffProp0 cCU)` | **the sharp ATE endpoints in closed form** (treated upper − control lower, treated lower − control upper), each a conditional-quantile candidate mean | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ate_mem_Icc_cutoff` | `ate ∈ Icc (…closed lower…) (…closed upper…)` | **the true ATE lies in the sharp closed-form interval** (endpoints + `ate_mem_Icc_calib` validity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Gaussian special case (`Gaussian.lean`) — the Dorn–Guo corollary under a conditional-Gaussian treated outcome law:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `calibLevel_eq` | `calibLevel Λ =ᵐ Λ/(Λ+1)` | **the cutoff quantile level is exactly `Λ/(Λ+1)`**, independent of the propensity (since `survTarget = e/(Λ+1)`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `GaussianTreatedModel m σ` | structure | the treated conditional law of `Y` given `X` is `N(m(X), σ(X)²)` (`condCDF treatedXYLaw a = Φ((·−m a)/σ a)`) | **Defined** |
| `gaussianCutoff` / `gaussianCutoff_calibrates` | `c(X)=m(X)+σ(X)·Φ⁻¹(Λ/(Λ+1))`; it solves `treatedSurv c =ᵐ survTarget Λ` | **the explicit Gaussian quantile cutoff** calibrates — no measurable-selection needed | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_gaussian` | `msmUpperCalib Λ = candMean (cutoffProp Λ (m(X)+σ(X)·Φ⁻¹(Λ/(Λ+1))))` | **the sharp upper bound at the explicit Gaussian cutoff** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

| `GaussianOutcomeModel` / `msmUpperCalib_gaussian_halfWidth` (`GaussianHalfWidth.lean`) | `msmUpperCalib Λ = ∫ m(X) + (Λ²−1)/Λ·φ(Φ⁻¹(Λ/(Λ+1)))·∫ (1−e(X))·σ(X)` | **the Dorn–Guo Gaussian closed-form half-width** — point-identified mean plus the half-width (`gaussianMSMHalfWidthFactor`); via the conditional Gaussian mean/truncated-mean moments + the per-stratum collapse `wMin·e+(wMax−wMin)e/(Λ+1)=1` | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

(The reusable standard-normal substrate `Φ`, `probit = Φ⁻¹`, the truncated first moment `∫_c^∞ tφ = φ(c)`, and the general Gaussian truncated moment `∫_c^∞ y dN(m,v) = m(1−Φ)+√v·φ` are in `Mathlib/Probability/StdNormalCDF.lean`, `StdNormalMoments.lean`, `GaussianMoments.lean`, §10a'''.)

Scope: Phases 1–7 complete for BOTH arms (`E[Y(1)]`, `E[Y(0)]`) and the **ATE** — the sharp interval has unconditional quantile-balancing closed forms at every endpoint — plus the **Gaussian special case in full closed form** (explicit quantile cutoff + the half-width number). The MSM sharp-bound program is complete. The atomless (continuous-outcome) hypothesis is the Dorn–Guo caveat, faithfully carried; the covariate space `γ` stays a general measurable space. See `doc/msm_sensitivity_plan.md`.

Control arm (`ControlSetup.lean`, `ControlSharp.lean`) — the `D=0` reflection of `Setup.lean`/`Sharp.lean`, bounding `E[Y(0)]` by IPW-weighting `1_{D=0}` against the complete control propensity `P[D=0 | σ(X,Y(0))]` (vs. observed `P[D=0 | σ(X)] = propScore false`). Every declaration mirrors the treated arm with a `0` suffix: `sigmaXY0`, `completeProp0`, `candMean0`, `Y0mean`, `MSMSet0`, `msmUpper0`/`msmLower0`, `Calibrated0`, `MSMSetCalib0`, `msmUpperCalib0`/`msmLowerCalib0`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `candMean0_completeProp0_eq_Y0mean` | `candMean0 e₀ = E[Y(0)]` | **IPW/tower bridge (control)** — the `D=0` mirror of the treated bridge | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y0mean_mem_Icc` / `Y0mean_mem_Icc_calib` | `E[Y(0)] ∈ Icc (msmLower0 Λ) (msmUpper0 Λ)` / `… Icc (msmLowerCalib0 Λ) (msmUpperCalib0 Λ)` | **the control ZSB / sharp interval is valid** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `completeProp0_calibrated` | `Calibrated0 e₀` | the true complete control propensity is calibrated (`E[(1−Z)/e₀\|σ(X)]=1`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet0_mono` / `msmUpper0_mono` / `msmLower0_anti` / `MSMSet0_one_eq` | budget monotonicity + `Λ=1` collapse (control) | mirrors the treated structural lemmas | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib0_le_msmUpper0` / `msmLower0_le_msmLowerCalib0` | sharp control interval tighter than ZSB | mirrors the treated tightness lemmas | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

ATE interval (`ATE.lean`) — combines the two arms into a partial-ID interval for `τ = E[Y(1)] − E[Y(0)]` by *opposing* the arm bounds (`τ⁺ = ψ_T⁺ − ψ_C⁻`, `τ⁻ = ψ_T⁻ − ψ_C⁺`):

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ate` | `ℝ := Y1mean − Y0mean` | the average treatment effect `τ` | **Defined** |
| `ateUpperCalib Λ` / `ateLowerCalib Λ` | `ℝ := msmUpperCalib Λ − msmLowerCalib0 Λ` / `msmLowerCalib Λ − msmUpperCalib0 Λ` | the **sharp** ATE interval endpoints (treated upper paired with control lower, and vice versa) | **Defined** |
| `ateUpper Λ` / `ateLower Λ` | `ℝ := msmUpper Λ − msmLower0 Λ` / `msmLower Λ − msmUpper0 Λ` | the ZSB ATE interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `sub_mem_Icc_of_mem_Icc` | `a∈[aₗ,aᵤ] → b∈[bₗ,bᵤ] → a−b∈[aₗ−bᵤ, aᵤ−bₗ]` | interval-subtraction arithmetic core | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ate_mem_Icc_calib` / `ate_mem_Icc` | `τ ∈ Icc (ateLowerCalib Λ) (ateUpperCalib Λ)` / `… (ateLower Λ) (ateUpper Λ)` | **the sharp / ZSB ATE interval is valid** (contains the true `τ`), from the two arm-wise validity statements | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ateCalib_subset` | `Icc (ateLowerCalib Λ) (ateUpperCalib Λ) ⊆ Icc (ateLower Λ) (ateUpper Λ)` | the sharp ATE interval nests inside the ZSB ATE interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

---

## 9j3. `PO/ID/Partial/BalkePearl/` — Balke-Pearl Sharp ATE Bounds (Binary IV, PO framework)

Formalizes sharp LP bounds on ATE from Balke & Pearl (1994) for a binary IV system.
All three variables Z, D, Y are binary (`Bool`). Split into four files:

- `BalkePearl/Setup.lean` — `POBalkePearlSystem` data layer, `ATE`, `cellProb`, `boolToReal`.
- `BalkePearl/Assumptions.lean` — `BaseAssumptions`, `cfBundle`.
- `BalkePearl/LatentTable.lean` — `latentProb` (16-cell table), partition lemmas, `ATE_eq_sum_latent`, `cellProb_eq_sum_latent`.
- `BalkePearl/Main.lean` — `BPFeasible`, `BPObjective`, `BPIdentifiedInterval`, necessity theorem.
- `BalkePearl/Sharp.lean` — canonical model construction, sharpness theorem.
- `BalkePearl/IntervalForm.lean` — necessity in closed-interval (`Set.Icc`) form via the SandwichInterval engine bridge.

### Setup (`BalkePearl/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBalkePearlSystem P` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZbool, hDbool, hYbool, hZD, hZY, hDY }` | Binary-IV subsystem: three distinct variables with Bool codomains | **Defined** |
| `boolToReal` | `Bool → ℝ` | `false ↦ 0`, `true ↦ 1` | **Defined** |
| `ATE` | `ℝ := ∫ (YofD_real true - YofD_real false) ∂P.μ` | Target parameter, average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `cellProb y d z` | `ℝ` | Observed joint probability `P(Y=y, D=d \| Z=z)` (as a real) | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`BalkePearl/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, exogeneity, ... }` | Structural assumptions: consistency of D and Y, exogeneity of Z | **Defined** |
| `cfBundle S` | auxiliary | Bundle of counterfactual random variables for the latent type partition | **Defined** |

### LatentTable (`BalkePearl/LatentTable.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dArm z d0 d1` | `Bool` | Treatment arm selector: `d0` if `z=false`, `d1` if `z=true` | **Defined** |
| `yArm d y0 y1` | `Bool` | Outcome arm selector: `y0` if `d=false`, `y1` if `d=true` | **Defined** |
| `latentSet d0 d1 y0 y1` | `Set P.Ω` | Event that unit has latent type `(D(0),D(1),Y(0),Y(1)) = (d0,d1,y0,y1)` | **Defined** |
| `latentProb d0 d1 y0 y1` | `ℝ` | Probability of latent type `(d0,d1,y0,y1)` | **Defined** |
| `latentProb_nonneg` | `0 ≤ latentProb d0 d1 y0 y1` | Nonnegativity of cell probabilities | **Proved** |
| `latentProb_sum_eq_one` | `∑ d0 d1 y0 y1, latentProb = 1` | The 16 latent cells partition Ω | **Proved** |
| `ATE_eq_sum_latent` | `ATE = ∑ d0 d1 y0 y1, (y1-y0) * latentProb` | ATE expressed as weighted sum over latent types | **Proved** |
| `cfCellEvent z d y` | `Set P.Ω` | Cf-cell `{ω | DofZ z ω = d ∧ YofD d ω = y}` — internal helper | **private** |
| `cfTuple` | `P.Ω → Bool⁴` | Product map `(D(0), D(1), Y(0), Y(1))` — independent of `Z` under exogeneity | **private** |
| `indepFun_factualZ_cfTuple hA` | `IndepFun factualZ cfTuple P.μ` | Factual `Z` independent of cf tuple (derived from `IndepCF.project`) | **private** |
| `cellProb_eq_sum_latent hA y d z` | `cellProb y d z = ∑ compatible latentProb` | Observed cell probability = sum over compatible latent types | **Proved** |

### Main (`BalkePearl/Main.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BPFeasible S hA π` | `structure { nonneg, sum_one, marginal }` | Feasibility predicate: π is nonneg, sums to 1, reproduces all observed cell probabilities | **Defined** |
| `BPObjective π` | `ℝ := ∑ d0 d1 y0 y1, (y1-y0) * π d0 d1 y0 y1` | LP objective functional on a latent table | **Defined** |
| `BPIdentifiedInterval S hA` | `Set ℝ` | Sharp LP identified interval for ATE: `IdentifiedInterval BPObjective (BPFeasible S hA)` | **Defined** |
| `latentProb_feasible hA` | `BPFeasible S hA S.latentProb` | The realized latent table is feasible | **Proved** |
| `ATE_eq_BPObjective` | `S.ATE = BPObjective S.latentProb` | ATE equals the LP objective at the realized table | **Proved** |
| `ATE_mem_BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | `S.ATE ∈ S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | NECESSITY: true ATE lies in the identified interval | **Proved** |
| `balkePearl_sharp hA π hπ` (in `Sharp.lean`) | `∃ P' S' hA', (∀ y d z, S'.cellProb y d z = S.cellProb y d z) ∧ S'.ATE = BPObjective π` | SHARPNESS: every feasible latent table is realised by a model that reproduces S's observed cell probabilities | **Proved** |
| `balkePearl_sharp_of_mem hA τ hτ` (in `Sharp.lean`) | `∃ P' S' hA', (∀ y d z, S'.cellProb y d z = S.cellProb y d z) ∧ S'.ATE = τ` | τ-form corollary of sharpness for `BPIdentifiedInterval` membership | **Proved** |

### IntervalForm (`BalkePearl/IntervalForm.lean`)

Restates necessity in the engine's closed-interval vocabulary. `BPObjective` is an
average of `±1`/`0` contrasts against a probability vector, hence always in `[-1,1]`;
this boundedness feeds the bridge `Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_csInf_csSup`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BPObjective_le_one hnn hsum` | `BPObjective π ≤ 1` | The LP objective is at most 1 on any feasible (nonneg, sum-one) table | **Proved** |
| `neg_one_le_BPObjective hnn hsum` | `-1 ≤ BPObjective π` | The LP objective is at least -1 on any feasible table | **Proved** |
| `bddBelow_BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | `BddBelow (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)` | The identified interval is bounded below (by -1) | **Proved** |
| `bddAbove_BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | `BddAbove (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)` | The identified interval is bounded above (by 1) | **Proved** |
| `ATE_mem_Icc_csInf_csSup hA` | `S.ATE ∈ Set.Icc (sInf (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)) (sSup (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA))` | NECESSITY, closed-interval form: the true ATE lies between the LP minimum and maximum, via the SandwichInterval bridge | **Proved** |

### Sharp (`BalkePearl/Sharp.lean`)

Canonical-model construction (~750 lines) discharging the sharpness direction.
The model has `V' = Fin 3` (Z, D, Y), `X' v = Bool`, sample space
`Ω' = Bool × (Bool × Bool × Bool × Bool)`, and product measure
`μ' = μ_Z ⊗ π_meas` where `μ_Z` is `S`'s Z-marginal and `π_meas` weights Bool⁴
by π. The eval cascades Z → D → Y, respecting interventions.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBalkePearlSharp.SOmega/SV/SX` | `Type`/`Type`/`SV → Type` | Canonical sample space, variable-index, codomain assignment | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.eval r ω` | `∀ v, SX v` | Cascade evaluator for the canonical model | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.zMeasure S` | `Measure Bool` | S's Z-marginal as a discrete measure | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.piMeasure π` | `Measure Bool⁴` | Discrete measure with weights π | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonicalMeasure S π` | `Measure SOmega` | Product `zMeasure ⊗ piMeasure` | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonicalPOSystem S π hπ_nn hπ_sum` | `POSystem` | Canonical PO system | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonicalBP S π hπ_nn hπ_sum` | `POBalkePearlSystem _` | Canonical BP system | **Defined** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_consistency` | `Consistency` | Factual + composition consistency on the canonical model | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_exclusion` | `YofZD z d = YofD d` | Pointwise (and a.e.) equality | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_exogeneity` | `IndepCF zVar cfBundle μ'` | Z ⊥ cf-bundle, by `indepFun_prod` on the product measure | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_posZ hA` | `0 < μ'(zEvent z)` | Inherited from `hA.posZ` via Z-marginal identity | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_baseAssumptions hA` | `S'.BaseAssumptions` | Bundle of the four | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_latentProb_eq` | `S'.latentProb = π` | Marginal identity | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_cellProb_eq hA hπ` | `S'.cellProb y d z = S.cellProb y d z` | Reproduces observed cell probabilities | **Proved** |
| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonical_ATE_eq hA` | `S'.ATE = BPObjective π` | LP objective realisation | **Proved** |
| `balkePearl_sharp hA π hπ` | (see Main row above) | The packaged sharpness theorem | **Proved** |

**Outstanding sorrys:** None. Both necessity (`ATE_mem_BPIdentifiedInterval`) and sharpness (`balkePearl_sharp`, `balkePearl_sharp_of_mem`) are fully proved.

---

## 9j4. `PO/ID/Partial/Proxy/` — Proximal Partial-Identification Bounds (Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2024, Sections 2-3)

Formalises the partial-identification bounds of arXiv 2304.04374 — partial bounds on
ATE / `E[Y(a) | A=¬a]` using proxy variables of an unobserved confounder, **without
the completeness condition** required for exact identification. Reuses
`POProximalSystem` from `PO/ID/Exact/Proximal/Setup.lean` (six POVars X, A, Z, W, Y, U).
Nine files (Helpers and ZBased are split into subdirectories):

- `Proxy/Setup.lean` — additional σ-algebras, target parameters, envelope predicates.
- `Proxy/Assumptions.lean` — three weakened bundles (`WBased`, `ZBased`, `TwoProxy`).
- `Proxy/Helpers.lean` — facade; re-exports `Helpers/{Common,BridgeW,BridgeWZ,CondExpQ}`.
- `Proxy/Helpers/Common.lean` — `meanYofA_eq_strata` + `YofA_essbound_*` clamp lemmas.
- `Proxy/Helpers/BridgeW.lean` — W-only bridge identity `condIntYofA_eq_h_arm`.
- `Proxy/Helpers/BridgeWZ.lean` — two-proxy bridge identity `condIntYofA_eq_hq_armSwap_twoProxy` (paper Theorem 3, identity (★), same-arm form, prob-ratio q convention).
- `Proxy/Helpers/CondExpQ.lean` — q-collapse on `{A=a}` σ_AX (prob-ratio q): `condExp_q_eq_stratumOddsRatio_arm_AX`.
- `Proxy/WBased.lean` — Theorem 1 + Corollary 1 (outcome-confounding-proxy).
- `Proxy/ZBased.lean` — Theorem 2 + Corollary 2 (imports `ZBased/ArmChain`).
- `Proxy/ZBased/ArmChain.lean` — paired arm-chain lemmas (`condIntYofA_le_envelope_arm` / `envelope_le_condIntYofA_arm`), ~600 lines. Both arm chains are now sorry-free in their bodies: the `hStep67` factorisation step is extracted as a private helper `condIntYq_factor_arm` and shared between the upper and lower variants.
- `Proxy/TwoProxy.lean` — Theorem 3 (two conditionally independent invalid proxies).
- `Proxy/IntervalForm.lean` — `Set.Icc` interval restatements of all five sandwich bounds (via the `SandwichInterval` bridge `mem_Icc_of_sandwich`).

### Setup (`Proxy/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `S.AX`, `S.σ_AX`, `S.σ_X` | tuple maps + σ-algebras | Conditioning targets `(A,X)` and `X` for partial-ID proofs | **Defined** |
| `S.σ_X_le_σ_AX` | `S.σ_X ≤ S.σ_AX` | σ_X is coarser than σ_AX since `X = snd ∘ (A,X)`. Used in `WBased.lean` to lift σ_X-measurable `stratumOddsRatio · Uenv(a,X)` to σ_AX for the pull-out step | **Proved** |
| `S.σ_AX_le_σ_AUX` | `S.σ_AX ≤ S.σ_AUX` | σ_AX is coarser than σ_AUX since `(A,X)` factors through `(A,U,X)` via projection. Used by the σ_AX-tower step in `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` | **Proved** |
| `S.σ_X_le_σ_AUX` | `S.σ_X ≤ S.σ_AUX` | Composition `σ_X ≤ σ_AX ≤ σ_AUX`. Used by `ArmChain.hStep9` to lift `Uenv(a,X)` σ_X-meas to σ_AUX for L1 pull-out | **Proved** |
| `S.σ_X_le_σ_UX` | `S.σ_X ≤ S.σ_UX` | `X = snd ∘ (U, X)`. Used by `ArmChain.hStep10` to lift `Uenv(a,X)` σ_X-meas to σ_UX for L2 arm-swap | **Proved** |
| `S.σ_AZX_le_σ_AZUX` | `S.σ_AZX ≤ S.σ_AZUX` | `(A,Z,X) = proj ∘ (A,Z,U,X)`. Reserved for σ_AZX-pull-out steps | **Proved** |
| `S.σ_AUX_le_σ_AZUX` | `S.σ_AUX ≤ S.σ_AZUX` | `(A,U,X) = proj ∘ (A,Z,U,X)`. Reserved for σ_AUX-tower steps | **Proved** |
| `S.meanYofA μ a` | `ℝ := ∫ Y(a) dμ` | Marginal potential outcome mean | **Defined** |
| `S.condMeanYofA μ a` | `ℝ := (μ {A≠a}).toReal⁻¹ * ∫_{A≠a} Y(a) dμ` | Conditional PO mean `E[Y(a) | A=¬a]` | **Defined** |
| `S.ATE μ` | `ℝ := meanYofA μ true - meanYofA μ false` | Average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `S.stratumOddsRatio μ a` | `P.Ω → ℝ` | σ_X-measurable `p(A=¬a|X)/p(A=a|X)` | **Defined** |
| `S.IsUpperEnvW μ a Uenv` | `Prop` | Integrated upper-envelope predicate for the W-density ratio (Thm 1) | **Defined** |
| `S.IsLowerEnvW μ a Lenv` | `Prop` | Integrated lower-envelope predicate, mirror of above | **Defined** |
| `S.IsUpperEnvZ μ a Uenv` | `Prop` | Pointwise upper bound `(μ[Y \| σ_AZX]) ≤ Uenv(a,X)` on `{A=a}` (Thm 2) | **Defined** |
| `S.IsLowerEnvZ μ a Lenv` | `Prop` | Mirror lower bound | **Defined** |
| `S.IsUpperEnvWZ μ a Uenv` | `Prop` | Same-arm joint-vs-product W-Z envelope (Thm 3): for nonneg measurable `φ_W(W,X)`, `φ_Z(Z,X)`, μ-a.e. on `{A=a}`, `μ[φ_W·φ_Z\|σ_AX] ≤ Uenv(a,X)·μ[φ_W\|σ_AX]·μ[φ_Z\|σ_AX]`. Operational form of `ρ(w,z,x)=p(w,z\|a,x)/(p(w\|a,x)·p(z\|a,x)) ≤ Uenv(a,x)`; canonical sharp choice `Uenv(a,x)=sup_{w,z} ρ(w,z,x)` | **Defined** |
| `S.IsLowerEnvWZ μ a Lenv` | `Prop` | Mirror with inequality reversed; canonical sharp `Lenv(a,x)=inf_{w,z} ρ(w,z,x)` | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`Proxy/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WBasedAssumptions S μ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_WA, h, h_nonneg, bridge, …, Y_bdd_below/above }` | Theorem 1 bundle: drops `proxy_YZ`, `completeness`, Z-fields. `h_nonneg : ∀ x, 0 ≤ h x` is bundled (paper Assumption 2: a non-negative bridge is *posited* to exist; not derived from `Y ⊆ [0, ∞)`, which does not force a bridge to be non-negative) | **Defined** |
| `ZBasedAssumptions S μ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_YZ, q, bridge_q, likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_condExpYofA_mul_L, integrable_condExpY_mul_q, integrable_Y_mul_q, …, overlap_strong }` | Theorem 2 bundle: drops `proxy_WAZ`, `completeness`. **Probability-ratio q convention**: `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec` operationally encodes `E[𝟙{A=a}\|σ_UX]·L =ᵐ E[𝟙{A=¬a}\|σ_UX]`, i.e. `L = p(¬a\|U,X)/p(a\|U,X)` on the support of `E[𝟙{A=a}\|σ_UX]`. This avoids RN derivatives; differs from the paper's density-ratio q `p(U\|¬a,X)/p(U\|a,X)` by a factor of stratumOddsRatio (both yield the same observable bound). `overlap_strong` encodes paper Assumption 3 positivity `p(A=a\|U,X) > 0`. Four explicit integrability fields cover finiteness gaps not implied by strict overlap (paper's "all integrals finite" convention; Remark 5 forbids a Y-clamp): `integrable_likelihoodRatio_swapA` (L ∈ L¹), `integrable_condExpYofA_mul_L` (`μ[Y(a)\|σ_UX]·L` ∈ L¹), `integrable_condExpY_mul_q` (`μ[Y\|σ_AZX]·q(Z,a,X)` ∈ L¹, used inside the σ_AZX-conditional envelope step), and `integrable_Y_mul_q` (`Y·q(Z,a,X)` ∈ L¹, used inside the proxy_YZ factorisation step that materialises `μ[Y\|σ_AZX]` from `μ[Y\|σ_AUX]·μ[q\|σ_AUX]`) | **Defined** |
| `TwoProxyAssumptions S μ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_WZ_indep, h, h_nonneg, q, bridge_h, bridge_q, likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_hq_arm, … }` | Theorem 3 bundle: requires both bridges, `h_nonneg`, and `W ⟂ Z \| (A,X,U)` (paper Assumption 6). No `W ⟂ A \| (U,X)` requirement (does not bundle `proxy_WA`); also drops `proxy_YZ`. Same prob-ratio q convention as `ZBasedAssumptions`. Bundles `integrable_hq_arm : ∀ a, Integrable (h(a,W,X)·q(Z,a,X)) μ` (separate L¹ does not imply product L¹) | **Defined** |

### Helpers (`Proxy/Helpers.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `meanYofA_eq_strata` | `meanYofA = ∫_{A≠a} Y(a) + ∫_{A=a} Y` | Marginalisation identity (consistency on the on-arm); used to derive Cor 1, 2 from Thm 1, 2 | **Proved** |
| `YofA_essbound_above` / `_below` | `Y(a)` inherits `Y`'s a.e. essential bound | Lifts essential bounds on `Y` to the counterfactual `Y(a)` via `latent_exch` + consistency-on-arm; consumed by the trivial-clamp steps in W-side and TwoProxy theorems | **Proved** |
| `condIntYofA_eq_h_arm` | `∫_{A=¬a} Y(a) dμ = ∫_{A=¬a} h(a,W,X) dμ` | Bridge-substitution identity for the W-only bundle | **Proved** |
| `condIntYofA_eq_hq_armSwap_twoProxy` | `∫_{A≠a} Y(a) dμ = ∫_{A=a} h(a,W,X) · q(Z,a,X) dμ` | Two-proxy bridge-substitution identity in **same-arm form** (paper Theorem 3, identity (★), prob-ratio q convention). No `stratumOddsRatio` weight: the off-arm-to-on-arm transport is absorbed into `q` itself via `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec`. The RHS is a clean `{A=a}` integral that the σ_AX-conditional envelope `IsUpperEnvWZ`/`IsLowerEnvWZ` applies to directly — no σ_AX-fiber transport needed. Proof structured as 4 named `have`s mirroring paper steps (a)–(d): `hStepA` (latent_exch + tower at σ_AUX → ∫_{A≠a} μ[Y(a)\|σ_UX]), `hStepC` (likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec arm-swap via L2 → ∫_{A=a} μ[Y(a)\|σ_UX]·L), `hStepB` (consistency + bridge_h substitution on `{A=a}` → swap μ[Y(a)\|σ_UX] for μ[h(a,W,X)\|σ_AUX]), `hStepD1` (bridge_q → swap L for μ[q(Z,a,X)\|σ_AUX]), `hStepD2` (proxy_WZ_indep + condExp_mul_of_condIndep on (W,X) ⟂ (Z,X) — built by two prodMk-left lifts via condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left + symm), `hStepD3` (tower {A=a} ∈ σ_AUX). Black-box dependencies: `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`, `condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left`; `setIntegral_eq_setIntegral_mul_of_likelihoodRatio_swap` (LikelihoodRatioSwap.lean) | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX_core` | `μ[Y \| σ_AX] =ᵐ[restrict {A=a}] μ[h(a,W,X) \| σ_AX]` | Raw-input collapse identity: takes a bridge function `h` together with `bridge`, `integrable_Y`, `integrable_h`, `integrable_h_arm` directly (no assumption-bundle dependency). Shared core used by both the WBased and TwoProxy wrappers. Proof: bridge equation + indicator-zero argument at σ_AUX, then σ_AX-tower via `σ_AX_le_σ_AUX` and `condExp_condExp_of_le` | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` | wrapper for `WBasedAssumptions` | Bundled-input collapse identity for the W-only bundle; specialises `_core` with `HA.h, HA.bridge, HA.integrable_Y, HA.integrable_h, HA.integrable_h_arm` | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX_twoProxy` | wrapper for `TwoProxyAssumptions` | Same as above, but for the two-proxy bundle (`HA.bridge_h` plays the role of `bridge`); `TwoProxyAssumptions` does not contain `proxy_WA` so it cannot be projected to `WBasedAssumptions` | **Proved** |
| `setIntegral_mul_condExp_of_stronglyMeasurableLeft` | `∫_s f·g dμ = ∫_s f·μ[g\|m] dμ` for m-stronglyMeasurable f, integrable g and f·g, m-measurable s | Generic Mathlib-style helper combining `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` with `setIntegral_condExp`. Used in `WBased.lean` to swap `h(a,W,X)` for `μ[Y\|σ_AX]` inside `{A=a}` integrals (Fix C) | **Proved** |
| `stratumOddsRatio_spec` (private) | `μ[𝟙_{A=a}\|σ_X] · stratumOddsRatio =ᵐ μ[𝟙_{A=¬a}\|σ_X]` | σ_X projection of `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec`: algebraic safe-division identity granted σ_X-positivity of `μ[𝟙_{A=a}\|σ_X]`. Closed via `field_simp` after invoking `Causalean.ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le` (the σ_X-projection of `overlap_strong`, isolated in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean`) | **Proved** (modulo the labelled `ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le` stub in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean`) |
| `condExp_q_eq_stratumOddsRatio_arm_AX` | `μ[q(Z,a,X) \| σ_AX] =ᵐ[restrict {A=a}] stratumOddsRatio` | Prob-ratio q-collapse on `{A=a}` σ_AX. Under the codebase's prob-ratio q convention, the σ_AX-conditional expectation of `q(Z,a,X)` collapses on `{A=a}` to the σ_X-measurable stratum-odds ratio. Used in `condMeanYofA_WZ_bounds` to remove the latent bridge `q` from the public statement (parallel to `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` for `h`). Proof plan (see docstring): `ae_eq_condExp_of_forall_setIntegral_eq` over `(μ.restrict {A=a}).trim` + σ_AX → σ_X test-set reduction + the L1+L2+L2 chain (`setIntegral_mul_condExp_of_stronglyMeasurableLeft` + `setIntegral_eq_setIntegral_mul_of_likelihoodRatio_swap` twice) + `bridge_q` + `stratumOddsRatio_spec`. All substantive measure-theoretic gaps now isolated upstream — remaining body is mechanical bookkeeping (descent/integrability/measurability) | **Sorry** (mechanical bookkeeping only; no substantive gap remains in this body — substantive gap is now in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` `ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le`) |

### WBased (`Proxy/WBased.lean`) — Theorem 1 + Corollary 1

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `condMeanYofA_W_bounds` | sandwich on `condMeanYofA μ a` | Theorem 1, observable form: `max(essinf Y, env-low) ≤ E[Y(a) \| A=¬a] ≤ min(essup Y, env-up)` where the envelope integrals contain the **observable** factor `μ[Y \| σ_AX]` (not the latent bridge `h`). Removed `h` from the public statement via the collapse identity `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` (Fix C). Takes 2 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses `hU_int_h`/`hL_int_h` for `(stratumOddsRatio · Uenv/Lenv(a,X)) · h(a,W,X)` (paper's implicit "all integrals finite" convention; not bundled because `Uenv`/`Lenv` are theorem-level parameters) | **Proved** |
| `meanYofA_W_bounds` | sandwich on `meanYofA μ a` | Corollary 1, observable form with **trivial-clamp** (paper arXiv lines 177-195): `max(essinfY · μ{A≠a}, envelope-lower) + ∫_{A=a} Y dμ ≤ E[Y(a)] ≤ min(essupY · μ{A≠a}, envelope-upper) + ∫_{A=a} Y dμ`. The trivial clamp `essinfY · μ{A≠a}` corresponds to the worst-case potential outcome on the off-arm stratum. Derived via `meanYofA_eq_strata` from the conditional bound, with the off-arm trivial clamp from `YofA_essbound_above`/`_below`. Takes 4 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses for `(stratumOddsRatio · Uenv/Lenv(a,X)) · h(a,W,X)` and the `μ[Y\|σ_AX]` analogues (paper's implicit "all integrals finite" convention; not bundled because `Uenv`/`Lenv` are theorem-level parameters) | **Proved** |

### ZBased (`Proxy/ZBased.lean`) — Theorem 2 + Corollary 2

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `condIntYofA_le_envelope_arm` / `envelope_le_condIntYofA_arm` | arm-level upper / lower envelope chain | Internal lemmas reducing the `∫_{A≠a} Y(a)` integral to envelope integrals via `bridge_q`, `proxy_YZ`, `latent_exch_to_condExp'`, `consistency_event'`. Chain restructured to use σ_AUX (not σ_UX) at step (4), avoiding the missing `Y ⟂ A \| σ_UX` lift. All ten steps closed in-body: hStep1/2 (tower at σ_AUX, latent_exch), hArmSwap_Y / hStep10 (L2 arm-swap via `setIntegral_eq_setIntegral_mul_of_likelihoodRatio_swap`), hStep4 (consistency + latent_exch transfer of E[Y(a)\|σ_UX] → E[Y\|σ_AUX] on `{A=a}`), hStep5 (bridge_q substitution), `hStep67` (proxy_YZ-based factorisation: lifts Z⟂Y\|σ_AUX to (Z,X)⟂Y\|σ_AUX via `condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left`, applies `condExp_mul_of_condIndep` to factor μ[Y·q\|σ_AUX] = μ[Y\|σ_AUX]·μ[q\|σ_AUX], towers back through `setIntegral_condExp` on σ_AUX, then re-towers q·Y → q·μ[Y\|σ_AZX] via `setIntegral_mul_condExp_of_stronglyMeasurableLeft` at m = σ_AZX — extracted as the private helper `condIntYq_factor_arm` shared by upper and lower variants), hStep8 (envelope inequality with q ≥ 0), hStep9 (L1 with m = σ_AUX, f = Uenv(a,X) σ_AUX-meas via σ_X ≤ σ_AUX). Takes three theorem-level integrability hypotheses (`hUInt`, `hU_q`, `hU_L`) for envelope · q and envelope · L pairings (paper's implicit-finiteness convention) | **Proved** |
| `condMeanYofA_Z_bounds` | sandwich on `condMeanYofA μ a` | Theorem 2: `(μ {A≠a})⁻¹ * ∫_{A≠a} Lenv(a,X) ≤ E[Y(a)\|A=¬a] ≤ (μ {A≠a})⁻¹ * ∫_{A≠a} Uenv(a,X)`. No clamp needed (Remark 5). Takes 6 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses (`hLInt`, `hUInt`, `hL_q`, `hU_q`, `hL_L`, `hU_L`) threaded into the arm-chain lemmas | **Proved** (modulo `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` stubs propagated through the arm chains) |
| `meanYofA_Z_bounds` | sandwich on `meanYofA μ a` | Corollary 2: marginal version via `meanYofA_eq_strata` + `linarith`. Takes the same 6 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses as `condMeanYofA_Z_bounds` | **Proved** (modulo `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` stubs propagated through the arm chains) |

|---|---|---|---|

| `meanYofA_W_mem_Icc` | `meanYofA μ a ∈ Set.Icc L U` | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the W-proxy marginal `E[Y(a)]` bound (`meanYofA_W_bounds`), via the `SandwichInterval` bridge | **Proved** |

## 9j5. `PO/ID/Partial/Lee/` — Lee Bounds for Sample-Selection ATT (PO framework)

Formalises `prop:po-lee-bounds` from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(§`subsec:po-lee-bounds`): a partial-identification sandwich bound for the
**always-selected ATT** `τ_AS = E[Y(1) - Y(0) | alwaysSelected]` under
sample selection. Hypotheses are random assignment (encoded as the
pair-level independence `A ⫫ (Y(a), Sel(a))` for each `a`), monotone sample
selection `Sel(0) ≤ Sel(1)`, and a finite-support restriction on
`L(Y | A=true, Sel=true)`. The bound takes the form
`underline_m₁ - m₀ ≤ τ_AS ≤ overline_m₁ - m₀`, where
`underline_m₁`, `overline_m₁` are the lower and upper Lee trimmed means and
`m₀ = E[Y | A=false, Sel=true]` is the observable selected-control mean.

The quantile form (rem:po-lee-quantile) is deferred to a follow-on file.

Layout (one file per topic, all proofs complete):

- `Lee/Setup.lean` — `POLeeSystem` data + factual / counterfactual
  accessors + observable cells `selectedTreated`, `selectedControl`.
- `Lee/Assumptions.lean` — `BaseAssumptions` (consistency, pair-level
  random assignment, positivity, integrability) + `MonotoneSelection`.
- `Lee/Trim.lean` — selected-cell probabilities `p0`, `p1`, ratio `rho`,
  observable density `f1`, `LeeTrimWeight`, mean `Mw`, trimmed means
  `lowerTrimMean` / `upperTrimMean`, observable selected-control mean `m0`.
- `Lee/PrincipalStrata.lean` — latent events `alwaysSelected`,
  `helpedSelected`, `harmedSelected`; the pure set equality
  `selOfATrueSet = alwaysSelected ∪ helpedSelected`; under monotone
  selection, the a.s. identities collapsing latent events to observables.
- `Lee/ControlMean.lean` — Step A: `m0_eq_eventCondExp_Y0_alwaysSelected`
  collapses observable `m₀ = E[Y|A=false,Sel=true]` to latent
  `E[Y(0)|alwaysSelected]`.
- `Lee/MixtureIdentity.lean` — selected-treated decomposition (the doc's
  "mixture law"): `selectedTreated_integral_split` (Step B),
  `selectedTreated_measure_split` (Step B'), and the indicator-integrand
  variant `selectedTreated_integral_split_indicator`.
- `Lee/LatentSupport.lean` — `YofA_true_in_finset_ae_alwaysSelected`:
  finite outcome support transfers from observable cell to latent strata.
- `Lee/TrimWeight.lean` — `f1AS` density + the
  `alwaysSelectedTrimWeight` `LeeTrimWeight` witness with all four field
  proofs (`nonneg`, `le_one`, `zero_off`, `sum_eq`).
- `Lee/TrimMean.lean` — `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS`:
  the witness's trimmed mean equals `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`.
- `Lee/TrimBound.lean` — `trimmed_bounds_condExp_Y1_AS`: corresponding
  sandwich `lowerTrimMean 𝒴 ≤ E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected] ≤ upperTrimMean 𝒴`.
  The `csInf`/`csSup` step is now routed through the engine bridge
  `Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_csInf_csSup` (see §9j2 `SandwichInterval.lean`);
  the trimmed-mean endpoints are exactly the inf/sup of `Mw` over the
  unidentified trim-weight nuisance.
- `Lee/Main.lean` — `lee_bounds_ATT_AS` (prop:po-lee-bounds): final
  assembly via `eventCondExp_sub` on `alwaysSelected`.
- `Lee.lean` — umbrella re-export.

### Setup (`Lee/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POLeeSystem P` | `structure { A, Sel, Y, hAbool, hSelbool, hYreal, hASel, hAY, hSelY }` | Lee sample-selection subsystem over a PO system: binary `A`, binary `Sel` (selection), real `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `aVar` / `selVar` / `yVar` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ℝ` | `POVar` wrappers for the three nodes | **Defined** |
| `SelOfA S a` | `P.Ω → Bool` | Counterfactual selection `Sel(a) := selVar.cfUnder aVar a` | **Defined** |
| `YofA S a` | `P.Ω → ℝ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(a) := yVar.cfUnder aVar a` | **Defined** |
| `factualA / factualSel / factualY` | `P.Ω → Bool / Bool / ℝ` | Factual treatment / selection / outcome | **Defined** |
| `aEvent S a` / `selEvent S s` | `Set P.Ω` | Factual events `{A = a}` / `{Sel = s}` | **Defined** |
| `selectedTreated` | `Set P.Ω := aEvent true ∩ selEvent true` | Observable selected-treated cell | **Defined** |
| `selectedControl` | `Set P.Ω := aEvent false ∩ selEvent true` | Observable selected-control cell | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurable_{SelOfA, YofA, factualA, factualSel, factualY}`, `measurableSet_{aEvent, selEvent, selectedTreated, selectedControl}` | One-line delegations to `POVar` | **Proved** |

### Assumptions (`Lee/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, randAssign, posSelectedTreated, posSelectedControl, posATrue, posAFalse, posSelTrFinite, posSelCtFinite, integrableY1, integrableY0 }` | Common bundle for prop:po-lee-bounds. Factual-`Y` integrability is *derived*, not bundled (see `integrableY`) | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.randAssign` | `∀ a : Bool, IndepFun S.factualA (fun ω => (S.YofA a ω, S.SelOfA a ω)) P.μ` | Pair-level random assignment; weaker than the doc's 4-tuple joint indep, but strictly stronger than mean independence — carries the conditional distribution of `(Y(a), Sel(a))` given `A` | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_YofA hA a` | `Integrable (S.YofA a) P.μ` | Binary-folded form of `integrableY1` / `integrableY0` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrableY hA` | `Integrable S.factualY P.μ` | Factual outcome integrability, *derived* from arm integrability via consistency (`factualY = Σ_a Y(a)·1{A=a}` a.e.); no longer an assumed field | **Proved** |
| `MonotoneSelection S` | `Prop { monotone }` | Monotone sample selection: `Sel(0) ≤ Sel(1)` a.s. | **Defined** |

### Trim (`Lee/Trim.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pSelGivenA S a` | `ℝ` | `eventCondExp μ (aEvent a) (selVar.indicator true)` — conditional selection probability | **Defined** |
| `p0` / `p1` | `ℝ` | Selected-cell probabilities `P(Sel=true | A=false)` / `P(Sel=true | A=true)` | **Defined** |
| `rho` | `ℝ := p0 / p1` | Trimming ratio | **Defined** |
| `f1 S y` | `ℝ` | Observable density `P(Y=y | A=true, Sel=true)` via `eventCondExp` of an `if-then-else` indicator | **Defined** |
| `LeeTrimWeight S 𝒴` | `structure { w, nonneg, le_one, zero_off, sum_eq }` | Lee trim weight on a finite outcome support `𝒴 : Finset ℝ` (def:po-lee-trim) | **Defined** |
| `Mw wt` | `ℝ := rho⁻¹ * ∑ y ∈ 𝒴, y * wt.w y * f1 y` | Mean of a trim weight | **Defined** |
| `lowerTrimMean 𝒴` / `upperTrimMean 𝒴` | `ℝ` | `sInf` / `sSup` of `Set.range (fun wt => Mw wt)` | **Defined** |
| `m0` | `ℝ := eventCondExp μ selectedControl factualY` | Observable selected-control mean | **Defined** |

### PrincipalStrata (`Lee/PrincipalStrata.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `alwaysSelected` | `Set P.Ω := {ω | SelOfA false ω = true ∧ SelOfA true ω = true}` | Latent always-selected stratum | **Defined** |
| `helpedSelected` | `Set P.Ω := {ω | SelOfA false ω = false ∧ SelOfA true ω = true}` | Latent treatment-induced-selected stratum | **Defined** |
| `harmedSelected` | `Set P.Ω := {ω | SelOfA false ω = true ∧ SelOfA true ω = false}` | Latent harmed stratum (a.s. empty under monotone selection) | **Defined** |
| `selOfATrueSet` / `selOfAFalseSet` | `Set P.Ω` | The latent sets `{Sel(1)=true}` / `{Sel(0)=true}` | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurableSet_{alwaysSelected, helpedSelected, harmedSelected, selOfATrueSet, selOfAFalseSet}` | One-line delegations to `Measurable.preimage` of singletons | **Proved** |
| `selOfATrueSet_eq_alwaysSelected_union_helpedSelected` | `S.selOfATrueSet = S.alwaysSelected ∪ S.helpedSelected` | Pure set equality (no a.s.) by case analysis on `SelOfA false ω : Bool` | **Proved** |
| `disjoint_alwaysSelected_helpedSelected` | `Disjoint S.alwaysSelected S.helpedSelected` | The two latent strata are disjoint | **Proved** |
| `harmedSelected_ae_empty` | `MonotoneSelection ⇒ μ harmedSelected = 0` | Harmed stratum is null under monotone selection | **Proved** |
| `selOfAFalseSet_ae_eq_alwaysSelected` | `MonotoneSelection ⇒ {Sel(0)=true} =ᵐ alwaysSelected` | Latent `{Sel(0)=true}` collapses (a.s.) to alwaysSelected | **Proved** |

### ControlMean (`Lee/ControlMean.lean`) — Step A

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `m0_eq_eventCondExp_Y0_alwaysSelected` | `BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection ⇒ m0 = eventCondExp μ alwaysSelected (YofA false)` | Identifies `m₀` (observable selected-control mean) as the latent always-selected mean of `Y(0)`, via consistency on `{A=false, Sel=true}` plus drop-of-conditioning from `randAssign false` plus monotone-selection collapse `{Sel(0)=true} =ᵐ alwaysSelected` | **Proved** |

### MixtureIdentity (`Lee/MixtureIdentity.lean`) — Step B / B' / indicator

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `selectedTreated_integral_split` | `BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection ⇒ ∫ in selectedTreated, factualY = (μ(aEvent true)).toReal · (∫ in alwaysSelected, YofA true + ∫ in helpedSelected, YofA true)` | Step B: selected-treated integral splits along the latent strata, with the `μ(A=true)` mass factor from drop-of-conditioning | **Proved** |
| `selectedTreated_measure_split` | `BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection ⇒ (μ selectedTreated).toReal = (μ(aEvent true)).toReal · ((μ alwaysSelected).toReal + (μ helpedSelected).toReal)` | Step B': mass-level analogue (constant integrand `1`) | **Proved** |
| `selectedTreated_integral_split_indicator y` | `BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection ⇒ ∫ in selectedTreated, 1_{factualY = y} = (μ(aEvent true)).toReal · (∫ in alwaysSelected, 1_{YofA true = y} + ∫ in helpedSelected, 1_{YofA true = y})` | Step B for indicator integrand `1_{Y=y}`; used by `TrimWeight` to bound the trim-weight from `f1` | **Proved** |

### LatentSupport (`Lee/LatentSupport.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `YofA_true_in_finset_ae_alwaysSelected` | `BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection, 𝒴 : Finset ℝ, hSupp ⇒ (∀ᵐ ω ∂(μ.restrict alwaysSelected), YofA true ω ∈ 𝒴) ∧ (∀ᵐ ω ∂(μ.restrict helpedSelected), YofA true ω ∈ 𝒴)` | Transfers the finite-support hypothesis from observable cell to latent strata via consistency + drop-of-conditioning + monotone selection | **Proved** |

### TrimWeight (`Lee/TrimWeight.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `f1AS y` | `ℝ := eventCondExp μ alwaysSelected (fun ω => if YofA true ω = y then 1 else 0)` | Conditional density of `Y(1)` on `alwaysSelected` at `y` | **Defined** |
| `alwaysSelectedTrimWeight hA hMono 𝒴 hSupp` | `LeeTrimWeight 𝒴` with `w(y) := if y ∈ 𝒴 ∧ S.f1 y ≠ 0 then S.rho * S.f1AS y / S.f1 y else 0` | Lee-trim-weight witness for the always-selected sub-distribution; the four field proofs (`nonneg`, `le_one`, `zero_off`, `sum_eq`) collapse via `selectedTreated_integral_split_indicator` and `YofA_true_in_finset_ae_alwaysSelected` | **Proved** |

### TrimMean (`Lee/TrimMean.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS` | `Mw (alwaysSelectedTrimWeight hA hMono 𝒴 hSupp) = eventCondExp μ alwaysSelected (YofA true)` | The trimmed mean of the always-selected witness equals `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`; the `ρ⁻¹` in `Mw` cancels the `ρ` numerator built into `w` | **Proved** |

### TrimBound (`Lee/TrimBound.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `trimmed_bounds_condExp_Y1_AS` | `lowerTrimMean 𝒴 ≤ eventCondExp μ alwaysSelected (YofA true) ≤ upperTrimMean 𝒴` | Sandwich corollary of `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS`: the witness lies in `Set.range Mw`, so its value sits between the inf and sup | **Proved** |

### Main (`Lee/Main.lean`) — prop:po-lee-bounds

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lee_bounds_ATT_AS` | `[IsFiniteMeasure μ], BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection, 𝒴 : Finset ℝ, hSupp ⇒ lowerTrimMean 𝒴 - m0 ≤ eventCondExp μ alwaysSelected (YofA true - YofA false) ≤ upperTrimMean 𝒴 - m0` | **Main theorem** prop:po-lee-bounds; subtracts Step A (`m0 = E[Y(0)|alwaysSelected]`) from Step C (sandwich for `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`) via `eventCondExp_sub` | **Proved** |

### Status

- Lee module is sorry-free; full `lake build Causalean.PO.ID.Partial.Lee` succeeds with zero errors and zero `sorry` warnings on Lee files.
- Quantile form (rem:po-lee-quantile) — deferred follow-on file `Lee/Quantile.lean`.

## 9k. `PO/ID/Exact/Frontdoor.lean` — Frontdoor ATE Identification Theorem (PO framework)

Formalizes `prop:po-frontdoor-ate` from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(§`subsec:po-frontdoor-ate`, lines ~1518–1691): **classical frontdoor
identification of the ATE** under binary treatment `A`, discrete mediator `M`
(finite value space `β`), and real outcome `Y`. The proof pattern relies on the
*composition* clause of `POSystem.Consistency` to collapse the two-variable
counterfactual `Y(a, M(a))` to `Y(a)` on `{M(a) = m}`, plus full mediation and
two exchangeability assumptions.  Event-level style throughout
(`eventCondExp`), matching `Manski.lean` / `DID.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POFrontdoorSystem P β` | `structure { A, M, Y, hAbool, hMequiv, hYreal, hAM, hAY, hMY }` | Frontdoor PO subsystem: binary treatment `A`, discrete mediator `M : β` (finite), real outcome `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{aVar, mVar, yVar}` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P β` / `POVar P ℝ` | `POVar` wrappers | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{YofA, MofA}` | `Bool → P.Ω → ℝ` / `Bool → P.Ω → β` | Single-target counterfactuals `Y(a)`, `M(a)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.regimeAM S a m` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Two-target intervention `{A ← a, M ← m}` as a `Regime.sqcup` of two singletons (disjointness from `hAM`) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM S a m` | `P.Ω → ℝ := yVar.cf (regimeAM a m)` | Two-variable counterfactual `Y(a, m)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{factualA, factualM, factualY}` | `P.Ω → Bool` / `β` / `ℝ` | Factual treatment / mediator / outcome | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.measurable_{YofA, MofA, YofAM, factualA, factualM, factualY}` | `Measurable …` | One-line measurability delegations to `POVar.measurable_{cf, cfUnder, factual}` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{aEvent, mEvent}` | `Set P.Ω` | Factual events `{A = a}`, `{M = m}` (indicators are now `POVar.indicator` on the corresponding `POVar`) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.measurableSet_{aEvent, mEvent}` | — | Measurability of the factual events | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.mUnderA S a` | `RegimedVar P β` | `M(a)` as a regimed variable, for bundling | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.mBundle S` | `POCFBundle P` | Two-entry bundle `[M(true), M(false)]`, used to state `A ⊥ (M(1), M(0))` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{pA, pMgivenA, EYgivenAM}` | `ℝ` | Observable `P(A=a)`, `P(M=m \| A=a)`, `E[Y \| A=a, M=m]` (event-level) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.frontdoorTerm S a` | `ℝ := ∑ m, (EYgivenAM 1 m · pA 1 + EYgivenAM 0 m · pA 0) · pMgivenA m a` | Frontdoor-adjusted functional `φ(a)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{ATE, frontdoorATE}` | `ℝ` | Target parameter `E[Y(1) - Y(0)]` and its observable counterpart `φ(1) - φ(0)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, fullMediation, exch_AM, exch_MY, posA, posAM, indep_Y_M, integrable_{factualY, YofA, YofAM} }` | Full bundle of PO-level frontdoor assumptions from `def:po-frontdoor-ate-assumptions`.  The extra field `indep_Y_M : ∀ a m, IndepFun (MofA a) (YofAM true m) μ` is a cross-world independence assumption needed for the drop-of-conditioning step; it is not derivable from the other fields in the bare PO framework (would follow from d-separation in the graphical frontdoor setup) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.MofA_eq_factualM_on_aEvent` | `P.Consistency → ω ∈ aEvent a → MofA a ω = factualM ω` | On `{A = a}`, the single-target counterfactual `M(a)` coincides with the factual mediator; one-line shim on `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM_eq_factualY_on_aMEvent` | `P.Consistency → ω ∈ aEvent a ∩ mEvent m → YofAM a m ω = factualY ω` | On `{A = a} ∩ {M = m}`, the two-variable counterfactual `Y(a,m)` coincides with the factual outcome; built on `POVar.cf_eq_factual_of_factualAgrees` via a `sqcup`-case-split factual-agreement proof | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM_eq_YofA_on_MofA_event` | `P.Consistency → MofA a ω = m → YofAM a m ω = YofA a ω` | **Composition lemma**: on `{M(a) = m}`, `Y(a,m) = Y(a)` pointwise; reduces the two-target counterfactual to the single-target one via `Consistency.composition` applied to the disjoint singletons `{A ← a}` and `{M ← m}` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.integral_eq_sum_integral_MofA` | `Integrable f μ → ∫ f ∂μ = ∑ m : β, ∫ ω in {MofA a = ·}, f ω ∂μ` | Finite-partition helper: decompose an integral along the level sets of the counterfactual mediator `MofA a` (valid since `β` is `Fintype`) | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.integral_eq_sum_eventCondExp_aEvent` | `Integrable g μ → ∫ g ∂μ = ∑ a' : Bool, eventCondExp μ (aEvent a') g · μ(aEvent a').toReal` | Bool-partition tower: express `∫ g` as the sum over treatment arms of arm-conditional means weighted by arm probability | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.EofY_eq_frontdoorTerm` | `S.Assumptions → ∀ a, ∫ YofA a ∂P.μ = frontdoorTerm a` | **Frontdoor identification (individual regime):** `E[Y(a)] = φ(a)` for each `a ∈ {0,1}`.  Closes in four blocks: (A) mediator-marginal identity `μ(MofA a = m).toReal = pMgivenA m a` via `exch_AM.component` + consistency; (B) on-slice composition + full-mediation rewrite `∫_{MofA a = m} YofA a = ∫_{MofA a = m} YofAM true m`; (C) drop-of-conditioning via `indep_Y_M`; (D) inner tower `∫ YofAM true m = ∑_{a'} EYgivenAM a' m · pA a'` via `integral_eq_sum_eventCondExp_aEvent` + `exch_MY` + `fullMediation` + `YofAM_eq_factualY_on_aMEvent` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.ate_frontdoor` | `S.Assumptions → S.ATE = S.frontdoorATE` | **Frontdoor identification of the ATE** `prop:po-frontdoor-ate`: immediate corollary of `EofY_eq_frontdoorTerm` at `a = 1, 0` by linearity of the integral | **Proved** |

---

## 9l. `PO/ID/Exact/Proximal/` — Proximal ATE Identification (PO framework, skeleton)

Proximal causal inference ATE identification under binary treatment `A`,
outcome-side proxy `W`, treatment-side proxy `Z`, latent confounder `U`, and covariate `X`.
Implements def:po-proximal-ate from Basic Concepts.tex §2555–2622.
Split into four files: **Setup** (data layer), **Assumptions** (assumption bundle), **Helpers** (CE manipulations), **Main** (identification theorems).

### `Proximal/Setup.lean` — Data layer

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POProximalSystem P γ_X γ_Z γ_W γ_U` | `structure { Xvar, Avar, Zvar, Wvar, Yvar, Uvar }` | Six `POVar`s: covariate `X`, binary treatment `A`, treatment-side proxy `Z`, outcome-side proxy `W`, real outcome `Y`, latent confounder `U` | **Defined** |
| `S.{X, A, Z, W, Y, U}` | `P.Ω → γ_*` | Factual maps for each variable | **Defined** |
| `S.YofA a` | `P.Ω → ℝ` | Potential outcome `Y(a) := Yvar.cfUnder Avar a` | **Defined** |
| `S.{AZX, AUX, UX, AZUX}` | `P.Ω → Bool × …` | Tuple maps used as conditioning targets | **Defined** |
| `S.{σ_AZX, σ_AUX, σ_UX, σ_AZUX}` | `MeasurableSpace P.Ω` | σ-algebras generated by the respective tuples | **Defined** |
| `S.{σ_AZX_le, σ_AUX_le, σ_UX_le, σ_AZUX_le}` | `S.σ_* ≤ inferInstance` | Sub-σ-algebra proofs for each | **Proved** |

### `Proximal/Assumptions.lean` — Assumption bundle

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POProximalSystem.Assumptions S μ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_YZ, proxy_WAZ, h, measurable_h, integrable_hAWX, bridge, positivity_arm, completeness, integrable_{Y,YofA0,YofA1,h0WX,h1WX} }` | Full proximal assumption bundle (def:po-proximal-assumptions): consistency, latent exchangeability Y(a) ⟂ A \| (U,X), outcome proxy Y ⟂ Z \| (A,U,X), treatment proxy W ⟂ (A,Z) \| (U,X), outcome bridge function `h` with E[Y − h(A,W,X) \| A,Z,X] = 0, `positivity_arm` (Miao 2018 Asm. 7: each treatment arm has positive conditional probability given (U,X)), stratum-wise Z-completeness for (U,X) functionals on each arm `{A=a}` (Miao 2018 Asm. 8), and integrabilities | **Defined** |

### `Proximal/Helpers.lean` — Conditional-expectation manipulation lemmas

Six helper lemmas for the proximal ATE proof.  The five "drop conditioning" lemmas remain `sorry`'d pending downstream proof cleanup around the now-proved generic drop-conditioning lemma; the sixth (`eq_zero_globally_of_eq_zero_on_arm`) is proved and uses `positivity_arm` to lift a stratum-wise zero σ_UX-measurable function to global μ-zero.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `condExp_drop_Z HA` | `μ[S.Y \| S.σ_AZUX] =ᵐ[μ] μ[S.Y \| S.σ_AUX]` | Drop Z from conditioning on Y: Y ⟂ Z \| (A,U,X) makes Z irrelevant in σ(A,Z,U,X) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `condExp_h_drop_Z HA a` | `μ[h(a,W,X) \| σ_AZUX] =ᵐ[μ] μ[h(a,W,X) \| σ_AUX]` | Drop Z from conditioning on h(a,W,X): via weak union from W ⟂ (A,Z) \| (U,X) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` + weak union) |
| `condExp_h_drop_A HA a` | `μ[h(a,W,X) \| σ_AUX] =ᵐ[μ] μ[h(a,W,X) \| σ_UX]` | Drop A from conditioning on h(a,W,X): W ⟂ A \| (U,X) (projection from W ⟂ (A,Z) \| (U,X)) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` + CI projection) |
| `latent_exch_to_condExp HA a` | `μ[S.YofA a \| S.σ_AUX] =ᵐ[μ] μ[S.YofA a \| S.σ_UX]` | Drop A from conditioning on Y(a): Y(a) ⟂ A \| (U,X) makes A irrelevant | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `consistency_event HA a hAY` | `μ[S.Y \| S.σ_AUX] =ᵐ[μ.restrict {A=a}] μ[S.YofA a \| S.σ_AUX]` | On {A=a}, Y = Y(a); requires `hAY : S.Avar.v ≠ S.Yvar.v`.  Step 1 (Y =ᵐ Y(a) on restrict) proved via `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` + `ae_restrict_of_forall_mem`.  Step 2 (lift to condExp on restrict) `sorry`'d | **sorry** (step 2 needs condExp-over-restrict lift lemma) |
| `eq_zero_globally_of_eq_zero_on_arm HA a hf_meas hf_zero_on_arm` | `f =ᵐ[μ] 0` (when `f` is σ_UX-measurable and zero on arm `{A=a}`) | Lift a stratum-wise zero σ_UX-measurable function to global μ-zero via `positivity_arm` (Miao 2018 Asm. 7). | **Proved** |

### `Proximal/Main.lean` — Identification theorems

Ties the helper lemmas together into the two main identification results.
`ate_proximal` is fully proved (one-line, reduces to `Eofyofa_eq_Eh`).
`Eofyofa_eq_Eh` is `sorry`'d pending resolution of the helper blockers and
the `g_a` measurable-selection construction.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Eofyofa_eq_Eh HA a hAY` | `∫ ω, S.YofA a ω ∂μ = ∫ ω, HA.h (a, S.W ω, S.X ω) ∂μ` | For each treatment level `a`, counterfactual mean equals bridge mean; requires constructing `g_a : γ_U × γ_X → ℝ` via σ-algebra factorisation and applying completeness | **sorry** (blocked on helpers + needs measurable-selection for `g_a`) |
| `ate_proximal HA hAY` | `∫ YofA true ∂μ − ∫ YofA false ∂μ = ∫ h(true,W,X) ∂μ − ∫ h(false,W,X) ∂μ` | ATE equals difference of bridge integrals; immediate from `Eofyofa_eq_Eh` for both treatment levels | **Proved** |

---

## 10a. `Mathlib/CondDistrib.lean` — Missing `condDistrib` lemmas (stubs to upstream)

Collects general-purpose conditional-distribution lemmas that are not
specific to this project and should plausibly be upstreamed to Mathlib.
Statements here are independent of the SCM / SWIG infrastructure.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.condDistrib_comp_right_measurableEquiv` | `∀ᵐ x ∂(μ.map X), condDistrib Y (e ∘ X) μ (e x) = condDistrib Y X μ x` (for `e : α ≃ᵐ β`) | Push-forward invariance of `condDistrib` under a measurable equivalence of the conditioning variable; proof via uniqueness of disintegration (`condDistrib_ae_eq_iff_measure_eq_compProd`) applied to the transported kernel `(condDistrib Y X μ).comap e.symm e.symm.measurable`. `Rule2Kernel`'s `hA_extract` step was closed inline without this stub. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.measure_eq_bind_marginal_condDistrib` | `μ = (μ.map Prod.snd).bind (fun c => (condDistrib Prod.fst Prod.snd μ c).map (fun b => (b, c)))` for finite `μ : Measure (β × γ)`, `β` standard Borel | Measure-theoretic disintegration / chain rule: a finite measure on `β × γ` equals the `bind` of its `γ`-marginal against the regular conditional distribution of `β | γ`; consumed by the backdoor completeness chain-rule step | **Proved** |

## 10a''. `Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/GraphMapProd.lean` — graph push-forward of a product measure

General measure-theoretic core of "exogenous noise + a structural mechanism gives a composition
product": pushing a product measure forward along a graph map preserving the first coordinate is a
`compProd`. Independent of the SCM/SWIG layer; used by Invariant Causal Prediction (`MechanismFactor`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.GraphMapProd.mechanismKernel` | `(νₗ : Measure γ) (Φ : β × γ → δ) : Kernel β δ`, with `mechanismKernel νₗ Φ o = νₗ.map (Φ (o, ·))` | The structural-mechanism kernel: at each value `o` of the first coordinate, push the second-factor law `νₗ` through the slice `Φ (o, ·)`. Markov when `νₗ` is a probability measure and `Φ` measurable. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.GraphMapProd.map_graph_prod_eq_compProd` | `Measure.map (fun p => (p.1, Φ p)) (α.prod νₗ) = α.compProd (mechanismKernel νₗ Φ)` (`α` sfinite, `νₗ` probability, `Φ` measurable) | Pushing the product `α ⊗ νₗ` along the graph map `(o,l) ↦ (o, Φ(o,l))` equals the composition product against `mechanismKernel`. | **Proved** |

## 10a'''. `Mathlib/Probability/StdNormalCDF.lean` & `StdNormalMoments.lean` — standard-normal CDF, probit, truncated moment

Self-contained one-dimensional standard-normal analysis (Mathlib gap; upstream-contribution candidate), used by the Gaussian special case of the Dorn–Guo MSM bound. `Φ` reuses Mathlib's `cdf (gaussianReal 0 1)` (inheriting monotonicity + `atBot/atTop` limits); the rest is proven here. All `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdNormalPDF` / `stdNormalCDF` | `ℝ → ℝ` | the standard-normal density `φ = gaussianPDFReal 0 1` and CDF `Φ = cdf (gaussianReal 0 1)` | **Defined** |
| `stdNormalCDF_continuous` | `Continuous stdNormalCDF` | `Φ` continuous — the standard normal is atomless (StieltjesFunction jump = 0) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `stdNormalCDF_strictMono` | `StrictMono stdNormalCDF` | `Φ` strictly increasing — positive mass on every interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `stdNormalCDF_pos` / `stdNormalCDF_lt_one` | `0 < Φ x` / `Φ x < 1` | full support: `Φ` stays in the open interval `(0,1)` | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `probit` | `ℝ → ℝ := sInf {x \| p ≤ Φ x}` | the standard-normal quantile `Φ⁻¹` (generalized inverse) | **Defined** |
| `stdNormalCDF_probit` / `probit_stdNormalCDF` | `Φ(Φ⁻¹ p)=p` on `(0,1)` / `Φ⁻¹(Φ x)=x` | exact two-sided inversion (IVT + strict monotonicity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `integral_Ioi_stdNormalPDF` | `∫_{c}^∞ φ = 1 − Φ(c)` | the survival integral | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `integral_Ioi_id_mul_stdNormalPDF` | `∫_{c}^∞ t·φ(t) dt = φ(c)` | the **truncated first moment** (antiderivative `−φ` + FTC) — the identity behind the closed-form Gaussian MSM half-width | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `gaussianMSMHalfWidthFactor` | `ℝ → ℝ := (Λ²−1)/Λ · φ(Φ⁻¹(Λ/(Λ+1)))` | the Dorn–Guo Gaussian half-width scalar | **Defined** |

`Mathlib/Probability/MeasurableCondQuantile.lean` (**proved, axiom-clean**) builds the remaining Dorn–Guo Phase-7 substrate from Mathlib's `condCDF`: `condQuantile ρ τ a = sInf {x | τ a ≤ condCDF ρ a x}` (the conditional generalized inverse) and `measurable_condQuantile_and_attains` — it is measurable in `a` (sublevel-set identity `{q ≤ t} = {a | τ a ≤ condCDF ρ a t}` + `measurable_condCDF`) and, under atomless conditional laws (`Continuous (condCDF ρ a)`), attains the level `condCDF ρ a (condQuantile ρ τ a) = τ a` (right-continuity ⇒ inf attained; IVT ⇒ exact). The conditional analogue of `Causalean.Stat.quantile`; an upstream-Mathlib-contribution candidate. It is consumed by `MSM/CutoffConstruct.lean` (§9j2msm) to construct the calibrating cutoff and close the sharp bound.

## 10a''''. `Mathlib/Analysis/SecondOrderDescent.lean` — one-dimensional smooth descent

Mathlib-shaped scalar optimization helper: a second-derivative upper bound on a real interval
gives a quadratic Taylor upper model, and a strictly negative initial slope gives an explicit
descent gap at the clipped model-optimal step. The module is objective-agnostic; callers supply
the C² regularity and pointwise curvature bound.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.SecondOrderDescent -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep` | `ℝ → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ` | The quadratic-model step chooses the endpoint when curvature is zero and otherwise clips the unconstrained optimizer at the available interval length. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_of_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep 0 c T = T` | With zero curvature bound, the clipped quadratic-model step is the endpoint. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_of_pos` | `0 < M → ∀ (c T : ℝ), Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T = min T (c / M)` | With a positive curvature bound, the clipped quadratic-model step is the smaller of the available interval length and the unconstrained quadratic-model optimizer. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_mem_Icc` | `0 ≤ M → 0 ≤ c → 0 ≤ T → Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ∈ Set.Icc 0 T` | The clipped quadratic-model step is feasible whenever the interval length, slope margin, and curvature bound are nonnegative. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_upper_bound` | `0 ≤ T → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ M) → ∀ {t : ℝ}, t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → f t ≤ f 0 + deriv f 0 * t + M / 2 * t ^ 2` | A twice differentiable real function whose second derivative is bounded above on an interval is no larger than its tangent at the left endpoint plus the quadratic curvature allowance. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.first_order_upper_bound` | `0 ≤ T → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ 0) → ∀ {t : ℝ}, t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → f t ≤ f 0 + deriv f 0 * t` | A real function with nonpositive second derivative on an interval lies below its tangent line at the left endpoint. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap` | `0 ≤ T → 0 < c → 0 ≤ M → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ M) → deriv f 0 ≤ -c → Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ∈ Set.Icc 0 T ∧ f 0 - f (Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T) ≥ c * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T - M / 2 * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ^ 2` | A negative initial slope plus an upper curvature bound gives a guaranteed decrease at the clipped quadratic-model step. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap_min` | `0 ≤ T → 0 < c → 0 < M → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ M) → deriv f 0 ≤ -c → f 0 - f (min T (c / M)) ≥ c / 2 * min T (c / M)` | With positive curvature, the clipped quadratic-model step gives at least half of the linear descent term. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.first_order_descent_gap` | `0 ≤ T → 0 < c → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ 0) → deriv f 0 ≤ -c → f 0 - f T ≥ c * T` | With zero curvature, the endpoint step gives the full linear descent guaranteed by the negative initial slope. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap_half` | `0 ≤ T → 0 < c → 0 ≤ M → ContDiffOn ℝ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) → DifferentiableAt ℝ f 0 → (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ≤ M) → deriv f 0 ≤ -c → Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ∈ Set.Icc 0 T ∧ f 0 - f (Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T) ≥ c / 2 * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T` | In both zero and positive curvature regimes, the clipped quadratic-model step gives a uniform half-linear decrease. |
<!-- /GEN -->

## 10a'. `Mathlib/CondDistribWitness.lean` — Witness-kernel form of a conditional distribution

The measure-theoretic core of a backdoor / Rule-2 argument, stated purely in terms of
Mathlib's `condDistrib` (no SCM machinery).  When the outcome is a measurable function of the
conditioning variable and a residual coordinate, its conditional law is the pushforward of the
residual's conditional law by that function — the "witness kernel".

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ProbabilityTheory.witnessKernel` | `Kernel (𝒳 × 𝒵) 𝒴`, `p ↦ (condDistrib C Z μ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2)` | Packages the posterior witness kernel `p ↦ (condDistrib C Z μ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2)` as a `Kernel`; Markov when `μ` is finite. | **Proved** |
| `ProbabilityTheory.condDistrib_map_of_condDistrib_fst_eq` | If `Y = h X Z C` pointwise and `C ⊥ X ∣ Z` (as `condDistrib C (X,Z) μ =ᵐ condDistrib C Z μ`), then `condDistrib Y (X,Z) μ =ᵐ (fun p => (condDistrib C Z μ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2))` | The witness-kernel form of a conditional distribution under conditional independence; the SCM-agnostic heart of the backdoor / Rule-2 identification. Proof by uniqueness of disintegration on rectangles. | **Proved** |
| `ProbabilityTheory.condDistrib_map_of_funext` | If `Y = H Z C` pointwise then `condDistrib Y Z μ =ᵐ[μ.map Z] (fun z => (condDistrib C Z μ z).map (H z))` | The **no-treatment** (`X`-free) specialization of the witness lemma: when the conditioning variable `Z` already carries all the information used to split `Y`, no conditional-independence hypothesis is needed. Consumed by the do-side bridge `doSide_M2_pullback_eq_M1_witness`. | **Proved** |

## 10b. `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` — Graph-independent CI axioms (re-export)

Re-exports the four sub-modules below.  Existing `import Causalean.Mathlib.CondIndep`
statements continue to work unchanged.

### 10b-i. `Mathlib/CondIndep/SemiGraphoid.lean` — Semi-graphoid axioms on `ValuesOn` projections

Weak union and contraction for `CondIndepFun` specialized to `ValuesOn I Ω` projections.
Requires `Causalean.SCM.Model.SCM`.

### 10b-ii. `Mathlib/CondIndep/CondExp.lean` — CondExp + CondIndepFun lemmas

Drop-of-conditioning, product factorization, weak union via `prodMk`, and extension
by an `m`-measurable function.  No SCM import required.

### 10b-iii. `Mathlib/CondIndep/AELift.lean` — A.e.-equality/inequality lifts under overlap

Single-arm a.e.-lift and essential-bound inheritance for potential-outcome variables.

### 10b-iv. `Mathlib/CondIndep/Integrability.lean` — Integrability via condExp indicators

σ-projection of indicator positivity and integrability bound from a condExp product identity.

**SemiGraphoid** (`CondIndep/SemiGraphoid.lean`):

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.condIndep_valuesProjection_weak_union` | `CondIndepFun (σ_Z) (valProjX) (valProjYW) μ → CondIndepFun (σ_ZW) (valProjX) (valProjY) μ` | Weak-union axiom on `ValuesOn` projections; proved by decomposing the union projection into `(Y,W)` and applying the generic `prodMk` weak-union helper | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndep_valuesProjection_contraction` | `CondIndepFun (σ_ZW) X Y μ → CondIndepFun σ_Z X W μ → CondIndepFun σ_Z X (valProjYW) μ` | Contraction axiom on `ValuesOn` projections; proved by `condIndepFun_contraction_of_prodMk` plus projection-union assembly | **Proved** |

**CondExp** (`CondIndep/CondExp.lean`):

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep_comap` *(private)* | `CondIndepFun m hm g f μ → StronglyMeasurable[σ(g)] Y → Integrable Y μ → μ[Y \| m ⊔ σ(f)] =ᵐ[μ] μ[Y \| m]` | Precise generic drop-of-conditioning helper for `σ(g)`-measurable real integrands; proved by indicator rectangles plus L1 density | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` | `CondIndepFun m hm g f μ → Measurable h → Integrable (h ∘ g) μ → μ[h ∘ g \| m ⊔ σ(f)] =ᵐ[μ] μ[h ∘ g \| m]` | Drop-of-conditioning: if `g ⟂ f \| m`, enlarging conditioning by `σ(f)` leaves `h ∘ g`'s condExp unchanged; reduces to the private `σ(g)`-measurable helper | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condExp_mul_of_condIndep` | `CondIndepFun m hm f g μ → μ[(u∘f)·(v∘g) \| m] =ᵐ μ[u∘f \| m] * μ[v∘g \| m]` | Product factorization of condExp under CI; core step of backdoor ATE proof | **Proved** (depends on `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_weak_union_of_prodMk` | `CondIndepFun m hm W (V,A) μ → CondIndepFun (m ⊔ σ_A) (sup_le hm _) W V μ` | Weak union via `prodMk`; proved by CE tower/pullout over the `(V,A)` conditioning algebra and drop-conditioning for the W-indicator | **Proved** (depends on `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left` | `CondIndepFun m hm W Z μ → Measurable[m] X → CondIndepFun m hm (W,X) Z μ` | Extension of `CondIndepFun` by an `m`-measurable factor | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_contraction_of_prodMk` | `CondIndepFun (m ⊔ σ(W)) X Y μ → CondIndepFun m X W μ → CondIndepFun m X (Y,W) μ` | Generic contraction via a product right side; proved by CE tower/pullout and π-system extension over measurable rectangles | **Proved** |

**AELift** (`CondIndep/AELift.lean`):

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.indicator_aeEq_of_aeEq_restrict` | `f =ᵐ[μ.restrict s] g → s.indicator f =ᵐ[μ] s.indicator g` | Pushes a restricted a.e.-equality to a global equality after multiplying by the set indicator | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.aeEq_restrict_of_indicator_aeEq` | `s.indicator f =ᵐ[μ] s.indicator g → f =ᵐ[μ.restrict s] g` | Recovers a restricted a.e.-equality from a global set-indicator equality | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condExp_indicator_aeEq_zero` | `s.indicator f =ᵐ[μ] 0 → s.indicator (μ[f \| m]) =ᵐ[μ] 0` | Propagates indicator-zero facts through conditional expectation when `s` is `m`-measurable | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.ae_eq_of_ae_eq_restrict_arm` | Two `m`-measurable real functions equal on `μ.restrict {A=a}` are equal μ-a.e., given overlap | Single-arm σ-pushforward lift; used in `Proxy/Helpers/BridgeW.lean` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.ae_le_YofA_of_ae_le_Y` | `Y ≤ M` a.e. + latent exchangeability + consistency → `Y(a) ≤ M` a.e. | Inherits Y's essential upper bound under overlap | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.ae_le_YofA_of_ae_le_Y_below` | Mirror of above for lower bound | Inherits Y's essential lower bound | **Proved** |
