| `Causalean.integrableOn_of_condExp_indicator_mul` | `Measurable[m] g → 0 ≤ᵐ g → μ⟦𝟙_B\|m⟧·g =ᵐ μ⟦𝟙_C\|m⟧ → IntegrableOn g B μ` | MCT-based integrability bound; used in `CondExpQ.lean` | **Proved** |

## 10c. `Mathlib/Indep.lean` — `IndepFun` / `CondIndepFun` plumbing (Block 1)

Generic facts about independence and conditional independence, candidates for
upstream inclusion in Mathlib.  Consumed by `SCM/Do/LocalMarkov.lean` to
discharge the latent-root branch of the local Markov property
(`full_local_markov_latent`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.indepFun_pi_of_disjoint` | `S T : Finset ι`, `Disjoint S T` → `IndepFun (coord-tuple on S) (coord-tuple on T) (Measure.pi μ)` for a family of probability measures | Coordinate-tuple projections at disjoint `Finset`s are `IndepFun` under `Measure.pi`.  Derived from `iIndepFun_pi` + `iIndepFun.indepFun_finset` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.finsetCoordProj` / `Causalean.measurable_finsetCoordProj` | `Finset ι → (∀ i, Ω i) → ValuesOn S Ω` and measurability | Generic finite-block tuple projection on a dependent product, packaged separately so product-measure independence lemmas can talk about coordinate blocks without depending on SCM-specific `ValuesOn` infrastructure | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual` / `Causalean.measurable_finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual` / `Causalean.finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual_comp` | Reassemble `π_S` from `(π_U, π_{S \\ U})`, with measurability and composition identity | Residual-coordinate bookkeeping used to reduce overlap-in-conditioning to a disjoint residual product statement | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_pi_cond_residual_of_disjoint` | pairwise-disjoint residual blocks `S0`, `T0`, `U` → `CondIndepFun σ(π_U) (π_U,π_S0) (π_U,π_T0) (Measure.pi μ)` | Focused finite-product conditional-independence fact: after conditioning on `π_U`, disjoint residual coordinate blocks are conditionally independent; proved from unconditional disjoint-block independence plus weak-union/prodMk lifting | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_pi_of_inter_subset` | `S ∩ T ⊆ U → CondIndepFun σ(π_U) π_S π_T (Measure.pi μ)` | Raw finite-product conditional-independence fact: coordinate tuple on `S` is CI of tuple on `T` given tuple on `U` whenever the overlap is absorbed by conditioning; reduces to the residual disjoint-block helper | **Proved** (depends on `condIndepFun_pi_cond_residual_of_disjoint`) |
| `Causalean.comap_eq_bot_of_subsingleton` | `Subsingleton β → MeasurableSpace.comap (f : α → β) inferInstance = ⊥` | The comap σ-algebra of a subsingleton-valued function is trivial — every preimage is either `∅` or `univ`.  Used to collapse the `FullCondIndep M X Y ∅ … μ` conditioning σ-algebra to `⊥` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_bot_of_indepFun` | `IsProbabilityMeasure μ` + `Measurable f` + `Measurable g` + `IndepFun f g μ` → `CondIndepFun ⊥ bot_le f g μ` | Bridge: `IndepFun` lifts to `CondIndepFun` with trivial (`⊥`) conditioning σ-algebra on a standard Borel space.  Proof via `condIndepFun_iff_condExp_inter_preimage_eq_mul` + `condExp_bot` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.indepFun_of_map` | `AEMeasurable φ ν` + measurable `X, Y` + `IndepFun (X ∘ φ) (Y ∘ φ) ν` → `IndepFun X Y (ν.map φ)` | Pushforward bridge for `IndepFun`: analogue of `LocalMarkov.condIndepFun_of_map` at the unconditional level.  Proof via `indepFun_iff_measure_inter_preimage_eq_mul` + `Measure.map_apply_of_aemeasurable` | **Proved** |

## 10d. `Mathlib/IndepIntegral.lean` — Drop-of-conditioning integral identity

Upstream candidate.  Contains no Causalean-specific definitions; namespaced
directly under `ProbabilityTheory` so it can be contributed back to Mathlib
verbatim.  Extracted from the IV/LATE first-stage and reduced-form proofs.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ProbabilityTheory.IndepFun.integral_restrict_preimage_eq_mul` | `IndepFun f g μ`, `Measurable f`, `Measurable g`, `MeasurableSet E`, `Measurable h : β → ℝ` → `∫ ω in f ⁻¹' E, h (g ω) ∂μ = (μ (f ⁻¹' E)).toReal * ∫ ω, h (g ω) ∂μ` | Drop-of-conditioning: under independence of `f` and `g`, conditioning on an event in the `f`-fibre factors out as a scalar multiple.  Proof rewrites the set integral as an indicator-weighted integral, applies `IndepFun.integral_fun_comp_mul_comp`, and evaluates via `integral_indicator_one` | **Proved** (upstream candidate — no Causalean types) |

## 10d''. `Mathlib/ELpNormMeasurable.lean` — Parametric L²-norm measurability

Upstream candidate.  Provides causal-agnostic measurability facts for the
parameter map `ω ↦ ‖g ω‖_{L²(P)}` by rewriting the L² norm as a parametric
lintegral and applying Tonelli measurability.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry` | `Measurable (Function.uncurry g)` → `Measurable (fun ω => (eLpNorm (g ω) 2 P).toReal)` | Top-σ-algebra version. Uses `Measurable.lintegral_prod_right'` on `‖g ω x‖ₑ^2`, rewrites `eLpNorm`, and post-processes with `.toReal`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry_of_factor` | `Measurable[mΩ.prod σ_X] (Function.uncurry g)` → `Measurable[mΩ] (fun ω => (eLpNorm (g ω) 2 P).toReal)` | Sub-σ-algebra product version. The theorem requires joint measurability with respect to the sub-σ-algebra product; coordinate/cylinder measurability alone is not enough to invoke Mathlib's parametric lintegral measurability lemma. | **Proved** |

## 10d'. `Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean` — Conditional 2nd-moment bound for centered i.i.d. sums

Upstream candidate.  Isolates the central probabilistic inequality used by the
fold-B empirical-process bound in `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`: for a
finite family of i.i.d. samples independent of a sub-σ-algebra `m_A`, the
expected square of the centered scaled sum is bounded by the expected
`L²(P)`-norm squared of the integrand.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lintegral_ofReal_inv_sqrt_smul_sum_sq_le` (private) | `0 < s.card`, unscaled square `∫⁻(Σ Y_i)² ≤ s.card · ∫⁻ B` → scaled `∫⁻ ((1/√s.card)·Σ Y_i)² ≤ ∫⁻ B` | Deterministic algebraic step: the `(1/√s.card)²` prefactor cancels the `s.card` factor on the RHS via `lintegral_const_mul'` and `Real.sq_sqrt`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` → `ENNReal.ofReal ((eLpNorm f 2 P).toReal ^ 2) = ENNReal.ofReal (∫ f^2 dP)` | Converts the real `L²` norm square back to the square integral, using `MemLp.eLpNorm_eq_integral_rpow_norm` and nonnegativity of `∫ f^2`. **Proved.** |
| `centered_sq_lintegral_le_eLpNorm_two_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` → `∫⁻ ofReal ((f - ∫f)^2) dP ≤ ofReal (‖f‖²₂)` | Diagonal variance bound: rewrites the centered square lintegral as `variance f P`, applies `variance_le_expectation_sq`, then uses `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` → under `Measure.pi (fun _ : s => P)`, `∫⁻ ofReal ((Σ_i (f(v_i)−∫f))²) ≤ |s|·ofReal(‖f‖²₂)` | Fixed-integrand finite-product estimate. Uses `variance_sum_pi` to eliminate cross terms, proves the centered coordinate mean is zero by `integral_map` along `measurePreserving_eval`, and bounds each variance by `variance_le_expectation_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` (private) | `Indep m_A (comap Z) μ`, `m_A ≤ mΩ`, measurable `Z` → `map (fun ω => (ω, Zω)) μ = (μ.trim hm_A_le).prod (map Z μ)` | Independent-joining bridge: converts sub-σ-algebra independence to `IndepFun id Z`, applies `indepFun_iff_map_prod_eq_prod_map_map`, and identifies the first marginal as the trimmed measure. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law` (private) | Joined product law `(ω, W_s ω) ∼ (μ.trim hm_A_le) × P^s` plus the public measurability/L² hypotheses → unscaled bound | Product-space form: rewrites the original lintegral through the joined law, applies Tonelli, uses `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` pointwise in `ω`, pulls out `|s|`, and rewrites the trimmed lintegral back to `μ`. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le` (private) | Same hypotheses as the public lemma but with conclusion `∫⁻ (Σ Y_i)² ≤ s.card · ∫⁻ ‖g ω‖²₂` (no `1/√s.card` factor) | Reduces the original hypotheses to the joined product law using `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` and `hW_iid_pi`, then calls `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` | `m_A ≤ mΩ`, `Indep m_A (comap (W on s)) μ`, `μ.map (W on s) = Measure.pi (fun _ => P)`, `Measurable[m_A.prod σ_X] (uncurry g)`, `∀ ω, MemLp (g ω) 2 P` → `∫⁻ ω, ENNReal.ofReal (((1/√s.card) * Σ_{i∈s} (g ω (W i ω) - ∫g ω dP))²) ∂μ ≤ ∫⁻ ω, ENNReal.ofReal (‖g ω‖²_{L²(P)}) ∂μ` | Public theorem. **Proved.** |

## 10e. `Mathlib/ConvergenceInDistribution.lean` — Scalar Slutsky / varying maps

Upstream candidates for convergence in distribution.  Contains no Causalean-specific
definitions; the project-level `Tendsto_dist` wrappers in `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`
consume these statements.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MeasureTheory.tendstoInMeasure_const_of_tendsto_real` | `aᵢ → a₀` → constant random variables `ω ↦ aᵢ` converge in measure to `ω ↦ a₀` | Deterministic topological convergence as convergence in measure for constant real-valued random variables | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.TendstoInDistribution.const_mul_of_tendsto_const` | `Xᵢ ⇒ Z` and `aᵢ → a₀` → `aᵢ·Xᵢ ⇒ a₀·Z` | Same-probability-space deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem; proved by Mathlib's product Slutsky theorem `continuous_comp_prodMk_of_tendstoInMeasure_const` with multiplication as the continuous map | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto` | `νᵢ → ν` weakly and `aᵢ → a₀` → `(x ↦ aᵢx)_#νᵢ → (x ↦ a₀x)_#ν` | Measure-level deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem for weak convergence on ℝ. Proved by rewriting `(x ↦ aᵢx)_#νᵢ` as the push-forward of `δ_{aᵢ} × νᵢ` by multiplication, then applying continuity of Dirac measures, product probability measures, and the ordinary continuous-mapping theorem | **Proved** |

## 10f. `Mathlib/FWL.lean` — Frisch–Waugh–Lovell on a finite-dim inner product space

Upstream candidate.  Self-contained algebraic content of the
Frisch–Waugh–Lovell theorem on a real inner-product space `V` with a
nuisance subspace `H` admitting an orthogonal projection.  Causal- and
panel-agnostic; depends only on Mathlib (`Submodule.orthogonalProjection`,
`Matrix.mulVec`, `Matrix.nonsing_inv`).  **Live consumer:**
`Panel/FWLInstanceL2.lean` (§9i0a-FWLInstanceL2)
instantiates this development on `Lp ℝ 2 μ` to give the conditional-mean
estimand papers a shared, measure-theory-native FWL engine.  (The finite
weighted-support tower `Weighted/FWL.lean` does **not** instantiate this file:
its inner product is only positive-semidefinite, so it carries its own FWL
built on `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection`; the two are connected at the data level
via `Weighted/OfProbabilityMeasure.lean`.)  NL artifact:
`doc/basic_concepts/mathlib/fwl.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize H v` | `Submodule ℝ V → V → V` | Orthogonal residual operator `M_H v = v - P_H v` as a self-map of `V`, defined via `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn` (which lands in `V`, not `↥H`) so the result is a `V`-vector. Requires `[H.HasOrthogonalProjection]`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.inner_residualize_of_mem` | `(v : V) {w : V} (hw : w ∈ H) → ⟪residualize H v, w⟫ = 0` | Residual orthogonality: `M_H v ⊥ H`. Direct unfolding of `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn_inner_eq_zero`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize_inner_swap_right` | `(v w : V) → ⟪residualize H v, w⟫ = ⟪residualize H v, residualize H w⟫` | Symmetric residualization in the right slot. Splits `w = M_H w + P_H w` and uses orthogonality of the residual to `H`. The key identity used by FWL: residualizing only one side suffices. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid H X` | `Submodule ℝ V → (Fin K → V) → Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ℝ` | Residualized Gram matrix `Q_{XX} j k := ⟪M_H (X j), M_H (X k)⟫`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec H X Y` | `… → V → (Fin K → ℝ)` | Residualized inner-product vector `r_j := ⟪M_H (X j), Y⟫`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef H X Y` | `… → V → (Fin K → ℝ)` | The FWL coefficient `θ̂ := Q_{XX}⁻¹ *ᵥ residInnerVec H X Y`. Defined unconditionally; only the normal-equations identity below needs invertibility of `Q_{XX}`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid_mulVec_fwlCoef` | `(Y : V) (hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) → (gramResid H X) *ᵥ fwlCoef H X Y = residInnerVec H X Y` | Residualized normal equations for the FWL coefficient. Pure matrix algebra: `A *ᵥ (A⁻¹ *ᵥ b) = b` via `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec`, `Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv`, `Matrix.one_mulVec`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec_eq_residualize_right` | `(Y : V) → residInnerVec H X Y = residInnerVec H X (residualize H Y)` | The FWL right-hand side is invariant under residualizing `Y`. Pointwise consequence of `residualize_inner_swap_right`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fittedValue X β` | `(Fin K → V) → (Fin K → ℝ) → V` | Finite fitted value `∑ j, β j • X j`; helper lemmas record linearity under coefficient subtraction and commutation with residualization. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective H X Y β` | `… → ℝ` | Residualized least-squares objective `⟪M_H Y − ∑ j β_j • M_H X_j, M_H Y − ∑ j β_j • M_H X_j⟫`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective X Y β h` | `… → V → ℝ` | Original least-squares objective with an explicit nuisance term `h`: `⟪Y − Xβ − h, Y − Xβ − h⟫`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective_eq_fwlCoef_add_error` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) → residualizedObjective H X Y β = residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) + ⟪X̃(fwlCoef−β), X̃(fwlCoef−β)⟫` | Residualized completing-square identity. The nonnegative error term yields residualized minimization and, with Gram invertibility, coefficient uniqueness. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_residualized_minimizes` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) → residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) ≤ residualizedObjective H X Y β` | Completing-square theorem: the matrix-defined FWL coefficient minimizes the residualized least-squares objective. Uses the residualized normal equations to kill the cross term. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective_eq_residualizedObjective_add_projectionError` | `(hh : h ∈ H) → originalObjective X Y β h = residualizedObjective H X Y β + ⟪P_H (Y − Xβ) − h, P_H (Y − Xβ) − h⟫` | Pythagorean decomposition of the original objective into residualized loss plus squared nuisance-projection error. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_original_minimizes` | `(hQ) (hh : h ∈ H) → originalObjective X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) (P_H (Y − X fwlCoef)) ≤ originalObjective X Y β h` | Standard finite-dimensional FWL optimizer theorem: the residualized coefficient paired with the projection of its raw residual minimizes the original least-squares problem over coefficients and nuisance terms in `H`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_eq_of_original_minimizer` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis → β = fwlCoef H X Y` | β-block uniqueness theorem: any minimizer of the original least-squares problem has coefficient vector equal to the residualized FWL coefficient. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.original_minimizer_eq_fwlCoef_projection` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis → β = fwlCoef H X Y ∧ h = P_H (Y − X fwlCoef)` | Full standard FWL uniqueness characterization: every original-problem minimizer is exactly the residualized coefficient paired with the nuisance projection of its raw residual. | **Proved** |

## 10h. `Mathlib/TwoStateMarkov.lean` — two-state Markov transition matrix, stationary projection, geometric ergodicity

Project-generic shim (depends only on Mathlib; no causal/panel/Causalean
content) for the two-state Markov chain on `Fin 2` with rows
`(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. Carries the two transition/stationary
matrix definitions and the substrate for long-T compact-uniform
convergence required by
`CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/LongT.lean`: spectral
decomposition of the transition matrix plus uniform geometric
convergence to the stationary projection on compact subsets of the open
transition-parameter square `(0,1)²`. **Both definitions and all 4
theorems are in place; all theorems proved.**

This file consolidates the two definitions (previously local to
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`) and the
four ergodicity theorems (previously in
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Mathlib/MarkovErgodicity.lean`, now deleted)
into a single neutral Causalean module. `Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`
re-exports the two definitions under the short names so existing
downstream code is unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix` | `(a b : ℝ) → Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ` | The 2×2 row-stochastic transition matrix with rows `(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.stationaryProjection` | `(a b : ℝ) → Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ` | The rank-one projector whose two rows both equal the stationary distribution `(b/(a+b), a/(a+b))`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_eq_spectral` | `(a b : ℝ) (ha_pos : 0 < a) (ha_lt_one : a < 1) (hb_pos : 0 < b) (hb_lt_one : b < 1) → ∀ k, M^k = Π + (1 - a - b)^k • (1 - Π)` | Spectral decomposition: powers of the transition matrix split into the stationary projection plus a geometrically-decaying correction along `1 - Π`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_abs_lt_one` | `(0 < a) (0 < b) (a < 1) (b < 1) → |1 - a - b| < 1` | Pointwise spectral gap on the open transition-parameter square. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_uniform_gap_on_compact` | `(K : Set (ℝ × ℝ)) (IsCompact K) (K ⊆ (0,1)²) → ∃ ρ < 1, ∀ p ∈ K, |1 - p.1 - p.2| ≤ ρ` | Uniform spectral gap on compact subsets via `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` + averaging the maximum with `1`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_tendsto_stationary_uniform` | `(K compact ⊆ (0,1)²) → ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N, ∀ k ≥ N, ∀ p ∈ K, ∀ i j, |((M p.1 p.2)^k - Π p.1 p.2) i j| ≤ ε` | Uniform geometric convergence of `M^k` to the stationary projection on compact subsets, combining the spectral decomposition (entries of `1 - Π` bounded by `1`) with the uniform gap (geometric decay rate `ρ < 1`). **Proved.** |

## 10g. `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection.lean` — semidefinite finite-dimensional projections

Upstream candidate for finite-dimensional projection under a symmetric
positive-semidefinite bilinear form.  This is the generic support-quotient
architecture consumed by `Panel/Subspace.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.exists_orthogonalProjection_of_posSemidef` | finite-dimensional `V`, bilinear form `B`, symmetry, `∀ x, 0 ≤ B x x`, submodule `H` → `∃ P : V →ₗ[ℝ] V, P X ∈ H ∧ B (X - P X) h = 0` | Generic semidefinite projection existence. Proof uses the restricted Gram map `H →ₗ Dual ℝ H`, shows each right-hand side lies in its range via the PSD radical fact and dual-annihilator/range duality, then chooses a linear right inverse of the range restriction. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10i. `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` — standard Gaussian on a finite-dim inner-product space

Constructs the standard Gaussian measure on an arbitrary finite-dimensional
real inner-product space `E` (mean zero, covariance = inner product) and proves
its basic moments. Construction: take `b := stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E`, build the
product of `n = finrank ℝ E` standard real Gaussians on `Fin n → ℝ`, transport
to `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)` via `toLp`, then pull back to `E` along the
isometry `b.repr.symm`. Upstream candidate.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian` | `[FiniteDimensional ℝ E] [BorelSpace E] → Measure E` | The standard Gaussian measure on `E`: `(prod of gaussianReal 0 1).map toLp` pulled back along `b.repr.symm`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isProbabilityMeasure_stdGaussian` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (stdGaussian E)` | Probability measure (pushforward of a `Measure.pi` of probability measures). | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isGaussian_stdGaussian` | `IsGaussian (stdGaussian E)` | Gaussian law; via `iIndepFun.hasGaussianLaw` for the product, then `isGaussian_map_equiv`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_mean` | `∫ x, x ∂(stdGaussian E) = 0` | Mean is zero; each coordinate `gaussianReal 0 1` has mean 0, pushed through the continuous-linear maps. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.covarianceBilin_stdGaussian` | `covarianceBilin (stdGaussian E) u v = ⟪u, v⟫_ℝ` | Covariance bilinear form equals the inner product. Via `covarianceBilin_map` (adjoint of isometry = inverse), `covarianceBilin_apply_pi`, coordinate covariances (Kronecker delta from independence + unit variance), and `OrthonormalBasis.repr` isometry. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi` | `(stdGaussian E).map ‖·‖² = (Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)).map (∑ (w i)²)` | Exposes the product-of-1-D-Gaussians structure of the squared-norm law (rotation-invariance of `‖·‖²` strips the orthonormal-basis transport), used for χ² atomlessness. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j. `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` — positive square root of a positive operator

Builds the **operator square root** of a positive symmetric operator on a
finite-dimensional real inner-product space — a result Mathlib lacks (it has the
spectral theorem and `LinearMap.IsPositive`, but no operator sqrt). Construction:
in the orthonormal eigenbasis `b` with real eigenvalues `μ` (both from
`LinearMap.IsSymmetric.eigenvectorBasis`/`eigenvalues`), `posSqrt` acts as
`√(μ i)` on each eigenvector (via `Basis.constr`). Upstream candidate. All in
namespace `LinearMap.IsPositive` (with `hT : T.IsPositive`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eigBasis` / `eigVals` | `OrthonormalBasis (Fin (finrank ℝ E)) ℝ E` / `Fin _ → ℝ` | Orthonormal eigenbasis and real eigenvalues of the positive operator `T`. | **Proved.** |
| `eigVals_nonneg` | `0 ≤ hT.eigVals i` | Eigenvalues of a positive operator are nonnegative (`μ i = ⟪T (b i), b i⟫ ≥ 0`). | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt` | `E →ₗ[ℝ] E` | The square root: `posSqrt (b i) = √(μ i) • b i`, extended by `Basis.constr`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_mul_self` | `hT.posSqrt ∘ₗ hT.posSqrt = T` | Square law, via `Real.mul_self_sqrt` and `Basis.ext`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_inner` | `⟪posSqrt x, y⟫ = ∑ i, √(μ i) ⟪b i,x⟫ ⟪b i,y⟫` | Symmetric weighted-sum form of the quadratic data. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_isSymmetric` / `posSqrt_isPositive` | `IsSymmetric` / `IsPositive` | `posSqrt` is itself symmetric and positive. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM` | `E →L[ℝ] E` | `posSqrt` packaged as a continuous linear map. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_isSelfAdjoint` / `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` | `IsSelfAdjoint posSqrtCLM` / `adjoint posSqrtCLM = posSqrtCLM` | Self-adjointness of the continuous square root. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` | `posSqrtCLM ∘L posSqrtCLM = T.toContinuousLinearMap` | Continuous square law. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j'. `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Cholesky.lean` — real Cholesky factorization (existence + uniqueness)

The **Cholesky factorization** of a real symmetric positive-definite matrix `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℝ`: there is a unique upper-triangular matrix `U` with strictly positive diagonal such that `M = Uᵀ U` (here "upper-triangular" means `j < i → U i j = 0`, all strictly-below-diagonal entries vanish). Existence is derived from Mathlib's LDL decomposition (`LDL.lower_conj_diag`) by absorbing the square roots of the diagonal factor: `U = √D · Lᵀ` where `L = LDL.lower` and `D = LDL.diag`. Uniqueness reduces to the fact that an orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity. Mathlib has LDL but no Cholesky; upstream candidate. Built for the causal-disentanglement formalization. Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra`. Sorry-free (axioms: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsUpperTri` / `isUpperTri_iff_blockTriangular` | `∀ i j, j < i → U i j = 0` / `↔ U.BlockTriangular id` | Strict upper-triangularity predicate and its identification with Mathlib's `BlockTriangular id`. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_diag_one` | `LDL.lowerInv hM i i = 1` | The Gram-Schmidt lower-inverse matrix is unitriangular (diagonal entries are `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_diagEntries_pos` | `0 < LDL.diagEntries hM i` | The LDL diagonal entries of a positive-definite matrix are strictly positive (each is `xᵀMx > 0` for the nonzero `x = lowerInv` row). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_blockTriangular` / `ldl_lower_blockTriangular` | `(…).BlockTriangular toDual` | `LDL.lowerInv` and `LDL.lower` are lower-triangular. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lower_diag_one` | `LDL.lower hM i i = 1` | `LDL.lower` is unitriangular (inverse of a unitriangular lower-triangular matrix). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_exists` | `∃ U, (∀ i j, j<i → U i j = 0) ∧ (∀ i, 0 < U i i) ∧ M = Uᵀ U` | **Existence.** Every real positive-definite matrix factors as `Uᵀ U` with `U` upper-triangular, positive-diagonal. | **Proved.** |
| `orthogonal_upperTri_pos_diag_eq_one` | `Wᵀ W = 1 → upper-tri → pos-diag → W = 1` | **Key uniqueness lemma.** An orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity (its transpose is both upper and lower triangular, hence diagonal, hence `±1`, hence `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_unique` | `M = Uᵀ U = Vᵀ V → (both upper-tri pos-diag) → U = V` | **Uniqueness.** The factorization is unique: `W = V U⁻¹` is orthogonal upper-triangular with positive diagonal, so `W = 1`. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10k. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/PartitionIntegral.lean` — finite-partition integral algebra

Decomposing an integral over the fibers of a finite-valued measurable map `H : Ω → ι` (a finite partition of the sample space). Causal-agnostic, Mathlib-shaped measure theory (a Mathlib-PR candidate); relocated here from `Stat/` so any layer (estimation, panel cell algebra, partial-ID) can use it without pulling in `Stat/` or `PO/`. Complements `Causalean.PO.integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` (the `eventCondExp`/measure-weighted form for an explicit set family): here the form is measurable-map / set-integral, plus the cell-constant pull-out the `PO` lemma does not provide. Index type carries `[Fintype ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] [MeasurableSingletonClass ι]`; no measurability of the cell-weight `c : ι → ℝ` is needed (it is constant on each fiber). Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.MeasureTheory`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `integral_eq_sum_setIntegral_fiber` | `∫ f = ∑ h, ∫_{H⁻¹{h}} f` | **Fiber decomposition.** For integrable `f` and finite-valued measurable `H`, the integral splits as the sum of set-integrals over the fibers. Wraps `integral_iUnion_fintype` over the fiber cover of `univ`. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst_mul` | `∫ c(H ω)·f ω = ∑ h, c h · ∫_{H⁻¹{h}} f` | **Cell-constant pull-out.** An integrand weighted by a cell-dependent value `c (H ω)` decomposes with `c h` pulled out per cell. Workhorse for panel cell-by-cell regression algebra and cell-weighted-average estimands. No measurability of `c` required. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst` | `∫ c(H ω) = ∑ h, c h · μ(H⁻¹{h})` | **Cell-weight aggregation.** For a finite measure, the integral of a cell-constant function is the cell-weighted sum of cell masses. Special case of `integral_cellConst_mul` with `f ≡ 1`. **Proved.** |

## 10k'. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/CondExpIndicatorTower.lean` — conditioning ↔ inner-regression weighting

Causal-agnostic conditional-expectation identity (a Mathlib-PR candidate; namespace `MeasureTheory`): the measure-theoretic kernel behind "regression adjustment = inverse-propensity weighting". Reused by `regression_adjustment` (§9g) and earmarked for the ATT/DTR sequential analogs.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `condExp_setIndicator_condExp_of_le` | `(hm : m ≤ m') (hm' : m' ≤ m0) [SigmaFinite (μ.trim hm')] (hs : MeasurableSet[m'] s) (hf : Integrable f μ) → μ[s.indicator f \| m] =ᵐ μ[s.indicator (μ[f\|m']) \| m]` | **Masked outcome ↔ inner regression.** Conditioning the masked `1_s·f` on coarse `m` equals conditioning its inner regression `μ[f\|m']` (with `s` `m'`-measurable). Proof: inner `condExp_indicator` + tower `condExp_condExp_of_le`. With `m=σ(X)`, `m'=σ(D,X)`, `s={D=d}`, dividing by `μ[1_s\|m]` turns the LHS into the IPW/adjustment functional, the RHS into the regression. **Proved.** |

## 12. `Stat/` — Causal-agnostic statistical primitives

Foundation layer for the estimation and inference modules: i.i.d. samples, convergence
modes, asymptotic linearity, Neyman orthogonality, and one-shot sample
splits. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation.tex` Sections 1–5. Files
here are deliberately project-agnostic and are candidates for upstream
contribution to Mathlib.

**Folder layout.** The top level keeps only the foundational `Sample.lean` and
the `SampleSplit.lean` umbrella; everything else is grouped by topic into
subfolders: `Limit/`
(convergence modes, continuous mapping, WLLN), `CLT/` (asymptotic linearity,
multivariate CLT, Gaussian limit, χ²), `Inference/` (Δ-method, variance
estimation, studentization, Wald), `Quantile/` (cdf/quantile inference stack),
`MEstimation/` (Z/M-estimator CLT), `Orthogonality/` (Neyman orthogonality,
conditional-op helpers), `SampleSplit/` (cross-fitting + fold-restricted CLT),
`Minimax/` (Le Cam / Fano / Assouad lower bounds, §12w),
and `Concentration/` (vendored toolbox, §12'). Module paths below reflect this.

### 12np. `Stat/Nonparametric/Approximation/HolderInterpolation.lean` — Multivariate Hölder interpolation

This reusable minimax lower-bound primitive turns a pointwise separation between
Hölder-smooth functions into a local L¹ separation. It supplies the generic
moment-cancelling product kernel and the resulting multidimensional interpolation
inequality used by two-point and Assouad arguments.

### 12a0. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/` — Product-loss monotone couplings

Coupling-based statistical primitives for extremal dependence with fixed real marginals.
This stack builds explicit comonotone and countermonotone quantile couplings, proves
the sharp Fréchet-Hoeffding cdf envelopes, derives Hoeffding's covariance identity,
and concludes that the product expectation is maximized and minimized by those two
monotone couplings under finite second moments.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling -->
_(no documented declarations in Causalean.Stat.Coupling)_
<!-- /GEN -->

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling -->
_(no documented declarations in Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling)_
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.1. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/PIT.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.PIT -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.unifOI` | `MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ` | The uniform probability measure on the open unit interval `(0,1)`, realised as Lebesgue measure restricted to `Ioo 0 1`. |
| `Stat.instIsProbabilityMeasure_unifOI` | `MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure Causalean.Stat.unifOI` | `unifOI` is a probability measure: `volume (Ioo 0 1) = 1`. |
| `Stat.monotoneOn_quantile` | `MonotoneOn (Causalean.Stat.quantile μ) (Set.Ioo 0 1)` | The quantile function is monotone on the open unit interval `(0,1)` (immediate from `quantile_mono`, whose hypotheses `0 < τ`, `τ' < 1` hold throughout the interior). |
| `Stat.aemeasurable_quantile_unifOI` | `AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.quantile μ) Causalean.Stat.unifOI` | The quantile function is a.e.-measurable with respect to the uniform measure on `(0,1)`; it is monotone there, and a monotone function is measurable. |
| `Stat.quantile_map_uniform` | `MeasureTheory.Measure.map (Causalean.Stat.quantile μ) Causalean.Stat.unifOI = μ` | Probability integral transform. For a Borel probability measure `μ` on `ℝ`, the pushforward of `Unif(0,1)` under the quantile function `quantile μ` equals `μ`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.2. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/Coupling.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.Coupling -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IsCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → Prop` | `IsCoupling π μ ν` states that `π` is a coupling of `μ` and `ν`: it is a probability measure on `ℝ × ℝ` whose first marginal is `μ` and whose second marginal is `ν`. This is membership in the Fréchet class `Π(μ, ν)`. |
| `Stat.comonotoneCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ)` | The comonotone (quantile) coupling of `μ` and `ν`: the pushforward of the uniform measure on `(0,1)` under `u ↦ (quantile μ u, quantile ν u)`. Both coordinates are driven by the *same* uniform draw, giving the maximal positive dependence within `Π(μ, ν)`. |
| `Stat.countermonotoneCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ → MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ)` | The countermonotone coupling of `μ` and `ν`: the pushforward of the uniform measure on `(0,1)` under `u ↦ (quantile μ u, quantile ν (1 - u))`. The reflection `u ↦ 1 - u` on the second coordinate produces the maximal negative dependence within `Π(μ, ν)`. |
| `Stat.map_one_sub_unifOI` | `MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun u => 1 - u) Causalean.Stat.unifOI = Causalean.Stat.unifOI` | The reflection `u ↦ 1 - u` preserves the uniform measure on `(0,1)`: `unifOI.map (fun u => 1 - u) = unifOI`. |
| `Stat.isCoupling_comonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling μ ν) μ ν` | The comonotone coupling is a coupling of `(μ, ν)`. Its marginals are computed by `Measure.map_map` composing with `Prod.fst`/`Prod.snd`, which reduces each to `unifOI.map (quantile ·)`, equal to the corresponding measure by the probability integral transform `quantile_map_uniform`. |
| `Stat.isCoupling_countermonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling μ ν) μ ν` | The countermonotone coupling is a coupling of `(μ, ν)`. The first marginal is `μ` by the PIT; the second marginal is `unifOI.map (quantile ν ∘ (1 - ·))`, which equals `ν` because `u ↦ 1 - u` preserves `unifOI` (`map_one_sub_unifOI`) followed by the PIT for `ν`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.3. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/FrechetHoeffding.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.FrechetHoeffding -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.jointCdf` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ` | The joint cdf of a measure `π` on `ℝ × ℝ`: the mass of the lower-left quadrant `Iic x ×ˢ Iic y`, as a real number. |
| `Stat.frechet_hoeffding_upper` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (x y : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π x y ≤ min (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) x) (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) y)` | Fréchet–Hoeffding upper bound. For any coupling `π` of `(μ, ν)`, the joint cdf is bounded above by the smaller marginal cdf: `H_π x y ≤ min (cdf μ x) (cdf ν y)`. Proof: `Iic x ×ˢ Iic y ⊆ Iic x ×ˢ univ` and `⊆ univ ×ˢ Iic y`, whose masses are `μ (Iic x)` and `ν (Iic y)` via the marginals. |
| `Stat.frechet_hoeffding_lower` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (x y : ℝ), max (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) x + ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) y - 1) 0 ≤ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π x y` | Fréchet–Hoeffding lower bound. For any coupling `π` of `(μ, ν)`, `max (cdf μ x + cdf ν y - 1) 0 ≤ H_π x y`. Proof: inclusion–exclusion on the complement of `Iic x ×ˢ Iic y`, using `π` a probability measure and the two marginals. |
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.4. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/FrechetHoeffdingAttainment.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.FrechetHoeffdingAttainment -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.jointCdf_comonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling μ ν) x y = min (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) x) (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) y)` | The comonotone coupling attains the Fréchet–Hoeffding upper bound: `H = min (cdf μ x) (cdf ν y)`. For `U ~ Unif(0,1)`, `{u \| quantile μ u ≤ x ∧ quantile ν u ≤ y} = {u \| u ≤ cdf μ x} ∩ {u \| u ≤ cdf ν y} = {u \| u ≤ min (cdf μ x) (cdf ν y)}`, of uniform mass `min (cdf μ x) (cdf ν y)`. |
| `Stat.jointCdf_countermonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling μ ν) x y = max (↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) x + ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) y - 1) 0` | The countermonotone coupling attains the Fréchet–Hoeffding lower bound: `H = max (cdf μ x + cdf ν y - 1) 0`. For `U ~ Unif(0,1)`, `{u \| quantile μ u ≤ x ∧ quantile ν (1-u) ≤ y} = {u \| u ≤ cdf μ x} ∩ {u \| 1 - cdf ν y ≤ u}`, of uniform mass `max (cdf μ x - (1 - cdf ν y)) 0 = max (cdf μ x + cdf ν y - 1) 0`. |
| `Stat.jointCdf_le_comonotone` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure ν] (x y : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π x y ≤ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling μ ν) x y` | Corollary: for any coupling `π` of `(μ, ν)`, its joint cdf is dominated pointwise by the comonotone joint cdf. This is the pointwise inequality that the covariance identity turns into optimality of `E[XY]`. |
| `Stat.countermonotone_le_jointCdf` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure ν] (x y : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling μ ν) x y ≤ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π x y` | Corollary: the countermonotone joint cdf is dominated pointwise by any other coupling's joint cdf. |
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.5. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/TailIntegral.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.TailIntegral -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.tailInd` | `ℝ → ℝ → ℝ` | `tailInd a s` is the indicator `𝟙{s < a}`, i.e. `1` when `s < a` and `0` otherwise, written as the indicator function of the ray `Iio a`. |
| `Stat.signedTail` | `ℝ → ℝ → ℝ` | `signedTail a s = 𝟙{s < a} - 𝟙{s < 0}`, the *signed tail indicator* of `a`. As a function of `s` it is `+1` on `[0, a)` when `a ≥ 0`, `-1` on `[a, 0)` when `a < 0`, and `0` elsewhere; its Lebesgue integral is exactly `a`. |
| `Stat.tailInd_apply` | `Causalean.Stat.tailInd a s = if s < a then 1 else 0` | The tail indicator equals one below the threshold and zero at or above it. |
| `Stat.signedTail_eq_indicator_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.signedTail a = ((Set.Ico 0 a).indicator fun x => 1) - (Set.Ico a 0).indicator fun x => 1` | `signedTail a` is, as a function of `s`, the difference of the indicators of the two intervals `Ico 0 a` and `Ico a 0` (at most one of which is nonempty). This is the normal form used to compute its integral and to see it is integrable. |
| `Stat.measurable_signedTail` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.signedTail a)` | `signedTail a` is measurable in the tail variable `s`. |
| `Stat.measurable_signedTail_uncurry` | `Measurable fun z => Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.1 z.2` | `signedTail` is jointly measurable in `(a, s)`, since `{z \| z.2 < z.1}` is an open (hence measurable) subset of `ℝ × ℝ`. |
| `Stat.abs_signedTail_le_one` | `\|Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s\| ≤ 1` | The signed tail indicator is bounded by `1` in absolute value. |
| `Stat.integrable_signedTail` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.signedTail a) MeasureTheory.volume` | `signedTail a` is Lebesgue integrable: it is a bounded function supported on a bounded interval. |
| `Stat.integral_signedTail` | `∫ (s : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s = a` | Signed tail representation. `∫ s, (𝟙{s < a} - 𝟙{s < 0}) ds = a`. |
| `Stat.integral_abs_signedTail` | `∫ (s : ℝ), \|Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s\| = \|a\|` | The `L¹` norm of the signed tail indicator is `\|a\|`: the integrand is `±1` on an interval of length `\|a\|`. |
| `Stat.integrable_signedTail_prod` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun q => Causalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume)` | The tensor product `(s, t) ↦ signedTail x s * signedTail y t` is integrable on `ℝ × ℝ` for Lebesgue×Lebesgue. |
| `Stat.integral_signedTail_prod` | `∫ (q : ℝ × ℝ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2 ∂MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume = x * y` | Product tail representation. `x * y` is the two-dimensional integral of the product of the two signed tail indicators: `∫∫ (𝟙{s<x} - 𝟙{s<0})(𝟙{t<y} - 𝟙{t<0}) ds dt = x * y`. |
| `Stat.integral_norm_signedTail_prod` | `∫ (q : ℝ × ℝ), ‖Causalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2‖ ∂MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume = \|x\| * \|y\|` | The `L¹` norm of the product tail representation is `\|x\| * \|y\|`. This is the domination bound that makes the Fubini swap against an `L²` coupling valid: its `π`-integral is `E\|XY\| < ∞` by Cauchy–Schwarz. |
<!-- /GEN -->

#### 12a0.6. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/Survival.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.Survival -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.survFst` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ` | The first marginal survival function of `π`: `SX s = π{p \| s < p.1}`. |
| `Stat.survSnd` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ` | The second marginal survival function of `π`: `SY t = π{p \| t < p.2}`. |
| `Stat.jointSurv` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (ℝ × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ → ℝ` | The joint survival function of `π`: `S s t = π (Ioi s ×ˢ Ioi t)`, the mass of the open upper-right quadrant. |
| `Stat.survFst_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (s : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.survFst π s = 1 - ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) s` | The first marginal survival function of a coupling is `1 - F`, where `F = cdf μ`. Proof: `Prod.fst ⁻¹' Ioi s` is the complement of `Prod.fst ⁻¹' Iic s`, whose `π`-mass is `μ (Iic s) = F s` by `h.map_fst`. |
| `Stat.survSnd_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (t : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.survSnd π t = 1 - ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) t` | The second marginal survival function of a coupling is `1 - G`, where `G = cdf ν`. |
| `Stat.jointSurv_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (s t : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.jointSurv π s t = 1 - ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) s - ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) t + Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π s t` | Inclusion–exclusion. The joint survival function of a coupling is `S s t = 1 - F s - G t + H_π s t`. Proof: `Ioi s ×ˢ Ioi t` is the complement of `(Prod.fst ⁻¹' Iic s) ∪ (Prod.snd ⁻¹' Iic t)`, and the mass of that union is `F s + G t - H_π s t` by `measureReal_union_add_inter`, the intersection being the lower-left quadrant `Iic s ×ˢ Iic t`. |
| `Stat.surv_gap_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → ∀ (s t : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.jointSurv π s t - Causalean.Stat.survFst π s * Causalean.Stat.survSnd π t = Causalean.Stat.jointCdf π s t - ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf μ) s * ↑(ProbabilityTheory.cdf ν) t` | Survival gap = cdf gap. For any coupling `π` of `(μ, ν)`, |

#### 12a0.8. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/HoeffdingFubiniIntegrability.lean`

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.HoeffdingFubiniIntegrability -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.integrable_tail_fst_prod` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.2.1 z.1) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod π)` | The single-coordinate tail kernel is integrable on `volume ⊗ π`. Domination: `∫ s, \|signedTail p.1 s\| ds = \|p.1\|`, which is `π`-integrable since `X ∈ L²(π)` and `π` is finite. |
| `Stat.integrable_tail_snd_prod` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.2.2 z.1) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod π)` | The single-coordinate tail kernel is integrable on `volume ⊗ π` (second coordinate). |
| `Stat.integrable_bigPhi` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.2.1 z.1.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.2.2 z.1.2) ((MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume).prod π)` | The key integrability. The product tail kernel `Φ q p = signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2` is integrable on `(volume ⊗ volume) ⊗ π`. Domination: for fixed `p`, `∫ q, ‖Φ q p‖ dq = \|p.1\| * \|p.2\|` (`integral_norm_signedTail_prod`), and `p ↦ \|p.1\| * \|p.2\|` is `π`-integrable by Cauchy–Schwarz on the `L²` marginals. Formally: apply `MeasureTheory.integrable_prod_iff'` with the joint measurability supplied by `measurable_signedTail_uncurry`. |
| `Stat.integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → ∫ (p : ℝ × ℝ), p.1 * p.2 ∂π = ∫ (q : ℝ × ℝ), ∫ (p : ℝ × ℝ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.1 q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.2 q.2 ∂π ∂MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume` | First swap. `E_π[XY]` is the Lebesgue double integral of the fibre integrals: `∫ p, p.1 * p.2 ∂π = ∫ q, (∫ p, Φ q p ∂π) dq`. Immediate from `integral_signedTail_prod` (pointwise in `p`) followed by `MeasureTheory.integral_integral_swap` applied to `integrable_bigPhi`. |
| `Stat.mean_fst_tail` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → ∫ (x : ℝ), x ∂μ = ∫ (s : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.survFst π s - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 s` | Tail formula for the mean. `E[X] = ∫ s, (SX s - 𝟙{s<0}) ds`, by the same swap on `volume ⊗ π` using `integral_signedTail` pointwise. |
| `Stat.mean_snd_tail` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → ∫ (y : ℝ), y ∂ν = ∫ (t : ℝ), Causalean.Stat.survSnd π t - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 t` | Tail formula for the mean (second coordinate). |
| `Stat.integrable_survFst_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun s => Causalean.Stat.survFst π s - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 s) MeasureTheory.volume` | The centred marginal survival function `s ↦ SX s - 𝟙{s<0}` is Lebesgue integrable (it is `E[signedTail X ·]`, integrable by `Integrable.integral_prod_left` on `integrable_tail_fst_prod`). |
| `Stat.integrable_survSnd_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun t => Causalean.Stat.survSnd π t - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 t) MeasureTheory.volume` | The centred marginal survival function `t ↦ SY t - 𝟙{t<0}` is Lebesgue integrable. |
| `Stat.integrable_fiber` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling π μ ν → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 μ → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 ν → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun q => ∫ (p : ℝ × ℝ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.1 q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.2 q.2 ∂π) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume)` | The fibre `q ↦ ∫ p, Φ q p ∂π` is integrable on `ℝ × ℝ`, by `Integrable.integral_prod_left` applied to `integrable_bigPhi`. |

#### 12a0.10. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/Optimality.lean`

The product expectation under the comonotone coupling is the integral of the product of quantile functions over `(0,1)`: |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12a. `Stat/Sample.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample Ω X μ P` | structure | I.i.d. sequence `Z : ℕ → Ω → X` on a single ambient space, with mutual independence (`iIndepFun`), identical distribution (`IdentDistrib`), and law-match (`μ.map (Z 0) = P`). Matches `def:est-iid-sample`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean` | `def` | `(1/n) Σ_{i<n} f (Z i ω)` for a real-valued statistic `f`. |

### 12b. `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb`, `Tendsto_L2`, `Tendsto_dist` | `def`s | Aliases for convergence in probability / `L²` / distribution (Lévy–Prokhorov-style at the pushforward-measure level). |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` | `Xn ⇒ Q` and `aₙ → a₀` ⇒ `aₙ·Xn ⇒ (x ↦ a₀x)_#Q` | Project wrapper around `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`; handles the pushforward-map bookkeeping for `Tendsto_dist`. **Proved** (depends on Mathlib stub). |
| `IsBigOp Xn rn μ`, `IsLittleOp Xn rn μ` | `def`s | `O_p(rn)` and `o_p(rn)` per `def:est-stoch-order`. |
| `IsLittleOp.mul_isBigOp` | `o_p(rn)·O_p(sn) = o_p(rn·sn)` | Requires `0 < rn n`, `0 < sn n` for all `n`. **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.add` | `O_p(rn) + O_p(rn) = O_p(rn)` | **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` | Slutsky-style: `O_p(1)·o_p(1) = o_p(1)` | **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.tightness` | `Xn ⇒ Q` ⇒ `Xn = O_p(1)` | Tightness of a single distributional limit on ℝ: pick a tail set `{|x| ≥ N}` with small `Q`-mass via continuity from above on the antitone family `{|x| ≥ n}`, then portmanteau on the closed tail to bound `limsup μ.map(Xn n) {|x| > N} ≤ Q {|x| ≥ N}`. **Proved by Codex.** Δ-method helper. |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` | `Xn = O_p(1)` ∧ `aₙ → 0` ⇒ `aₙ·Xn = o_p(1)` | Degenerate Slutsky: bounds the bad event `{ε < |aₙ Xn|}` by `{M < |Xn|}` once `|aₙ| < ε/M`, using only the deterministic shrinkage `|aₙ| → 0`. **Proved by Codex.** Δ-method helper. |

### 12b'. `Stat/Limit/ConvergenceVec.lean`

Vector analogue of `Convergence.lean`'s `Tendsto_dist` plus the Slutsky lemmas needed by the multivariate Δ-method (`Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`) and the vector asymptotic-normality corollary (`Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist_vec` | `def` | Vector convergence in distribution: `μ.map (Xn n)` converges weakly to `Q` in `ProbabilityMeasure E` for `E` a pseudo-metric space with `OpensMeasurableSpace`. Direct port of scalar `Tendsto_dist`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one` | vector Slutsky absorption | `Xn ⇒ Q` and `‖Yn − Xn‖ = o_p(1)` ⇒ `Yn ⇒ Q`, on `[NormedAddCommGroup E] [BorelSpace E]`. **Proved by Codex** as a metric-space port of the scalar proof using the bounded-Lipschitz portmanteau characterization (`tendsto_iff_forall_lipschitz_integral_tendsto`); integrand `F : E → ℝ` is split over `{‖F (Yn) − F (Xn)‖ < L·ε/2}` and its complement, with the complement controlled via Lipschitz pullback through `‖Yn − Xn‖`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | Vector analogue of `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` | continuous-map pushforward | `g : E → F` continuous ⇒ `Tendsto_dist_vec Xn Q μ` ⇒ `Tendsto_dist_vec (g ∘ Xn) (Q.map g) μ`. **Proved by Codex** via `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_of_tendsto_of_continuous`. Used by the multivariate Δ-method to push forward by the Fréchet derivative `Dg`. |

### 12c0. `Stat/CLT/GaussianTail.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussian_tail_small` | `0<v → 0<ε → ∃ R>0, N(0,v)((-∞,-R]) ≤ ε ∧ N(0,v)([R,∞)) ≤ ε` | Gaussian half-line tail control: both one-sided tails of `N(0,v)` go below any `ε` for a large cutoff (continuity-from-above on half-line families shrinking to `∅`). Factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof; reused by `PartialID/Inference/ImbensManski.lean` | **Proved** |

### 12c. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearity.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure m σ²` | `def` | Real-typed wrapper around `ProbabilityTheory.gaussianReal m σ².toNNReal`; negative variances clip to `0` (Dirac at `m`). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasureGaussianMeasure` | instance | `IsProbabilityMeasure (gaussianMeasure m v)` for every real `m, v`, inherited from Mathlib's `gaussianReal`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinear θn θ₀ ψ S I` | structure | Asymptotic linearity per `def:est-asym-linear`: `mean_zero`, `finite_var` (encoded as `Integrable (ψ²)`), `remainder = o_p(1)` over an explicit index family `I` (classically `I n = {0, ..., n-1}`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S ψ I n` | `def` | The normalised partial sum `(1/√|I n|) Σ_{i∈I(n)} ψ(Z_i)` along an i.i.d. sample and an index family `I`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator θn θ₀ I n` | `def` | The rescaled estimator `√|I n| (θn n − θ₀)` for index family `I`. |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` | `(1/√n) Σ ψ(Zᵢ) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ² dP)` | CLT contact point: needs `ψ` measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable. **Proved** via `ProbabilityTheory.central_limit` from the upstream `RemyDegenne/CLT` package, with variance normalisation and a zero-variance degenerate branch. |
| `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` | Slutsky absorption | If `Xn ⇒ Q` in distribution and `Yn − Xn = o_p(1)`, then `Yn ⇒ Q`. **Proved** at the `Tendsto_dist` (measure-level) wrapper via the bounded-Lipschitz characterisation of weak convergence. |
| `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | If `Xn ⇒ Q` and `Xn n =ᵐ Yn n` eventually, then `Yn ⇒ Q`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` | deterministic scalar Slutsky for Gaussian targets | If `Xn ⇒ N(0, v)` and deterministic `a n → a₀`, then `a n · Xn ⇒ N(0, a₀²v)`. **Proved** (depends on `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`) by `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` plus `gaussianReal_map_const_mul`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` | `√|I n|(θn − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ² dP)` | Headline corollary `prop:est-al-implies-an`: combines `clt_normalized_sum` with `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb the asymptotically-linear remainder (classical corollary uses `I n = Finset.range n`). **Proved.** |

### 12c'. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`

Vector-θ companion to `AsymptoticLinearity.lean`. Adds the vector-valued asymptotic-linearity predicate `IsAsymLinearVec` parametric in a Euclidean space `E`, plus a bridge to the scalar form when `E = ℝ`, and the headline multivariate asymptotic-normality corollary.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsAsymLinearVec θn θ₀ ψ S I` | structure | Vector-valued asymptotic linearity at `θ₀ : E` with vector IF `ψ : X → E`. Fields: `mean_zero` (Bochner integral), `finite_var` (`‖ψ‖² ∈ L¹`), `remainder` (norm `o_p(1)`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.normalizedSum`, `rescaledEstimator` | `def`s | Vector versions of the scalar accessors. |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.toScalar` | bridge | When `E = ℝ`, `IsAsymLinearVec` reduces to scalar `IsAsymLinear`. **Proved**: field-by-field rewrite using `‖x‖ = |x|` and `r • x = r * x` on `ℝ`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.toVec` | bridge | Scalar-to-vector lift. **Proved** (mirror of `toScalar`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` | `theorem` | Multivariate analogue of `tendsto_normal`: given a vector CLT contact `_hCLT` (the partial-sum pushforward converges to `Q : ProbabilityMeasure E`), the rescaled estimator pushforward also converges to `Q`. **Proved by Codex** by absorbing `_h.remainder` into `_hCLT` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. The multivariate CLT itself is left to the caller. |

### 12d. `Stat/Orthogonality/Orthogonality.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal m η₀ θ₀ P` | `def` | Moment condition `∫ m η₀ x θ₀ dP = 0` plus vanishing Gâteaux derivative along every direction `η ∈ H`. Restricted realization sets are imposed at instantiation (cf. `H_ε` in `Estimation/ATE/InfluenceFunction.lean`). |

### 12e. `Stat/SampleSplit.lean` (umbrella)

Re-export umbrella over per-strategy split modules:
- `Stat/SampleSplit/OneShot.lean` — `OneShotSplit` (the original API, unchanged).
- `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean` — `KFoldSplit` (K-fold cross-fitting).

Existing consumers that import `Causalean.Stat.SampleSplit` continue to work.

#### 12e.OneShot

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit S` | structure | Fold-size schedule `n₁ : ℕ → ℕ` with `bound`, `grow`, `cogrow` per `def:est-one-shot-split`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA n` | `def` | `{0, …, n₁ n − 1}`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB n` | `def` | `{n₁ n, …, n − 1}` (filtered range). |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA_disjoint_foldB` | `Disjoint (foldA n) (foldB n)` | Pure combinatorics. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.folds_indep` | `IndepFun (Z\|foldA) (Z\|foldB) μ` | Direct corollary of `iIndepFun.indepFun_finset` plus disjointness. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card` | `(foldB n).card = n - n₁ n` | Cardinality of the suffix-shaped fold-B (filter on `range n`). **Proved.** Used to derive eventual positivity of `(foldB n).card` from `cogrow`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card_tendsto` | `Tendsto (fun n => (foldB n).card) atTop atTop` | Direct corollary of `cogrow` + `foldB_card`. **Proved.** |

#### 12e.2. `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean`

K-fold sample split (cross-fitting). Sibling to `OneShot.lean`. Used by `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit S K` | structure | K disjoint folds with `partition`, `cover`, `grow`, `ratio` (`fold(n,k).card/n → 1/K`). |
| `KFoldSplit.trainComplement n k` | `def` | `{0,…,n-1} \ fold(n,k)`. |
| `KFoldSplit.fold_disjoint_trainComplement` | `lemma` | Disjointness via `Finset.mem_sdiff`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.folds_indep` | `theorem` | `(Z i)_{i ∈ fold(n,k)}` independent of `(Z i)_{i ∈ trainComplement(n,k)}` under `μ`. **Proved** (one-line `S.indep.indepFun_finset` mirroring `OneShotSplit.folds_indep`). |

### 12f. `Stat/Orthogonality/ConditionalOp.lean`

Envelope-based stochastic-order helpers + Cauchy–Schwarz packaging. Consumed by `PlugIn.lean` (single-factor bias) and `Remainder.lean` (product-rate AIPW bias). Causal-agnostic; candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution once stable.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)² ≤ cn n a.s.` ∧ `cn = O_p(rn²)` ⇒ `Xn = O_p(rn)` | Square-envelope ⇒ `O_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and limsup monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `IsLittleOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)² ≤ cn n a.s.` ∧ `cn = o_p(rn²)` ⇒ `Xn = o_p(rn)` | Square-envelope ⇒ `o_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and convergence-to-zero monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `abs_integral_le_eLpNorm_two` | `IsProbabilityMeasure ν` ⇒ `\|∫ f dν\| ≤ ‖f‖_{L²(ν)}` | Constant-case Cauchy–Schwarz via the L¹/L² comparison on probability spaces. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_le_eLpNorm_mul_eLpNorm` | `∫ \|f·g\| dν ≤ ‖f‖₂ · ‖g‖₂` | Cauchy–Schwarz for L²·L² via Mathlib Hölder. **Proved.** |
| `integral_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 ν = o_p(rn)` ⇒ `∫ fn dν = o_p(rn)` | Lifts L²-rate to integrated bias by pointwise domination. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 ν = o_p(sn)` ∧ fixed `g ∈ L²` ⇒ `∫ \|fn·g\| dν = o_p(sn · ‖g‖₂)` | Lifts L²-rate to integrated absolute product, splitting the zero/nonzero fixed-norm cases. **Proved.** |

### 12g. `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`

Fold-restricted CLT for one-shot sample splits. Provides the CLT contact and asymptotic-normality corollary at rate `√|B(n)|` for split estimators (PlugIn / DML), plus a √n-rate conversion under a fixed split ratio `|B(n)|/n → c ∈ (0,1)` with the standard variance inflation `σ²/c`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedFoldB` | `(1/√\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i∈B(n)} ψ(Z_i) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ²dP)` | Fold-B CLT. **Proved** by reindexing fold-B through a finite-set characteristic-function bridge and composing the existing `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` CLT wrapper along `\|B(n)\| → ∞`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` | AL on fold B ⇒ `√\|B(n)\|·(θn − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, σ²)` | Mirror of `tendsto_normal` for `I = split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (4-line composition of `clt_normalizedFoldB` + `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB_sqrt_n` | `\|B(n)\|/n → c ∈ (0,1)` ∧ AL on fold B ⇒ `√n·(θn − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, σ²/c)` | √n-rate conversion under fixed split ratio. **Proved** (depends on the Mathlib varying-map stub); handles fold-B measurability, deterministic `√n/√\|B(n)\| → 1/√c`, eventual a.e. equality, and variance algebra. |

### 12h. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`

Empirical-process bounds on a centered evaluation-fold sum of a training-fold-measurable random function. Consumed by `DMLChernozhukov.lean` (one-shot) and `DMLCrossFit.lean` (K-fold). The file is structured in three layers:

1. **Abstract layer (private)** — parameterised over an arbitrary evaluation-fold schedule `eval : ℕ → Finset ℕ`, training σ-algebra `m_train : ℕ → MeasurableSpace Ω`, plus independence and iid-product-law hypotheses.
2. **OneShot wrappers** — preserved public signatures `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one`, now thin instantiations of the abstract layer.
3. **K-fold corollaries** — new public lemmas under `KFoldSplit.*` namespace.

#### Abstract layer (private)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le_core` / `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le` | `∫⁻ ω, ((1/√\|eval n\|) Σ_{eval n} (g(Z_i) − ∫g dP))² ∂μ ≤ ∫⁻ ω, ‖g(n,ω)‖²_{L²(P)} ∂μ` | Second-moment bound on the centered eval-sum, parametric in `(eval, m_train, h_train_le, h_indep, h_iid)`. **Proved** via the generic `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` helper in `Causalean/Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean`. |
| `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` | bounded `g` with `∫⁻ ‖g‖²₂ → 0` ⇒ centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Markov / Chebyshev in `ENNReal` plus bounded convergence. **Proved by Codex (A0)** via `MeasureTheory.meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`. Eventual `0 < (eval n).card` from a separate `h_eval_card_grow` hypothesis. |
| `evalSum_isLittleOp_one` | `‖g(n,ω)‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(1)` (random-norm rate) ⇒ centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Truncation at L²-norm ≤ 1, then `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` + `IsLittleOp.of_eq_on_asymptotic`. **Proved.** |
| `evalSum_bias_isLittleOp_one` | `(eval n).card / n → c ∈ (0,1)` ∧ `‖f‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ⇒ `√\|eval n\|·∫f dP = o_p(1)` | Bias term, fully parametric. **Proved.** |

#### OneShot wrappers (public; signatures preserved for backward compat)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | fold-A-measurable `f` with `‖f‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(1)` ⇒ `(1/√\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i ∈ B(n)} (f(n,ω,Z_i ω) − ∫f dP) = o_p(1)` | Thin wrapper around the abstract layer, instantiating `eval n := split.foldB n`, `m_train n := comap (foldA n) Z`, `h_indep := split.folds_indep n` (definitional `IndepFun`-to-`Indep` bridge), `h_iid := S.indep.precomp + IdentDistrib.map_eq + S.law`, `h_eval_card_grow := split.foldB_card_tendsto`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `\|B(n)\|/n → c ∈ (0,1)` ∧ `‖f‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ⇒ `√\|B(n)\|·∫f dP = o_p(1)` | OneShot bias wrapper. **Proved.** |

#### KFold corollaries (public; new)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | per `k : Fin K`, training-complement-measurable `f` with `‖f‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(1)` ⇒ `(1/√\|fold n k\|) Σ_{fold n k} (f(Z_i) − ∫f dP) = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-evaluation-fold centered sum. Instantiates the abstract layer with `eval n := split.fold n k`, `m_train n := comap (trainComplement n k) Z`, `h_indep` via `split.folds_indep n k` (with `.symm` since the K-fold version is stated `IndepFun fold trainComplement`), `h_eval_card_grow := split.grow k`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.sqrtFold_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `1 < K` ∧ `‖f‖_{L²(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ⇒ per `k`, `√\|fold n k\|·∫f dP = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-fold bias. Uses `c := (K:ℝ)⁻¹` ∈ (0, 1) (whence the `1 < K` requirement) and `split.ratio k`. **Proved.** |

### 12i. `Stat/MEstimation/InfluenceFunction.lean`

Light data structure packaging the three properties an influence function must satisfy: measurability, mean-zero, and finite second moment. Mirrors `def:sp-pathwise-diff` from `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/semi_parametric_inference.tex` at the *assumption* level — pathwise differentiability is deferred.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `InfluenceFunction P ψ` | `Prop`-valued structure | An influence function for parameter `θ : E` under law `P : Measure X` is a measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable function `ψ : X → E`. Fields: `measurable`, `mean_zero`, `finite_var`. |

### 12j. `Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`

Smooth-function CLT. Two forms; both proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `deltaMethod_scalar` | `theorem` | If `√n (T_n − t₀) ⇒ N(0, σ²)` and `g : ℝ → ℝ` differentiable at `t₀` with derivative `g'`, then `√n (g T_n − g t₀) ⇒ N(0, g'² σ²)`. **Proved by Codex.** Pipeline: tightness of `√n (T_n − t₀)` via `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n − t₀ →_p 0` via `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` against `(√n)⁻¹ → 0`; remainder `Rn := √n · (g(T_n) − g t₀ − g' (T_n − t₀))` is `o_p(1)` from `HasDerivAt.isLittleO_sub` on the localizing event `{|T_n − t₀| < ρ}`; finally `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` for `g' · √n (T_n − t₀)` and `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb `Rn`. |
| `deltaMethod` | `theorem` | Multivariate form: `√n • (T_n − t₀) ⇒ Q` (vector limit) and `g : E → F` Fréchet-differentiable at `t₀` with derivative `Dg : E →L[ℝ] F` ⇒ `√n • (g T_n − g t₀) ⇒ Q.map Dg`. **Proved by Codex.** Vector port of the scalar pipeline: `‖√n • (T_n − t₀)‖` is `O_p(1)` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` along `‖·‖ : E → ℝ` plus `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n − t₀ →_p 0` in norm; remainder controlled by `HasFDerivAt.isLittleO_sub`; linear pushforward `Dg` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` again; vector Slutsky absorption via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. |

### 12j'. `Stat/Inference/HadamardDeriv.lean` + `Stat/Inference/FunctionalDelta.lean`

**Directional (Hadamard) functional delta method.** The Fréchet delta method (12j) cannot reach lattice functionals: an interval-identified parameter's sharp endpoints are `sup'`/`inf'` of separately estimable quantities (`PartialID/.../IntersectionBound.lean` `iInter_Icc_eq_Icc : ⋂ⱼ [Lⱼ,Uⱼ] = [sup' L, inf' U]`; Manski MIV envelopes; combined MTR+MTS bounds), and `max`/`min` are only *Hadamard directionally* differentiable — with a **nonlinear** derivative at a tie, which is exactly the binding/kink regime where bound inference is delicate (Hirano–Porter 2012; Fang–Santos 2019). References: van der Vaart (1998) §20.2; Shapiro (1991).

`HadamardDeriv.lean` (analysis core, no probability):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasHadamardDirDerivAt φ φ' θ` | def | Sequential Hadamard directional differentiability over normed spaces: for every `h`, every `hₙ → h`, every `tₙ → 0⁺`, `(tₙ)⁻¹ • (φ(θ + tₙ•hₙ) − φθ) → φ' h`. `φ'` is positively homogeneous, *not* assumed linear. |
| `HasFDerivAt.hasHadamardDirDerivAt` | theorem | **Fréchet ⇒ Hadamard**: a Fréchet-differentiable `φ` (derivative `L`) is Hadamard directionally differentiable with derivative `L`. *(One `sorry`: the `(tₙ)⁻¹ • rₙ → 0` remainder step — standard `IsLittleO` divide-by-rate, off the critical path; the `max`/`min` results below do not use it.)* |
| `maxDirDeriv a b` / `minDirDeriv a b` | def | Directional derivative of `max`/`min : ℝ×ℝ → ℝ` at `(a,b)`: a coordinate projection off the diagonal, and `max`/`min` itself (sublinear) at the tie `a=b`. |
| `continuous_maxDirDeriv` / `continuous_minDirDeriv` | theorem | The directional derivatives are continuous. |
| `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_max` / `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_min` | theorem | `max`/`min` are Hadamard directionally differentiable at every `(a,b)` with the derivatives above. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Tie case: `max(b+t h₁, b+t h₂) = b + t·max(h₁,h₂)`; off-diagonal: the dominating coordinate is eventually selected. |

`FunctionalDelta.lean` (the delta theorem; consumes the existing `Tendsto_dist_vec` weak-convergence infra):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` | theorem | Reusable Slutsky device: if `μ{Yₙ ≠ Xₙ} → 0` then `Yₙ − Xₙ = o_p(1)`. The ingredient for the *off-diagonal* case (lattice functional selects one coordinate except when the estimated ordering is wrong, an event of vanishing probability). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_map_max` / `_min` | instance | The lattice pushforward `Q.map (z ↦ max/min z.1 z.2)` of a probability measure `Q` is a probability measure. Global instance, so the `Tendsto_dist` conclusions below need *not* assume it. |
| `sqrt_mul_max_sub` / `sqrt_mul_min_sub` | lemma | **The exact tie identity** `√n·(max/min âₙ b̂ₙ − a) = max/min(√n(âₙ−a), √n(b̂ₙ−a))` — the rescaled image equals the lattice functional applied to the joint rescaled deviation, with no remainder. |
| `maxStat_aemeasurable` / `minStat_aemeasurable` | lemma | The image statistic `√n·(max/min âₙ b̂ₙ − a)` is `AEMeasurable` for every `n`, *derived* from the joint deviation's measurability via the tie identity — so the delta theorems need *not* assume it. |
| `deltaMethod_max_tie` | theorem | **Directional delta method for `max` at a tie.** If `√n • ((âₙ,b̂ₙ) − (a,a)) ⇒ Q` on `ℝ×ℝ`, then `√n·(max âₙ b̂ₙ − a) ⇒ Q.map (z ↦ max z.1 z.2)` (generally *non-Gaussian* — the law of `max(Z₁,Z₂)`). The binding/kink case the Fréchet method cannot reach. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Proof: the exact identity `√n(max âₙ b̂ₙ − a) = max(√n(âₙ−a), √n(b̂ₙ−a)) = max∘Sₙ` (`sqrt_mul_max_sub`), then continuous mapping (`Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous`) + `congr_ae`; no linearization remainder. Hypotheses: only the joint-deviation measurability `hSn_meas` and the joint CLT `hCLT` — the pushforward-probability instance and the image-statistic measurability are now discharged automatically (via the instance / `maxStat_aemeasurable`). |
| `deltaMethod_min_tie` | theorem | Companion for `min`. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Same discharged-assumption shape. |

*Off-diagonal (`a ≠ b`) and the abstract `sup'`/`inf'`-over-finite-families forms are the natural follow-ups; the tie case is the novel/hard one and the reusable `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` is the device for the off-diagonal reduction.*

### 12k. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimator.lean`

Parametric-inference regularity bundle (structure layer). Houses only `ZEstimatorRegularity`; the headline theorem `zEstimator_clt` lives downstream in `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean` to keep the import DAG acyclic (the proof needs `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`, which itself imports this file for the structure).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ZEstimatorRegularity ψ θ₀ P` | structure | Identification (`∫ ψ(·;θ₀) dP = 0`), Jacobian `J₀ : E →L[ℝ] E` + invertibility witness, Fréchet-derivative spec, finite var, measurability, plus three smoothness fields: `jacobian_continuity` (`ContinuousAt` of `θ ↦ ∫ ψ(·;θ) dP` at `θ₀`; redundant with `J₀_spec` but exposed for downstream convenience), `psi_int_neighborhood` (`ψ(·;θ)` is `P`-integrable on a neighborhood of `θ₀`), and `score_envelope` (integrable `L²` derivative envelope: ∃ `δ>0`, ∃ nonneg measurable `F : X → ℝ` with `F²∈L¹(P)` such that `‖ψθz−ψθ₀z‖ ≤ ‖θ−θ₀‖·F z` for all `z : X` and all `‖θ−θ₀‖<δ` (pointwise — produced by MVT on smooth scores; needed to transfer the bound to random sample-dependent `θn`) — this is the LaTeX-spec form of `def:par-smoothness` and is what discharges `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` via Markov + iid LLN on `F²`). Reference: `def:par-smoothness` (van der Vaart 1998 §5.6). |

### 12l. `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`

Empirical-process / linearization layer feeding `zEstimator_clt`. References: `def:par-smoothness`, `thm:par-z-clt` in `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/parametric_inference.tex`; van der Vaart (1998) §5.6 Thm 5.41; Newey-McFadden (1994) §7.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `StochEquicontAt ψ θ₀ P μ S θn` | `def` | Asymptotic-equicontinuity predicate at `θ₀` along `θn`: `∀ ε>0, ∃ δ>0` such that the empirical-process gap `(√n)⁻¹ Σ (ψ(θn,Z_i)−ψ(θ₀,Z_i)) − √n ∫ (ψ(θn,·)−ψ(θ₀,·)) dP` exceeding `ε` on `{‖θn−θ₀‖<δ}` has vanishing probability as `n→∞`. Standard Donsker-class hypothesis (van der Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.24 / §19.4); exposed as a hypothesis pending Mathlib's empirical-process layer. |
| `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity` (in particular `score_envelope`) + consistency `θn →_p θ₀`, the sample mean square `(1/n) Σ_{i<n} ‖ψ(θn,Z_i) − ψ(θ₀,Z_i)‖²` is `o_p(1)`. Proof: dominated by `‖θn−θ₀‖² · (1/n) Σ F(Z_i)²` on the `δ`-good event (pointwise envelope bound), where the second factor satisfies a Markov bound `μ{Mn>M} ≤ K/M` with `K := ∫F² dP` (using `IIDSample.identDist + S.law` to push `∫ω F(S.Z i ω)² dμ = ∫z F z² dP`); the `δ`-bad event has probability → 0 by `hConsistent (τ/2)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalScoreDiff_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Centered empirical-process control: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, and `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt ψ θ₀ P μ S θn`, `‖(1/√n) Σ (ψ(θn,Z_i) − ψ(θ₀,Z_i)) − √n · ∫ (ψ(θn,·) − ψ(θ₀,·)) dP‖ = o_p(1)`. (Subtracting `√n · ∫` is the iid bias correction.) Proof: combine `hStochEquicont(ε)` with `hConsistent(δ/2)` to remove the conditioning on `{‖θn−θ₀‖<δ}` via measure-union bound. **Proved.** |
| `populationScoreDiff_eq_jacobian_plus_remainder` | `theorem` | Population Fréchet expansion: `∫ (ψ(θ,·) − ψ(θ₀,·)) dP − J₀ (θ − θ₀) =o[𝓝 θ₀] ‖θ − θ₀‖`. Rephrasing of `J₀_spec` as `Asymptotics.IsLittleO`. **Proved.** |
| `localStochasticExpansion` | `theorem` | Headline expansion: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, `hStochEquicont`, and the rate `hRate : ‖θn − θ₀‖ = O_p(1/√n)`, the norm `‖(1/√n) Σ (ψ(θn,Z_i) − ψ(θ₀,Z_i)) − √n · J₀ (θn − θ₀)‖` is `o_p(1)`. Combines (b)+(c)+`hRate` via triangle. Plugged directly into `zEstimator_clt`. **Proved.** |

### 12l'. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean`

Headline theorem layer for the parametric-inference workhorse. Z- / M-estimator CLT for vector-valued estimating equations. OLS, MLE, IV-2SLS instantiate it. Imports `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zEstimator_clt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, asymptotic equicontinuity (`hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt ψ θ₀ P μ S θn`), the rate hypothesis `hRate : ‖θn − θ₀‖ = O_p(1/√n)`, and empirical-moment vanishing, `θn` is asymptotically linear at `θ₀` with IF `−J₀⁻¹ · ψ(·; θ₀)` (Chernozhukov form). Conclusion is `IsAsymLinearVec`. Reference: van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.41. **Proved.** All three `IsAsymLinearVec` fields discharged: `mean_zero` (push `−J₀⁻¹` through Bochner integral via `identification`), `finite_var` (operator-norm bound on `J₀_inv` + `finite_var`), and `remainder` (combine `localStochasticExpansion` + `hMoment`, then bound the rescaled remainder by `J₀_inv` and the finite-dim left-inverse derived from `J₀_inverse`). The Donsker-step content is encapsulated in the `hStochEquicont` hypothesis, to be discharged per-instance until Mathlib's empirical-process layer lands. |

### 12l''. `Stat/MEstimation/ExtremumConsistency.lean` — discharging the CLT's consistency and equicontinuity hypotheses

Additive bridge: re-exposes the headline CLTs (`zEstimator_clt`, `gmm_asymptotically_linear`) with their two *opaque* hypotheses derived from clean primitives. **Consistency** `hConsistent` is derived from the primitive extremum conditions via the empirical-process layer's `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` (§12ep.c) — classical reduction (Newey–McFadden 1994 Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.7): consistency follows from a Glivenko–Cantelli criterion plus a well-separated population maximum. **Equicontinuity** `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt` is derived from the *class-level* Donsker property `AsymptoticEquicont` (§12ep.d) via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` — the estimator-indexed modulus is reconstructed from the function-class property plus consistency. The original `hConsistent`/`hStochEquicont`-taking theorems are unchanged and remain available for callers who establish those by other means. Imports `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/Modulus.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `consistent_lt_norm_of_le_dist` | `(∀ ε>0, μ{ε ≤ dist (θn n) θ₀} → 0) ⇒ (∀ ε>0, μ{ε < ‖θn n − θ₀‖} → 0)` | **Format bridge** between the consistency conclusion of the empirical-process layer (`≤ dist`) and the form the CLT layer consumes (`< ‖·‖`); on a normed group `dist = ‖·−·‖` and `{ε<‖·‖} ⊆ {ε≤dist}`, so the measures squeeze to 0. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT from extremum primitives.** Replaces `hConsistent` by `(m, hGC : GlivenkoCantelli S m, hArgmax : θn maximizes Pₙ m over θ₀, hSep : well-separated population max at θ₀)`; consistency is derived and fed to `zEstimator_clt`. The Donsker modulus `hStochEquicont`, rate `hRate`, and score FOC `hMoment` are unchanged. Typically `ψ = ∂m/∂θ`, so one estimator satisfies both `hMoment` and `hArgmax`; `m`/`ψ` kept independent to avoid differentiability bookkeeping. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec θn θ₀ prob.influence …` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue.** Discharges `hConsistent` from a Glivenko–Cantelli GMM criterion `m` (classically `m θ = −ḡₙ(θ)ᵀ W ḡₙ(θ)`) with well-separated population max of which `θn` is a sample maximiser. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT with the equicontinuity hypothesis discharged.** Replaces `hStochEquicont` by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont ψ θ₀ P μ S` (§12ep.d); `StochEquicontAt` is reconstructed via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` from `hAEC` + `hConsistent`. Keeps `hConsistent`, `hRate`, `hMoment`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Both opaque hypotheses discharged.** Assumes neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont`: takes the extremum package `(m, hGC, hArgmax, hSep)` and the Donsker property `hAEC`. The single derived consistency conclusion is threaded into *both* the linearisation and the `StochEquicontAt` reduction. Fully primitive Z-estimator CLT. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec θn θ₀ prob.influence …` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker`.** `hStochEquicont` replaced by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont prob.score prob.θ₀ P μ S`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
