| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec θn θ₀ prob.influence …` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker`.** Neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont` assumed: consistency from the GC GMM criterion, equicontinuity from `hAEC`. Fully primitive GMM asymptotic-linearity theorem. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |

### 12m. `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean`

Estimator-agnostic convergence-in-probability primitives. Extracted from the (formerly `private`) helpers in `Estimation/NPIV/DR/AsymptoticNormal.lean` so any estimator can reuse them. Consumed by `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb.comp_continuousAt` | `Xn →_p c` ∧ `ContinuousAt g c` ⇒ `g∘Xn →_p g c` | Continuous-mapping theorem in probability at a **constant** limit `c`. ε-δ pullback of `ContinuousAt` through `tendstoInMeasure_iff_dist`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.pi_comp_continuousAt` | finite `ι`, coordinatewise `Yₙᵢ →_p cᵢ`, `ContinuousAt g c` ⇒ `g(Yₙ) →_p g(c)` | Finite-Pi continuous-mapping theorem in probability. Converts coordinate tails to a finite union bound, then applies continuity in the product metric. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.matrix_comp_continuousAt` | entrywise `Mₙᵢⱼ →_p M₀ᵢⱼ`, `ContinuousAt g M₀` ⇒ `g(Mₙ) →_p g(M₀)` | Matrix-valued corollary of the finite-Pi theorem, reindexing entries by `K×K`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.inv` | `Yn →_p Y₀` ∧ `Y₀ ≠ 0` ⇒ `1/Yn →_p 1/Y₀` | Reciprocal continuity (special case of `comp_continuousAt` via `continuousAt_inv₀`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub_const` | `Yn →_p Y₀` ⇒ `Yn − Y₀ →_p 0` | Center a convergence-in-probability statement at its constant limit. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub` | `Xn →_p a` ∧ `Yn →_p b` ⇒ `Xn − Yn →_p a − b` | Difference of two convergent-in-probability sequences (constant limits); ε/2 union bound (Mathlib lacks `TendstoInMeasure.sub`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isLittleOp_one` | `Yn →_p 0` ⇒ `Yn = o_p(1)` | Convergence in probability to zero is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isBigOp_one` | `Xn →_p c` ⇒ `Xn = O_p(1)` | In-probability tightness at a constant limit. **Proved.** |

### 12n. `Stat/Limit/WLLN.lean`

Generic weak law of large numbers for `IIDSample`, plus the consistent-variance corollary. Generalizes the Panel-specific `iidPanel_sampleMean_ae` (boundedness weakened to integrability; a.s. limit converted to in-probability). Supplies the `σ̂ₙ →_p σ²` consistency input that the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleMean` | `Measurable g` ⇒ `Measurable (S.sampleMean g N)` | Measurability of the sample mean (finite sum of `g ∘ Z i`, scaled). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (g∘Z₀)` ⇒ `S.sampleMean g →_p ∫ g dP` | WLLN: i.i.d. sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Wraps `strong_law_ae_real` (a.s.) then `tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_ae` on the finite measure `μ`; law transfer via `integral_map` + `S.law`. Requires `[IsProbabilityMeasure P]`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleSecondMoment_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (ψ²∘Z₀)` ⇒ `S.sampleMean ψ² →_p ∫ ψ² dP` | Consistent variance for a known influence function `ψ`: `(1/n)Σψ(Zᵢ)² →_p σ²`. Direct corollary with `g := ψ²`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBWLLN.lean`

Fold-B weak law of large numbers: the analogue of `sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` averaged over the evaluation fold `B(n)` of a `OneShotSplit` (a growing index subset) instead of the full sample. Unlike the full-sample WLLN (an SLLN/a.e. argument) this is a finite-variance Chebyshev argument over the fold, so it restricts cleanly to `B(n)`. Supplies the missing substrate for fold-B sample-mean consistency (e.g. the Jacobian-consistency input of the partially linear feasible-DML estimator).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `MemLp g 2 P` ⇒ `(1/\|B(n)\|)Σ_{i∈B(n)} g(Zᵢ) →_p ∫ g dP` | **Fold-B WLLN.** For a fixed finite-variance `g`, the fold-B sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Proof: per-index variance/mean transport via `IdentDistrib.variance_eq`/`map`, `IndepFun.variance_sum` over the finset `B(n)` gives `Var = Var(g)/\|B(n)\|`, Chebyshev (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`) and `\|B(n)\|→∞` (`foldB_card_tendsto`) close it (empty folds handled via the eventual filter). **Proved.** |

### 12n''. `Stat/Inference/VarianceEstimation.lean`

Consistent variance/covariance estimation for the i.i.d. sample type — the `σ̂ →ₚ σ₀` input the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis. Builds directly on the WLLN.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_mul_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)Σ g₁(Zᵢ)g₂(Zᵢ) →ₚ ∫ g₁g₂ dP` | Empirical mean of a product (WLLN with `g := g₁·g₂`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.abs_apply_mul_le_norm_sq` | `\|φ(ψ x)·φ'(ψ x)\| ≤ (‖φ‖‖φ'‖)·‖ψ x‖²` | Operator-norm domination helper. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleCov_entry_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)Σ φ(ψ(Zᵢ))φ'(ψ(Zᵢ)) →ₚ ∫ φ(ψ)φ'(ψ) dP` | **Covariance consistency** for `ψ : X → E` via continuous coordinate functionals `φ φ' : E →L[ℝ] ℝ` (specializes to entrywise Σ̂ₖₗ via `EuclideanSpace.proj`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` | `Vn →ₚ v₀ ⇒ √Vn →ₚ √v₀` | Continuous-mapping for `Real.sqrt`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrt_var_tendsto_inProb` | `σ̂² →ₚ σ₀² ∧ σ₀>0 ⇒ √(σ̂²) →ₚ σ₀` | **Studentization-ready** standard-error consistency; feeds `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''. `Stat/CLT/MultivariateCLT.lean`

Multivariate CLT via the **Cramér–Wold device**, the vector analogue of `clt_normalized_sum`. Discharges the multivariate CLT contact `_hCLT` taken as a hypothesis by `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` and `deltaMethod` (vector Δ-method). Weak convergence on a finite-dim real inner-product space `E` is reduced to pointwise charFun convergence (Lévy continuity, `ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_iff_tendsto_charFun` from the `Clt` package); each direction's charFun is the scalar charFun of the projected sum `⟪t, vecNS⟫`, handled by the scalar CLT.

**Limit-law status:** the Cramér–Wold reduction and per-direction charFun limit `→ exp(−½∫⟪t,ψ⟫² dP)` are fully proven, sorry-free. The theorems here keep the target `Q` abstract, parameterized by `charFun Q t = exp(−½∫⟪t,ψ⟫² dP)`. That abstract hypothesis is **discharged concretely** in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (see 12n'''c): `gaussianLimit ψ` is the covariance-`Σ` Gaussian built explicitly, and `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` is the multivariate CLT against it with no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis remaining.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inner_normalizedSum` | `⟪t, vecNS n ω⟫ = scalarNS_{⟪t,ψ⟫} n ω` | Projection identity. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_vec_charFun_tendsto` | `charFun(μ.map vecNS)(t) → exp(−½∫⟪t,ψ⟫² dP)` | **Per-direction charFun limit** from the scalar CLT via `integral_map`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.of_charFun_tendsto` | pointwise charFun convergence ⇒ `Tendsto_dist_vec` | **Cramér–Wold wrapper**. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec_of_charFun` | Gaussian-charFun `Q` ⇒ `vecNS ⇒ Q` | **Multivariate CLT contact**; discharges `_hCLT`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec_clt` | vector asym-linearity + Gaussian-charFun `Q` ⇒ rescaled estimator ⇒ Q | **End-to-end vector asymptotic normality** (no CLT hypothesis). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''b. `Stat/CLT/GaussianCharFunBridge.lean`

Verified glue converting a *concrete* centered Gaussian into the abstract `hQ` characteristic-function target taken as a hypothesis by `MultivariateCLT`/`WaldVec`/`RatioDeltaMethod`. The multivariate-Gaussian charFun formula is `ProbabilityTheory.IsGaussian.charFun_eq'` (`Mathlib/Probability/Distributions/Gaussian/CharFun.lean`), proven at the current pin. The concrete `Q` is now constructed in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (12n'''c), completing de-abstraction.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `charFun_isGaussian_centered` | `IsGaussian Q` ∧ `∫ x ∂Q = 0` ⇒ `charFun Q t = exp(−½·covarianceBilin Q t t)` | centered-Gaussian charFun; specialises Mathlib `IsGaussian.charFun_eq'`. **Proved** (axioms: propext/choice/Quot). |
| `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq` | + `∀ t, covarianceBilin Q t t = ∫⟪t,ψ⟫²dP` ⇒ `charFun Q t = exp(−½∫⟪t,ψ⟫²dP)` | matches the exact `hQ` shape of the multivariate CLT contact, ready to discharge it once `Q` is built. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''c. `Stat/CLT/SecondMomentOperator.lean` + `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean`

**De-abstraction of the multivariate CLT.** Together with `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` (operator √, 10j) and `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` (10i), these build the concrete covariance-`Σ` Gaussian limit and discharge the abstract `hQ` of `MultivariateCLT`. `SecondMomentOperator` is the operator `Σ t = ∫ ⟪t,ψ x⟫ • ψ x ∂P`; `GaussianLimit` sets `gaussianLimit ψ := stdGaussian.map √Σ` and proves its charFun is the CLT target.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `secondMomentLM` | `E →ₗ[ℝ] E` | Second-moment operator `Σ t = ∫⟪t,ψ⟫•ψ ∂P` (Bochner integral); linearity via `integral_add`/`integral_smul`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_inner` | `⟪Σ t, s⟫ = ∫⟪t,ψ⟫⟪s,ψ⟫ ∂P` | Quadratic-form identity, via `integral_inner`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_isPositive` | `(secondMomentLM ψ).IsPositive` | `Σ` is positive (symmetric + `⟪Σt,t⟫ = ∫⟪t,ψ⟫² ≥ 0`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit` | `Measure E` | The CLT limit: `(stdGaussian E).map √Σ`, centered Gaussian with covariance `Σ`. `IsGaussian`/`IsProbabilityMeasure` instances. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_mean` | `∫ x, x ∂(gaussianLimit ψ) = 0` | Centered (mean pushes through the continuous-linear √Σ; `stdGaussian_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_covarianceBilin` | `covarianceBilin (gaussianLimit ψ) t t = ∫⟪t,ψ⟫⟪t,ψ⟫ ∂P` | Covariance recovers the asymptotic-variance integral, via `covarianceBilin_map`, `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` (self-adjoint), `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` (√Σ∘√Σ=Σ), `covarianceBilin_stdGaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_charFun` | `charFun (gaussianLimit ψ) t = exp(−½∫⟪t,ψ⟫² dP)` | Exactly the abstract `hQ`; via `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` | centered sq-int `ψ` ⇒ `vecNS ⇒ gaussianLimit ψ` | **Multivariate CLT against the concrete Gaussian limit** — no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis. **Proved** (axiom-clean). |

### 12n''''. `Stat/Inference/WaldVec.lean`

Multivariate Wald / confidence-ellipsoid asymptotic coverage — the vector analogue of the scalar `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage`. The Wald statistic `Wₙ = (√n(θ̂ₙ−θ₀))ᵀ Σ̂ₙ⁻¹ (√n(θ̂ₙ−θ₀))` is already ℝ-valued, so an ellipsoid `{θ : Wald ≤ c}` has coverage `χ(Iic c)` via one-sided portmanteau. The construction of `Wₙ` and the proof `Wₙ ⇒ χ` (continuous mapping of the joint limit through `(t,S) ↦ tᵀS⁻¹t`) is the caller's input, exactly as the scalar `wald_coverage` takes `Sₙ ⇒ N(0,1)`. The limit `χ` is kept abstract here, but identifying the image of the concrete Gaussian `gaussianLimit` (12n'''c) under the quadratic form `S ↦ SᵀΣ⁻¹S` as `χ²_d` is now **closed** in `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean` (12n'''d).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic` | `Wₙ ⇒ χ` ∧ `χ(∂Iic c)=0` ∧ bridge ⇒ `coverProb → χ(Iic c)` | **Ellipsoid coverage** via one-sided portmanteau on `Set.Iic c`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic_of_noAtoms` | `Wₙ ⇒ χ` (`[NoAtoms χ]`) ∧ bridge ⇒ `coverProb → χ(Iic c)` | Same, boundary-null discharged automatically for any atomless limit (e.g. non-degenerate χ²). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''d. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquared.lean` + `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean`

**χ² identification of the Wald limit.** `ChiSquared` *defines* the χ²_d law and `ChiSquaredWald` whitens `gaussianLimit` through the Wald quadratic form to recognise its image as χ²_d, closing the open step flagged in `WaldVec`. χ²_d is defined as the law of `‖W‖²` for `W ~ stdGaussian` (textbook squared-norm definition; the `Gamma(d/2,1/2)` density form is not developed — Mathlib has no χ²). The whitening: `gaussianLimit = stdGaussian.map √Σ`, and with `Σ` non-degenerate (injective ⇒ invertible in finite dim), `⟪√Σ w, Σ⁻¹(√Σ w)⟫ = ‖w‖²` by self-adjointness of `√Σ`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `chiSqDist` | `ℕ → Measure ℝ` | The χ²_d law: `(stdGaussian (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d))).map ‖·‖²`. `IsProbabilityMeasure` instance. **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_linearIsometryEquiv` | `(stdGaussian E).map ι = stdGaussian F` for `ι : E ≃ₗᵢ F` | `stdGaussian` is isometry-invariant; matched characteristic functions (`charFun_isGaussian_centered` + `Measure.ext_of_charFun`, covariance preserved by `ι`). **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq` | `(stdGaussian E).map ‖·‖² = chiSqDist (finrank ℝ E)` | The squared-norm law depends only on dimension. **Proved.** |
| `noAtoms_pi_normSq` / `noAtoms_chiSqDist` | `1 ≤ d ⇒ NoAtoms (chiSqDist d)` | χ²_d is atomless for `d ≥ 1` (squared first coordinate atomless, Fubini over the independent remainder; reduced to a product of 1-D Gaussians via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi`). **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentInv` | `E →L[ℝ] E` | `Σ⁻¹ = (√Σ)⁻¹∘(√Σ)⁻¹` from injectivity of `Σ`; `secondMomentInv_secondMomentLM` / `secondMomentLM_secondMomentInv` certify it is the two-sided inverse. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_waldForm_map` | `(gaussianLimit ψ).map (S ↦ ⟪S, Σ⁻¹ S⟫) = chiSqDist (finrank ℝ E)` | **The χ² identification** (whitening through `√Σ`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_chiSq` | `Wₙ ⇒ χ²_d` (`1≤d`) ∧ bridge ⇒ `coverProb → χ²_d(Iic c)` | WaldVec ellipsoid coverage specialised to the identified atomless limit `chiSqDist d`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''e. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquaredProjection.lean` — squared-norm of a projected Gaussian is χ²

The law of `‖P z‖²` for an **orthogonal projection** `P` (self-adjoint idempotent) and `z ~ stdGaussian` is `χ²_{rank P}`. Reusable beyond GMM (F-tests, score/LM tests, rank-deficient Wald). Proof: corestrict `P` to its range `S`, transport by the orthonormal-basis isometry `S ≃ₗᵢ EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin r)`; the pushforward of `stdGaussian E` is `stdGaussian S` (centered, covariance = inner product, via `Measure.ext_of_charFun` — the crux is that the corestriction's adjoint is the inclusion because `P` fixes its range), then `stdGaussian_map_normSq`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection` | `IsSelfAdjoint P` ∧ `P ∘L P = P` ⇒ `(stdGaussian E).map (‖P·‖²) = chiSqDist (finrank ℝ (range P))` | **Projected-Gaussian χ².** The squared norm of an orthogonally-projected standard Gaussian is `χ²` with rank-of-projection degrees of freedom. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12o. `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`

Generic studentized-CLT and Wald-coverage engine, abstracted off the concrete TRAE-DR estimator (the NPIV/DR theorems now instantiate these). Portmanteau / Gaussian-boundary helpers are made public here.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier` | `Xn ⇒ Q` ∧ `Q(∂E)=0` ⇒ `(μ.map Xn)(E) → Q(E)` | Continuity-set portmanteau for the project's `Tendsto_dist` wrapper. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_singleton` | `N(0,1)({x}) = 0` | Standard normal has no atoms. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Icc` | `0 < z` ⇒ `N(0,1)(∂[−z,z]) = 0` | Zero boundary mass for a symmetric interval. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian` | `Xn ⇒ N(0,σ₀²)` ∧ `σ̂ →_p σ₀` ∧ `σ₀>0` ⇒ `Xn/σ̂ ⇒ N(0,1)` | **Studentized CLT** (random-denominator Slutsky). Scales `Xn` by `1/σ₀` (`const_mul_tendsto_gaussian`); the remainder `Xn·(1/σ̂ − 1/σ₀)` is `O_p(1)·o_p(1)` via `tightness` + `comp_continuousAt`; absorbs with `add_isLittleOp_one`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` | `Sn ⇒ N(0,1)` ∧ `coverProb − μ{Sn∈[−z,z]} → 0` ⇒ `coverProb → N(0,1)([−z,z])` | **Wald coverage** (generic): portmanteau on the closed interval plus the caller-supplied event-equivalence bridge. Specialize at `z = z_{1−α/2}` for `1 − α` coverage. **Proved.** |

### 12o'. `Stat/Bootstrap/` — Nonparametric bootstrap standard error

The econometric workhorse use of the bootstrap (`vce(bootstrap)`): estimate the standard error of an asymptotically-linear estimator by the bootstrap, then form a studentized / Wald interval. The key fact is that the nonparametric (multinomial / Efron) bootstrap variance of `√n(X̄* − X̄)` is **exactly** the empirical variance `(1/n)Σ(ψ(Zᵢ) − ψ̄ₙ)²` — not an asymptotic approximation — so a *consistent* bootstrap SE plugs straight into the generic studentized CLT / Wald coverage of `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`. The nonparametric percentile / percentile-t refinements (which need conditional weak convergence) remain out of scope, but the **Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap** below now supplies an *exact* distributional law on the conditioned sample. Three files: `Variance.lean` (the exact variance identity + consistency), `CI.lean` (studentized CLT + Wald coverage), and `GaussianMultiplier.lean` (exact multiplier-bootstrap law).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar` | `noncomputable def`, `ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | Bootstrap variance: the conditional variance `E*[ψ²] − (E*ψ)² = sampleMean(ψ²) − (sampleMean ψ)²` of a single multinomial-bootstrap draw `ψ(Z*₁)` over the empirical distribution. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_eq_centered` | `bootstrapVar = (1/n)Σ(ψ(Zᵢ) − ψ̄ₙ)²` | **Exact variance identity** (`E*[(ψ−E*ψ)²]`), the centered empirical second moment; holds for every `n` (`field_simp`/`ring` after the centered-sum expansion). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_nonneg` | `0 ≤ bootstrapVar S ψ n ω` | Nonnegativity — it is a sum of squares (`bootstrapVar_eq_centered`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_tendsto_inProb` | meas/int/sq-int/mean-zero `ψ` ⇒ `bootstrapVar →ₚ ∫ ψ² dP` | **Consistency of the bootstrap variance**: `sampleMean(ψ²) →ₚ ∫ψ²` and `(sampleMean ψ)² →ₚ (∫ψ)²=0`, subtracted via `Tendsto_inProb.sub`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE` | `noncomputable def`, `√(bootstrapVar)` | Bootstrap standard error of `√n θ̂` — the bootstrap estimate of the asymptotic SD `√(∫ψ²)`. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE_tendsto_inProb` | same hyps ⇒ `bootstrapSE →ₚ √(∫ ψ² dP)` | **SE consistency**; `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` of the variance consistency. Supplies the `σ̂ →ₚ σ₀` input of `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapStudentized` | `noncomputable def`, `√n(θ̂−θ₀)/σ̂ₙ` | The bootstrap studentized statistic (full-sample index family). |
| `bootstrap_studentized_tendsto` | `IsAsymLinear θn θ₀ ψ S range` ∧ `0<∫ψ²` ⇒ `√n(θ̂−θ₀)/σ̂ₙ ⇒ N(0,1)` | **Bootstrap studentized CLT**: `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` (numerator `⇒ N(0,∫ψ²)`) + bootstrap-SE consistency through `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `bootstrap_wald_coverage` | + `z>0` ∧ coverage-event bridge ⇒ `coverProb → N(0,1)([−z,z])` | **Bootstrap Wald asymptotic coverage**; instantiates `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` on the bootstrap studentized statistic. `z = z_{1−α/2}` gives the `1−α` interval `θ̂ₙ ± z_{1−α/2}·σ̂ₙ/√n`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12o''. `Stat/Bootstrap/GaussianMultiplier.lean` — exact Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap law

The multiplier/wild bootstrap (van der Vaart §23; Mammen) attaches i.i.d. mean-zero multipliers `ξᵢ` to the recentred data `aᵢ = xᵢ − x̄ₙ`. With **standard Gaussian** multipliers the perturbed statistic is, on a *fixed* data vector, **exactly** a centred Gaussian — no CLT and no conditional-distribution machinery, because a linear combination of independent Gaussians is Gaussian with variances adding. Both declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `map_weighted_sum_gaussian` | `iIndepFun ξ`, each `μ.map (ξ i) = gaussianReal 0 1` ⇒ `μ.map (ω ↦ ∑ i, a i · ξ i ω) = gaussianReal 0 ⟨∑ i, (a i)², _⟩` | **Core fact**: a weighted sum of i.i.d. standard Gaussians has law `N(0, ∑ aᵢ²)`. `Finset` induction peeling one summand: each `aᵢ ξᵢ ~ N(0, aᵢ²)` (`gaussianReal_map_const_mul`), the partial sum is independent of the new term (`iIndepFun.indepFun_finset_sum_of_notMem`), and their laws convolve (`gaussianReal_add_gaussianReal_of_indepFun`). |
| `multiplierBootstrap_law` | as above with `aᵢ = n^{-1/2}(xᵢ − x̄)` ⇒ law of `Tₙ* = n^{-1/2} ∑ i, (xᵢ − x̄) ξᵢ` is `gaussianReal 0 ⟨sₙ², _⟩` | **Exact law of the studentized multiplier-bootstrap mean**: the bootstrap statistic is exactly `N(0, sₙ²)` with `sₙ² = (1/n) ∑ (xᵢ − x̄)²` the fixed sample variance. Since `sₙ² →ₚ σ²`, this is the distributional bootstrap counterpart of the CLT limit `N(0, σ²)`. |

---

#### 12p. `Stat/Quantile/Quantile.lean` — Quantile function (generalized inverse of a cdf)

Causal-agnostic statistical primitive: the lower quantile function of a real
probability measure, `quantile μ τ := sInf {x | τ ≤ cdf μ x}`, the
left-continuous generalized inverse of Mathlib's `ProbabilityTheory.cdf`.
Mathlib has the cdf but no generalized inverse; this file supplies it plus the
order (Galois) characterisation. Project-agnostic; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileSet μ τ` | `def`, `Set ℝ` | The super-level set `{x | τ ≤ cdf μ x}` defining the quantile. |
| `quantile μ τ` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `sInf (quantileSet μ τ)` — the `τ`-quantile (left-continuous generalized inverse of the cdf). |
| `quantileSet_up_closed` | `lemma` | `x ∈ quantileSet μ τ → x ≤ x' → x' ∈ quantileSet μ τ` (cdf monotone). **Proved.** |
| `bddBelow_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `0 < τ`, the super-level set is bounded below (cdf → 0 at −∞). **Proved.** |
| `nonempty_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `τ < 1`, the super-level set is nonempty (cdf → 1 at +∞). **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_quantile` | `lemma` | For `τ < 1`, `τ ≤ cdf μ (quantile μ τ)`. Uses right-continuity of the cdf. **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_of_quantile_le` | `lemma` | For `τ < 1`, `quantile μ τ ≤ x → τ ≤ cdf μ x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_of_le_cdf` | `lemma` | For `0 < τ`, `τ ≤ cdf μ x → quantile μ τ ≤ x` (`csInf_le`). **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_iff` | `theorem` | **Galois connection** on `(0,1)`: `quantile μ τ ≤ x ↔ τ ≤ cdf μ x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_mono` | `lemma` | Quantile monotone in `τ` on `(0,1)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12q. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalCDF.lean` — Empirical cumulative distribution function

Sample analogue of `cdf P`, `F̂ₙ(y) = (1/n) Σ 1{Z_i ≤ y}` (the `sampleMean` of the indicator `cdfStat y`), with its fixed-`y` influence function `cdfIF P y z = 1{z ≤ y} − F(y)`. Because `√n (F̂ₙ(y) − F(y))` *is* the normalized influence-function sum, the empirical cdf is asymptotically linear with an identically-zero remainder, giving the fixed-`y` CLT directly. Causal-agnostic base layer for `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean`; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cdfStat y` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ → ℝ` | The lower-ray indicator `1{· ≤ y}` (= `Set.indicator (Iic y) 1`). |
| `measurable_cdfStat`, `cdfStat_nonneg`, `cdfStat_le_one`, `cdfStat_sq` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `0 ≤ 1{·≤y} ≤ 1`; idempotence `(1{·≤y})² = 1{·≤y}`. **Proved.** |
| `integrable_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `cdfStat y` is integrable (bounded on a finite measure). **Proved.** |
| `integral_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `∫ 1{·≤y} dP = cdf P y` (= `P.real (Iic y)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF S y` | `noncomputable def`, `ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | The empirical cdf `F̂ₙ(y)`, the `sampleMean` of `cdfStat y`. |
| `cdfIF P y` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ → ℝ` | Fixed-`y` influence function `1{z ≤ y} − F(y)`. |
| `measurable_cdfIF`, `cdfIF_mean_zero`, `abs_cdfIF_le_one`, `cdfIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `∫ cdfIF dP = 0`; `|cdfIF| ≤ 1`; square-integrability. **Proved.** |
| `cdfIF_variance` | `lemma` | `∫ cdfIF² dP = F(y)(1 − F(y))`, the Bernoulli variance of `{Z ≤ y}`. **Proved.** |
| `isLittleOp_zero` | `lemma` | The zero sequence is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `rescaledEmpiricalCDF_eq_normalizedSum` | `lemma` | **Key identity** `√n (F̂ₙ(y) − F(y)) = (1/√n) Σ cdfIF(Z_i)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | `F̂ₙ(y)` is `IsAsymLinear` at `F(y)` with IF `cdfIF P y` (remainder ≡ 0). **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Empirical-cdf CLT** `√n (F̂ₙ(y) − F(y)) ⇒ N(0, F(y)(1−F(y)))`. **Proved** (instance of `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal`). |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_inProb` | `theorem` | **Consistency** (WLLN) `F̂ₙ(y) →ₚ F(y)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12r. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean` — Sample-quantile asymptotics (Bahadur representation)

`√n`-asymptotic normality of a quantile-estimator sequence `q̂ₙ` for the `τ`-quantile of `P`, via the Bahadur representation with influence function `ψ_τ(z) = (τ − 1{z ≤ q₀})/f₀` and asymptotic variance `τ(1−τ)/f₀²`. The hard empirical-process (Donsker / asymptotic-equicontinuity) content — the `o_p(n^{-1/2})` Bahadur remainder — is **exposed as a hypothesis** in the `QuantileRegularity` bundle, mirroring the `StochEquicontAt` pattern of `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`; everything downstream is proved. Reusable by any quantile estimator (plain sample quantile, IPW-weighted QTE quantile, …) satisfying the expansion. References: Bahadur (1966); van der Vaart (1998) §21.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileIF τ q₀ f₀` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ → ℝ` | Sample-quantile influence function `(τ − 1{z ≤ q₀})/f₀`. |
| `measurable_quantileIF` | `lemma` | Measurability of `quantileIF`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_mean_zero` | `lemma` | `∫ ψ_τ dP = 0`, given `cdf P q₀ = τ`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma` | `ψ_τ` is square-integrable (bounded by `(|τ|+1)/|f₀|`). **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_variance` | `lemma` | `∫ ψ_τ² dP = τ(1−τ)/f₀²`, the classical sample-quantile asymptotic variance. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity S qn τ q₀ f₀` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity bundle: `τ ∈ (0,1)`, density `f₀ > 0`, `cdf P q₀ = τ`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) f₀ q₀`, and `bahadur` — the exposed Donsker remainder (= `IsAsymLinear.remainder` for `(q̂ₙ, q₀, ψ_τ)`). |
| `QuantileRegularity.isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | The bundle is an `IsAsymLinear` witness for `q̂ₙ` at `q₀` with IF `ψ_τ`. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity.tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Sample-quantile CLT** `√n (q̂ₙ − q₀) ⇒ N(0, τ(1−τ)/f₀²)`. **Proved** (specializes `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` with the closed-form variance). |

#### 12s. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalQuantile.lean` — Sample quantile (empirical-cdf inverse)

The `τ`-sample quantile `q̂ₙ(τ) = quantile νₙ τ`, the generalized inverse of the empirical cdf, where `νₙ = (1/n) Σ_{i<n} δ_{Zᵢ}` is the empirical measure. Supplies the deterministic/structural facts (switching relation, monotonicity, atom bound) feeding the Bahadur derivation. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure S n ω` | `noncomputable def` | Empirical measure `(1/n) Σ_{i<n} δ_{Zᵢ}`. |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_isProbabilityMeasure` | `lemma` | For `0 < n`, `νₙ` is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_real_Iic` | `lemma` | `νₙ.real (Iic y) = F̂ₙ(y)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_cdf` | `lemma` | **cdf bridge** `cdf νₙ = F̂ₙ`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile S τ` | `noncomputable def` | Sample quantile `q̂ₙ(τ) = quantile νₙ τ`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_le_iff` | `lemma` | **Switching relation** `q̂ₙ(τ) ≤ x ↔ τ ≤ F̂ₙ(x)` (`0<τ<1`, `0<n`). **Proved** (from `quantile_le_iff` + cdf bridge). |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF_monotone` | `lemma` | `y ↦ F̂ₙ(y)` is monotone. **Proved**. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_bound` | `lemma` | **Atom bound** `\|F̂ₙ(q̂ₙ) − τ\| ≤ 1/n` a.s., under atomless `P` (`Continuous (cdf P)`); needs `[IsProbabilityMeasure μ]`. **Proved** (no-atoms from continuous cdf via `measure_singleton`/`leftLim`; a.s. tie-free from `IndepFun`+`NoAtoms`; discrete count `card(Cle q)=card(Clt)+card(Ceq)`, ties `≤1`, strict part `≤ nτ` via threshold below `q`). |

#### 12t. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur.lean` — Bahadur representation (DERIVED)

Derives the Bahadur remainder of the **sample** quantile from elementary tools (Chebyshev + monotone-grid oscillation) — no Donsker/empirical-process hypothesis. Turns `SampleQuantile.QuantileRegularity.bahadur` from a hypothesis into a theorem for `q̂ₙ`. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** This file is now a thin re-export umbrella; the derivation is split by layer under `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur/`: `Oscillation.lean` (L2 Chebyshev increment + L3 monotone-grid oscillation, incl. `SampleQuantileReg`/`empProcess`), `Rate.lean` (L4 root-`n` rate), `Linearity.lean` (L5 inversion + L6 assembly). Existing imports of `Causalean.Stat.Quantile.SampleQuantileBahadur` are unaffected. The declarations below are listed by their owning layer.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SampleQuantileReg P τ q₀ f₀` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity: `τ∈(0,1)`, `f₀>0`, `cdf P q₀=τ`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) f₀ q₀`, **and** `cont` (atomless `P`). |
| `IIDSample.empProcess S n ω y` | `noncomputable def` | Centered scaled empirical process `Gₙ(y) = √n (F̂ₙ(y) − F(y))`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_increment_tendsto_zero` | `lemma` (L2) | `Gₙ(q₀+u/√n) − Gₙ(q₀) →ₚ 0` (Chebyshev second-moment via `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_cell_sandwich`, `empProcess_node_max_tendsto_zero`, `exists_grid_bracket` | `lemma`s | L3 helpers: per-cell monotone sandwich; finite-grid node-max `→ₚ 0`; uniform-mesh bracketing. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_oscillation` | `lemma` (L3, **crux**) | At random `Uₙ=O_p(1)`: `Gₙ(q₀+Uₙ/√n) − Gₙ(q₀) →ₚ 0` (monotone-grid sandwich). **Proved.** |
| `cdf_increment_sqrt_tendsto` (in `.Oscillation`), `Tendsto_dist.limsup_measure_closed_le`, `gaussian_tail_small`, `IIDSample.empProcess_q0_tendsto_normal`/`_bigO` | `lemma`s | L4 helpers: Taylor limit `√n(F(q₀+a/√n)−F(q₀))→f₀a` (single canonical lemma defined in `Oscillation.lean`, reused by `Rate.lean` — the former duplicate `_pre`/`Rate` copies are consolidated); closed-set portmanteau; Gaussian-tail smallness; `Gₙ(q₀) ⇒ N(0,τ(1−τ))` + its `O_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_rate` | `lemma` (L4) | `√n(q̂ₙ−q₀) = O_p(1)` (two-sided tail via switching + portmanteau). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_term_tendsto_zero`, `sampleQuantile_taylor_remainder_tendsto_zero`, `Tendsto_inProb.add_zero_zero`/`of_isLittleOp_one`/`const_mul_zero` | `lemma`s | L5/L6 helpers: `√n(F̂ₙ(q̂ₙ)−τ)→ₚ0`; `√n·R(q̂ₙ)→ₚ0`; `Tendsto_inProb` `+`/bridge/scalar-mul algebra. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_inversion` | `lemma` (L5) | `Gₙ(q̂ₙ) = −f₀·√n(q̂ₙ−q₀) + o_p(1)` (switching + atom bound + Taylor). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_quantileIF_eq` | `lemma` | `(1/√n) Σ ψ_τ(Zᵢ) = −Gₙ(q₀)/f₀`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` (L6) | **Derived** `IsAsymLinear` for `q̂ₙ` (Bahadur remainder proved `o_p(1)`: `f₀·R̃ₙ = L5 − L3`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_quantileRegularity` | `theorem` | `q̂ₙ` satisfies `QuantileRegularity` with `bahadur` derived ⇒ scalar CLT `√n(q̂ₙ−q₀)⇒N(0,τ(1−τ)/f₀²)` for free. **Proved.** |

#### 12u. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileJoint.lean` — Joint normality of a quantile vector

Joint asymptotic normality of `(q̂ₙ(τ₁),…,q̂ₙ(τ_k))` via the per-coordinate derived Bahadur + the project multivariate CLT (Cramér–Wold). Target `Q` kept abstract via Gaussian charFun, as in the rest of the multivariate-CLT stack. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `eucl v` | `noncomputable abbrev` | Pack `Fin k → ℝ` into `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` (the `PiLp 2` equiv). |
| `eucl_apply`, `norm_sq_eucl`, `norm_eucl_le_sum_abs` | `lemma`s | `(eucl v) j = v j`; `‖eucl v‖² = ∑ vⱼ²`; `‖eucl v‖ ≤ ∑ \|vⱼ\|`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIFVec τ q f` | `noncomputable def` | Joint IF `ψ(z)_j = (τⱼ−1{z≤qⱼ})/fⱼ`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec S τ` | `noncomputable def` | Sample-quantile vector `(q̂ₙ(τⱼ))_j`. |
| `measurable_quantileIFVec` | `lemma` | Measurability of `ψ`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_cross` | `lemma` | **Covariance entry** `∫ ψ_{τⱼ}ψ_{τₗ} dP = (min(τⱼ,τₗ)−τⱼτₗ)/(fⱼfₗ)` (`1{≤qⱼ}1{≤qₗ}=1{≤min}`, `F(min)=min(τ)` by monotonicity). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_isAsymLinearVec` | `theorem` | Vector asymptotic linearity (coordinatewise from scalar Bahadur; `eucl` commutes with `•`/`−`/`∑`; norm `≤ ∑\|Rₙⱼ\|`, each `o_p(1)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Headline** `√n(q̂ₙ(τⱼ)−qⱼ)_j ⇒ N(0,Σ)`, `Σ_{jl}=(min(τⱼ,τₗ)−τⱼτₗ)/(fⱼfₗ)` (`Q` abstract Gaussian-charFun via `tendsto_normal_vec_clt`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12v. `Stat/GMM/` — Generalized Method of Moments (Hansen 1982)

Abstract GMM stack over a moment model `g : E → X → F` (parameter `θ : E`,
moment vector valued in `F`, over-identified `dim E ≤ dim F`).  Three layers:
the pure operator-algebra **optimal-weighting theorem** (`VarianceAlgebra`), the
statistical **problem bundle + efficiency** (`Setup`), and **asymptotic
normality** as a corollary of the Z-estimator CLT (`AsymptoticNormality`).  All
declarations are namespace `Causalean.Stat`.  Spec: Hansen (1982); Newey &
McFadden (1994), §3.  All **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

#### 12v.1. `Stat/GMM/VarianceAlgebra.lean` — sandwich/efficient variance algebra

Pure finite-dimensional operator algebra (no measure theory) on continuous
linear maps between real inner-product spaces.  Inverses are carried as *data
with two-sided witnesses* (mirroring `ZEstimatorRegularity.J₀_inv`), so no
operator inverse is constructed.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmBread G W` | `(adjoint G) ∘L W ∘L G : E →L E` | The **bread** `GᵀWG` of Jacobian `G : E →L F` and weighting `W : F →L F`; symmetric when `W` is, invertible at full-rank `G`, `W ≻ 0`. |
| `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | `breadInv ∘L Gᵀ ∘L W ∘L Cov ∘L W ∘L G ∘L breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** `(GᵀWG)⁻¹ GᵀW Σ WG (GᵀWG)⁻¹`, given a two-sided inverse `breadInv` of the bread. (`Cov` = covariance `Σ`, since `Σ` is a reserved Lean token.) |
| `adjoint_inv_self` | `adjoint M = M` ∧ `M ∘L N = id` ⇒ `adjoint N = N` | Self-adjointness of a right inverse of a self-adjoint operator (left-inverse uniqueness). Single shared copy in `VarianceAlgebra.lean` (imported by `OverID.lean`; the former duplicate in `OverID.lean` is removed). |
| `adjoint_gmmBread` | `adjoint W = W` ⇒ `adjoint (gmmBread G W) = gmmBread G W` | (private) The bread is self-adjoint when `W` is. |
| `gmm_efficiency` | `(gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv − effInv).IsPositive` | **GMM optimal-weighting theorem (Hansen 1982, Thm 3.2).** The sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance `effInv = (GᵀCov⁻¹G)⁻¹` in the Löwner order, with equality at `W = Cov⁻¹`. Proof: `V(W) − V★ = (A−B)Σ(A−B)ᵀ ⪰ 0` with `A = (GᵀWG)⁻¹GᵀW`, `B = (GᵀΣ⁻¹G)⁻¹GᵀΣ⁻¹`; the cross terms collapse to `V★` because `Σ`/`Σ⁻¹` cancel against the bread, and positivity is `IsPositive.conj_adjoint` on `Σ ⪰ 0`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.2. `Stat/GMM/Setup.lean` — GMM problem, influence function, efficiency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmScore G W g` | `fun θ x => adjoint G (W (g θ x))` | The **combined GMM score** `ψ(θ,x) = GᵀW g(θ,x)`; its empirical zero is the (linearized) GMM estimator (FOC of `ḡᵀWḡ`). |
| `gmmIF G W breadInv g θ₀` | `fun x => −(breadInv (adjoint G (W (g θ₀ x))))` | The **GMM influence function** `−(GᵀWG)⁻¹GᵀW g(θ₀,·)`. |
| `GMMProblem P` | structure | Moment model bundle: `g`, truth `θ₀`, weighting `W` (`hWsa : adjoint W = W`), Jacobian `G`, identification `∫ g(θ₀) dP = 0`, measurability/finite-variance of `g(θ₀)`, Fréchet-derivative spec `jac_spec`, covariance `Cov` with second-moment characterization `hCov`, and two-sided inverse witnesses for `Cov`, `GᵀWG`, `GᵀCov⁻¹G`. |
| `GMMProblem.score` / `influence` | `noncomputable def`s | The problem's combined score / influence function. |
| `GMMProblem.asympVar` | `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** of the problem. |
| `GMMProblem.effVar` | `effInv` | The **efficient variance** `(GᵀCov⁻¹G)⁻¹`. |
| `GMMProblem.cov_isPositive` | `prob.Cov.IsPositive` | The covariance is a positive operator — it is a second moment (`⟪Cov t,t⟫ = ∫ ⟪t,g⟫² ≥ 0`, symmetric). **Proved.** |
| `GMMProblem.efficiency` | `(prob.asympVar − prob.effVar).IsPositive` | **Hansen optimal-weighting theorem, statistical form**: the sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance in the Löwner order; the efficient GMM weighting is `W = Cov⁻¹`. Instantiates `gmm_efficiency`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.3. `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` — GMM CLT via the combined-score Z-estimator

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | `IsAsymLinearVec θn θ₀ prob.influence S (range n)` | **GMM asymptotic linearity.** If `θn` solves the combined empirical moment `(1/n) Σ GᵀW g(Z_i;θn) = 0`, is consistent at rate `O_p(1/√n)`, and the combined score `prob.score` satisfies `ZEstimatorRegularity` (with `reg.J₀_inv = breadInv`), then `θn` is asymptotically linear with IF `−(GᵀWG)⁻¹GᵀW g(θ₀,·)`. Hence `√n(θn−θ₀) ⇒ N(0, asympVar)` via `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec`. Reduces GMM to `zEstimator_clt` on `gmmScore`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.4. `Stat/GMM/OverID.lean` — over-identification (J / Hansen–Sargan) test

The whitened J-test: with the moment covariance normalised to the identity (the change of variables the `Σ̂⁻¹`-weighting performs), the residual maker `M = I − G(GᵀG)⁻¹Gᵀ` is an orthogonal projection of rank `m − k`, so the J-statistic limit `‖Mw‖²` is `χ²_{m−k}` via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection`. The general-covariance case is the change of variables `z ↦ Σ^{-1/2} z`. Inverses carried as data+witnesses (`heL`, `heR`).

**Deferred (known gap).** This file establishes only the *limit law* `‖Mw‖² ⇒ χ²_{m−k}` for a standard Gaussian `w` on the moment space. Wiring the **empirical** J-statistic `n·ḡₙ(θ̂)ᵀ Σ̂⁻¹ ḡₙ(θ̂)` to this limit — i.e. proving the empirical moments converge to `Mw` under the efficient weighting and applying continuous mapping — is left as future work (needs the moment-CLT contact point + the whitening change-of-variables at the estimator). The χ² target law itself is fully proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | `G ∘L effInv ∘L adjoint G` | The **hat matrix** `H = G(GᵀG)⁻¹Gᵀ`, orthogonal projection onto `range G`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker G effInv` | `id − gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | The **residual maker** `M = I − H`, orthogonal projection onto `(range G)^⊥`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker_isSelfAdjoint` / `_idempotent` | `IsSelfAdjoint M` / `M ∘L M = M` | `M` is an orthogonal projection (uses `effInv` self-adjoint + the inverse witnesses). **Proved.** |
| `gmmResidualMaker_finrank_range` | `finrank (range M) = finrank F − finrank E` | **Hansen–Sargan dof** `m − k`. Proof: `range M = ker H`, `range H = range G`, `G` injective (from `heL`); rank–nullity (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`) + `LinearMap.finrank_range_of_inj`. **Proved.** |
| `jStatistic_chiSq` | `(stdGaussian F).map (‖M·‖²) = chiSqDist (finrank F − finrank E)` | **Headline: the whitened GMM J-statistic limit law is `χ²_{m−k}`.** Applies the projected-Gaussian χ² lemma to `M`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12w. `Stat/Minimax/` — Minimax lower bounds (Le Cam two-point)

The converse direction of the inference stack: machinery to certify that *no*
estimator can do better than a given rate (optimality / sharpness), as opposed
to the achievability results in §12c–§12v. The whole program reduces to one
chain: *risk lower bound ← reduction to testing ← testing error ≥ 1 − TV ← TV ≤ f(KL)*.
The first three links are elementary (this folder); the KL bridge (Pinsker)
lives in `Pinsker.lean`. Project-agnostic; candidates for upstream Mathlib.

Three reduction tiers share the same testing core: **Le Cam** (`LeCam.lean`, §12w.2)
packs two hypotheses; **Fano** (`Fano.lean`, §12w.7) packs many pairwise-separated
ones (rates from `log`-cardinality); **Assouad** (`Assouad.lean`, §12w.8) packs a
`d`-cube of coordinate-wise tests (rates that grow with dimension). Fano and Assouad
are both proven *unconditionally* from the elementary testing bound — no entropy or
product-measure machinery — so the divergence input enters only through `tvDist`.

#### 12w.1. `Stat/Minimax/TotalVariation.lean` — statistical TV distance

Mathlib only carries `SignedMeasure.totalVariation` (Jordan norm); this file develops the testing-oriented statistical TV between two measures.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tvDist μ ν` | `⨆ A measurable, \|μ.real A − ν.real A\|` | **Total variation distance**: sup over measurable sets of the probability gap. |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_one` | `\|μ.real A − ν.real A\| ≤ 1` | Each defining term is bounded by 1 (probability measures). **Proved.** |
| `bddAbove_tvDist_range` | `BddAbove (range …)` | The defining family is bounded above by 1. **Proved.** |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `\|μ.real A − ν.real A\| ≤ tvDist μ ν` | Each measurable set's gap is `≤ tvDist` (via `le_ciSup`). **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `ν.real A − μ.real A ≤ tvDist μ ν` | Signed-gap form. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_nonneg`, `tvDist_le_one` | `0 ≤ tvDist μ ν ≤ 1` | Range. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_symm` | `tvDist μ ν = tvDist ν μ` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_test` | `1 − tvDist μ ν ≤ μ.real A + ν.real Aᶜ` | **The testing bound**: the total error of any test (rejection region `A`) is at least `1 − tvDist`. The single inequality driving the two-point method. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.2. `Stat/Minimax/LeCam.lean` — Le Cam two-point method

The reduction from estimation to testing. Parameters live in a `[PseudoMetricSpace Θ] [OpensMeasurableSpace Θ]`; estimators are arbitrary measurable `est : Ω → Θ`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `measurableSet_error` | `MeasurableSet {ω \| s ≤ dist (est ω) θ}` | The error region of a measurable estimator is measurable (closed-set preimage). **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_error_sum` | `1 − tvDist P₀ P₁ ≤ P₀.real {s ≤ dist(est,θ₀)} + P₁.real {s ≤ dist(est,θ₁)}` | **Summed form.** Under `2s`-separation `2s ≤ dist θ₀ θ₁`, the two error probabilities of any estimator sum to at least `1 − tvDist`. Proof = testing bound + triangle inequality + monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error` | `(1 − tvDist P₀ P₁)/2 ≤ max (P₀.real {…}) (P₁.real {…})` | **Headline (max form).** Every estimator has worst-case error probability at least `½(1 − tvDist P₀ P₁)`: the canonical minimax lower bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.3. `Stat/Minimax/Pinsker.lean` — KL bridge

Bridges `tvDist` to Mathlib's `InformationTheory.klDiv`, making the two-point bound usable for concrete families (KL is computable for Gaussians etc.). **Pinsker's inequality is now proven unconditionally** — both the scalar core (`klFun_lower_bound`) and the measure-theoretic half (Scheffé `≤` + weighted Cauchy–Schwarz). `PinskerBound` remains as a reusable `Prop` packaging but is no longer a substrate gate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `klFun_mul_lower_aux` | `0 ≤ (x+2)·klFun x − (3/2)(x−1)²` for `x ≥ 0` | Cleared-denominator Pinsker scalar bound. Proved by full two-level calculus on `φ` (`HasDerivAt` for φ, φ′; `φ″ = 2(log x + 1/x − 1) ≥ 0` via `log_le_sub_one`; monotonicity ⇒ `φ ≥ φ(1)=0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `klFun_lower_bound` | `(3/2)(x−1)²/(x+2) ≤ klFun x` for `x ≥ 0` | Division-form Pinsker scalar engine. **Proved.** |
| `PinskerBound μ ν` | `Prop`: `tvDist μ ν ≤ √((klDiv μ ν).toReal/2)` | Reusable `Prop` packaging of the Pinsker bound for a pair. **Proven** by `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top`. |
| `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` | `μ ≪ ν` + `klDiv μ ν ≠ ⊤` ⇒ `PinskerBound μ ν` | **Pinsker's inequality, proven unconditionally**: Scheffé `≤` (`tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv`) + weighted Cauchy–Schwarz (`√(p+2)` weight) + `klFun_lower_bound`. Both hyps are genuinely needed (`.toReal` collapses `klDiv = ⊤` to `0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pinskerBound_pi_iid` | one-sample `μ ≪ ν` + `Integrable (llr μ ν) μ` ⇒ `PinskerBound (μ^{⊗n}) (ν^{⊗n})` | i.i.d.-product Pinsker: product AC + `klDiv ≠ ⊤` discharged from marginals via `ProductKLLeCam.pi_iid_*`, then the unconditional inequality. **Proved.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound_of_pinsker` | from `PinskerBound P₀ P₁` + 2s-separation: worst-case error `≥ (1 − √(KL/2))/2` | KL-form minimax lower bound from a supplied `PinskerBound` term; composes with `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error`. **Proved from hypothesis.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound` | `P₀ ≪ P₁` + `klDiv P₀ P₁ ≠ ⊤` + 2s-separation ⇒ worst-case error `≥ (1 − √(KL/2))/2` | **Unconditional** KL-form minimax lower bound: derives the Pinsker bridge via `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` (no `PinskerBound` hypothesis). **Proved.** |

#### 12w.4. `Stat/Minimax/Scheffe.lean` — variational TV identity (≤ direction)

The Scheffé `≤` bound — the analytic workhorse for both the χ² route (`TV ≤ ½√χ²`) and discharging `PinskerBound`. Proved via the symmetric splitting `∫_A f = −∫_{Aᶜ} f` (sidesteps posPart/negPart machinery). The reverse `≥` (full equality) was deliberately skipped — the `≤` suffices downstream.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `abs_setIntegral_le_half_integral_abs_of_integral_eq_zero` | for integrable `f`, `∫ f ∂ν = 0`, measurable `A`: `\|∫ x in A, f ∂ν\| ≤ ½∫\|f\| ∂ν` | Generic, probability-free; clean Mathlib-contribution candidate. **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_eq_setIntegral_rnDeriv_sub_one` | under `μ ≪ ν`: `μ.real A − ν.real A = ∫ x in A, ((μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal − 1) ∂ν` | Signed-gap as a density integral. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv` | `tvDist μ ν ≤ ½∫\|(μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal − 1\| ∂ν` (prob measures, `μ ≪ ν`) | **Scheffé `≤`.** **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.5. `Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean` — χ² divergence link

`chiSqDiv` and the χ² control of total variation — the divergence that makes two-point indistinguishability computable (and tensorizes over iid samples). Named `chiSqDiv` to avoid colliding with the χ² *distribution* `chiSqDist` (§12n'''d).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `chiSqDiv μ ν` | `∫ ((μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal − 1)² ∂ν` | χ²-divergence of μ relative to ν. |
| `chiSqDiv_nonneg` | `0 ≤ chiSqDiv μ ν` | Integrand is a square. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv` | `tvDist μ ν ≤ ½·√(chiSqDiv μ ν)` (prob, `μ≪ν`, sq-deviation integrable) | **Cauchy–Schwarz on Scheffé** (Hölder `2,2` with `g≡1`, `∫1²∂ν=1`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_eq` | `chiSqDiv μ ν = (∫ (μ.rnDeriv ν x).toReal² ∂ν) − 1` (`μ≪ν`) | Expanded form via `∫ dμ/dν ∂ν = 1`. **Proved.** |

| `rnDeriv_prod_eq` | `(μ₁.prod μ₂).rnDeriv (ν₁.prod ν₂) =ᵐ fun z ↦ rnDeriv μ₁ ν₁ z.1 · rnDeriv μ₂ ν₂ z.2` | Product-measure RN-derivative factorization (assembled from `withDensity_rnDeriv_eq` + `prod_withDensity₀` + `rnDeriv_withDensity₀`; Mathlib has no packaged version). Reusable. **Proved.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod` | `1 + chiSqDiv (μ₁.prod μ₂) (ν₁.prod ν₂) = (1+chiSqDiv μ₁ ν₁)(1+chiSqDiv μ₂ ν₂)` | **χ² tensorization** (binary). Via `rnDeriv_prod_eq` + `chiSqDiv_eq` + Fubini (`integral_prod_mul`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` | `chiSqDiv (μ.map e) (ν.map e) = chiSqDiv μ ν` for `e : Ω ≃ᵐ Ω'` (σ-finite) | **χ² is invariant under a measurable equivalence** (relabelling the sample space). Via `integral_map_equiv` + `MeasurableEmbedding.rnDeriv_map`. Reusable. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_self` | `chiSqDiv ρ ρ = 0` (σ-finite) | Self-divergence vanishes (`rnDeriv_self =ᵐ 1`); the `n=0` base case. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_absolutelyContinuous` | `μ ≪ ν ⇒ Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ μ) ≪ Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ ν)` | i.i.d. product a.c. (Mathlib has no `pi`-level a.c. lemma); induction via `piFinSuccAbove` + `AbsolutelyContinuous.prod`/`.map`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ μ)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ ν)) = (1 + chiSqDiv μ ν)ⁿ` (finite `Ω`, prob, `μ≪ν`) | **n-fold i.i.d. χ² tensorization** — the iid scaling consumed by `n`-sample two-point/Le Cam bounds. Induction on `Fin n` via `measurePreserving_piFinSuccAbove` + `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` + binary `chiSqDiv_prod`; integrability discharged for free by `Integrable.of_finite` (restricted to `[Fintype Ω] [MeasurableSingletonClass Ω]`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev` | `Integrable ((d(μ^⊗n)/d(ν^⊗n) − 1)²) (ν^⊗n)` from the one-sample integrability `hint` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `μ≪ν`) | **Integrability propagation** to the i.i.d. product: the squared density deviation stays integrable under `n`-fold products (L²(ν)-tensorization of the density). Supplies the side-condition that `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` got for free on a finite space. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid_general` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ μ)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ ν)) = (1 + chiSqDiv μ ν)ⁿ` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `μ≪ν`, sq-deviation integrable) | **n-fold i.i.d. χ² tensorization on a GENERAL (possibly continuous) measurable space** — the continuum analogue of `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` (whose `[Fintype]` only discharged integrability for free). Same `chiSqDiv_prod` induction, with integrability supplied by `hint` + `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev`. Needed by minimax two-point lower bounds whose sample space is a continuum (e.g. a covariate space). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary` | `chiSqDiv (μ.prod ρ) (ν.prod ρ) = chiSqDiv μ ν` (probs, `μ≪ν`, `hint` for `μ,ν`) | **Ancillary factor invariance**: a common product factor `ρ` cancels (`1 + χ²(ρ‖ρ) = 1`). The formal "an ancillary observation carries no information"; the bridge from finite-cell to continuous-`X` (§13sa.6). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.5b. `Stat/Minimax/ChiSquaredFinite.lean` — finite / product specializations of χ²

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `rnDeriv_mul_measure_singleton` | `ν {x} · (dμ/dν)(x) = μ {x}` (`μ≪ν`, measurable singletons) | **Discrete Radon–Nikodym bridge** (a genuine Mathlib gap): pointwise density inversion on atoms, via `setLIntegral_rnDeriv` + `restrict_singleton`/`lintegral_dirac'`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `finite_one_add_chiSqDiv` | `1 + chiSqDiv μ ν = ∑ x, (μ.real {x})² / (ν.real {x})` (finite `Ω`, probs, `μ≪ν`) | **Finite χ² formula**: turns χ² into a finite sum of squared point-mass ratios (`chiSqDiv_eq` + `integral_fintype` + the bridge). The enabling step for the Ingster bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_real_singleton` | `(Measure.pi μ).real {ω} = ∏ i, (μ i).real {ω i}` (finite index, probs) | **Product point mass** (real-valued): `{ω} = univ.pi {ωᵢ}` + `Measure.pi_pi` + `ENNReal.toReal_prod`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.6. `Stat/Minimax/MinimaxRisk.lean` — real-functional / n-sample packaging

Causal-agnostic specialization of Le Cam to a real-valued functional estimated from `n` i.i.d. samples — the shape consumed by concrete minimax lower bounds (the causal structure-agnostic ATE theorem lives in `Estimation/`, not here).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `real_two_point_lower_bound` | `2s ≤ \|θ₀−θ₁\|` ⇒ `(1−tvDist P₀ P₁)/2 ≤ max (P₀.real{s≤\|est−θ₀\|}) (P₁.real{s≤\|est−θ₁\|})` | Le Cam at `Θ=ℝ` (`dist a b = \|a−b\|`). **Proved.** |
| `two_point_lower_bound_of_tvDist_le` | same with explicit `tvDist P₀ P₁ ≤ c` ⇒ bound `(1−c)/2` | Convenience for plugging a TV upper bound. **Proved.** |
| `iid_two_point_lower_bound` | for laws `P₀,P₁` on `S`, functional `τ`, `n` samples (`Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ↦ Pⱼ)`), `2s ≤ \|τ P₀ − τ P₁\|` ⇒ worst-case miss prob `≥ ½(1 − tvDist)` between the `n`-fold laws | **The n-sample minimax form.** **Proved.** |
| `two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le` | `chiSqDiv P₀ P₁ ≤ c` (+ `≪`, integrability) ⇒ worst-case miss prob `≥ (1 − ½√c)/2` | **χ²-form bound** (via `tvDist ≤ ½√χ²`); the directly-certifiable form since χ² is computable and tensorizes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.7. `Stat/Minimax/Fano.lean` — multiple-hypothesis (M-ary) lower bound

Generalizes Le Cam from *two* hypotheses to *many* pairwise-`2s`-separated ones — the testing/TV form of Fano's lemma (Tsybakov §2.6, Le Cam–Birgé). An estimator induces disjoint acceptance regions `A i = {ω | dist(est ω, θ i) < s}`; comparing each `Pᵢ(A i)` to a *reference* hypothesis `i₀` costs one `tvDist`, bounding the total correct-recovery mass. Proven **unconditionally** from `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` — no entropy/mutual-information machinery; divergence enters only through `tvDist` (bound it by KL/χ² via §12w.3/§12w.5). Uses a reference index `i₀` rather than the centroid mixture, so it does **not** depend on `Mixture.lean` (the symmetric mixture-centroid form is a strict sharpening that can be layered on).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `acceptanceRegion est θ s` | `{ω \| dist (est ω) θ < s}` | Points the estimator places strictly within `s` of `θ`. |
| `measurableSet_acceptanceRegion` | `MeasurableSet (acceptanceRegion est θ s)` | Complement of the (closed) error region. **Proved.** |
| `acceptanceRegion_disjoint` / `_pairwiseDisjoint` | `2s ≤ dist θ₀ θ₁ ⇒ Disjoint (A θ₀) (A θ₁)`; pairwise version over a family | A point within `s` of both targets would force them within `2s`. **Proved.** |
| `sum_refReal_acceptance_le` | `∑ᵢ (P i₀).real (A i) ≤ 1` | Reference mass over disjoint regions is `≤ 1` (finite additivity + `≤ univ`). **Proved.** |
| `sum_correct_le` | `∑ᵢ (P i).real (A i) ≤ 1 + ∑ᵢ tvDist (P i₀) (P i)` | **Heart of Fano**: each `Pᵢ(A i)` vs reference costs one `tvDist`. **Proved.** |
| `fano_average_error` | `1 − (1 + ∑ᵢ tvDist (P i₀) (P i))/N ≤ (∑ᵢ (P i).real {s ≤ dist(est,θ i)})/N` (`N = card ι`) | **Average-error minimax lower bound** over the family. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `fano_exists_error` | under `tvDist (P i₀) (P i) ≤ β`: `∃ i, 1 − 1/N − β ≤ (P i).real {s ≤ dist(est,θ i)}` | **Usable form**: large `N` + small `β` ⇒ some hypothesis forces error. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.8. `Stat/Minimax/Assouad.lean` — hypercube lower bound (Assouad's lemma)

Packs a `d`-cube `{P τ : τ ∈ (Fin d → Bool)}` in which each coordinate is an independent two-point test — converting a `d`-fold product into a `d`-fold *sum* of testing bounds (rates that grow with dimension). A cube estimator `est : Ω → (Fin d → Bool)` decodes each bit; its expected **Hamming risk** counts mis-decoded coordinates. Proof = coordinate-flip averaging: pairing each vertex `τ` with its `j`-flip via an involution (`flipPerm`), the two coordinate-`j` masses are `(P τ).real S + (P (flip j τ)).real Sᶜ`, lower-bounded by `1 − tvDist` via `one_sub_tvDist_le_test`. **Unconditional**, no product-measure machinery.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `flipBit j τ` / `flipPerm j` | `Function.update τ j (!τ j)`; as an `Equiv.Perm` | Flip the `j`-th cube coordinate (an involution; the perm reindexes cube-sums). **Proved involutive.** |
| `hammingRisk P est τ` | `∑ⱼ (P τ).real {ω \| est ω j ≠ τ j}` | Expected number of coordinates the estimator decodes incorrectly at vertex `τ`. |
| `measurableSet_decode_ne` | `MeasurableSet {ω \| est ω j ≠ b}` from `MeasurableSet {ω \| est ω j = b}` | Decoding-error sets are complements of level sets. **Proved.** |
| `decode_ne_flip_compl` | `{ω \| est ω j ≠ (flip j τ) j} = {ω \| est ω j ≠ τ j}ᶜ` | The `j`-th error set at the flipped vertex is the complement at `τ` (`a ≠ !b ↔ a = b`). **Proved.** |
| `hammingRisk_pair_ge` | `1 − tvDist (P τ) (P (flip j τ)) ≤ (P τ).real Sⱼτ + (P (flip j τ)).real Sⱼ(flip)` | **Per-vertex pair bound** = two-point testing bound on the `j`-th decoded bit. **Proved.** |
| `sum_decode_ge` | `∑_τ (1 − tvDist (P τ)(P (flip j τ))) ≤ 2·∑_τ (P τ).real {est j ≠ τ j}` | Per-coordinate cube-sum bound via the flip involution (`Equiv.sum_comp`). **Proved.** |
| `assouad_average` | under `tvDist (P τ)(P (flip j τ)) ≤ β`: `(d/2)(1 − β) ≤ (∑_τ hammingRisk P est τ)/card` | **Assouad (average form).** Large `d` + small `β` ⇒ many mis-decoded coordinates. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `assouad_exists` | same hypotheses: `∃ τ, (d/2)(1 − β) ≤ hammingRisk P est τ` | **Existence form**: some vertex forces large Hamming risk. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.9. `Stat/Minimax/Mixture.lean` — finite mixtures of measures

Generic finite mixture `∑ᵢ wᵢ • Pᵢ` and the **domination lemma** behind the fuzzy-hypothesis / second-moment method: a mixture over in-class hypotheses with a common functional value never assigns more mass than the worst single hypothesis, so an averaged risk is `≤` the supremum. Consumed by the structure-agnostic ATE lower bound (§13sa) for the `dominated` (realizability) obligation of `TwoPointWitness`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mixture w P` | `∑ i, w i • P i` (`w : Fin K → ℝ≥0∞`) | Finite mixture of measures with `ℝ≥0∞` weights. |
| `mixture_apply` | `mixture w P A = ∑ i, w i · P i A` | Mass on a set is the weighted sum of the parts' masses. **Proved.** |
| `mixture_isProbabilityMeasure` | `∑ i, w i = 1 ⇒ IsProbabilityMeasure (mixture w P)` (parts prob) | A convex combination of probability measures is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `mixtureReal_le` | `∑ i, w i = 1, (∀ i, (P i).real A ≤ B) ⇒ (mixture w P).real A ≤ B` (parts prob) | **Domination**: a weighted average of per-part masses is `≤` a common bound. The averaging step in the fuzzy-hypothesis reduction. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `exists_real_ge_mixture` | `∑ i, w i = 1 ⇒ ∃ i, (mixture w P).real A ≤ (P i).real A` (parts prob, `[Nonempty ι]`) | **Witness extraction**: a finite average is `≤` its maximal component, so some part carries at least the mixture's mass. Pulls a single hard DGP out of a mixture hypothesis. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

## 12ep. `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/` — Uniform LLN (Glivenko–Cantelli) & extremum-estimator consistency

Empirical-process layer written for the econometrics workflow (van der Vaart
1998, Ch. 19; Newey & McFadden 1994, §2). A *function class* is an indexed family
`f : ι → X → ℝ`; the empirical process measures how far the sample means `Pₙ f_i`
sit from the population means `P f_i = ∫ f_i dP`, **uniformly over the class**.
This is the uniform law of large numbers that the M-estimation CLT stack
(`Stat/MEstimation/`) previously had to assume. All declarations **proved,
sorry-free**.

### 12ep.a `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Basic.lean` — vocabulary

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalProcess f i n` | `def`, `Ω → ℝ` | The centred-scaled process `√n · (Pₙ f_i − P f_i)` at class member `i` — the object whose weak limit (a Gaussian process) is the subject of Donsker theory. |
| `IIDSample.supDeviation f n` | `def`, `Ω → ℝ` | Finite-class sup deviation `⨆ i, \|Pₙ f_i − P f_i\|` (meaningful as a statistic when `ι` is finite). |
| `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | `def`, `Prop` | **(Weak) uniform LLN**: for every `ε > 0`, `μ {ω | ∃ i, ε ≤ \|Pₙ f_i − P f_i\|} → 0`. Stated existentially (not via `⨆`) so it is meaningful for infinite/uncountable classes without lattice-junk artefacts. |
| `L1Bracketing f P ε` | `structure` | A finite `L¹(P)` `ε`-bracketing: `m` brackets `[lo j, hi j]` (`j : Fin m`) with integrable endpoints, `P`-mean width `∫ (hi j − lo j) dP ≤ ε`, and an `assign : ι → Fin m` sandwiching each member `lo (assign i) ≤ f i ≤ hi (assign i)`. |
| `HasL1Bracketing f P` | `def`, `Prop` | Finite `L¹(P)` bracketing numbers: an `ε`-bracketing exists for every `ε > 0`. The classical sufficient condition for Glivenko–Cantelli. |

### 12ep.b `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/GlivenkoCantelli.lean` — uniform LLN theorems

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_fintype` | `[Finite ι]`, each `f i` measurable & `P`-integrable ⇒ `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A **finite** class of integrable functions is Glivenko–Cantelli. Union bound over coordinates, each vanishing by the pointwise WLLN (`IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb`). **Proved.** |
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` | each `f i` measurable, `HasL1Bracketing f P` ⇒ `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A class with **finite `L¹(P)` bracketing numbers** is Glivenko–Cantelli (van der Vaart 1998, Thm 19.4; Newey–McFadden Lemma 2.4). Each member is sandwiched in a bracket of mean-width `≤ ε/2`, so its deviation is controlled by the `2m` bracket-endpoint deviations plus `ε/2`; the endpoints vanish by the finite case. **Proved.** |

Private helpers: `tendsto_measure_zero_of_le` (measure squeeze), `integrable_pullback` (transfer `P`-integrability to `μ`), `dev_tendsto_zero` (single-statistic WLLN rephrased from the `edist`/`ℝ≥0∞` `TendstoInMeasure` to the real `\|·\|` event), `sampleMean_mono`.

### 12ep.c `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` — extremum-estimator consistency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` | `GlivenkoCantelli S m` + sample-argmax domination over `θ₀` + well-separated population max ⇒ `∀ ε>0, μ {ω | ε ≤ dist (θ̂ₙ ω) θ₀} → 0` | **Consistency of extremum / M-estimators** (Newey–McFadden 1994, Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998, Thm 5.7). `θ̂ₙ` maximizing the sample criterion `θ ↦ Pₙ m(θ,·)` is consistent for the population maximizer `θ₀`. **Consumes** the `GlivenkoCantelli` engine: the uniform-convergence hypothesis is exactly that predicate. Proof is the classical `M(θ₀) − M(θ̂ₙ) ≤ 2·sup_θ\|Mₙ−M\|` sandwich versus the separation gap `η`. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_bracketing` | finite `L¹(P)` bracketing of `m` + argmax domination + well-separated max ⇒ consistency | **Bracketing headline corollary**: composes `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` with the consistency theorem, so a verifiable bracketing condition (instead of an assumed uniform LLN) yields consistency directly. **Proved, sorry-free.** |

### 12ep.d `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/` — stochastic equicontinuity & the `StochEquicontAt` reduction

The empirical-process / Donsker layer at the **parametric** scale, discharging the
`StochEquicontAt` hypothesis consumed by `zEstimator_clt` and
`gmm_asymptotically_linear`. **Key structural finding:** `StochEquicontAt`
(fixed `δ`, inner limit `0`, gap evaluated at the *random* estimator `θn`) is only
true when `θn →_p θ₀` — it secretly bundles consistency. The honest decomposition
is **class-level asymptotic equicontinuity (`AsymptoticEquicont`, independent of
`θn`) + consistency ⟹ `StochEquicontAt`**. The uniform-in-`n` control of the
empirical process comes from the variance identity `Var(√n(Pₙ−P)f) = Var_P f` via
Chebyshev — no CLT and no chaining for the base cases. All declarations below are
**proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**. `E` is a finite-dimensional real inner-product
space (the `Z`-estimator parameter/value space).

`Process.lean` — vocabulary:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empProcVec S f n` | `def`, `Ω → E` | **Vector-valued centered empirical process** `Gₙ(f)(ω) = (√n)⁻¹ • Σ_{i<n} f(Zᵢ ω) − √n • ∫ f dP`; the `E`-valued analogue of `empiricalProcess`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcVec_eq_stochEquicont_gap` | `rfl` bridge | `empProcVec` of the score difference `ψ(θ,·)−ψ(θ₀,·)` equals the centered gap `R_n` of `StochEquicontAt`. |

`SecondMoment.lean` — the uniform-in-`n` Chebyshev workhorse:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_empProcVec` | `Measurable (fun ω => empProcVec f n ω)` | Measurability of the process in `ω`. |
| `empProcVec_sq_lintegral_le` | `f ∈ L²(P)` ⇒ `∫⁻ ‖Gₙ(f)‖² dμ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal (∫ ‖f‖² dP)` | **Second-moment / variance identity at the empirical scale**, uniformly in `n`. Proved coordinate-wise: Parseval (`OrthonormalBasis.sum_sq_inner_right`) reduces `‖Gₙ(f)‖²` to a sum of scalar centered i.i.d. sums, each bounded by `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. No CLT, no chaining. |
| `empProcVec_chebyshev` | `0<ε` ⇒ `μ {‖Gₙ(f)‖ > ε} ≤ ENNReal.ofReal (∫ ‖f‖² dP) / ENNReal.ofReal (ε²)` | **Chebyshev bound** (second moment + Markov, `meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`). The uniform-in-`n` modulus that drives equicontinuity: as `‖f‖_{L²(P)}` shrinks, the tail shrinks at a rate independent of sample size. |

`Modulus.lean` — the class-level predicate and the reduction:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AsymptoticEquicont ψ θ₀ P μ S` | `def`, `Prop` | **Class-level asymptotic equicontinuity** (the Donsker content, independent of any estimator): `∀ ε>0, ∀ η>0, ∃ δ>0, ∀ᶠ n, μ {ω | ∃ θ, ‖θ−θ₀‖<δ ∧ ε < ‖Gₙ(ψ(θ,·)−ψ(θ₀,·))(ω)‖} ≤ η`. Existential over `θ` (not a non-measurable `sup`) so outer-measure bookkeeping is automatic. |
| `empProcVec_atEstimator_tendsto_zero` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `θn →_p θ₀` ⇒ `Gₙ(ψ(θn,·)−ψ(θ₀,·)) →_p 0` (unconditional) | The empirical process at the estimator vanishes — textbook union bound (split on `{‖θn−θ₀‖≥δ}` via consistency; on the complement the gap is witnessed by `θ=θn`). |
| `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `θn →_p θ₀` ⇒ `StochEquicontAt ψ θ₀ P μ S θn` | **The reduction.** Discharges the estimator-indexed `StochEquicontAt` hypothesis of `zEstimator_clt`/`gmm_asymptotically_linear` from the clean class-level Donsker property plus consistency. |
| `empProcVec_isLittleOp_of_L2` | deterministic `θn` with `∫ ‖ψ(θn,·)−ψ(θ₀,·)‖² dP → 0` ⇒ gap is `o_p(1)` | **Deterministic-curve equicontinuity** (non-vacuousness witness): direct from `empProcVec_chebyshev`, no chaining, no consistency. The base case certifying the `AsymptoticEquicont` machinery is satisfiable. |

`MaximalInequality.lean` — sub-exponential maximal inequality (chaining primitive):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_abs_ge_le` | `μ.real {ε ≤ \|X\|} ≤ 2 exp(−ε²/(2(v+bε)))` | Two-sided sub-exponential tail, from the one-sided `measure_ge_le` applied to `X` and `−X`. |
| `measure_exists_abs_ge_le` | finite family ⇒ `μ.real {∃ i∈t, ε ≤ \|Yᵢ\|} ≤ t.card · 2 exp(−ε²/(2(v+bε)))` | **Maximal inequality** for the max of finitely many mean-zero sub-exponential variables (union bound). The reusable primitive a bracketing/chaining maximal inequality is built from. |
| `measure_sup'_ge_le` | same bound for `μ.real {ε ≤ t.sup' (fun i => \|Yᵢ\|)}` | `sup'` form of the maximal inequality. |

**Remaining frontier.** Discharging `AsymptoticEquicont` for an *infinite* class
over a continuum of `θ` (genuine bracketing-entropy / Ossiander chaining, van der
Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.34) is the real Mathlib gap — the `√n` bracketing bias is
intrinsic and cannot be tamed without chaining (finite classes are trivially
equicontinuous). `MaximalInequality.lean` provides the single-scale primitive for
that future chaining argument.

### 12ep.e `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean` — conditional / cross-fit `O_p` rate

Paper-agnostic workhorse behind every cross-fit / DML "remainder-is-negligible"
step: the centered empirical mean of a (possibly cross-fit / conditional) score is
`O_p(n^{-1/2} · (second-moment)^{1/2})`, stated over an arbitrary score and
σ-field. Reuses the existing `O_p` predicate `Causalean.Stat.IsBigOp` (no second
predicate) and the conditional second-moment engine
`Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. The Markov "L²-envelope ⇒ `O_p`"
step (previously inlined privately in `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`)
is extracted here as the reusable primitive. All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.mono_rate` | `0 ≤ rₙ ≤ sₙ`, `O_p(rₙ)` ⇒ `O_p(sₙ)` | **Weaken to a larger rate** — a larger envelope is a weaker statement. |
| `IsBigOp.scale_rate` | `c>0`, `O_p(c·rₙ)` ⇒ `O_p(rₙ)` | **Absorb a positive constant rate factor** into the witness `M`. |
| `IsBigOp.const_rate_collapse` | `0 ≤ N`, `O_p(fun _ => N)` ⇒ `O_p(fun _ => 1)` | **Collapse a fixed constant rate to the unit rate** (normalizes fold-sum `O_p(1)` conclusions). |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul` | `O_p(rₙ)` ⇒ `c·Xₙ = O_p(rₙ)` | **Constant multiple** of the random sequence keeps the rate. |
| `IsBigOp.add'` | `O_p(rₙ) + O_p(sₙ) = O_p(rₙ+sₙ)` (nonneg rates) | **Additivity at the sum rate** (`IsBigOp.add` is the `rₙ=sₙ` special case). |
| `IsBigOp.of_abs_le` | pointwise `\|Xₙ\| ≤ \|Yₙ\|`, `Yₙ = O_p(rₙ)` ⇒ `Xₙ = O_p(rₙ)` | **Domination rule** for stochastic big-O. |
| `IsBigOp.zero` | `0 = O_p(rₙ)` | Constant-zero sequence is stochastically bounded at any rate. |
| `IsBigOp.finset_sum` | finite sum of terms each `O_p(rₙ)` ⇒ sum is `O_p(rₙ)` | Finite-sum bookkeeping for stochastic big-O; constants absorb the finite index set. |
| `IsBigOp.mul` | `0≤rₙ`, `0≤sₙ`, `Xₙ=O_p(rₙ)`, `Yₙ=O_p(sₙ)` ⇒ `XₙYₙ=O_p(rₙsₙ)` | **Product rule** for stochastic big-O at two nonnegative rates. |
| `IsBigOp.of_sq_lintegral_le` | `Xₙ` a.e.-meas., `0 ≤ Vₙ`, `∫⁻ (Xₙ)² dμ ≤ Vₙ` ⇒ `Xₙ = O_p(√Vₙ)` | **The Markov primitive.** A deterministic second-moment envelope yields the matching `O_p` rate (Chebyshev: `μ{M√Vₙ<\|Xₙ\|} ≤ 1/M²`). The reusable core every cross-fit rate proof consumes. |
| `isBigOp_centered_crossFit_sum` | eval set `s n` ⊥ sub-σ-field `m_A n`, conditionally iid; score `g n` `(m_A n ⊗ σ_X)`-measurable & `L²(P)`; deterministic `Vₙ ≥ ∫_Ω ‖g n ω‖²_{L²(P)} dμ` ⇒ centered fold sum `= O_p(√Vₙ)` | **Lemma B — the conditional / cross-fit lift** (the deliverable). `∫ g n ω dP = 𝔼[g\|m_A n](ω)` is the conditional mean (eval fold ⊥ `m_A n`), so the sum is `√\|s n\|·(ℙₙg − 𝔼[g\|m_A n])`. Second moment via the engine, rate via `of_sq_lintegral_le`. Estimand-agnostic; weak-overlap clipped-AIPW is the first consumer. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_sq_lintegral_le` | iid sample, `f ∈ L²(P)`, `0<n` ⇒ `∫⁻ (ℙₙf − ∫f dP)² dμ ≤ E_P[f²]/n` | **Lemma A second-moment bound.** `(1/√n)Σ(f(Zᵢ)−m) = √n(ℙₙf−m)`, so the engine bound divides by `n`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_meas_ge_le` | `0<n`, `t>0` ⇒ `μ{t ≤ \|ℙₙf − ∫f dP\|} ≤ E_P[f²]/(n·t²)` | **Chebyshev tail** for the centered sample mean (second-moment bound + Markov). |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` | iid sample, `f ∈ L²(P)` ⇒ `(ℙₙf − ∫f dP) = O_p(√(E_P[f²]/n))` | **Lemma A — the unconditional `O_p` rate.** Feeding the centered statistic `f−∫f` (whose `E_P[(·)²]=Var_P f`) gives the sharp `O_p(√(Var_P f/n))` form. The `(ℙₙ−P)φ^bd` consumer. |

## 12x. `Stat/UStatistic/` — U-statistics: Hoeffding decomposition, exact variance, and the CLT

The reusable statistical primitive behind variance estimators, rank/sign statistics,
and the higher-order influence-function estimators (§HOIF). The development has two
layers with a one-way bridge:

- **Order-`m` (the general theory).** `OrderM/` builds the fixed-order U-statistic on
  ordered injective `m`-tuples, its Hájek expansion, the exact order-`m` second moment,
  and — the **headline** — the fixed-order central limit theorem
  `uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` (`OrderM/CLT.lean`): a regular order-`m` U-statistic
  is `√n`-asymptotically normal with the Hájek variance `∫ ψ² dP`. Its one genuinely
  probabilistic input, negligibility of the higher-order remainder, is discharged from
  a first-order-degeneracy `L² ≤ C/n` keystone (`OrderM/FirstDegenKernel.lean` →
  `OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` → `OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean`).
- **Order-2 (a thin `m = 2` face).** `Basic.lean`/`Variance.lean` keep the natural
  pairwise-kernel API (`h : X → X → ℝ`, off-diagonal `uStatistic`, `DegenKernel`, `ζ`)
  that the HOIF estimators consume. The order-2 *variance* now routes through the
  order-`m` `ExactVariance` internally (via `pairKernel`); the order-2 CLT is recovered
  as the `m = 2` specialization `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM`
  (`OrderM/OrderTwo.lean`).

_Per-declaration tables below are generated from docstrings — do not hand-edit; run `npm run doc:gen`. Edit the Lean docstring to change a description._

### 12x.1. `Stat/UStatistic/Basic.lean` — order-2 Hoeffding decomposition

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Basic -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatistic` | `(X → X → ℝ) → ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | The order-2 U-statistic with kernel `h` over the first `n` sample points: `(n(n−1))⁻¹ Σ_{(i,j) ∈ offDiag (range n)} h(Zᵢ, Zⱼ)`. The off-diagonal sum ranges over ordered pairs `i ≠ j`; for a symmetric kernel this is the usual `(n choose 2)⁻¹ Σ_{i<j}`. |
| `Stat.uMean` | `(X → X → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → ℝ` | Population mean of the kernel: `θ = ∬ h dP dP`. |
| `Stat.uProj` | `(X → X → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → X → ℝ` | First Hoeffding projection (centered): `h₁(x) = ∫ h(x, ·) dP − θ`. |
| `Stat.uDegen` | `(X → X → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → X → X → ℝ` | Degenerate second-order kernel: `g(x, y) = h(x, y) − θ − h₁(x) − h₁(y)`. |
| `Stat.hoeffding_decomp` | `h x y = Causalean.Stat.uMean h P + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P y + Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y` | Hoeffding decomposition (pointwise identity). `h(x, y) = θ + h₁(x) + h₁(y) + g(x, y)`. Purely algebraic. |
| `Stat.uProj_integral_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (y : X), h x y ∂P) P → ∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x ∂P = 0` | The first projection integrates to zero: `∫ h₁ dP = 0`. |
| `Stat.uProj_integrable` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (y : X), h x y ∂P) P → MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProj h P) P` | The first projection is integrable whenever `x ↦ ∫ h(x, ·) dP` is. |
| `Stat.uDegen_integral_right_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (y : X), h x y ∂P) P → ∀ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P → ∫ (y : X), Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y ∂P = 0` | Degeneracy of the second-order kernel. For every fixed `x`, `∫ g(x, ·) dP = 0`. This is the defining property that makes the U-statistic's quadratic remainder negligible at the `√n` scale. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.2. `Stat/UStatistic/Hajek.lean` — generic `o_p` transfer helper

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Hajek -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.isLittleOp_of_eventuallyEq` | `Causalean.Stat.IsLittleOp g r μ → (∀ᶠ (n : ℕ) in Filter.atTop, f n = g n) → Causalean.Stat.IsLittleOp f r μ` | Transfer `o_p` along an eventual pointwise equality of the sequences. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.3. `Stat/UStatistic/Variance.lean` — order-2 exact variance (thin `m = 2` face for HOIF)

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Variance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.DegenKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X → (X → X → ℝ) → Prop` | Bundled hypotheses on a degenerate order-2 kernel `g`. |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.integrable` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => g p.1 p.2) (P.prod P)` | `g` is L¹ on the product law (probability space, from L²). |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.deg'` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → ∀ (y : X), ∫ (x : X), g x y ∂P = 0` | Left degeneracy, from symmetry and right degeneracy. |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.toOrderDegenKernel` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel g)` | Bridge to the order-`m` theory (`m = 2`). A degenerate order-2 kernel `g` gives a completely-degenerate order-`2` kernel `pairKernel g` (`z ↦ g (z 0) (z 1)`): symmetry of `pairKernel g` under `Equiv.Perm (Fin 2)` is `hg.symm`; the single-coordinate integrals are `hg.deg` / `hg.deg'`; square-integrability transports across the `Fin 2 → X` ≃ `X × X` product-law equivalence. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zeta` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X → (X → X → ℝ) → ℝ` | `ζ = ∬ g² dP dP`, the second moment of the kernel. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zeta_eq_zetaOrder` | `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel g)` | The order-2 second moment `ζ` equals the order-`m` second moment `ζ_m` of the paired kernel: `∬ g² dP dP = ∫ (pairKernel g)² dP²`. A change of variables along the `Fin 2 → X` ≃ `X × X` measure equivalence. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zeta_nonneg` | `0 ≤ Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g` | `ζ ≥ 0`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_offDiag_sum_sq` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), ∫ (ω : Ω), (∑ p ∈ (Finset.range n).offDiag, g (S.Z p.1 ω) (S.Z p.2 ω)) ^ 2 ∂μ = 2 * ↑(Finset.range n).offDiag.card * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g` | Second moment of the off-diagonal sum. `E[(Σ_{p ∈ offDiag} g(Z_{p.1},Z_{p.2}))²] = 2 · \|offDiag\| · ζ`. The `m = 2` case of `integral_injectiveTuples_sum_sq_degen`, using `sum_injectiveTuples_two_eq_offDiag`, `toOrderDegenKernel`, and `zeta_eq_zetaOrder` (`2! = 2`, `injectiveTupleCount 2 n = \|offDiag\| = n(n−1)`). |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_sq` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ}, 2 ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), (√↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n ω) ^ 2 ∂μ = 2 * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g / (↑n - 1)` | L² bound on the rescaled degenerate U-statistic. For `n ≥ 2`, `E[(√n · Gₙ)²] = 2ζ / (n−1)`. The `m = 2` case of `integral_rescaled_order_sq_degen` (`n · 2! · ζ / n^{(2)} = 2ζ/(n−1)`), via `uStatisticOrder_two_eq_uStatistic` and `zeta_eq_zetaOrder`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_rescaled` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n ω) 2 μ` | The rescaled degenerate U-statistic is in `L²`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | The rescaled degenerate U-statistic has mean zero. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.4. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Basic.lean` — fixed-order U-statistic on injective tuples

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.Basic -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.injectiveTuples` | `(m n : ℕ) → Finset (Fin m → Fin n)` | Ordered injective `m`-tuples from the first `n` sample indices. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount` | `ℕ → ℕ → ℝ` | The number of ordered injective `m`-tuples from the first `n` sample indices. |
| `Stat.injectiveSubtypeEquivEmbedding` | `(m n : ℕ) → { t // Function.Injective t } ≃ (Fin m ↪ Fin n)` | Injective functions `Fin m → Fin n` as elements of the finite embedding type. |
| `Stat.injectiveTuples_card_eq_descFactorial` | `(Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n).card = n.descFactorial m` | The ordered injective tuple count is the falling factorial `n (n-1) ...`. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount_eq_descFactorial` | `Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n = ↑(n.descFactorial m)` | The real-valued ordered injective tuple count is the falling factorial. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder` | `{m : ℕ} → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | The fixed-order U-statistic averages a kernel over ordered injective sample tuples from the first `n` observations. |
| `Stat.uMeanOrder` | `((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → ℝ` | Population mean of an order-`m` kernel under the product law. |
