| `Stat.insertCoord` | `(j : Fin m) → X → ({ k // k ≠ j } → X) → Fin m → X` | Insert one distinguished coordinate into the remaining coordinates. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt` | `Fin m → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → X → ℝ` | First Hoeffding projection of an order-`m` kernel, centred at its population mean. The distinguished coordinate is supplied explicitly; for symmetric kernels all choices agree. |
| `Stat.uProjOrder` | `((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → X → ℝ` | First Hoeffding projection of a positive-order kernel, using coordinate `0` as the distinguished coordinate. |
| `Stat.uDegenOrder` | `((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → (Fin m → X) → ℝ` | Higher-order residual kernel after removing the mean and all first Hoeffding projection terms. |
| `Stat.hoeffding_decomp_order` | `h z = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P + ∑ j, Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P (z j) + Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z` | Pointwise first-order Hoeffding decomposition for the residual kernel. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_integrable` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P → MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P) P` | The coordinatewise first projection is integrable whenever the corresponding slice-averaged kernel is integrable. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_integral_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P → ∫ (x : X), ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ∂P = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P → ∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x ∂P = 0` | The coordinatewise first projection integrates to zero once the slice-averaged representation of the population mean is available. The additional equality is the standard finite-product/Fubini identity for the chosen coordinate. |
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_integrable` | `(∀ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P) P) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∑ j, Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x) P` | The summed first-order influence function is integrable if every coordinatewise first projection is integrable. |
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_integral_eq_zero` | `(∀ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P) P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), ∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x ∂P = 0) → ∫ (x : X), ∑ j, Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x ∂P = 0` | The summed first-order influence function is centered if every coordinatewise first projection is centered. |
| `Stat.integral_pi_eval_eq` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable f P → (∫ (z : ι → X), f (z i) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) = ∫ (x : X), f x ∂P` | Integrating a function of one coordinate under a finite product law recovers the one-dimensional integral. |
| `Stat.uDegenOrder_integral_tail_eq_zero` | `(∀ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), ∫ (x : X), ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ∂P = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) → (∀ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) → ∀ (j : Fin m) (x : X), (∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) = 0` | The first-order Hoeffding residual has zero conditional mean in each coordinate after integrating over all other coordinates, provided the usual finite-product/Fubini identities and slice integrability assumptions hold. |
| `Stat.pairKernel` | `(X → X → ℝ) → (Fin 2 → X) → ℝ` | Encode a binary kernel as a kernel on `Fin 2 → X`. |
| `Stat.sum_injectiveTuples_two_eq_offDiag` | `(∑ t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples 2 n, Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) = ∑ p ∈ (Finset.range n).offDiag, h (S.Z p.1 ω) (S.Z p.2 ω)` | Ordered injective `Fin 2` tuples are the same data as off-diagonal ordered pairs. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_two_eq_uStatistic` | `Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) n = Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h n` | The order-2 fixed-order statistic agrees with the existing ordered off-diagonal U-statistic for the corresponding pair kernel. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.5. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Hajek.lean` — Hájek expansion + asymptotic linearity

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.Hajek -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder` | `((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → MeasureTheory.Measure X → X → ℝ` | The first-order influence function of an ordered fixed-order kernel: the sum of its coordinatewise first Hoeffding projections. For a symmetric kernel this is `m` times the usual first projection. |
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_eq_card_mul_of_common_projection` | `(∀ (j : Fin m) (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x = φ x) → Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P = fun x => ↑m * φ x` | If all coordinatewise first projections agree with a common projection, then the order-`m` influence function is `m` times that projection. |
| `Stat.uRemainderOrder` | `{m : ℕ} → [NeZero m] → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → ℕ → Ω → ℝ` | The higher-order remainder statistic for an order-`m` U-statistic. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X → {m : ℕ} → [NeZero m] → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → Prop` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel under product measure `P^m`. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible` | `{m : ℕ} → [NeZero m] → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → Prop` | The higher-order order-`m` Hájek remainder is negligible at the `√n` scale. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount_ne_zero` | `m ≤ n → Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n ≠ 0` | If `m ≤ n`, there is at least one ordered injective `m`-tuple in `Fin n`. |
| `Stat.injectiveTuples_fiber_card_eq` | `{t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| t j = y}.card = {t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| t j = y'}.card` | Fibres of a fixed coordinate map on ordered injective tuples have the same cardinality. The bijection composes tuples with the codomain transposition swapping the two fibre values. |
| `Stat.sum_injectiveTuples_apply_eq_range` | `m ≤ n → ∀ (j : Fin m) (f : ℕ → ℝ), ∑ t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, f ↑(t j) = Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n / ↑n * ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, f i` | For each coordinate of an ordered injective tuple, every sample index appears equally often. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_sub_uMean_eq` | `m ≤ n → ∀ (ω : Ω), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω - Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P = (↑n)⁻¹ * ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P (S.Z i ω) + Causalean.Stat.uRemainderOrder S h n ω` | Hájek decomposition for a fixed-order U-statistic. For positive order `m` and sample size `n ≥ m`, the centered statistic equals its linear Hájek projection plus the higher-order residual U-statistic. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_remainder_eq` | `m ≤ n → (fun ω => √↑(Finset.range n).card * (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω - Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) - (√↑(Finset.range n).card)⁻¹ * ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P (S.Z i ω)) = fun ω => √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uRemainderOrder S h n ω` | The Hájek remainder in `IsAsymLinear` form is the rescaled higher-order residual U-statistic. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_isAsymLinear` | `∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ∂P = 0 → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P → Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h → Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) S fun r => Finset.range r` | Fixed-order U-statistic asymptotic linearity. If the summed first projection is centered and square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `√n` scale, then the fixed-order U-statistic is asymptotically linear with influence function equal to the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.6. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Variance.lean` — completely-degenerate transport

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.Variance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.map_fintype_tuple_eq` | `Function.Injective r → MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun ω i => S.Z (↑(r i)) ω) μ = MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P` | The joint law of any finite collection of distinct sample coordinates is the corresponding product law. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.map_tuple_eq` | `Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun ω j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) μ = MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P` | The joint law of an injectively indexed order-`m` sample tuple is `P^m`. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel.integrable` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → MeasureTheory.Integrable g (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)` | A square-integrable order-`m` degenerate kernel is integrable under the product law. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel.integral_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel has zero product-law mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_orderKernelTerm` | `Measurable h → MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => h fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) μ` | An order-`m` kernel term along an injective tuple is integrable whenever the kernel is integrable under the product law. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_orderKernelTerm_eq` | `Measurable h → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → ∫ (ω : Ω), h fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω ∂μ = ∫ (z : Fin m → X), h z ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P` | The expectation of an injectively indexed order-`m` kernel term equals the kernel's product-law mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_orderKernelTerm_eq_zero_of_uMean_zero` | `Measurable h → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), h fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω ∂μ = 0` | An injectively indexed order-`m` kernel term has mean zero whenever the kernel has zero product-law mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_uStatisticOrder_eq_uMean` | `Measurable h → MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω ∂μ = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P` | The fixed-order U-statistic is unbiased: its expectation is the product-law kernel mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_uMean_zero` | `Measurable h → MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → m ≤ n → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω ∂μ = 0` | A fixed-order U-statistic with product-law mean zero has expectation zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_uStatisticOrder_eq_sqrt_mul_uMean` | `Measurable h → MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω ∂μ = √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P` | The rescaled fixed-order U-statistic has mean equal to the same rescaling of the product-law kernel mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_uMean_zero` | `Measurable h → MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → m ≤ n → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ω ∂μ = 0` | If an order-`m` kernel has product-law mean zero, then the rescaled fixed-order U-statistic has mean zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_degenKernel_uMean_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → m ≤ n → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel whose product-law mean is zero gives a mean-zero fixed-order U-statistic. The product-law mean-zero assumption is kept explicit here rather than inferred from coordinatewise degeneracy. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_degenKernel` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel gives a mean-zero fixed-order U-statistic in the nonempty sampling regime `m ≤ n`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_orderTerm_eq_zero_of_degenKernel_uMean_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω ∂μ = 0` | An injectively indexed fully degenerate order-`m` kernel term has mean zero when its product-law mean is zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_orderTerm_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → ∫ (ω : Ω), g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω ∂μ = 0` | An injectively indexed fully degenerate order-`m` kernel term has mean zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_degenKernel_uMean_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → m ≤ n → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0 → ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel whose product-law mean is zero gives a mean-zero rescaled fixed-order U-statistic. This is a mean statement only; it does not assert the variance bound or negligibility. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_uStatisticOrder_eq_zero_of_degenKernel` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel gives a mean-zero rescaled fixed-order U-statistic in the nonempty sampling regime `m ≤ n`. This is a mean statement only; it does not assert the variance bound or negligibility. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X → {m : ℕ} → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → ℝ` | `ζ_m = E[g(Z₁,…,Z_m)^2]`, the second moment of an order-`m` kernel under the product law `P^m`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_orderTerm` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) μ` | An order-`m` kernel term along an injective tuple is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_orderTerm_sq` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) ^ 2) μ` | The square of an order-`m` kernel term along an injective tuple is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.orderTerm_diag` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → ∫ (ω : Ω), (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) ^ 2 ∂μ = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Diagonal second moment of a single injective order-`m` kernel term. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_orderTerm` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) 2 μ` | Each injective order-`m` kernel term is in `L²`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_orderTerm_mul` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {t q : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Function.Injective q → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) * g fun j => S.Z (↑(q j)) ω) μ` | The product of two injective order-`m` kernel terms is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_injectiveTuples_sum` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => ∑ t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) μ` | The injective-tuple sum of an order-`m` degenerate kernel is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_injectiveTuples_sum` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => ∑ t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) 2 μ` | The injective-tuple sum of an order-`m` degenerate kernel is in `L²`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_rescaled_order` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω) 2 μ` | The rescaled order-`m` degenerate U-statistic is in `L²`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder_nonneg` | `0 ≤ Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | `ζ_m` is nonnegative. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.7. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/ExactVariance.lean` — exact order-`m` second moment

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.ExactVariance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zeta_of_image_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ} {t q : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Function.Injective q → Finset.image t Finset.univ = Finset.image q Finset.univ → ∫ (ω : Ω), (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) * g fun j => S.Z (↑(q j)) ω ∂μ = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Equal-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have the same image, then `q` is a reordering of `t`, so by symmetry `g(Z_q) = g(Z_t)` and the cross expectation is `ζ_m = zetaOrder P g`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_image_ne` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ} {t q : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Function.Injective q → Finset.image t Finset.univ ≠ Finset.image q Finset.univ → ∫ (ω : Ω), (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) * g fun j => S.Z (↑(q j)) ω ∂μ = 0` | Distinct-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have different images, complete degeneracy kills the cross expectation: some index of `t` is absent from `q`, and integrating that coordinate out gives `0`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.card_injectiveTuples_image_eq` | `Function.Injective t → {q ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| Finset.image q Finset.univ = Finset.image t Finset.univ}.card = m.factorial` | For an ordered injective `m`-tuple `t`, the ordered injective tuples with the same image are exactly its `m!` reorderings. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_injectiveTuples_sum_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ (n : ℕ), ∫ (ω : Ω), (∑ t ∈ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) ^ 2 ∂μ = ↑m.factorial * Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Exact second moment of the injective-tuple sum. `E[(Σ_{t} g(Z_t))²] = m! · n^{(m)} · ζ_m`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ}, m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), (√↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω) ^ 2 ∂μ = ↑n * ↑m.factorial * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g / Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n` | Exact variance of the rescaled degenerate fixed-order U-statistic. `E[(√n · Uₙ)²] = n · m! · ζ_m / n^{(m)}`. For `m = 2` this is `2ζ / (n−1)`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.8. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/FirstDegenKernel.lean` — first-order degenerate kernel

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.FirstDegenKernel -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X → {m : ℕ} → [NeZero m] → ((Fin m → X) → ℝ) → Prop` | A first-order degenerate order-`m` kernel: measurable, square-integrable, and mean-zero in each coordinate after integrating out the other `m − 1` coordinates. |
| `Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel.integrable` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → MeasureTheory.Integrable g (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)` | A first-order degenerate square-integrable kernel is integrable under the product law. |
| `Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel.integral_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0` | The product-law mean of a first-order degenerate kernel is zero: split off the first coordinate with `measurePreserving_piEquivPiSubtypeProd` (as in `OrderDegenKernel.integral_eq_zero`) and apply the coordinate-`0` first-projection identity `firstDeg`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_sqKernelTerm_sq` | `Measurable g → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => g z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) ^ 2) μ` | The square of an order-`m` kernel term along an injective tuple is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_sqKernelTerm` | `Measurable g → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => g z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → ∀ {t : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) 2 μ` | Each injective order-`m` kernel term is in `L²`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_sqKernelTerm_mul` | `Measurable g → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => g z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → ∀ {t q : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Function.Injective q → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ω => (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) * g fun j => S.Z (↑(q j)) ω) μ` | The product of two injective order-`m` kernel terms is integrable. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_rescaled_sqKernel` | `Measurable g → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => g z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ω => √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω) 2 μ` | The rescaled injective-tuple sum `√n · Uₙ` is in `L²`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.9. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` — keystone `L² ≤ C/n` bound

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderSecondMoment -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_shared_le_one` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ} {t q : Fin m → Fin n}, Function.Injective t → Function.Injective q → (Finset.image t Finset.univ ∩ Finset.image q Finset.univ).card ≤ 1 → ∫ (ω : Ω), (g fun j => S.Z (↑(t j)) ω) * g fun j => S.Z (↑(q j)) ω ∂μ = 0` | Cross-term vanishing. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples share at most one sample index, the expected product of the corresponding kernel terms is zero. Zero shared indices ⇒ independence + mean zero; one shared index ⇒ condition on it and use first-order degeneracy of each factor. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_le` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → ∃ C, 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ {n : ℕ}, m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), (√↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω) ^ 2 ∂μ ≤ C / ↑n` | `L²` bound on the rescaled higher-order remainder. For a first-order degenerate order-`m` kernel there is a constant `C` (depending only on `m` and `ζ_m = E[g²]`) with `E[(√n · Uₙ)²] ≤ C / n` for all `n ≥ m`. This is the keystone estimate; it packages cross-term vanishing, the Cauchy–Schwarz bound `\|E[g_t g_q]\| ≤ ζ_m`, the `O(n^{2m-2})` count of surviving tuple pairs, and the `n · (n^{(m)})⁻²` normalization. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.10. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean` — negligibility discharge

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderNegligible -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → ∀ {n : ℕ}, m ≤ n → ∫ (ω : Ω), √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω ∂μ = 0` | The rescaled higher-order remainder has mean zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.orderDegenerateNegligible_of_firstDegen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g → Causalean.Stat.IsLittleOp (fun n ω => √↑n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ω) (fun x => 1) μ` | Negligibility of the higher-order remainder. Under first-order degeneracy, `√n · Uₙ → 0` in probability, i.e. it is `o_p(1)`. Proof: `L²` boundedness (`memLp_rescaled_sqKernel`) with mean zero and variance `≤ C/n → 0` (`integral_rescaled_order_sq_le`), via Chebyshev — mirror the order-2 `degenerateNegligible_of_degenKernel`. |
| `Stat.orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), ∫ (x : X), ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ∂P = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) → (∀ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) → Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h` | The higher-order remainder of a fixed-order U-statistic is negligible. For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual `uDegenOrder h P` is measurable and square-integrable and whose slice/Fubini integrability side conditions hold, the rescaled remainder `√n · Gₙ` is `o_p(1)`. This discharges the `OrderDegenerateNegligible` hypothesis consumed by the order-`m` CLT `uStatisticOrder_clt` (`Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT`). |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.11. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/CLT.lean` — headline: fixed-order U-statistic CLT

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) → ∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ∂P = 0 → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P → Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h → ∀ (hθn_meas : ∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) μ), (∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) μ) → Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 ∂P)) μ hθn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT. If the first-order influence function is measurable, centered, square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `√n` scale, then the U-statistic is asymptotically normal with variance `∫ ψ² dP`, where `ψ` is the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) → (∀ (j : Fin m), ∫ (x : X), ∫ (tail : { k // k ≠ j } → X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ∂P = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) → (∀ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) → Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) → ∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ∂P = 0 → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P → ∀ (hθn_meas : ∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) μ), (∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) μ) → Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (∫ (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 ∂P)) μ hθn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT (end-to-end). For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual is regular (measurable, square-integrable, with the slice/Fubini side conditions) and whose summed first projection is measurable, centered, and square-integrable, the fixed-order U-statistic is `√n`-asymptotically normal with variance `∫ ψ² dP`, `ψ = Σⱼ uProjOrderAt j h P`. This composes `uStatisticOrder_clt` with `orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual`, so no negligibility hypothesis is required from the caller — the order-`m` analogue of `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.12. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/OrderTwo.lean` — `m = 2` bridge to the order-2 CLT

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.OrderTwo -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uMeanOrder_pairKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) → Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = Causalean.Stat.uMean h P` | The order-`2` population mean of `pairKernel h` is the order-2 mean of `h`. Requires product-integrability of `h` so that the joint integral over `Fin 2 → X` agrees (via Fubini) with the iterated integral defining `uMean`. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(∀ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) → (∀ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) → ∀ (j : Fin 2), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = Causalean.Stat.uProj h P` | Each coordinate first projection of `pairKernel h` equals the order-2 first projection of `h`, for a symmetric kernel. (For `j = 0` no symmetry is needed; for `j = 1` it is used to swap the integration coordinate.) |
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(∀ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) → (∀ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) → Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x` | The order-`2` influence function of `pairKernel h` is `2 · h₁`, matching the order-2 CLT influence function `ψ = 2 · uProj h P`. |
| `Stat.uDegenOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(∀ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) → (∀ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) → Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P (z 0) (z 1)` | The order-`2` degenerate residual of `pairKernel h` is the order-2 degenerate kernel `uDegen h P`, evaluated at the two coordinates. |
| `Stat.uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM` | `(∀ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) → (Measurable fun p => h p.1 p.2) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P p.1 p.2 ^ 2) (P.prod P) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ∫ (y : X), h x y ∂P) P → (∀ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) → MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x ^ 2) P → ∀ (hθn_meas : ∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) μ), (∀ (n : ℕ), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) μ) → Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) fun m => Finset.range m) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (∫ (x : X), (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) x ^ 2 ∂P)) μ hθn_meas` | Order-2 U-statistic CLT, derived from the fixed-order-`m` CLT. For a symmetric, square-integrable kernel `h` with finite first-projection variance `ζ₁ = ∫ h₁² dP`, the order-2 U-statistic is `√n`-asymptotically normal, `√n (Uₙ − θ) ⇒ N(0, ∫ (2 h₁)² dP) = N(0, 4 ζ₁)`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

## 12wo. `Stat/PolynomialTail/` — Polynomial lower-tail inverse-moment asymptotics

Causal-agnostic real-analysis / measure-theory infrastructure for the small-value
(lower-tail) asymptotics of a bounded positive random variable.  This is the moment
calculus underlying *any* inverse-weighted estimand whose weight is floored at `1/λ` —
not tied to one paper.  It specializes to (and quantitatively refines) the qualitative
`Overlap`/positivity assumption in `Identification/`: taking `U = min(e(X), 1−e(X))`
recovers the variance/rate ingredients of IPW, AIPW, and Hájek estimators under thin
overlap, trimming/truncation bias, and weak-overlap minimax rates.  Fix a probability
measure `P` and a measurable `U : Ω → ℝ` with `0 < U ≤ 1` a.s.  The two-sided
polynomial lower-tail hypothesis `PolyTail P U κ t₀ cm cp` says
`cm·tᵏ ≤ P{U ≤ t} ≤ cp·tᵏ` on `(0,t₀]` (`0 < κ`, `0 < t₀ < 1`, `0 < cm < cp`).  The
library bounds the two truncated inverse moments (`λ`-truncation, `0 < λ ≤ t₀`)

    I P U λ = ∫ U/(max U λ)²   (= 1/U on {U≥λ}, U/λ² on {U<λ}),
    J P U λ = ∫ (max U λ)⁻¹    (= 1/U on {U≥λ}, 1/λ  on {U<λ}),

and the leftover trimming weight `wλ = 1 − U/(max U λ) ∈ [0,1]` (supported on `{U<λ}`).
The key trichotomy is in the tail exponent `κ`: `λ^{κ-1}` (κ<1), `log(1/λ)` (κ=1),
bounded (κ>1).  The lower bounds genuinely use **both** tail constants (the gap
`cm < cp` breaks any one-sided argument).  Method: Mathlib's layer-cake theorem
`MeasureTheory.Integrable.integral_eq_integral_Ioc_meas_le` reduces `J` to a
threshold integral of `P{U ≤ t⁻¹}`, evaluated against `∫ t^{-κ}` (`integral_rpow` /
`integral_inv`).  All declarations **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

### 12wo.1. `Stat/PolynomialTail/Basic.lean` — setup, integrands, basic facts

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PolyTail P U κ t₀ cm cp` | `structure : Prop` | The polynomial lower-tail hypothesis: `cm·tᵏ ≤ P{U≤t} ≤ cp·tᵏ` on `(0,t₀]`, with `0<κ`, `0<t₀<1`, `0<cm<cp`. Fields `kappa_pos/t0_pos/t0_lt_one/cm_pos/cm_lt_cp/tail_lower/tail_upper`. |
| `PolyTail.cp_pos` | `0 < cp` | Upper constant positive. |
| `TailSetup P U` | `structure : Prop` | Structural hypotheses on `U`: `Measurable U`, `0 < U` a.s., `U ≤ 1` a.s. |
| `truncDen U λ ω` | `def := max (U ω) λ` | Truncated denominator. |
| `invMomentI P U λ` | `def := ∫ U/(max U λ)²` | Truncated inverse second moment. |
| `invMomentJ P U λ` | `def := ∫ (max U λ)⁻¹` | Truncated inverse first moment. |
| `trimWeight U λ ω` | `def := 1 − U/(max U λ)` | Leftover weight `∈[0,1]`, supported on `{U<λ}`. |
| `measurable_*` | measurability | The three integrands and the denominator are measurable. |
| `measureReal_lt_le` | `P{U<λ} ≤ cp·λᵏ` | Mass of the open lower level set (monotonicity + upper tail). |
| `invMomentI_integrand_mem` / `invMomentJ_integrand_mem` / `trimWeight_mem` | pointwise `∈[0,1/λ²]` / `[0,1/λ]` / `[0,1]` | Pointwise integrand sandwiches. |
| `integrable_invMomentI_integrand` / `…J…` / `integrable_trimWeight` | `Integrable … P` | Integrability (bounded a.e. on a probability space). |

### 12wo.2. `Stat/PolynomialTail/LayerCakeReduction.lean` — layer-cake reduction

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tailIntegral P U λ` | `def := ∫ t in (1,λ⁻¹], P{U ≤ t⁻¹}` | The threshold ("tail") integral. |
| `monotone_cdf` | `Monotone (s ↦ P{U≤s})` | The lower CDF is monotone. |
| `measurable_tailIntegrand`, `tailIntegrand_nonneg`, `tailIntegrand_le_one`, `integrableOn_tailIntegrand` | regularity | Measurability/`∈[0,1]`/integrability of `t ↦ P{U≤t⁻¹}`. |
| `invMomentJ_eq_one_add_tailIntegral` | `J P U λ = 1 + tailIntegral P U λ` (`0<λ<1`) | **Master layer-cake reduction.** Levels `t≤1` contribute exactly `1` (`(max U λ)⁻¹ ≥ 1` a.s.); levels `(1,λ⁻¹]` reproduce `P{U≤t⁻¹}`. |
| `tailIntegral_nonneg` | `0 ≤ tailIntegral P U λ` | Nonnegativity. |

### 12wo.3. `Stat/PolynomialTail/PowerIntegral.lean` — power-law interval integrals

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zero_notMem_uIcc` | `0 ∉ [[a,b]]` (`0<a≤b`) | Side condition for `integral_rpow`/`integral_inv`. |
| `integral_rpow_neg_Ioc` | `∫ t in (a,b], t^{-κ} = (b^{1-κ}−a^{1-κ})/(1−κ)` (`κ≠1`, `0<a≤b`) | Power integral via `integral_rpow`. |
| `integral_inv_neg_Ioc` | `∫ t in (a,b], t^{-1} = log b − log a` (`0<a≤b`) | `κ=1` case via `integral_inv`. |

### 12wo.4. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TailIntegralBounds.lean` — tail-integral sandwich

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inv_rpow_eq_rpow_neg` | `(t⁻¹)^κ = t^{-κ}` (`t>0`) | rpow identity. |
| `tailIntegrand_upper_window` / `tailIntegrand_lower_window` | `cm·t^{-κ} ≤ P{U≤t⁻¹} ≤ cp·t^{-κ}` on `(t₀⁻¹,λ⁻¹]` | Polynomial-window integrand sandwich. |
| `integrableOn_rpow_neg_window` | `IntegrableOn (t^{-κ}) (t₀⁻¹,λ⁻¹]` | Integrability of the power integrand. |
| `tailIntegral_le` | `tailIntegral ≤ (t₀⁻¹−1) + cp·∫_{(t₀⁻¹,λ⁻¹]} t^{-κ}` | **Upper** sandwich (bounded shell `(1,t₀⁻¹]` + polynomial window). |
| `tailIntegral_ge` | `cm·∫_{(t₀⁻¹,λ⁻¹]} t^{-κ} ≤ tailIntegral` | **Lower** sandwich. |

### 12wo.5. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentJBounds.lean` — three-regime bounds for `J`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `powInt_ne_one` | `∫_{(t₀⁻¹,λ⁻¹]} t^{-κ} = (λ^{κ-1}−t₀^{κ-1})/(1−κ)` (`κ≠1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `powInt_eq_one` | `… = log(1/λ) − log(1/t₀)` (`κ=1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_lt` | `∃ 0<a≤A, ∀λ∈(0,t₀], a·λ^{κ-1} ≤ J ≤ A·λ^{κ-1}` (`κ<1`) | **κ<1.** Constants depend only on `(κ,cm,cp,t₀)`: `A=t₀⁻¹+cp/(1−κ)`, `a=min(cm/(1−κ), t₀^{1−κ})`. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_eq` | `∃ 0<a≤A, a·log(1/λ) ≤ J ≤ A·log(1/λ)+A` (`κ=1`) | **κ=1.** |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_gt` | `∃ 0<a≤A, a ≤ J ≤ A` (`κ>1`) | **κ>1.** — inverse first moment is *bounded* (`a=1` since `J≥1`). |

### 12wo.6. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentIBounds.lean` — bounds for `I` + IsTheta

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `invMomentI_le_invMomentJ` | `I P U λ ≤ J P U λ` | Pointwise `U/(max U λ)² ≤ (max U λ)⁻¹`. Gives the **upper** half of the `I` bounds from the `J` bounds. |
| `invMomentJ_sub_invMomentI_le` | `J P U λ − I P U λ ≤ cp·λ^{κ-1}` | Gap controlled by tail mass: pointwise `= wλ/(max U λ) ≤ λ⁻¹·wλ`, and `∫wλ ≤ P{U<λ} ≤ cp·λᵏ`. The bridge for the `κ=1` `I`-lower bound. |
| `invMomentI_antitone` | `I P U λ₂ ≤ I P U λ₁` (`λ₁≤λ₂`) | `I` decreases as `λ` grows (`max U λ` grows). Bridge for the `κ>1` constant `I`-floor. |
| `invMomentI_ge_shell` | `(cm/2)·(cm/(2cp))^{1/κ}·λ^{κ-1} ≤ I P U λ` | **Shell lower bound.** The shell `(ρλ,λ]`, `ρ=(cm/(2cp))^{1/κ}`, has mass `≥(cm/2)λᵏ` (uses **both** tails) on which `U/λ² ≥ ρ/λ`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_lt` | `∃ 0<a≤A, ∀λ∈(0,t₀], a·λ^{κ-1} ≤ I ≤ A·λ^{κ-1}` (`κ<1`) | Lower = shell, upper = `I ≤ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_eq` | `∃ 0<a≤A, a·log(1/λ) ≤ I ≤ A·log(1/λ)+A` (`κ=1`) | Lower = `I ≥ J − cp` (relation) uniformized with the shell floor; upper = `I ≤ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_gt` | `∃ 0<a≤A, a ≤ I ≤ A` (`κ>1`) | Bounded inverse second moment: lower `I(λ)≥I(t₀)≥` shell-at-`t₀` (antitone); upper = `I ≤ J`. |
| `isTheta_of_two_sided` | two-sided bound ⟹ `f =Θ[𝓝[>]0] g` | Generic `IsTheta` packager. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_lt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_lt` | `J ≍ λ^{κ-1}`, `I ≍ λ^{κ-1}` (`κ<1`) | **IsTheta** on `𝓝[>] 0`. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_gt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_gt` | `J ≍ 1`, `I ≍ 1` (`κ>1`) | **IsTheta** (bounded regime). |

### 12wo.7. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TrimBias.lean` — general bias bound

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `trimWeight_bias_bound` | `\|∫ wλ·g ∂P\| ≤ Cβ·cp·λ^{κ+β}` | For `g` with Hölder envelope `\|g\|≤Cβ·λ^β` on `{U<λ}`: tail-mass (`≤cp·λᵏ`) × envelope. Elementary (no layer cake). |

## 12'. `Stat/Concentration/` — Vendored concentration toolbox (Phase-4a)

Selectively vendored from
[auto-res/lean-rademacher](https://github.com/auto-res/lean-rademacher)
(commit `72d28921dc960f47691640fb973303a1be9d13ca`, MIT License). Audit
trail and porting policy live in
[Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md](../Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md).
Phase 4a covers definitions and the headline statements only; lower-level
proof chains are scaffolded with `sorry` and filled by Codex in a
separate pass.

#### 12'.a `Stat/Concentration/Rademacher.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Signs n` | `def`, `Type` | `{−1, +1}`-valued sign vectors of length `n`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity n f S` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `(1 / 2ⁿ) Σ_σ supᵢ |(1/n) Σₖ σ k · f i (S k)|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity n f μ X` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Population version: `E[empiricalRademacherComplexity n f (X ∘ ω)]` under `ω ∼ μⁿ`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_without_abs` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Variant without the outer absolute value (used in some symmetrization steps). |
| `uniformDeviation n f μ X S` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `supᵢ |(1/n) Σₖ f i (S k) − E[f i ∘ X]|`. |
| `uniformDeviation_measurable` | `theorem` | Measurability of `uniformDeviation` for countable function classes with each `f i` measurable. **Proved.** |

#### 12'.a' `Stat/Concentration/ERMOracle.lean` — generic ERM oracle inequality

The method-agnostic learning-theory rate: empirical risk minimization over a countable
hypothesis class with bounded loss has excess risk controlled by the Rademacher complexity of
the loss class plus a McDiarmid tail. The engine that turns a complexity bound for a specific
method's class into a concrete excess-risk rate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `erm_excess_le_two_uniformDeviation` | `theorem` | **Deterministic ERM basic inequality:** an empirical minimizer beating a comparator on the sample has excess population risk `≤ 2·uniformDeviation`. **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality` | `theorem` | **Oracle inequality:** `μⁿ{ 4·𝔯ₙ + 2ε < R(ĥ) − R(h⋆) } ≤ exp(−ε² t n)`, chaining the basic inequality with FoML's symmetrization + McDiarmid tail (`𝔯ₙ` = Rademacher complexity of the loss class). **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality_separable` | `theorem` | Separable-class version (`SeparableSpace`/`FirstCountableTopology ι`, loss continuous in the parameter) — the form that applies to the uncountable-but-separable L²/L¹-ball linear classes (kernel, lasso). **Proved.** |

#### 12'.b `Stat/Concentration/BoundedDifference.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `uniformDeviation_bounded_difference` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | `uniformDeviation n f μ X` has bounded differences with constant `(1/n)·2b` in each coordinate, when each `f i` is bounded by `b`. |
| `bounded_difference_of_bounded` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | The map `ω ↦ supᵢ |Σₖ f i (X (ω k)) − n·E[f i ∘ X]|` has bounded differences with constant `2b`. |

#### 12'.c `Stat/Concentration/McDiarmid.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | McDiarmid upper-tail: bounded-difference family `(X i)` ⇒ `P(f − E f ≥ ε) ≤ exp(−2ε² t)` whenever `t Σ cᵢ² ≤ 1`. |
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_neg` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Symmetric lower-tail: `P(f − E f ≤ −ε) ≤ exp(−2ε² t)`. |
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos'` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Single-`X` form on the product measure `μⁿ` — the version actually consumed by `OSL/Modulus.lean`. |

#### 12'.d `Stat/Concentration/Symmetrization.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `expectation_le_rademacher` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | `E[supᵢ |Σₖ f i (X (ω k)) − n·E f i|] ≤ 2 n · rademacherComplexity n f μ X`. Wainwright Thm 4.10. |

#### 12'.e `Stat/Concentration/Separable.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `separableSpaceSup_eq` | `theorem` | For continuous bounded `f : X → E` with `X` separable, `⨆ x, f x = ⨆ i, f (denseSeq X i)`. **Proved.** |
| `separableSpaceSup_eq_real` | `theorem` | Real-valued specialisation handling the unbounded case. **Proved.** |

#### 12'.e2 `Stat/Concentration/CoveringNumber.lean`

Metric covering numbers for totally bounded sets, ported from
`auto-res/lean-rademacher` for the Dudley entropy-integral chain.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `coveringNumber_exists` | `TotallyBounded A`, `ε > 0` → `∃ n t, t.card = n ∧ A ⊆ ⋃ y ∈ t, Metric.ball y ε` | Extracts a finite ε-net for a totally bounded set and records its cardinality. |
| `coveringNumber` | `TotallyBounded A → ℝ → ℕ` | Minimal finite ε-cover cardinality for positive ε, and zero for nonpositive ε. |
| `coveringNumber_eq` | `ε > 0` → `coveringNumber ha ε = Nat.find …` | Unfolds the positive-radius branch of `coveringNumber`. |
| `converingNumber_antitone` | `AntitoneOn (coveringNumber ha) (Set.Ioi 0)` | Covering number is antitone in the radius on positive radii. |
| `coveringNumber_nonzero` | `A.Nonempty`, `ε > 0` → `0 < coveringNumber ha ε` | Nonempty totally bounded sets have positive covering number at positive radius. |
| `converingNumber_aemeasurable` | `AEMeasurable (coveringNumber ha) volume` | Measurability of the covering-number function on the real radius line. |
| `coveringFinset` | `TotallyBounded A`, `ε > 0` → `Finset X` | Chooses a finite ε-net attaining `coveringNumber`. |
| `coveringFinset_cover` | `A ⊆ ⋃ y ∈ coveringFinset ha hε, Metric.ball y ε` | The chosen finset covers the target set by ε-balls. |
| `coveringFinset_card` | `(coveringFinset ha hε).card = coveringNumber ha ε` | The chosen finset has cardinality equal to the covering number. |

#### 12'.e3 `Stat/Concentration/DudleyEntropy.lean`

The Dudley entropy-integral chaining bound controls empirical Rademacher
complexity by the covering-number entropy integral of the empirical
pseudometric function space.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dudley_entropy_integral'` | `ε > 0`, total boundedness, positive sample size, empirical-norm radius `c`, and `ε < c/2` → empirical Rademacher complexity without absolute value is bounded by `4ε` plus the entropy integral from `ε` to `c/2` | Core Dudley entropy-integral bound for a fixed sample and function class. |
| `dudley_entropy_integral_bound` | Same bound with the public sample-size parameter name `n` | Headline wrapper ported from FoML `Main.lean`. |

#### 12'.e3b `Stat/Concentration/SqrtLogIntegral.lean`

Elementary real-analysis evaluation used to simplify Dudley entropy-integral
upper bounds with logarithmic kernels.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sqrt_le_add_one_div_two` | `0 ≤ t → sqrt t ≤ (t + 1) / 2` | AM-GM bound for the square root, proved from nonnegativity of `(sqrt t - 1)^2`. |
| `log_div_nonneg_of_mem_Icc` | `0 < ε`, `x ∈ [ε, δ]` → `0 ≤ log (δ / x)` | Positivity of the logarithmic ratio kernel on the integration interval. |
| `integral_log_div_eq` | `0 < ε`, `ε ≤ δ` → `∫ x in ε..δ, log (δ / x) = δ - ε - ε * log (δ / ε)` | Exact closed-form evaluation of the logarithmic ratio integral. |
| `integral_log_div_le` | `0 < ε`, `ε ≤ δ` → `∫ x in ε..δ, log (δ / x) ≤ δ - ε` | Interval-length upper bound obtained from `log (δ / ε) ≥ 0`. |
| `sqrtLog_integral_le` | `0 < ε`, `ε ≤ δ` → `∫ x in ε..δ, sqrt (log (δ / x)) ≤ δ` | Dudley entropy-integral evaluation using the AM-GM pointwise bound and the logarithmic-ratio integral estimate. |

#### 12'.e4 `Stat/Concentration/VCCovering.lean`

VC-dimension control of empirical covering numbers for real-valued classes
whose sample evaluations factor through a Boolean class.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `restrictionPattern` | `(𝒳 → Bool) → (Fin n → 𝒳) → Finset (Fin n)` | Records the sample coordinates at which a Boolean classifier is true. |
| `growthFamily` | `(ι → 𝒳 → Bool) → (Fin n → 𝒳) → Finset (Finset (Fin n))` | The finite family of Boolean restriction patterns realized on the sample. |
| `card_growthFamily_le_sum_choose` | `𝒜.vcDim ≤ d → 𝒜.card ≤ ∑ k ∈ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Sauer-Shelah cardinality bound for a finite family on an `n`-point sample. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_growth` | Binary factorization of `F` through `π` on the sample and `ε > 0` → `coveringNumber h' ε ≤ (growthFamily π S).card` | Bounds the empirical covering number by choosing one representative per realized Boolean pattern. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_sum_choose` | Same hypotheses plus `(growthFamily π S).vcDim ≤ d` → `coveringNumber h' ε ≤ ∑ k ∈ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Combines the finite self-cover with Sauer-Shelah. |
| `sum_choose_le_exp_mul_div_pow` | `0 < d`, `d ≤ n` → `∑_{k≤d} (n choose k) ≤ (e n / d)^d` | Standard binomial-sum estimate; currently the only intentionally scaffolded arithmetic lemma in this file. |
| `log_coveringNumber_le` | Nonempty class, binary factorization, `ε > 0`, `0 < d`, `d ≤ n`, and VC dimension at most `d` → logarithmic covering bound | Converts the VC covering bound into the usual entropy display using the standard binomial-sum estimate. |

#### 12'.e4b `Stat/Concentration/HausslerPacking.lean`

Haussler-style finite-VC packing bounds for weighted Boolean Hamming metrics,
isolating the random-coordinate extraction core from the deterministic
Sauer-Shelah cardinality step.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `weightedHammingSq` | Coordinate weights and two Boolean vectors on `Fin n` → weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared | Sums the weights of coordinates where the two Boolean vectors differ. |
| `subsamplePattern` | Coordinate map `Fin m → Fin n` and Boolean vector → `Finset (Fin m)` | Records the true coordinates after restricting a Boolean vector to a sampled coordinate list. |
| `weightedHammingSq_nonneg` | Nonnegative weights → `0 ≤ weightedHammingSq w a b` | Basic nonnegativity of the weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared. |
| `card_subsample_family_le_succ_pow` | Restricted trace VC dimension at most `d` → restricted trace cardinality at most `(m + 1)^d` | Applies Sauer-Shelah and a binomial-sum bound to a sampled coordinate family. |
| `subsample_image_vcDim_le` | Original true-set family VC dimension at most `d` → every coordinate restriction has VC dimension at most `d` | Shows restriction along any coordinate map cannot increase VC dimension. |
| `finite_averaging_exists_separating_subsample` | Total weight at most `r²`, `ε²`-separation, `2 ≤ #P`, and `0 < ε ≤ r` → a separating restricted trace with `m ≤ 1 + (2*r²/ε²)*log #P` | Isolates the finite product-sum averaging step for the random-coordinate extraction; currently the remaining `sorry` in this module. |
| `exists_separating_subsample` | VC control, total weight at most `r²`, `ε²`-separation, `2 ≤ #P`, and `0 < ε ≤ r` → a separating restricted trace with `m ≤ 1 + (2*r²/ε²)*log #P` and restricted VC dimension at most `d` | Combines finite averaging with VC-dimension preservation for sampled coordinate families. |
| `self_log_solve` | `L ≤ d*log(2 + a*L)` with `d ≥ 1`, `a ≥ 2`, and `L ≥ 0` → an explicit `1 + 2*d*log(2*a*d)` bound | Scalar self-logarithm inequality used after Sauer-Shelah. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_packing_card_le` | VC control, total weight at most `r²`, and `ε²`-separation → `log #P ≤ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*r²/ε²)` | Logarithmic Haussler ε-packing bound with no dependence on the ambient coordinate count, modulo the isolated extraction lemma. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_log_packing_le` | Same hypotheses → `log #P ≤ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*r²/ε²)` | Alias for the clean logarithmic weighted VC packing bound. |

#### 12'.e4c `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedCovering.lean`

Metric bridges connecting binary-factored real classes with the weighted
Boolean Hamming geometry used in localized finite-VC covering arguments.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `empiricalDist_sq_eq_weightedHammingSq` | Binary factorization `F i (S j) = φ j (π i (S j))` → squared empirical L² distance equals weighted Hamming squared distance with weights `(φ j true − φ j false)²/n` | Converts empirical metric separation for a binary-factored real class into weighted Boolean trace separation on the same sample. |

#### 12'.e5 `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedRegime.lean`

Localized finite-VC Rademacher regime used by the sharp localized uniform
deviation layer.  The elementary sub-root and critical-radius algebra is proved
directly.  The remaining Massart finite-realization construction is isolated as
an explicit analytic sublemma rather than packaged as a hypothesis field.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BinaryFactoredVCClass` | Real-valued class `F` factors through a Boolean class whose sample growth family has VC dimension at most `d` | Packages the binary trace substrate used by VC covering arguments. |
| `BinaryTraceEntropyControl` | Either samplewise VC-dimension control or direct `(m + 1)^d` growth-cardinality control | Common entropy input for the localized star-hull theorem. |
| `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Empirical radius and total boundedness fields | Deterministic hypothesis bundle for the sample-path localized Dudley step. |
| `localizedVCDudleyHypotheses_of_empiricalRadius` | Bounded binary-factored finite-VC class plus an explicit samplewise empirical-radius bridge → `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Constructor for the localized Dudley hypothesis bundle. For population L² localization norms, the empirical-radius bridge remains a real extra obligation; boundedness alone does not give deterministic empirical-radius control on every sample. |
| `starHullZeroOut_empirical_rademacher_massart_vc` | Binary trace entropy + localized hypotheses → Massart finite-realization bound with rate `r * sqrt((2 d log(n+1)+2)/n)` | Isolates the remaining analytic finite-pattern Massart construction. |
| `absolute_dudley_vc_starHullZeroOut_linear_residual_shared` | Same localized star-hull bound with either VC-dimension or direct growth-cardinality trace control, assuming `K ≥ 1` | Shared residual used by the downstream VC localized bounds. |
| `vcLocalizedRademacherUpperBound` | Binary-factored finite-VC class + localized hypotheses → local Rademacher upper bound and critical-radius rate | Headline localized finite-VC Rademacher envelope. |
| `vcLocalizedEnvelope` | Finite-VC localized assumptions → `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` envelope | Connects the finite-VC Rademacher regime to the localized uniform-deviation theorem. |

#### 12'.f `Stat/Concentration/Hoeffding.lean`

Causalean-facing corollaries of Mathlib's sub-Gaussian framework
(`Mathlib.Probability.Moments.SubGaussian`) specialised to the `IIDSample`
model. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.map_eq` | `lemma` | Each sample point has law `P`: `μ.map (S.Z i) = P`. |
| `IIDSample.integral_comp_eq` | `lemma` | Population-mean identity `∫ f (S.Z i) ∂μ = ∫ f ∂P` for measurable `f`. |
| `IIDSample.iIndepFun_comp` | `lemma` | Independence of the composed family `fun i ↦ f ∘ S.Z i` from `S.indep`. |
| `hoeffding_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Hoeffding: for `f` valued in `[a,b]` (`a<b`) `P`-a.e., `P(X̄ₙ − E[f] ≥ ε) ≤ exp(−2nε²/(b−a)²)`. |
| `Concentration.measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided` | `lemma` | Generic two-sided assembly: from one-sided bounds `μ.real{ε ≤ T−m} ≤ B` and `μ.real{ε ≤ −T+m} ≤ B`, concludes `μ.real{ε ≤ |T−m|} ≤ 2B`. Shared by `hoeffding_abs_ge` and `bernstein_abs_ge` (the former duplicated union+calc boilerplate). |
| `hoeffding_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Hoeffding: `P(|X̄ₙ − E[f]| ≥ ε) ≤ 2·exp(−2nε²/(b−a)²)`. Two-sided assembly via `measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided`. |

#### 12'.g `Stat/Concentration/SubExponential.lean`

Sub-exponential MGF framework mirroring Mathlib's sub-Gaussian design, in the
`(v, b)` parameterization (validity interval encoded as `b·|t| < 1`, so `b = 0`
recovers the sub-Gaussian/all-`t` case). All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF X v b μ` | `structure` | `exp(t·X)` integrable and `mgf X μ t ≤ exp(v t²/2)` for every `t` with `b·|t| < 1`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.isFiniteMeasure` | `lemma` | A sub-exponential variable lives on a finite measure. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.neg` | `lemma` | Closure under negation (same `(v,b)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.congr` | `lemma` | Transfer along an a.e.-equality of variables. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.const_mul` | `lemma` | Scaling `r·X` has parameters `(r²v, |r|b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.add_of_indepFun` | `lemma` | Independent variables add: `X+Y` has parameters `(vX+vY, max bX bY)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.zero` | `lemma` | The constant `0` is sub-exponential with parameters `(0, b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_ge_le` | `theorem` | Chernoff tail `P(X ≥ ε) ≤ exp(−ε²/(2(v + bε)))` (single explicit minimizer `t = ε/(v+2bε)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.sum_range_of_iIndepFun` | `lemma` | A sum of `n` independent variables with common `(v,b)` is sub-exponential with `(n•v, b)`. |

#### 12'.h Mathlib sub-Gaussian re-use index

The sub-Gaussian half of the toolbox is consumed directly from Mathlib's
`ProbabilityTheory` namespace (no Causalean duplication); the Causalean wrappers above
build on it. Key upstream names:

| Mathlib declaration | Description |
|---|---|
| `HasSubgaussianMGF X c μ` | Sub-Gaussian MGF predicate `mgf X μ t ≤ exp(c t²/2)` for all `t`. |
| `hasSubgaussianMGF_of_mem_Icc` | Hoeffding's lemma: a `[a,b]`-valued RV is sub-Gaussian with proxy `((b−a)/2)²` after centering. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_ge_le` | Sub-Gaussian Chernoff right tail. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_sum_range_ge_le_of_iIndepFun` | Hoeffding's inequality for sums of independent sub-Gaussians (used by `hoeffding_ge`). |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.add_of_indepFun` / `sum_of_iIndepFun` | Independent sums (parameters add). |

#### 12'.i `Stat/Concentration/Bernstein.lean`

Bernstein's inequality routed through the constant-`v` sub-exponential framework.
**Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (all four declarations are axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only). The earlier power-series blocker
is avoided entirely: the sub-exponential packaging only needs the MGF bound on
the validity interval `c|t| < 1`, where `|tX| ≤ 1` a.e. lets the elementary
pointwise bound `exp u ≤ 1 + u + u²` integrate term-by-term.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `exp_le_one_add_add_sq` | `lemma` | Pointwise `exp u ≤ 1 + u + u²` for `|u| ≤ 1`; a real corollary of `Real.norm_exp_sub_one_sub_id_le`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_mgf_le_exp_sq` | `lemma` | For mean-zero `X`, `|X| ≤ c`, `E[X²] ≤ σ²`: `mgf X μ t ≤ exp(σ²t²)` when `c|t| ≤ 1`. Integrates the pointwise bound using `E[X]=0`, `E[X²]≤σ²`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` | `lemma` | A bounded mean-zero variable is sub-exponential with parameters `(2σ², c)` (note `2σ²·t²/2 = σ²t²`). **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Bernstein: `P(X̄ₙ − E[f] ≥ ε) ≤ exp(−n ε² / (2(2σ² + cε)))`. From `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` + independent-sum + Chernoff. **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Bernstein: `P(|X̄ₙ − E[f]| ≥ ε) ≤ 2·exp(−n ε² / (2(2σ² + cε)))`. **Proved.** |

> **Constant note.** The variance enters with proxy `2σ²` and the scale is `c`
> (so the tail is `exp(−nε²/(2(2σ²+cε)))`). The sharper textbook constant
> `exp(−nε²/(2(σ²+cε/3)))` is *not* attainable through the constant-`v`
> sub-exponential structure — it needs a Bernstein-specific Chernoff optimisation
> on the denominator-form MGF — and was deliberately traded for this cleaner,
> looser constant. The `c|t| < 1` validity interval forces the scale `b ≥ c`, so
> `b = c` is optimal for this route.

---

#### 12'.j `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval.lean`

Inverts the two-sided Hoeffding and Bernstein tails (12'.f, 12'.i) into explicit
*finite-sample* confidence half-widths. For a confidence level `δ ∈ (0,1]` and
sample size `n`, each result gives a half-width `w(n,δ)` such that the population
mean `m = ∫ f ∂P` is covered by the random interval `[X̄ₙ − w, X̄ₙ + w]` with
probability `≥ 1 − δ`. Both a miss-probability form (`P(w ≤ |X̄ₙ − m|) ≤ δ`) and
its coverage complement are provided. Holds at every fixed `n`, unlike the
asymptotic Wald interval. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free.**

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffdingCIHalfWidth` | `(a b : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (δ : ℝ) : ℝ` | Hoeffding half-width `(b−a)·√(log(2/δ)/(2n))` for an `[a,b]`-valued statistic. | **Proved** (def) |
| `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(c σ : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (δ : ℝ) : ℝ` | Variance-adaptive Bernstein half-width `2σ·√(log(2/δ)/n) + 2c·(log(2/δ)/n)`; leading term scales with `σ`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `hoeffding_ci_miss` | `… : μ.real {ω | hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n δ ≤ |X̄ₙ − m|} ≤ δ` | Miss bound: the mean falls outside the Hoeffding interval w.p. `≤ δ`. Inverts `hoeffding_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_miss` | `… : μ.real {ω | bernsteinCIHalfWidth c σ n δ ≤ |X̄ₙ − m|} ≤ δ` | Miss bound for the variance-adaptive Bernstein interval; exact quadratic-slack inversion of `bernstein_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_ci_cover` | `… : 1 − δ ≤ μ.real {ω | |X̄ₙ − m| < hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n δ}` | Coverage complement of `hoeffding_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_cover` | `… : 1 − δ ≤ μ.real {ω | |X̄ₙ − m| < bernsteinCIHalfWidth c σ n δ}` | Coverage complement of `bernstein_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |

---

#### 12'.k `Stat/Concentration/EmpiricalBernstein.lean`

Sample-variance infrastructure plus an **elementary, fully rigorous** data-driven
("empirical") Bernstein confidence interval. The full Maurer–Pontil bound is out
of scope; instead the population variance `σ² = ∫(f−m)²` is bounded above by the
*observed* sample variance `V̂ₙ` plus a deterministic slack `τ` (via two Hoeffding
deviations — one for `f`, one for `f²` — at level `δ/2` each), and `σ ≤ √(V̂ₙ+τ)`
is fed into the Bernstein half-width using monotonicity in `σ`. Constants are
looser than Maurer–Pontil's (the second-moment Hoeffding step is wasteful) but
every step is elementary. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only).

The explicit slack (with `M := max |a| |b|`, `d := √(log(4/δ)/(2n))`) is
`τ = M²·d + 2 M (b−a)·d`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance` | `(S : IIDSample Ω X μ P) (f : X → ℝ) (n : ℕ) : Ω → ℝ` | Biased (`1/n`-normalised) empirical variance `(1/n) ∑_{i<n} (f(Zᵢ ω) − X̄ₙ(ω))²` (in namespace `Causalean.Stat` for dot-notation). | **Proved** (def) |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_nonneg` | `… (ω) : 0 ≤ S.sampleVariance f n ω` | The sample variance is nonnegative (a scaled sum of squares). | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleVariance` | `(hf : Measurable f) (n) : Measurable (S.sampleVariance f n)` | The sample variance is measurable in the sample point `ω`. | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_eq` | `… (ω) : S.sampleVariance f n ω = (n:ℝ)⁻¹ * ∑ i ∈ range n, (f (Z i ω))^2 − (X̄ₙ ω)^2` | Computational form: empirical second moment minus squared empirical mean. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalVarianceSlack` | `(a b : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (δ : ℝ) : ℝ` | The deterministic slack `τ = M²·d + 2M(b−a)·d` (`M = max|a||b|`, `d = √(log(4/δ)/(2n))`). | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_variance_concentration` | `(hf) (hab : a<b) (hbound : f∈[a,b] a.e.) (n) (hn) (hδ0) (hδ1) : μ.real {ω | V̂ₙ + τ < σ²} ≤ δ` | Data-driven variance bound: w.p. `≥ 1−δ`, `σ² ≤ V̂ₙ + empiricalVarianceSlack a b n δ`. Union of two level-`δ/2` Hoeffding events (for `f` and `f²`) plus a null set for the a.e. `|X̄ₙ| ≤ M` bound. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(S) (f) (a b c : ℝ) (n) (δ) : Ω → ℝ` | Data-driven half-width: `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` with oracle `σ` replaced by the observed `√(V̂ₙ(ω) + τ)`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_bernstein_ci_miss` | `(hf) (hab) (hbound_ab) (hc : 0≤c) (hbound : |f−m|≤c a.e.) (hposvar : 0<σ²) (n) (hn) (hδ0) (hδ1) : μ.real {ω | empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth S f a b c n δ ω ≤ |X̄ₙ − m|} ≤ 2δ` | Data-driven Bernstein CI miss bound: the mean falls outside the observed-variance interval w.p. `≤ 2δ`. Unions the oracle Bernstein miss (`σ=√σ²`) with the variance-bound failure, using monotonicity of the width in `σ` on the good event. | **Proved** |

---

## 12g. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/` — Abstract DML estimation and inference framework

Generic moment-functional layer that consolidates the DML asymptotic-linearity
machinery so any concrete instance (currently AIPW) reduces to instantiating
the abstract structure. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dml_ate.tex`
generalised away from AIPW-specific algebra.

### 12g.1 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/MomentFunctional.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GeneralMoment Ω μ Z P_Z H` | structure | Abstract bundle: moment functional `m : H → Z → ℝ → ℝ`, truth `η₀ : H`, target `θ₀ : ℝ`, perturbation set `H_ε ⊆ H`, bilinear seminorms `(ρ₁, ρ₂) : H → H → NNReal`, joint measurability of `m _ · _`, and `η₀ ∈ H_ε`. |
| `GeneralMoment.J₀` | field, `ℝ` | Jacobian `∂_θ ∫ m(η₀, z, θ) dP_Z |_{θ=θ₀}` of the population moment. Scalar form (vector-θ generalisation deferred). For AIPW (linear score `m = ψ − θ`), `J₀ = −1`. |
| `GeneralMoment.J₀_ne_zero` | field, `Prop` | Non-singularity witness; ensures `J₀_inv` is well-defined. |
| `GeneralMoment.J₀_inv` | `noncomputable def` | `J₀⁻¹`. **Proved.** |
| `GeneralMoment.J₀_mul_J₀_inv` | `lemma`, `@[simp]` | `J₀ * J₀_inv = 1`. **Proved.** |
| `MeanZero M` | `Prop` | `∫ z, M.m M.η₀ z M.θ₀ ∂P_Z = 0`. |
| `H_ε_PerturbClosed M` | `Prop` | `M.H_ε` is closed under convex combinations between `η₀` and `η ∈ H_ε`. |

##### `LinearMoment` (linear-score extension)

Extension of `GeneralMoment` carrying the decomposition `m η z θ = m_a η z * θ + m_b η z` (Chernozhukov 2018 Thm 3.1 "linear score" form). AIPW is the canonical instance with `m_a η z = -1` and `m_b η z = ψ_AIPW(η, z)`, giving `J₀ = -1`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearMoment` | structure (extends `GeneralMoment`) | Adds `m_a`, `m_b`, measurability, decomposition `m_decomp`, and consistency `J₀_eq : J₀ = ∫ m_a η₀ z dP_Z`. |

### 12g.2 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DirectionalDeriv.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasDirDeriv M` | structure | Pointwise directional derivative data: function `dM : H → Z → ℝ`, pointwise `Tendsto` witness for the difference quotient along the segment `η₀ → η`, and measurability of `dM η`. |

### 12g.3 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/NeymanOrthogonal.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal M D` | `Prop` | `∀ η ∈ M.H_ε, ∫ z, D.dM η z ∂P_Z = 0`. |
| `DiffQuotientEnvelope M` | `Prop` | Existence of an L¹(P_Z) envelope dominating the difference quotient near `t = 0`. |
| `integratedMoment_diffQuotient_tendsto_zero` | `theorem` | DCT bridge: `NeymanOrthogonal` + `DiffQuotientEnvelope` ⇒ integrated difference quotient → 0 along `𝓝[≠] 0`. **Proved** (forward-compat; not on AIPW path). |

### 12g.4 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/RemainderBound.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BilinearRemainder M C` | `Prop` | `∀ η ∈ M.H_ε, |∫ z, M.m η z M.θ₀ ∂P_Z| ≤ C · ρ₁(η, η₀) · ρ₂(η, η₀)`. |
| `bilinear_remainder_of_smoothness` | `theorem` | NeymanOrthogonal + uniform second-order envelope on the linearization residual ⇒ `∃ C, BilinearRemainder M C`. **Proved** (after signature strengthening: uniform `K, g`). |

### 12g.5. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLChernozhukov.lean`

Classical Chernozhukov-form DML theorem; conclusion uses IF `−J₀⁻¹ · m(η₀, ·, θ₀)` and asymptotic variance `J₀⁻¹ Σ J₀⁻ᵀ`. Matches Chernozhukov et al. 2018 Thm 3.1 / 3.2. The earlier zero-centered `dml_asymptoticLinear` (`Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DML.lean`) was deleted on 2026-05-08 since AIPW migrated and no other instance used it.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlChernozhukovEstimator M sample split η_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-step Chernozhukov estimator: `θ̂_n := θ₀ − J₀⁻¹ · Pₙ_B m(η̂, ·, θ₀)`. Reduces to `(1/|B|) Σ ψ(η̂, Z_i)` for AIPW (J₀ = −1). |
| `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` at `M.θ₀` with IF `−J₀⁻¹ · m(η₀, ·, θ₀)`, indexed over `split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (G+B decomposition with the Chernozhukov-form score `m(η̂, ·, θ₀)` and a `−J₀⁻¹` constant-multiplication wrapper; bundled an inline `const_mul_isLittleOp_one` helper). **API refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, and `_h_in_Hε : ∀ n ω, η_hat n ω ∈ M.H_ε` parameters; replaced the ∀-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` with the per-η̂_n bound `_hBR_at : ∀ n ω, |∫ z, M.m (η_hat n ω) z M.θ₀ ∂P_Z| ≤ Crem · ρ₁(η̂_n, η₀) · ρ₂(η̂_n, η₀)`. Net effect: instances supply only what they actually need (orthogonality is implicit in the per-η̂_n bound), removing the artificial ∀-over-`H_ε` hypothesis. **Further trimmed 2026-05-09:** dropped the unused `_hc_lt : c < 1`, `_h_indiv_rate_ρ₁`, `_h_indiv_rate_ρ₂` parameters (all discarded in the body — the abstract proof needs only the product rate); callers (`AIPWInstance`, `AutoDebias.DML`) keep them at their own level when needed locally. |

### 12g.5'. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`

K-fold cross-fitted version of `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Same Chernozhukov form, K folds, score evaluated independently of nuisance training fold per Chernozhukov et al. 2018 §3.2. Concludes asymptotic linearity at the **full-sample** index `Finset.range n` (the K fold-k sub-aggregations are reweighted into a `(1/√n)`-normalised sum modulo `o_p(1)`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlCrossFitEstimator M sample split η_hat` | `noncomputable def` | K-fold averaged Chernozhukov estimator: `θ̂ := M.θ₀ − J₀⁻¹ · K⁻¹ · Σ_k \|fold n k\|⁻¹ · Σ_{i ∈ fold n k} m(η̂^{(-k)}, Z_i, θ₀)`. |
| `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/√\|fold n k\|) Σ_{i ∈ fold n k} ψ(Z_i) = O_p(1)` whenever `ψ` is mean-zero and `L²(P_Z)`. Markov + the abstract `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` with trivial training σ-algebra `m_A = ⊥` (independence is automatic via `ProbabilityTheory.indep_bot_left`); mean-zero collapses centered = uncentered. **Proved.** |
| `foldNormalizedSumOverN_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/√n) Σ_{i ∈ fold n k} ψ(Z_i) = O_p(1)`. Bound by `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` × `√(\|fold n k\|/n)` (the latter is bounded eventually via `split.cover` + `split.partition` giving `(fold n k).card ≤ n`). **Proved.** |
| `dml_crossFit_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlCrossFitEstimator` at `M.θ₀` with IF `−J₀⁻¹ · m(η₀, ·, θ₀)`, indexed over `fun n => Finset.range n`. Hypotheses: per-fold measurability/integrability (`_h_m_meas`, `_h_m_train`, `_h_m_train_uncurry`, `_h_m_int`, `_h_m_sq_int`), per-fold score-difference rate (`_h_score_diff_rate k`), per-(n,k,ω) bilinear remainder bound `_hBR_at`, individual ρ-rates, product rate, and `_hK_pos : 1 < K` (tightened from `0 < K` because the K-fold bias step needs `(K:ℝ)⁻¹ ∈ (0, 1)`; `K = 1` reduces to the one-shot estimator and is out of scope). Proof structure: `mean_zero` and `finite_var` by constant-rescaling of `MeanZero M` / `_hFV`; the `remainder` decomposes into `h_fold_centered` + `h_fold_bias` (per-fold, lifted from the OneShot proof in `DMLChernozhukov.lean` via `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and an inline bias derivation analogous to OneShot `hB`) and `h_full_reweighting` (the cover/partition + ratio rescaling). The reweighting step uses `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` for the weight-discrepancy "ratio piece" and `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` for the "score piece"; finite K-fold sum closed via a small inline `IsLittleOp` finite-Finset additivity helper. **Proved by Codex (A5).** **API refactored 2026-05-09:** mirrors the OneShot 2026-05-06 refactor — dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, `_h_in_Hε`, and ∀-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` parameters; replaced by the per-(n,k,ω) bound `_hBR_at`. |

### 12g.6 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AIPWInstance.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwGeneralMoment S h_e_pointwise` | `noncomputable def` | AIPW realization of `GeneralMoment` with `ρ₁ η η' = ‖Δμ_T‖_{L²(P_X)} + ‖Δμ_F‖_{L²(P_X)}`, `ρ₂ η η' = ‖Δe‖_{L²(P_X)}`, `H_ε = ATE.H_ε ε`. |
| `aipwGeneralMoment.J₀`, `J₀_ne_zero` | fields | AIPW Jacobian `J₀ = −1` (linear score `m = ψ − θ`). |
| `aipw_meanZero` | `theorem` | Wraps `aipw_mean_zero`. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | `aipw_remainder_bound` reshaped into `BilinearRemainder` via `Fintype.sum_bool` distribution. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` | Headline AIPW DML AL, retargeted to use `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Conclusion: `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (aipwGeneralMoment S _) _ _ _) S.θ₀ (fun z => −J₀_inv · aipwMomentFunctional S.η₀ z S.θ₀)`. **Refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the ∀-quantified `h_L2 : ∀ η ∈ H_ε ε, …` hypothesis (and the `hε_pos` / `hε_le` extracts that only fed the now-removed `aipw_neymanOrthog` / `aipwHasDirDeriv` calls). Now takes per-η̂_n L²: `h_mu_diff_memLp : ∀ n ω a, MemLp (μ̂(n,ω,a) − μ_val a) 2 P_X` and `h_e_diff_memLp : ∀ n ω, MemLp (ê(n,ω) − e_val) 2 P_X`. Body builds the per-η̂_n bilinear remainder bound directly via `aipw_remainder_bound` (followed by `Fintype.sum_bool` to align with the `ρ₁ · ρ₂ = (‖Δμ_T‖ + ‖Δμ_F‖) · ‖Δe‖` shape) and feeds it as `_hBR_at` to the refactored abstract theorem. The Gâteaux-derivative `aipwHasDirDeriv` / `aipw_neymanOrthog` wrappers (and the underlying `Neyman.lean` / `NeymanAux/` machinery) are no longer on the AL critical path and were archived to `Estimation/ATE/Archived/` on 2026-05-06. **Proved.** |

### 12g.7. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Riesz.lean`

Riesz representer pattern for linear-in-γ functionals (ATE, policy value, average derivative). Light skeleton; concrete instances live next to their estimators.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RieszRepresentation H_γ γ_target L P_X` | structure | A Riesz representer `α₀ : X → ℝ` with `representation : ∀ γ, L γ = ∫ x, α₀ x · γ x ∂P_X`, plus measurability and L¹ witnesses. |
| `rieszScore γ_target L proj_X Y_obs γ α θ z` | `noncomputable def` | The Riesz orthogonal score `m(z; θ, γ, α) := L γ + α(proj_X z) · (Y_obs z − γ_target γ (proj_X z)) − θ`. |
| `rieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Under the regression-orthogonality assumption `∫ z, α₀(proj_X z)·(Y_obs z − γ_target γ₀ (proj_X z)) ∂P_Z = 0`, the population score at the truth `(γ₀, α₀, L γ₀)` integrates to zero. **Proved.** |
| `rieszScore_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder identity: `∫ z, m(z; L γ₀, γ, α) ∂P_Z − ∫ z, m(z; L γ₀, γ₀, α₀) ∂P_Z = − ∫ x, (α x − α₀ x)·(γ_target γ x − γ_target γ₀ x) ∂P_X` under pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, dual regression-orthogonality, integrability of the perturbed residual, and integrability of the four product terms. **Proved.** |

### 12g.8. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Parametric.lean`

Parametric (no-nuisance) specialisation of `GeneralMoment` (`H = Unit`). With `H = Unit`, the abstract `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` collapses to the classical parametric one-step estimator and the rescaled remainder is identically zero. OLS, IV-2SLS, MLE instantiate this pattern.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `parametricMoment m_par θ₀ J₀ hJ m_meas` | `noncomputable def` | Constructs `GeneralMoment Ω μ Z P_Z Unit` from a user-supplied moment `m_par : ℝ → Z → ℝ`, target `θ₀`, and Jacobian `J₀ ≠ 0`. Bilinear seminorms are zero (no nuisance to perturb). |
| `parametric_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (parametricMoment …) sample split (fun _ _ => ())) θ₀ (fun z => −J₀⁻¹ · m_par θ₀ z) sample split.foldB` from population mean-zero `∫ m_par(θ₀, ·) dP_Z = 0` and L²-finite `Integrable ((m_par θ₀)²) P_Z`. The remainder collapses identically: the parametric one-step estimator equals the influence-function partial sum exactly. **Proved.** |

### 12g.9. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/SecondStageOperator.lean`

Abstract second-stage regression operator and stability framework for the DR-Learner CATE estimator (Kennedy 2023). Operator-level / target-agnostic content: takes a real-valued pseudo-outcome `f : γ × Bool × ℝ → ℝ` and returns a real-valued estimate at a query point `x : γ`. No AIPW algebra, no causal content. Mirrors `def:est-cate-second-stage` and `def:est-cate-stability` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SecondStageOperator Ω μ γ` | structure | Abstract bundle: `evalAt : ℕ → Ω → (γ × Bool × ℝ → ℝ) → γ → ℝ` taking sample size, randomness scope, pseudo-outcome, and query point; plus a minimal joint-measurability stub `meas_evalAt_const`. Linearity in input is left as a separate predicate `IsLinearInInput`. |
| `SecondStageOperator.IsLinearInInput op` | `Prop` | `evalAt n ω (f + g) x = evalAt n ω f x + evalAt n ω g x`. Linear smoothers satisfy this; tree mean estimators with random splits typically do not. |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleEstimator op f` | `def` | Operator applied to a fixed "true" pseudo-outcome `f` (Kennedy's `\tilde\tau_n`). |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleRiskScale op f target x n` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `R^*_n(x) := √(∫ ω, (op.evalAt n ω f x − target x)² ∂μ)`. Pointwise oracle risk scale. |
| `IsConditionalBias` | removed | The old placeholder `Prop` alias (`True`) was removed. Conditional-bias identification now enters through a caller-supplied `BiasIdent` predicate parameter on `Stable` and `oracle_expansion`. |
| `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent` | `Prop` | Def 4 stability: for any `(fHat_n, f, bHat_n)` with `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n`, `d_n →_p 0` implies the operator-level discrepancy `op.evalAt(fHat_n) − op.evalAt(f) − op.evalAt(bHat_n)` is `o_p(R^*_n(x))`. |
| `oracle_expansion` | `theorem` | Headline: under `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent`, `Tendsto_inProb d_n 0`, and a `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n` witness, the operator-level oracle expansion `op.evalAt(fHat_n) − op.evalAt(f) − op.evalAt(bHat_n) = o_p(R^*_n(x))` holds. **Proved by Codex** (one-line application of `Stable`). |

**Refactored 2026-05-09:** the placeholder `IsConditionalBias := True` was replaced by an explicit Prop predicate parameter `BiasIdent` that callers supply, restoring semantic content.

### 12g.10. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/LinearSmoother.lean`

Abstract weighted-Hölder bound for linear-smoother second-stage operators. Mirrors `prop:est-cate-linear-smoother-bound` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearSmootherOp Ω μ γ` | structure (extends `SecondStageOperator`) | Adds `weights : ℕ → Ω → γ → (γ × Bool × ℝ) → ℝ`. The actual weighted-sum identity `evalAt n ω f x = Σ w · f(z_i)` is supplied as a separate predicate `IsLinearSmoother` rather than baked in. |
| `LinearSmootherOp.IsLinearSmoother op n ω x B w xs` | `Prop` | Predicate witnessing the weighted-sum identity `∀ f, op.evalAt n ω f x = Σ_{i ∈ B} w i · f (xs i)` for explicit weights `w : ι → ℝ` and data points `xs : ι → γ × Bool × ℝ`. |
| `WeightedNorm B w g p` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Weighted Lᵖ norm `(Σ_i ∈ B, |w i| / (Σ_j |w j|) · |g i|^p)^(1/p)`. Index type `ι` abstract; uses `Real.rpow`. |
| `WeightedNorm_nonneg` | `lemma` | Non-negativity of `WeightedNorm`. **Proved.** |
| `smoother_bias_holder` | `theorem` | `|op.evalAt n ω (fun z => g z.1) x| ≤ c_n · WeightedNorm B w (g ∘ xs.1) 1` under `IsLinearSmoother op n ω x B w xs` and `Σ |w| ≤ c_n`. **Proved by Codex** via triangle inequality + factor-out-`Σ|w|` rearrangement of the weighted-L¹ norm. |
