| `smoother_bias_product_holder` | `theorem` | Hölder for `g = g₁·g₂`: `|op.evalAt n ω (fun z => g₁(z.1) · g₂(z.1)) x| ≤ c_n · ‖g₁ ∘ xs.1‖_{w,p} · ‖g₂ ∘ xs.1‖_{w,q}` under `IsLinearSmoother`, `Σ |w| ≤ c_n`, and `Real.HolderConjugate p q`. **Proved by Codex** via `Real.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq_of_nonneg` applied to `f i := |w i|^{1/p} · |g₁ (xs i).1|`, `g i := |w i|^{1/q} · |g₂ (xs i).1|` with `1/p + 1/q = 1`. |

### 12g.11. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/` — Automatic debiasing module

Reusable construction that converts any **linear-in-regression target** (or, more generally, any **regression-nuisance moment**) into a Neyman-orthogonal score by adding the Riesz-representer correction `α(X)·(Y − g(X))`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/automatic_debiasing.tex`. The module contains an algebraic core (linear functional case), a general-moment layer, and a one-shot Auto-DML estimator with its asymptotic-linearity theorem.

#### 12g.11.a `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/Linear.lean` — Linear functional core

Defs 1, 3, 5 and Props 4, 6 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinRegFnSys` | structure | Linear regression-functional system: types `Z, X, H_γ` with measure/AddCommGroup/Module instances, `proj_X, Y_obs, γ_target` (linear in `H_γ`), observation-level linear-in-γ functional `m_lin : Z → H_γ → ℝ`, regression truth `g₀`, pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, and the integrated dual-orthogonality `regression_resid_orthog`. |
| `L_of_m S γ` | `noncomputable def` | Population linear functional `γ ↦ ∫ m_lin(z, γ) dP_Z`. |
| `L_of_m_add`, `L_of_m_smul` | `theorem`, `theorem` | Linearity of `L_of_m` in `γ` (under integrability). **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore S γ α θ z` | `noncomputable def` | Alias of `rieszScore` from `Estimation.OrthogonalMoments.Riesz` specialised to a `LinRegFnSys`. |
| `linRieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Population score at the truth `(g₀, α₀, L g₀)` integrates to zero (Prop 6, mean-zero). Wraps `rieszScore_meanZero` via `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the regression direction: `(∫ m_lin z ν_g dP_Z) − ∫ rep.α₀ · γ_target ν_g dP_X = 0` (Prop 6, part b). Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_α_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the representer direction: residual orthogonality of `ν_α` against `Y_obs − γ_target g₀` (Prop 6, part c). Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss S α` | `noncomputable def` | Linear Riesz loss `∫ (γ_target α x)² dP_X − 2 · L_of_m S α` (Def 3). |
| `linRieszLoss_excess_eq_l2dist` | `theorem` | Prop 4, second half: `linRieszLoss α − linRieszLoss α₀_idx = ∫ (γ_target α − γ_target α₀_idx)² dP_X` when `rep.α₀ = γ_target α₀_idx` pointwise. Complete-the-square via `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer` | `theorem` | Prop 4, first half: `α₀_idx` is a directional minimizer of `linRieszLoss` along every line `α₀_idx + t • ν` iff `L(ν) = ∫ γ_target α₀_idx · γ_target ν dP_X` for every `ν`. Parabolic-identity FOC argument with quadratic-coefficient case split on `a = ∫ (γ_target ν)² dP_X`. **Proved.** |

#### 12g.11.b `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/General.lean` — General-moment layer

Defs 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and Prop 11 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RegNuisanceMomentSys` | structure | Regression-nuisance moment system. Same data layer as `LinRegFnSys`; replaces the linear functional `m_lin` with a general moment `M : ℝ → H → ℝ` (kernel `m : H → Z → ℝ → ℝ`, `M_truth : M θ₀ g₀ = 0`, `m_population : M θ g = ∫ m g z θ dP_Z`) and a precomputed Gateaux derivative `D_g_M : H → ℝ` with linearity laws. |
| `AutoDebiasRepresenter S` | structure | The representer `α₀ : X → ℝ` characterised by `D_g_M ν = ∫ α₀ · γ_target ν dP_X` (Def 9). |
| `autoDebiasedScore S g α θ z` | `noncomputable def` | Observation-level form: `m g z θ + α(proj_X z) · (Y_obs z − γ_target g (proj_X z))`. |
| `autoDebiasedMoment S g α θ` | `noncomputable def` | `∫ z, autoDebiasedScore S g α θ z ∂P_Z` (Def 10). |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_meanZero_at_truth` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part a: `autoDebiasedMoment S g₀ rep.α₀ θ₀ = 0` from `M_truth` + `regression_resid_orthog` + `m_population`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part b: `D_g_M ν_g − ∫ rep.α₀ · γ_target ν_g dP_X = 0`. Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_α_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part c: residual orthogonality of arbitrary measurable `ν_α`. Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `genRieszLoss S α` | `noncomputable def` | General Riesz loss `∫ (γ_target α x)² dP_X − 2 · D_g_M α` (Def 12). FOC ↔ representer characterisation is structurally the same as `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer`; not re-proved here. |
| `finDiffRieszLoss S Z_data θ_hat g_hat ε α C n ω` | `noncomputable def` | Finite-difference representer loss (Def 13): empirical second moment minus a centred finite-difference approximation of `D_g M(θ̂_n, ĝ_n)[α]` with perturbation scale `ε n`. Pure definition; intended target of future RieszNet-style analysis. |

#### 12g.11.c `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/DML.lean` — Auto-DML estimator and AL theorem

Def 6 and Thm 7 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `linAutoNuisance S` | `def` | Joint nuisance type `S.H_γ × (S.X → ℝ)` with componentwise `AddCommGroup` (`Prod.instAddCommGroup`) and `Module ℝ` (`Prod.instModule`). |
| `linAutoGeneralMoment μ S rep ε hε_nn h_score_meas` | `noncomputable def` | `GeneralMoment` instance over `linAutoNuisance S`. `m (γ, α) z θ := linRieszScore S γ α θ z`; `η₀ := (S.g₀, rep.α₀)`; `θ₀ := L_of_m S S.g₀`; `H_ε := { (γ, α) | ‖γ_target γ − γ_target g₀‖_{L²(P_X)} ≤ ε ∧ ‖α − α₀‖_{L²(P_X)} ≤ ε }`; `ρ₁`/`ρ₂` are the corresponding L² norms; `J₀ = −1`. The measurability witness `h_score_meas : ∀ η θ, Measurable (fun z => linRieszScore S η.1 η.2 θ z)` and non-negativity `hε_nn : 0 ≤ ε` are passed as parameters because `linAutoNuisance` does not internally constrain `α : X → ℝ` to be measurable. |
| `linAuto_meanZero` | `theorem` | `MeanZero` of the linear Auto-DML moment at the truth. Direct call to `linRieszScore_meanZero`. **Proved.** |
| `linAuto_int_pred S rep η` | `private def` | Per-η integrability bundle (10 components) exposing the exact API shape required by `rieszScore_bilinearRem`. Used as a hypothesis in `linAuto_bilinearRem`. |
| `linAuto_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder bound: `\|∫ z, score η z θ₀ ∂P_Z\| ≤ C · ρ₁(η, η₀) · ρ₂(η, η₀)` for `η ∈ H_ε` with `linAuto_int_pred S rep η` and explicit `MemLp` witnesses for the two L²(P_X) differences. Proof factors via `rieszScore_bilinearRem` then applies Hölder-2 (`integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq`). **Proved.** |
| `linAutoDMLEstimator S rep ε hε_nn h_score_meas sample split η_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-shot linear Auto-DML estimator (Def 6), built as `dmlChernozhukovEstimator (linAutoGeneralMoment μ S rep ε hε_nn h_score_meas) sample split η_hat`. Equivalently: `(1/\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i ∈ B(n)} [m_lin(Z_i; ĝ_n) + α̂_n(X_i)·(Y_i − ĝ_n(X_i))]`. |
| `linAutoDML_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Thm 7 headline: `IsAsymLinear (linAutoDMLEstimator …) (L_of_m S S.g₀) (fun z => −J₀_inv · linRieszScore S S.g₀ rep.α₀ θ₀ z) sample split.foldB` under `MeanZero`, finite L² of the oracle score, per-η bilinear remainder, the standard fold-A measurability/integrability hypotheses, and the score-difference / individual / product rate hypotheses inherited from `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Proof: direct composition. **Proved.** |

The module skips the ATE-equivalence remark from the tex (Remark 7) per design.

## 13'''. `Estimation/CATE/` — DR-Learner CATE estimation (Kennedy 2023)

Specific-system layer instantiating `BackdoorEstimationSystem` for the function-valued target `τ₀(x) = μ_val(true, x) − μ_val(false, x)`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`. The DR-Learner is built on top of the same AIPW pseudo-outcome already used for ATE; the new content is (1) the function-valued target with `condDistrib`-style pointwise interpretation, (2) the DR conditional-bias identity (Prop 2 / `prop:est-cate-dr-bias-identity`, the milestone), and (3) the second-stage regression operator with stability hypothesis driving the oracle expansion (Theorem 5).

### 13'''.a `Estimation/CATE/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CATEEstimationSystem P γ` | structure (extends `BackdoorEstimationSystem`) | Empty extension — the CATE target `τ_val` is purely derived. All back-door substrate (consistency, exchangeability, overlap, integrability, `μ_val` / `e_val`, σ(X)-compatibility) is inherited. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.τ_val S x` | `def`, `ℝ` | Value-space CATE: `τ_val x := μ_val true x − μ_val false x`. Definition `def:est-cate-system`. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.measurable_τ_val` | `lemma` | Measurability of `τ_val`. **Proved.** |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.tau_val_eq_CATE` | `theorem` | `(fun ω => τ_val (factualX ω)) =ᵐ μ[Y(true) − Y(false) | σ(X)]` under the back-door assumptions. **Proved by Codex** (combine `μ_compat true/false` with `condExp_sub`). |

### 13'''.b `Estimation/CATE/PseudoOutcome.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi_eta z η` | `noncomputable def` | Uncentered AIPW pseudo-outcome (Kennedy's `φ_η`): `aipwMoment z η.μ_fn η.e_fn 0`. Definition `def:est-cate-dr-pseudo-outcome`. |
| `phi₀ S z` | `noncomputable def` | True pseudo-outcome `φ_0 := φ_{η_0}`. |
| `measurable_phi_eta`, `measurable_phi₀` | `lemma` | Measurability in the data argument. **Proved by Codex** (mirror `measurable_ψ_AIPW`). |

### 13'''.c `Estimation/CATE/PseudoOutcomeMean.lean` (Prop 1)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi₀_factualZ_cond_exp` | `theorem` | σ(X)-conditional form of `prop:est-cate-dr-cond-mean`: `μ[φ₀(factualZ) | σ(X)] =ᵐ τ_val ∘ factualX` under back-door + strict overlap. **Proved by Codex** via local `weighted_residual_cond_exp_zero` helper + `cond_exp_residual_zero` + `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left`. |
| `phi_eta_cond_exp_eq_tau` | `theorem` | Value-space `P_X`-a.e. form: `∀ᵐ x ∂P_X, ∫ z, φ₀(z) ∂condDistrib factualZ factualX P.μ x = τ_val x`. Hypotheses add `[StandardBorelSpace γ] [Nonempty γ]` (for `condDistrib`) and `Integrable (φ₀ ∘ factualZ) P.μ`. **Proved by Codex** by transporting `phi₀_factualZ_cond_exp` through `condExp_ae_eq_integral_condDistrib` and pushing forward to `P_X` via `MeasureTheory.ae_map_iff` (measurability of the equality set comes from `StronglyMeasurable.integral_condDistrib` for the LHS and `measurable_τ_val` for the RHS). |

### 13'''.d `Estimation/CATE/ConditionalBias.lean` (Prop 2 — milestone)

The DR conditional-bias identity. Pure σ(X)-conditional algebra; the closed-form cross-product remainder driving the double-robustness corollary (`rem:est-cate-double-robust`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `condBias η η₀ x` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Closed form: `Σ_{a∈Bool} ((η.e_fn x − η₀.e_fn x)·(η.μ_fn a x − η₀.μ_fn a x)) / (if a then η.e_fn x else 1 − η.e_fn x)`. |
| `measurable_condBias` | `lemma` | Measurability in `x`. **Proved by Codex** via `Finset.measurable_sum` + product-of-measurables. |
| `cond_exp_residual_at_h` | `private lemma` | Generalized form of `cond_exp_residual_zero` (parameterised over arbitrary measurable `h : γ → ℝ`): `μ[1{D=d}·(Y − h(X)) | σ(X)] =ᵐ propScore d · (μ_val d (factualX) − h (factualX))`. **Proved by Codex** mirroring `MeanZero.lean` lines 109–196. |
| `phi_eta_minus_phi₀_cond_exp` | `theorem` | σ(X)-conditional bias identity (Prop 2 form on Ω): `μ[φ_η(factualZ) − φ_0(factualZ) | σ(X)] =ᵐ condBias η η₀ ∘ factualX` under per-arm outcome-regression integrability and strict overlap. **Proved by Codex** with a 100-line expansion: 4 calls to `cond_exp_residual_at_h` + `e_compat` / `propScore_false_ae` substitution + `field_simp` + `ring`. **Hypothesis additions vs. plan**: `(h_μ_η_int : ∀ a, Integrable (η.μ_fn a ∘ factualX) P.μ)` and `(hε_pos : 0 < ε)` were added so the algebra is well-defined. |
| `phi_eta_minus_phi₀_at_x` | `theorem` | Value-space `P_X`-a.e. form: `∀ᵐ x ∂P_X, ∫ z, (phi_eta z η − phi₀ S z) ∂condDistrib factualZ factualX P.μ x = condBias η η₀ x`. Hypotheses add `[StandardBorelSpace γ] [Nonempty γ]`, `0 < ε`, and per-arm η-integrability. **Proved by Codex** by transporting `phi_eta_minus_phi₀_cond_exp` through `condExp_ae_eq_integral_condDistrib` and pushing forward to `P_X` via `MeasureTheory.ae_map_iff` (measurability of the equality set from `StronglyMeasurable.integral_condDistrib` and `measurable_condBias`). |
| `condBias_zero_of_propensity_match`, `condBias_zero_of_outcome_match` | `lemma` | Double-robustness corollaries (`rem:est-cate-double-robust`): `condBias = 0` if propensity matches truth, or if both outcome arms match. **Proved by Codex** via `Finset.sum_eq_zero` + `sub_eq_zero` substitution. |
| `cond_exp_phi_eta_dir_deriv_at_truth_zero` | `theorem` | Differentiated companion of `phi_eta_minus_phi₀_cond_exp`: σ(X)-conditional of `phi_eta_dir_deriv η₀ v (factualZ)` is zero a.e. under strict overlap on `η₀` and bounded `v` (uniform bounds on `v.μ_fn b` and `v.e_fn`). **Proved by Codex** by 5-summand decomposition of the closed form; the two `e_fn`-residual terms vanish via `cond_exp_residual_zero` (d=true / d=false) and the two `μ_fn`-pullouts cancel via `S.e_compat` (propScore = e_val a.e.) plus the derived `propScore false =ᵐ 1 − e_val ∘ factualX`. |

### 13'''.d′ `Estimation/CATE/PhiEtaDeriv.lean`

Closed-form directional derivative of the DR pseudo-outcome `phi_eta` in its nuisance argument `η`, evaluated at an anchor `g₀ : NuisanceVec γ` along a direction `v : NuisanceVec γ`. Because `phi_eta` is reciprocal in `η.e_fn`, the value-difference `phi_eta z η − phi_eta z g₀` is **not** the directional derivative; this file computes the genuine derivative and proves the pointwise difference-quotient tendsto under strict overlap.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi_eta_dir_deriv g₀ v z` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Closed-form directional derivative `D_g phi_eta(z, g₀)[v] = (v.μ_fn true X − v.μ_fn false X) + indA(z)·(−v.e_fn(X)/g₀.e²·(Y − g₀.μ_fn true X) − v.μ_fn true X / g₀.e_fn X) − (1 − indA(z))·(v.e_fn(X)/(1 − g₀.e_fn X)²·(Y − g₀.μ_fn false X) − v.μ_fn false X / (1 − g₀.e_fn X))`. Used by `Estimation/CATE/OSL/DRLearner/Analytic.lean` to identify the opaque `NuisanceDirDeriv.dPhi` field via uniqueness of limits. |
| `measurable_phi_eta_dir_deriv` | `lemma` | Measurability in `z`. Proved by composing the measurabilities of the five summands. |
| `phi_eta_dir_deriv_tendsto` | `theorem` | Pointwise tendsto: `(phi_eta z (g₀ + t • v) − phi_eta z g₀) / t → phi_eta_dir_deriv g₀ v z` as `t → 0` along `𝓝[≠] 0`, under strict overlap (`g₀ ∈ H_ε ε`, `0 < ε`). **Proved by Codex** via a private `phi_eta_real_line_hasDerivAt` HasDerivAt helper on ℝ + standard real-analysis combinators; the strict-overlap hypothesis ensures the denominators `g₀.e_fn X` and `1 − g₀.e_fn X` are nonzero. |

### 13'''.e `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/DRLearner.lean`

Thin instantiation of `SecondStageOperator` to the AIPW pseudo-outcome.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `drLearnerEstimator S op η_hat n ω x` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `op.evalAt n ω (fun z => phi_eta z (η_hat n ω)) x`. Kennedy's `\hat\tau^{DR}_n(x)`. |
| `drOracleEstimator S op n ω x` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `op.evalAt n ω (fun z => phi₀ S z) x`. Kennedy's `\tilde\tau_n(x)`. |
| `drOracleRiskScale S op x n` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Specialisation of `oracleRiskScale` to `(phi₀ S, S.τ_val, x)`. |
| `drOracleEstimator_eq_oracleEstimator` | `lemma` | Sanity unfolding: `drOracleEstimator S op = op.oracleEstimator (phi₀ S)`. **Proved** by `rfl`. |

### 13'''.f `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/OracleExpansion.lean` (Theorem 5)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dr_oracle_expansion` | `theorem` | AIPW specialisation of `General.oracle_expansion`: under `CATEEstimationSystem`, `Stable op S.τ_val d_n x BiasIdent`, `Tendsto_inProb d_n 0`, and a `BiasIdent` witness for `phi_eta`, `phi₀`, and `condBias`, `(drLearner − drOracle − op.evalAt(condBias)) = o_p(R^*_n(x))`. **Proved by Codex** as a direct call to `General.oracle_expansion`. |
| `dr_oracle_efficient` | `theorem` | Corollary: if additionally the smoothed-bias term is `o_p(R^*_n(x))`, then `(drLearner − drOracle) = o_p(R^*_n(x))`. **Proved by Codex** via `IsLittleOp.add_eventually_nonneg_rate` (newly exposed from `Estimation/ATE/RemainderIdentity.lean`) + algebraic cancellation via `convert ... ; funext ; ring`. |

### 13'''.g `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/LinearSmootherCATE.lean` (Prop 6)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cate_linear_smoother_bias_bound` | `theorem` | Hölder bound for the smoothed DR-bias term: `\|op.evalAt n ω (condBias η_hat η₀ ∘ z.1) x\| ≤ aipw_rem_const ε · c_n · ‖Δπ‖_{w,p} · Σ_a ‖Δμ_a‖_{w,q}` under `IsLinearSmoother op n ω x B w xs`, `Σ \|w\| ≤ c_n`, `Real.HolderConjugate p q`, and `η_hat n ω ∈ H_ε ε`. **Proved by Codex (filled)** — codex left 4 mechanical errors (unknown `Finset.sum_bool` instead of `Fintype.sum_bool`; summand-order mismatch; sum-distributivity rearrangements) which were patched inline. The proof: bound `\|condBias y\| ≤ aipw_rem_const ε · Σ_a \|Δπ y\| · \|Δμ_a y\|` pointwise (mirrors `aipw_remainder_bound` in `RemainderBound.lean`), then apply `smoother_bias_product_holder` arm-by-arm to a constructed `absOp` with absolute weights. |
| `cate_dr_oracle_efficient_linear` | `theorem` | Corollary plugging a linear-smoother `LinearSmootherOp` into `dr_oracle_efficient` (uses `op.toSecondStageOperator` projection). **Proved** by direct application of `dr_oracle_efficient`. |

### 13'''.h `Estimation/CATE/SecondOrderBias.lean` (honest second-order product bias — reusable core)

The genuine double-robustness content: the DR-Learner nuisance bias weighted by an arbitrary bounded σ(X)-test function `w` is a **product** of the two nuisance L²-errors. Generalises `aipw_remainder_bound` (the `w ≡ 1` / ATE case) to a test function. All sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `integral_phiDiff_mul_eq_condBias` | `theorem` | Conditioning identity: `∫ z, (phi_eta z η − phi₀ S z)·w z.1 ∂P_Z = ∫ x, condBias η η₀ x · w x ∂P_X` for measurable `w`. **Proved** by pulling the σ(X)-measurable factor `w(X)` through `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` and substituting `phi_eta_minus_phi₀_cond_exp` (the `dr_scoreZero` pull-out idiom). |
| `abs_integral_phiDiff_mul_le_product` | `theorem` | Second-order product bound: `\|∫ z, (phi_eta z η − phi₀ S z)·w z.1 ∂P_Z\| ≤ (B/ε)·Σ_a ‖η.μ_fn a − μ_val a‖_{L²(P_X)}·‖η.e_fn − e_val‖_{L²(P_X)}` for `\|w\| ≤ B`, `η ∈ H_ε`. **Proved** by the identity + pointwise `\|condBias·w\| ≤ (B/ε)(\|Δμ_T·Δe\| + \|Δμ_F·Δe\|)` (strict overlap) + `integral_abs_mul_le_eLpNorm_mul_eLpNorm` per arm — the Cauchy–Schwarz route of `aipw_remainder_bound`. |

### 13'''.i `Estimation/CATE/OSL/SecondOrderBias.lean` (DR-Learner `Bias_n` is second-order)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.drBias_le_product` | `theorem` | `\|Bias_n (drOSLSystem) ((drMixedDirDeriv).Dθ_at η₀) ((drMixedDirDeriv).Dθ_at h) θ̂\| ≤ (2B/ε)·Σ_a ‖h.μ_fn a − μ_val a‖₂·‖h.e_fn − e_val‖₂` for `\|D.dEval θ̂\| ≤ B`, `h ∈ H_ε`. **Proved**: both `drMixedDirDeriv` bundles have `dℓ_θ = -2·(phi_eta z η − eval θ₀ z.1)·D.dEval θ z.1` (literal field), so `Bias_n` telescopes (the `eval θ₀` term cancels) to `2·∫(phi_eta·h − phi₀)·D.dEval θ̂`, then `abs_integral_phiDiff_mul_le_product` with `w := D.dEval θ̂`. **Replaces the trivial `OSL.Bias_taylor_form` existential placeholder.** Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.j `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/DRLearnerHonest.lean` (honest fixed-nuisance Kennedy oracle inequality)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.oracle_inequality_drLearner_highProb_honest` | `theorem` | Same setup as `oracle_inequality_drLearner_highProb`, with the DD bundles fixed to `drMixedDirDeriv` and a uniform bound `\|D.dEval θ\| ≤ B` on `Θ_set`. Conclusion replaces the symbolic `Bias_n` term with the **explicit** product bias: `‖τ̂ − θ₀‖² ≤ (4(1+σ)/σ²)·ρ²_{n,δ} + (4/σ)·(2B/ε)·Σ_a ‖Δμ_a‖₂‖Δe‖₂ + (4/σ)·r_opt n` w.p. ≥ 1−δ. **Proved** by composing the existing end-to-end with `drBias_le_product` (`(4/σ)·Bias_n ≤ (4/σ)·\|Bias_n\| ≤ …`). Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.k `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/DRLearnerRandomNuisance.lean` (un-pinned random cross-fitted nuisance)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.oracle_inequality_drLearner_random_nuisance` | `theorem` | Un-pins the nuisance from fixed `h` to a random fold-A estimate `ĥ : ℕ → Ω → NuisanceVec γ` (each `ĥ n ω ∈ H_ε`). Conclusion carries the **random** product bias `(4/σ)·(2B/ε)·Σ_a ‖ĥ n ω.μ_fn a − μ_val a‖₂·‖ĥ n ω.e_fn − e_val‖₂` on a high-prob set per `n`. **Proved** by the deterministic `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM` (already accepts random `ghat`) + `drBias_le_product` at `ĥ n ω` (already holds ∀ `h ∈ H_ε`). Takes the realised empirical-process modulus event `hMod` as a hypothesis — discharging it is the cross-fitting frontier (keystone now proved, see `RandomParam.lean`). Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.l `Estimation/OSL/Modulus/RandomParam.lean` (cross-fit conditioning keystone)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.randomParam_event_le` | `theorem` | **Cross-fit conditioning keystone.** Given a fold-A sub-σ-algebra `m_A`, the fold-B coordinate map `Y : Ω → β` with `μ.map Y = ν` and `Indep m_A σ(Y)`, a fold-A-measurable bad-set family `Bad : Ω → Set β` (joint-measurable in `m_A.prod mβ`) with each section `ν (Bad ω) ≤ δ`, then the *random* event `μ {ω : Y ω ∈ Bad ω} ≤ δ` — plugging a fold-A-measurable random parameter into fold-B events of uniform mass ≤ δ preserves the bound. **Proved** via `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` (product factorisation `μ.map (ω ↦ (ω,Yω)) = (μ.trim m_A).prod ν`) + `Measure.prod_apply` (Tonelli on the ν-section) + `∫(≤δ)=≤δ`. Axiom-clean. The measure-theoretic heart of lifting the fixed-nuisance modulus to a random cross-fitted nuisance — now consumed by `RandomNuisance.lean` below. |

### 13'''.l.2 `Estimation/OSL/Modulus/RandomNuisance.lean` (cross-fit lift of the modulus to a random nuisance)

Closes the OSL/Kennedy G2 frontier: the fixed-`g` bridge `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` is lifted to a *random fold-A-measurable nuisance* `ĥ : ℕ → Ω → G` via the keystone. All declarations sorry-free and axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.LocalEmpProcessModulusRandom S S_iid split ρ δ ĥ` | `def`, `Prop` | Random-`g` analogue of `LocalEmpProcessModulus`: for each `n` a high-prob (`≥ 1−δ`) event on which the centred excess risk at the random nuisance `ĥ n ω` obeys the modulus inequality uniformly in `θ`. Exactly the `hMod` shape consumed by `oracle_inequality_drLearner_random_nuisance`. |
| `OSL.foldBCoord S_iid split n` | `noncomputable def`, `Ω → Fin (foldB n).card → Z` | Fold-B coordinate map (reindex of the fold-B subsample by `orderIsoOfFin`). |
| `OSL.badDataSet S S_iid split n b δ R g` | `noncomputable def`, `Set (Fin (foldB n).card → Z)` | Product-space bad set for nuisance `g`: `{s : 2•rademacherComplexity + 2b√(2 log(1/δ)/m) ≤ uniformDeviation (centred-loss class at g) s}`. |
| `OSL.modulusRadius split b δ R` | `noncomputable def`, `ℕ → ℝ` | Deterministic radius `ρ n = √(if card=0 then 2b else 2 R n + 2b√(2 log(1/δ)/card))`. |
| `OSL.foldBCoord_law` | `theorem` | `μ.map (foldBCoord S_iid split n) = Measure.pi P_Z` — the fold-B coordinate law (mirrors the monolith `hY_law`, via `foldB_pi_law` + `piCongrLeft`). |
| `OSL.foldBCoord_meas` | `theorem` | `foldBCoord` is measurable. |
| `OSL.badDataSet_mass_le` | `theorem` | `Measure.pi P_Z (badDataSet … g) ≤ δ` (McDiarmid tail bound `uniform_deviation_tail_bound_separable_of_pos` at the centred class; needs `0 < b`, `0 < card`). |
| `OSL.modulus_of_not_badData` | `theorem` | Off the bad set (`foldBCoord ω ∉ badDataSet … g`), the modulus inequality holds uniformly over `Θ_set` (mirrors the monolith on-event branch; re-derives the dense-range Rademacher bridge inline since the monolith's bridge lemmas are `private`). |
| `OSL.localEmpProcessModulus_random_of_bounded_rademacher` | `theorem` | **Cross-fit lift (frontier capstone).** Given a random nuisance `ĥ` with uniform bound `b>0`, continuity, and Rademacher bound `R` *at every realisation* `ĥ n ω`, plus the cross-fit structural facts `hindep n` (fold A ⊥ fold-B coords) and `hbad_joint n` (the bad set depends jointly measurably on `(ω,s)` w.r.t. `m_A n ⊗ σ(foldB)`), concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulusRandom S S_iid split (modulusRadius …) δ ĥ`. **Proved** by `badDataSet_mass_le` (per-section mass) + `randomParam_event_le` (condition on fold A) for the high-prob event, `modulus_of_not_badData` on the complement, with an `card=0` trivial branch. Axiom-clean. `hindep`/`hbad_joint` are named structural hypotheses (true by the cross-fit construction; same spirit as taking `RademacherBound` as a hypothesis). |

## 13''''. `Estimation/OSL/` — Orthogonal Statistical Learning (function-valued targets)

Sibling submodule to `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/` and `Estimation/CATE/`: when the target is a function (e.g. CATE) instead of a scalar functional, "orthogonality" is Neyman-orthogonality of the *loss*, and the headline guarantee is a deterministic **oracle inequality** in the target-space norm rather than a CLT. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/orthogonal_statistical_learning.tex`. Phase-1 + Phase-2 + Phase-3 land the deterministic theorems and the DR-Learner orthogonality witness (abstract — concrete discharge of the mixed-DD bundle / DCT bridge / Riesz cancellation deferred to `Estimation/CATE/OSL/DRLearner/Analytic.lean`); concentration / modulus realisation (Phase 4) is deferred.

### 13''''.a `Estimation/OSL/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSLSystem Ω μ Z P_Z Θ G` | `structure` | Bundle: convex `Θ_set ⊆ Θ` (Hilbert-space-like), nuisance set `G_set ⊆ G`, loss `ℓ : Z → Θ → G → ℝ`, joint measurability, truth `(θ₀, g₀)`. Statistical-learning analogue of `GeneralMoment`. Definition `def:est-osl-system`. |
| `OSLSystem.L S θ g` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Population risk `∫ ℓ z θ g ∂P_Z`. |
| `Θ_PerturbClosed`, `G_PerturbClosed` | `def`, `Prop` | Closure of `Θ_set` (resp. `G_set`) under line segments from the truth. |

### 13''''.b `Estimation/OSL/DirectionalDeriv.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasDirDerivTheta S g` | `structure` | Pointwise dir derivative of `θ ↦ ℓ z θ g` along `θ₀ → θ`, with measurability and tendsto witnesses. Anchored at any nuisance `g`. |
| `HasDirDerivG S` | `structure` | Pointwise dir derivative of `g ↦ ℓ z θ₀ g` along `g₀ → g`. |
| `HasMixedDirDeriv S` | `structure` | Family `Dθ_at : ∀ g, HasDirDerivTheta S g` plus mixed-derivative value `dℓ_θg θ g z` with tendsto + measurability. Captures the natural-language `D_g D_θ ℓ` at `(θ₀, g₀)`. |
| `FirstOrderInequality S Dθ` | `def`, `Prop` | `∀ θ ∈ Θ_set, 0 ≤ ∫ z, Dθ.dℓ_θ θ z ∂P_Z` — population KKT inequality at the truth. |

### 13''''.c `Estimation/OSL/NeymanOrthogonal.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogLoss S M` | `def`, `Prop` | Integrated mixed dir derivative vanishes: `∀ θ ∈ Θ_set, ∀ g ∈ G_set, ∫ M.dℓ_θg θ g z ∂P_Z = 0`. Definition `def:est-osl-neyman-loss`. |
| `DiffQuotientEnvelopeTheta`, `DiffQuotientEnvelopeG` | `def`, `Prop` | L¹(P_Z) envelopes for the target / nuisance difference quotients (DCT bridge inputs). |
| `neymanOrthog_iff_score_deriv_zero` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Score reformulation: `NeymanOrthogLoss` ↔ score map `g ↦ ∫ Dθ.dℓ_θ θ z` has zero derivative at `g₀`. **Proof deferred to Phase 4 (DCT bridge).** |

### 13''''.d `Estimation/OSL/PluginERM.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `empRiskFoldB S S_iid split n ω θ g` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Estimation-fold empirical risk `(1/|B(n)|) Σ_{i∈B(n)} ℓ (Z_i ω) θ g`. |
| `SampleSplitPluginERM S S_iid split θhat ghat r_opt` | `structure` | Predicate: `θhat n ω ∈ Θ_set` and ε-minimizer of fold-B empirical risk against `ghat`, with non-negative slack `r_opt`. Definition `def:est-osl-plugin-erm`. |

### 13''''.e `Estimation/OSL/LocalModulus.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LocalEmpProcessModulus S S_iid split ρ δ g` | `def`, `Prop` | Hypothesis-form modulus: per-`n`, an event of measure `≥ 1 - δ` carrying the centred-process bound `(L θ g − L θ₀ g) − (L_emp θ − L_emp θ₀) ≤ ρ_n ‖θ − θ₀‖ + ρ_n²` uniformly over `Θ_set`. Definition `def:est-osl-local-modulus`. |

### 13''''.f `Estimation/OSL/SecondOrderBias.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Bias_n S Dθ_truth Dθ_at_ghat θhat` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Loss-gradient nuisance bias `∫ Dθ_truth.dℓ_θ θhat − ∫ Dθ_at_ghat.dℓ_θ θhat`. Definition `def:est-osl-second-order-bias`. |
| `Bias_taylor_form` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Second-order Taylor representation placeholder; full formalisation needs a `D²_g` bundle (Phase 4). |

### 13''''.g `Estimation/OSL/OracleInequality.lean` (Phase-1 milestone)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM` | `theorem` | Deterministic oracle inequality: under σ-strong convexity of `L(·, ghat n ω)`, FOI at the truth, plug-in ERM, and the modulus inequality at the sample point, `‖θhat n ω − θ₀‖² ≤ 4(1+σ)/σ² · (ρ n)² + (4/σ)·Bias_n + (4/σ)·r_opt n`. Theorem `thm:est-osl-plugin-erm-oracle`. **Proved by Codex.** Constant on `r_opt` is `4/σ` rather than the note's `2/σ` (the AM–GM proof actually yields `4/σ` — see file docstring). |
| `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM_highProb` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | **High-probability** oracle inequality: consumes `LocalEmpProcessModulus S S_iid split ρ δ g` (the real probability statement) and concludes that for every `n`, with `μ`-probability ≥ `1 − δ` over the ambient sample, `‖θhat n ω − θ₀‖² ≤ 4(1+σ)/σ²·(ρ n)² + (4/σ)·Bias_n + (4/σ)·r_opt n`. Pinned to a fixed nuisance value `g` (`ghat ≡ g`); fold-A randomisation deferred. Phase-1 ↔ Phase-4a contact point. **Sorry**, awaiting Codex fill. |

### 13''''.h `Estimation/OSL/Sparse/Setup.lean`

Finite-dimensional specialisation (Phase 2). `Θ := EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin p)`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `l1Norm ν T`, `l1Full ν` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Coordinate ℓ¹ norm restricted to a `Finset T`, and the full sum. |
| `linftyDev hp ν` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Coordinate ℓ∞ deviation `max_i |ν i|` via `Finset.sup'` on a non-empty index set. |
| `RestrictedCone S₀` | `noncomputable def`, `Set` | Cone `{ν : ‖ν_{S₀ᶜ}‖₁ ≤ 3 ‖ν_{S₀}‖₁}`. Definition `def:est-osl-sparse-plugin-erm` (cone). |
| `mem_RestrictedCone_iff` | `lemma` | Unfolding lemma. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `l1Full_eq` | `lemma` | `l1Full ν = l1Norm ν S₀ + l1Norm ν (univ \ S₀)`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `l1Norm_supp_le_card_sqrt_mul_l2norm` | `lemma` | Cauchy–Schwarz on the support: `l1Norm ν S₀ ≤ √S₀.card · ‖ν‖`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `SparsePluginERM empRiskFn θhat lambda` | `structure` | Exact minimiser of `empRiskFn θ + λ‖θ‖₁`. Definition `def:est-osl-sparse-plugin-erm`. |

### 13''''.i `Estimation/OSL/Sparse/RSC.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RestrictedStrongConvexity empRiskFn gradEmp θ₀ S₀ σn` | `def`, `Prop` | σₙ-RSC at `θ₀` over `S₀`: for ν ∈ cone, `empRiskFn(θ₀+ν) − empRiskFn θ₀ − ⟪gradEmp θ₀, ν⟫ ≥ (σn/2)‖ν‖²`. Definition `def:est-osl-rsc`. |

### 13''''.j `Estimation/OSL/Sparse/PluginGuarantee.lean` (Phase-2 milestone)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sparse_plugin_guarantee` | `theorem` | Deterministic sparse plug-in guarantee: under RSC, `λ ≥ 2 ‖∇emp θ₀ − popGrad‖_∞`, FOC at the truth, and a subgradient inequality, the sparse plug-in ERM `θhat` satisfies `θhat − θ₀ ∈ RestrictedCone S₀` and `‖θhat − θ₀‖ ≤ 12 λ √s / σn`. Theorem `thm:est-osl-sparse-plugin-guarantee`. **Proved by Codex.** Signature carries an explicit `h_subgrad : ∀ θ, empRiskFn θ − empRiskFn θ₀ ≥ ⟪gradEmp θ₀, θ − θ₀⟫` because the basic-inequality step requires `gradEmp θ₀` to act as a subgradient at `θ₀` (the natural-language note implicitly uses this; we make it explicit). |
| `LinftyDevTailBound hp dev ρ δ` | `def`, `Prop` | Predicate: there is a measurable event `E ⊆ Ω` with `μ E ≥ 1 − δ` on which `linftyDev hp (dev ω) ≤ ρ`. The gradient-deviation analogue of `LocalEmpProcessModulus`. |
| `sparse_plugin_guarantee_highProb` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | **High-probability** sparse plug-in ERM guarantee: under `LinftyDevTailBound` on the empirical-gradient deviation and `λ ≥ 2 ρ`, with probability ≥ `1 − δ`, `θhat ω − θ₀ ∈ RestrictedCone S₀` and `‖θhat ω − θ₀‖ ≤ 12 λ √s / σn`. Phase-2 ↔ concentration contact point. **Sorry**, awaiting Codex fill. |

### 13''''.k `Estimation/CATE/OSL/DRLearner.lean` (Phase-3 milestone)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀` | `def`, `Set (NuisanceVec γ)` | Bounded-direction slice anchored at `η₀`: the set of `η` whose deviation `η − η₀` has uniformly bounded outcome-regression and propensity components (`∃ Cμ, ∀ b x, |(η − η₀).μ_fn b x| ≤ Cμ` and analogous for `e_fn`). Used as the nuisance slice `G_set` of `drOSLSystem` so that the score-zero obligation can be discharged constructively via `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded`. |
| `anchor_mem_boundedNuisanceDirs η₀` | `lemma` | `η₀ ∈ BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀`, witnessed by `Cμ = Ce = 0`. |
| `drOSLSystem S Θ Θ_set Θ_convex θ₀ θ₀_mem eval eval_meas eval_θ₀` | `noncomputable def`, `OSLSystem` | DR-Learner OSL system: data law from `S.toBackdoorEstimationSystem.P_Z`; loss `(z, θ, η) ↦ (phi_eta z η − eval θ z.1)²`; nuisance `G := NuisanceVec γ` with `G_set := BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀` (uniformly bounded perturbation directions); truth-witness `eval θ₀ = τ_val`. |
| `drNeymanOrthog_witness` | `theorem` | DR-Learner squared loss is Neyman-orthogonal at `(τ₀, η₀)`, consuming a concrete `M : HasMixedDirDeriv` and the analytic inputs `hBridge : MixedScoreDCTBridge` and `hScoreFlat` (the score-form orthogonality tendsto, quantified over `BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀` to match `drOSLSystem.G_set`). Proved as a thin wrapper around `neymanOrthog_iff_score_deriv_zero`. Proposition `prop:est-osl-dr-loss-orthogonal`. The concrete instantiation of `M`, with closed-form `Dθ_at`/`dℓ_θg` and the pointwise tendsto obligations discharged, lives in `Estimation/CATE/OSL/DRLearner/Analytic.lean`. |

### 13''''.k' `Estimation/CATE/OSL/DRLearner/Analytic.lean` (Phase-3 analytic discharge)

Refines `drNeymanOrthog_witness` by *constructing* the closed-form `HasMixedDirDeriv` for the DR-Learner squared loss and discharging the pointwise tendsto obligations. The DCT swap and integrated orthogonality remain as hypotheses — see footnote.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `EvalDirDeriv Θ_set θ₀ eval` | `structure` | Directional-derivative bundle for the candidate-evaluation map `eval : Θ → γ → ℝ`: carries `dEval θ x` (the dir-deriv value at `θ₀` in direction `θ − θ₀`), pointwise tendsto, measurability, and a uniform `L∞` bound on `dEval θ` (needed for DCT). For the linear function-class case `eval θ x = θ x`, `dEval θ x = θ x − θ₀ x`. |
| `NuisanceDirDeriv g₀` | `structure` | Directional-derivative bundle for `phi_eta` in the *nuisance* direction at `g₀`: carries `dPhi : NuisanceVec γ → (γ × Bool × ℝ) → ℝ` with pointwise tendsto and measurability. Required because `phi_eta` is reciprocal in `η.e_fn`, hence non-linear in `η`, and the value-difference `phi_eta z η − phi_eta z g₀` is **not** the directional derivative. |
| `drMixedDirDeriv S hA hε_pos h_overlap_η₀ Θ Θ_set Θ_convex θ₀ θ₀_mem eval eval_meas eval_θ₀ D ND` | `noncomputable def`, `HasMixedDirDeriv (drOSLSystem ...)` | Closed-form mixed directional-derivative bundle for the DR-Learner loss: `(Dθ_at η).dℓ_θ θ z = −2·(phi_eta z η − eval θ₀ z.1)·D.dEval θ z.1` and `dℓ_θg θ η z = −2·ND.dPhi η z·D.dEval θ z.1`. Pointwise tendsto for both fields **proved by Codex** via the chain rule `a² − b² = (a−b)(a+b)` on `(phi_eta z η − eval θ z.1)²` for the target field, and via direct substitution + `ND.pointwise` for the mixed field. |
| `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded` | `lemma` | **Honest discharge of the integrated-orthogonality obligation.** For any candidate target `θ` and any nuisance perturbation `η : NuisanceVec γ` whose direction `η − η₀` has uniformly bounded `μ_fn`/`e_fn`, `∫ z, ND.dPhi η z · D.dEval θ z.1 ∂P_Z = 0`. **Proved by Codex** by (i) identifying `ND.dPhi η z = phi_eta_dir_deriv η₀ (η − η₀) z` via uniqueness of `nhdsWithin` limits applied to `ND.pointwise` and `phi_eta_dir_deriv_tendsto`, (ii) converting the `P_Z`-integral to a `P.μ`-integral via `MeasureTheory.integral_map`, (iii) applying the conditional-expectation tower + σ(X)-measurable pull-out of `D.dEval ∘ factualX`, and (iv) plugging in `cond_exp_phi_eta_dir_deriv_at_truth_zero` to get the inner conditional is `0` a.e. Replaces the tautological `dr_mixedScoreDCTBridge` passthrough that previously sat in this file. |
| `dr_scoreFlat ... D ND hBridge` | `theorem` | Score-flatness on `BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀`: for every `θ ∈ Θ_set` and every `η ∈ BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀`, the integrated score difference quotient tends to zero as `t → 0`. The single remaining analytic hypothesis is `hBridge` (the DCT swap); the integrated-orthogonality obligation is discharged inside the proof by `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded`, applied to the boundedness witnesses extracted from `η ∈ BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀`. The closing computation is `∫ M.dℓ_θg = −2 · ∫ ND.dPhi · D.dEval = −2 · 0 = 0`. |
| `drNeymanOrthog ... D ND hBridge` | `theorem` | `NeymanOrthogLoss` for the DR-Learner loss at `(τ₀, η₀)` over `BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀`, parameterised by `D : EvalDirDeriv` and `ND : NuisanceDirDeriv`. The score-zero obligation is fully internalised: the only remaining analytic hypothesis is `hBridge` (the DCT swap). One-line proof: feed `hBridge` and `dr_scoreFlat` into `neymanOrthog_iff_score_deriv_zero`. Restricting `drOSLSystem.G_set` to `BoundedNuisanceDirs η₀` is the standard semiparametric move that lets `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded` discharge the integrated-orthogonality obligation directly without integral-defaults-to-zero conventions for unbounded directions. |

### 13''''.l `Estimation/OSL/Modulus.lean` (Phase-4a bridge)

Bridge between the vendored Rademacher / McDiarmid / Symmetrization
chain in `Causalean/Stat/Concentration/` and the OSL `LocalEmpProcessModulus`
predicate.  Pure consumption of FoML headlines plus
`Stat/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.foldB_pi_law` for the
`μⁿ`-on-`Fin m → Ω` ↔ `μ`-on-`Ω` transport, and a generic-`DenseRange`
analog of `separableSpaceSup_eq_real` for the sup-lifting step.

**Why not `[Countable S.Θ_set]`?**  Combined with `S.Θ_convex`,
countability forces `S.Θ_set.Subsingleton` (a non-trivial convex subset
of a real vector space contains a line segment, which is uncountable).
The bridge therefore takes an **explicit countable enumeration**
`idx : ℕ → S.Θ_set` with `DenseRange idx` plus a continuity hypothesis
`LossContinuousOnΘset`, and uses the dense-range analog of FoML's
`separableSpaceSup_eq_real` to lift sup-over-ℕ to sup-over-`↥S.Θ_set`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `foldB_pi_law` | `lemma` | Public alias of the (private) `oneShot_iid` from `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`: `μ.map (ω ↦ (i ↦ S.Z i ω)) = Measure.pi (fun _ ↦ P)` over `i ∈ split.foldB n`. The bridge consumed by the McDiarmid/symmetrization chain to transport `μⁿ`-conclusions to fold B on `μ`. |
| `RademacherBound S S_iid split g idx R` | `def`, `Prop` | Hypothesis: for every `n`, `0 ≤ R n` and `rademacherComplexity (split.foldB n).card (k ↦ ℓ z (idx k).val g − ℓ z θ₀ g) μ (S_iid.Z 0) ≤ R n`. Indexed over the *countable* index `ℕ` via the user-supplied `idx : ℕ → S.Θ_set`. |
| `UniformlyBoundedLoss S g b` | `def`, `Prop` | `∀ z, ∀ θ ∈ Θ_set, |ℓ z θ g| ≤ b`. |
| `LossContinuousOnΘset S g` | `def`, `Prop` | `∀ z, Continuous (fun (θ : ↥S.Θ_set) => S.ℓ z θ.val g)`. Needed by the dense-range lifting. |
| `separableSup_via_denseRange` | `private lemma` | Generic `DenseRange` analog of `FoML.separableSpaceSup_eq_real`: for continuous `f : X → ℝ` on nonempty topological `X` with `DenseRange idx`, `⨆ x : X, f x = ⨆ k : ℕ, f (idx k)`. Proved. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_eq_denseRange`, `rademacherComplexity_eq_denseRange` | `private lemma` | Empirical / population Rademacher complexity is preserved under re-indexing from `X` to `ℕ` via dense range, given continuity in `θ`. Proved. |
| `rademacherComplexity_map_id` | `private lemma` | `rademacherComplexity n f (μ.map sample) id = rademacherComplexity n f μ sample`. Pushforward simplification used in the transport step. Proved. |
| `populationRisk_abs_le_of_uniform`, `populationRisk_sub_le_two_mul_bound` | `private lemma` | `|S.L θ g| ≤ b` and `S.L θ g − S.L θ₀ g ≤ 2 b` for `θ ∈ Θ_set`, derived from `UniformlyBoundedLoss` + `[IsProbabilityMeasure P_Z]`. The boundedness fallback used in the empty-fold (`m = 0`) case. Proved. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` | `theorem` | **Phase-4a headline (proved).** Under `UniformlyBoundedLoss S g b`, `LossContinuousOnΘset S g`, an explicit countable dense `idx : ℕ → S.Θ_set`, and `RademacherBound` against `idx`, conclude `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with `ρ n := √(if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then 2b else 2 R n + 2b · √(2 log(1/δ) / (split.foldB n).card))`. The nonempty-fold branch is the textbook `√(2 R_n + ε)` rate with `ε = 2b · √(2 log(1/δ)/|B(n)|)`; the `m = 0` branch uses `√(2b)` to dominate the boundedness fallback `|L θ g − L θ₀ g| ≤ 2b`. Proof: case split on `m = 0` vs `m ≥ 1`, then on `b = 0` vs `b > 0`; for `m ≥ 1, b > 0` chains `expectation_le_rademacher` + `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos'` + `uniformDeviation_bounded_difference` on the ℕ-indexed centred class, transports via `foldB_pi_law` + `Finset.orderIsoOfFin`, and lifts to sup-over-`↥S.Θ_set` via `separableSup_via_denseRange`. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_singleton` | `theorem` | Smoke test: `Θ_set = {θ₀}` ⇒ modulus holds with `ρ ≡ 0`. Proved. |

### 13''''.m `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/DRLearner.lean` (Phase-4a end-to-end)

Apply the Phase-4a bridge to the DR-Learner OSL system.  Real success
criterion for Phase 4a: yields `LocalEmpProcessModulus` for `(τ̂_n, ĥ_n)`
under bounded eval / outcomes / nuisance L∞ / overlap.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DREvalBounded Θ_set eval M_Θ` | `def`, `Prop` | Uniform `|eval θ x| ≤ M_Θ` over `Θ_set × γ`. |
| `DROutcomeBounded S M_Y` | `def`, `Prop` | Bounded outcomes type-level: `0 ≤ M_Y ∧ ∀ z : γ × Bool × ℝ, |z.2.2| ≤ M_Y`. The `∀ z` form (rather than `∀ ω`) is needed because `UniformlyBoundedLoss` quantifies over all `z`. |
| `DRNuisanceOverlap S Θ_set h ε` | `def`, `Prop` | Strict-overlap predicate: `0 < ε ∧ ε ≤ 1/2 ∧ ∀ x, ε ≤ h.e_fn x ∧ h.e_fn x ≤ 1 − ε`. Carries the propensity-bracketing slice condition (no longer a `True`-stub). |
| `DRNuisanceMuBounded h M_μ` | `def`, `Prop` | Uniform L∞ bound on the outcome-regression nuisance: `0 ≤ M_μ ∧ ∀ b x, |h.μ_fn b x| ≤ M_μ`. Required for boundedness of the AIPW pseudo-outcome. |
| `dr_loss_uniformly_bounded` | `theorem` | Bounded eval + outcomes + nuisance L∞ + overlap ⇒ DR squared loss is uniformly bounded over `Θ_set` at `h` by the **explicit** value `b := (M_Θ + 2·M_μ + 2·(M_Y + M_μ)/ε)²`. Proved by Codex via the AIPW pseudo-outcome triangle bound `\|phi_eta z h\| ≤ 2 M_μ + 2 (M_Y + M_μ) / ε`. (Returns `UniformlyBoundedLoss ... b` directly — no existential — so callers can write FS-style hypotheses against the explicit value.) |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_drLearner` | `theorem` | End-to-end: instantiates `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` for the DR-Learner `OSLSystem`. Takes `idx : ℕ → ↥(drOSLSystem ...).Θ_set`, `idx_dense : DenseRange idx`, and `LossContinuousOnΘset` for the DR loss in addition to the `(M_Θ, M_Y, M_μ, ε)` quartet. Concludes the tightened piecewise rate, with textbook `√(2 R_n + ε)` on nonempty folds and `√(2b)` when the fold is empty. Proved as a thin wrapper. |

### 13''''.n `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/DRLearnerEndToEnd.lean` (Phase-3 ↔ Phase-4a chain)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `oracle_inequality_drLearner_highProb` | `theorem` | **Phase-3 ↔ Phase-4a end-to-end chain (proved).** Composes `localEmpProcessModulus_drLearner` (Phase-4a DR modulus discharge) with `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM_highProb` (Phase-1 high-probability oracle inequality) to derive a closed-form high-probability rate `‖τhat n ω − τ₀‖² ≤ (4(1+σ)/σ²)·(√(if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then 2b else 2 R n + 2b·√(2 log(1/δ)/(split.foldB n).card)))² + (4/σ)·Bias_n + (4/σ)·r_opt n` with `μ`-probability ≥ `1 − δ` for the DR-Learner OSL system. Threads `idx`, `idx_dense`, `LossContinuousOnΘset`, and `DRNuisanceMuBounded` through. Phase 4a verification step 3 — **complete and sorry-free**. |

### 13''''.o `Stat/Concentration/{StarHull,Contraction,CriticalRadius,LocalRademacher,UniformDeviationLocalized}.lean` (Phase-4b localized chain)

Path-B (McDiarmid + symmetrization + contraction) localized empirical-process toolbox. Sibling to Phase-4a `Stat/Concentration/{Rademacher,...}` shims, but contains *new* formalization (not vendored). Headline: `localized_uniform_deviation` with shape `r·δ_n + δ_n²` matching Foster–Syrgkanis 2019 Lemma 29 up to log factors. Plan reference: `.claude/plans/i-just-added-a-rosy-hennessy.md`, Phase 4b.

#### 13''''.o.1 `Stat/Concentration/StarHull.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `starHull F` | `def`, `Set (𝒳 → ℝ)` | `{α • F i : i ∈ ι, α ∈ [0,1]}`. Bartlett–Bousquet–Mendelson 2005 star-hull around 0. |
| `starHullIndex F` | `noncomputable def` | Subtype re-index `{f // f ∈ starHull F}` for downstream Rademacher arguments. |
| `mem_starHull_self`, `zero_mem_starHull`, `starHull_mono`, `starHull_smul_mem` | `lemma`s (`sorry`) | Algebra: `F i ∈ starHull F`, `0 ∈ starHull F`, monotonicity, scalar closure on `[0,1]`. |

#### 13''''.o.2 `Stat/Concentration/Contraction.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LipschitzAt0 φ L` | `def`, `Prop` | `φ 0 = 0 ∧ ∀ x y, |φ x − φ y| ≤ L |x − y|`. The Lipschitz-with-`φ(0)=0` regime needed by Ledoux–Talagrand. |
| `rademacher_contraction` | `theorem` | **Ledoux–Talagrand 1991 Thm 4.12 (signed form).** `R̂_n^{noabs}(φ ∘ F, S) ≤ L · R̂_n^{noabs}(F, S)` for L-Lipschitz `φ` with `φ 0 = 0`. **Proved by Codex** via the hybrid-induction argument: a sign-pair Lipschitz inequality (`sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled`) bounds the two-sign sum at one coordinate, then a hybrid average `hybridAverage` interpolating between `φ ∘ F` and `L · F` is shown monotone in the number of swapped coordinates (`hybridAverage_mono_step`); telescoping recovers the headline. |
| `rademacher_contraction_abs` | `theorem` | Absolute-value form: `R̂_n(φ ∘ F, S) ≤ 2·L·R̂_n(F, S)`. **Proved by Codex** by reducing to the signed form applied to `φ` and `−φ` (via `lipschitzAt0_neg`), bounding `|x| ≤ x⁺ + x⁻` over the Rademacher sup. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_contraction_abs_of_bddAbove` | `theorem` | **Infinite-index contraction.** Same `R̂_n(φ ∘ F, S) ≤ 2L·R̂_n(F, S)` as `rademacher_contraction_abs` but for an ARBITRARY `[Nonempty ι]` (not just `Fintype`), assuming a uniform bound `|F i x| ≤ M`. Reduces the infinite-index case to the Fintype lemma by choosing, for each of the finitely many sign vectors, an `ε`-approximate maximizer; their finite collection is a finite subindex. This is what lets the squared-loss kernel/lasso ERM (whose weight ball is infinite) use the contraction. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_sub_le` | `theorem` | **Sub-additivity over class differences.** `R̂_n(F − G, S) ≤ R̂_n(F, S) + R̂_n(G, S)` (uniform bounds on `F`, `G`). Splits the centered squared loss `⟪w,x⟩² − 2y⟪w,x⟩` into its quadratic and cross parts. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_smul_class`, `empiricalRademacherComplexity_without_abs_smul_class` | `theorem` | Smoke test: `R̂_n(c•F) = |c|·R̂_n(F)` (resp. signed analogue). **Proved.** |
| `rademacher_swap_step`, `rademacher_telescope` | `private lemma` | Sign-swap proof scaffolds; **proved** as wrappers around the headline `rademacher_contraction` after the hybrid-induction proof landed. Kept private to flag the proof outline. |
| `SignAtom`, `flipSign`, `flipSignEquiv` | `private def` / `Equiv` | `Signs n` infrastructure: `SignAtom = {-1, 1}` finset element, single-coordinate sign flip and the sign-flip equivalence used to re-index `∑_σ` after a coordinate swap. **Proved.** |
| `sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled` | `private lemma` | Sign-pair Lipschitz inequality on `iSup`: `(⨆ a + c·s·φ b) + (⨆ a − c·s·φ b) ≤ (⨆ a + c·s·L·b) + (⨆ a − c·s·L·b)` for `s ∈ {−1, +1}`, `c ≥ 0`. The single-coordinate engine of the contraction proof. **Proved.** |
| `hybridInner`, `hybridAverage`, `hybridBase` | `private noncomputable def` | The hybrid signed-Rademacher quantity: at parameter `m`, replaces `φ(F i (S k))` by `L·F i (S k)` for `k < m` and keeps `φ` for `k ≥ m`. `hybridInner` is the per-`σ`-per-`i` summand; `hybridAverage` is `(1/2ⁿ) Σ_σ ⨆_i hybridInner`; `hybridBase` factors out the `k`-th coordinate for the swap step. |
| `hybridAverage_mono_step` | `private lemma` | Monotonicity of the hybrid average in `m`: `hybridAverage m ≤ hybridAverage (m+1)` for `m < n`, by applying `sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled` to the two-sign sum at coordinate `m` after re-indexing via `flipSignEquiv`. **Proved.** |
| `rademacher_contraction_core` | `private lemma` | The actual contraction inequality on the without-abs form, before the `L`-scaling re-arrangement. Iterates `hybridAverage_mono_step` from `m = 0` to `m = n`, then closes by the without-abs scaling lemma. **Proved.** |
| `lipschitzAt0_neg` | `private lemma` | `LipschitzAt0 φ L → LipschitzAt0 (fun x => −φ x) L`. **Proved.** |
| `iSup_abs_le_iSup_add_iSup_neg_of_exists_zero` | `private lemma` | `⨆ |x i| ≤ (⨆ x i) + (⨆ (−x i))` when `0` lies in the family (i.e. for any class containing the zero function). **Proved.** |
| `withZero F` | `private def` | The class `F` augmented with the zero function, used to ensure both sup's are non-negative for the abs-form bound. |

#### 13''''.o.3 `Stat/Concentration/CriticalRadius.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `starHullBall F norm r` | `def`, `Set (𝒳 → ℝ)` | `starHull F ∩ {f : norm f ≤ r}`. |
| `starHullZeroOut F norm r` | `noncomputable def`, `starHullParam ι → 𝒳 → ℝ` | Radius-localized star-hull family: active rays evaluate as `α·F_i`, inactive rays are zero. |
| `starHullZeroOutScaleCoeff`, `starHullZeroOut_inner_term_eq`, `starHullZeroOut_inner_sup_eq` | scale coefficient and Rademacher inner-term identities | Public star-hull scale-contraction helpers used by finite-VC measurability and Massart arguments. |
| `RademacherUpperBound F norm μ X n ψ` | `def`, `Prop` | Uniform Rademacher-complexity envelope on `starHullBall` (deterministic / population — Risk #7 option (a)). |
| `criticalRadius ψ` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `sInf {δ > 0 : ψ δ ≤ δ²}` — Bartlett–Bousquet–Mendelson 2005 critical radius. |
| `SubRoot ψ` | `def`, `Prop` | Sub-root: `ψ ≥ 0`, `ψ r / r` non-increasing, `ψ` non-decreasing. |
| `criticalRadius_nonneg`, `criticalRadius_le` | `lemma`s | Basic structural lemmas. **Proved by Codex.** (Earlier scaffold included `criticalRadius_pos_of_subRoot`; removed because it is false for `ψ ≡ 0` under the displayed sub-root hypotheses alone — needs an additional non-degeneracy / continuity assumption that no downstream consumer in Phase 4b needs.) |
| `subRoot_homogeneity` | `lemma` | **Sub-root inequality.** For sub-root `ψ` and `δ* := criticalRadius ψ`, if `r ≥ δ*` then `ψ r ≤ r·δ*`. The single line everything downstream cites. **Proved by Codex.** |

#### 13''''.o.4 `Stat/Concentration/LocalRademacher.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `localRademacherComplexity F norm μ X n r` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Localized Rademacher complexity, **zero-out form**: the FoML `rademacherComplexity` of the family `fun g x => if norm g.val ≤ r then g.val x else 0` over `starHullIndex F`. The earlier `sInf`-over-upper-bounds scaffold was anti-monotone in `r`; the zero-out form fixes the direction (more `r` ⇒ more non-zero entries ⇒ larger sup). |
| `localRademacherComplexity_nonneg` | `lemma` | Non-negativity via `integral_nonneg` + `Real.iSup_nonneg` + `abs_nonneg` (each summand of `empiricalRademacherComplexity` is an absolute value). **Proved.** |
| `localRademacherComplexity_mono_r` | `lemma` (`sorry`) | Monotonicity in radius. The pointwise comparison is straightforward; lifting through the integral needs an integrability witness on the upper function. **Phase-4b TODO.** |
| `localRademacherComplexity_le_starHull` | `lemma` (`sorry`) | **Star-hull lifting** (BBM 2005 step 2). Same integrability TODO as `_mono_r`. |
| `localRademacher_le_critical_radius` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | **Sub-root inequality, headline form.** For `r ≥ criticalRadius ψ`, `localRademacherComplexity F norm μ X n r ≤ r · criticalRadius ψ`. Combines `_le_starHull` with `subRoot_homogeneity`. **Phase-4b TODO.** |

#### 13''''.o.5 `Stat/Concentration/UniformDeviationLocalized.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LocalizedRegime Ω ι 𝒳 F norm μ X` | `structure` | Input bundle (Phase 4b redesign): uniform bound `b` on `|F i (X ω)|`, and a sub-root Rademacher envelope `ψ` on `starHullZeroOut F norm r` (uniform in `n`). The Lipschitz `φ` slot has been **dropped** — the envelope `ψ` lives directly on the centred loss class. Callers wanting a parameter-class envelope + Lipschitz `φ` link can layer a contraction wrapper (deferred — see `Contraction.lean`). |
| `localized_uniform_deviation` | `theorem` | **Path-B fixed-`n` headline.** Under `LocalizedRegime`, with probability `≥ 1 − δ` over `μⁿ`, uniformly over `i` of `norm`-radius `≤ r`, the centred deviation is bounded by `4·r·δ_n + b·√(2 log(1/δ)/n)` where `δ_n := criticalRadius ψ`. The `L_φ` factor is **gone** (absorbed into `ψ`). Quarantines the McDiarmid step inside the proof so a future Talagrand upgrade is a drop-in (Risk #9). **Proved.** |
| `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` | `theorem` | Reusable Foster–Syrgkanis Lemma 29 peeling layer over `localized_uniform_deviation`: for any public upper radius `ρ ≥ criticalRadius (ψ n)`, diameter cap `Rmax`, radius-uniform Rademacher prerequisites, and peeling slack `b·√(2 log(2(K+1)/δ)/n) ≤ ρ²`, produces a single `μⁿ`-event on which each class element satisfying `0 ≤ norm(F_i) ≤ Rmax` obeys `|(P_n − P)F_i| ≤ 8ρ·norm(F_i) + 5ρ²`. This is the generic abstraction intended for OSL, NPIV, and future localized EP users. **Proved.** |
| `localized_uniform_deviation_singleton` | `theorem` | Singleton smoke-test stub. **Proved.** |

#### 13''''.o.6 `Stat/Concentration/LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean`

In-expectation (and self-localizing offset) forms of the localized deviation bound, for downstream rate arguments that need an expectation rather than a high-probability envelope. Both are general empirical-process facts over a `LocalizedRegime`; the margin-coupled / VC specializations (turning the `norm`-radius into a problem-specific regret radius) are supplied by the caller. Built to discharge the localized finite-VC envelope gates of `stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `localized_uniform_deviation_expectation` | `theorem` | **Fixed-radius in-expectation envelope.** Steps 1–2 of the Path-B argument (symmetrization via FoML `uniform_deviation_expectation_le_two_smul_rademacher_complexity` + critical radius via `localRademacher_le_critical_radius`), stopping before the McDiarmid tail. For the radius-`r` zero-out localization, `𝔼[uniformDeviation over {norm(F i) ≤ r}] ≤ 2·r·criticalRadius(ψ n)`. The in-expectation analogue of `localized_uniform_deviation`. **Proved (Codex), axiom-clean.** |
| `localized_offset_expectation` | `theorem` | **Self-localizing offset positive-part envelope.** Given a margin coupling `norm(F i) ≤ A·(Δ i)^κ` (the caller's regret radius `Δ`, `0<κ<1`), `𝔼[⨆ i, {2·|(Pₙ−P)F i| − Δ i/4}_+] ≤ offsetPeelingConstantC (1/8) κ · (16ρA)^{1/(1−κ)} + 10ρ² + 4·R.b·δ`. Assembled from the high-probability `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp`, the deterministic Young/AM-GM optimisation `OffsetPeeling.offset_peeling_coeff`, and a bounded bad-event tail split. Caller picks `ρ` = VC critical-radius rate and `δ` polynomially small for a clean `(B²/n)^{A_α}(log n)^p` rate. **Proved (Codex), axiom-clean.** |

### 13''''.p `Estimation/OSL/Modulus/Localized.lean` (Phase-4b bridge)

Sibling to `OSL/Modulus.lean` (Phase 4a). Discharges `LocalEmpProcessModulus` from `localized_uniform_deviation` with the localized rate `ρ_n ≈ criticalRadius ψ`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSLLocalizedRegime S S_iid split g idx norm ψ b` | `def`, `Prop` | Phase 4b redesigned predicate: `0 ≤ b`, centred-loss boundedness `|ℓ z θ g − ℓ z θ₀ g| ≤ b`, `SubRoot ψ`, and `RademacherUpperBound` for the centred ℕ-indexed loss class (re-indexed via dense `idx : ℕ → S.Θ_set`) on a fold-B-sized population sample. The Lipschitz-link slot `L_φ` is **dropped** — the envelope `ψ` lives directly on the centred loss class. |
| `empRiskFoldB_centered_abs_le` | `private lemma` | Pointwise centred-loss bound `\|ℓ z θ g − ℓ z θ₀ g\| ≤ b` averages to `\|empRiskFoldB θ − empRiskFoldB θ₀\| ≤ b`. **Proved.** |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_bounded` | `theorem` | **Uniform-bound fallback bridge (legitimate low-hypothesis option).** Under `OSLLocalizedRegime` + a population centred-risk bound `hpop_center : ∀ θ ∈ Θ_set, \|S.L θ g − S.L θ₀ g\| ≤ b`, concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the *constant* envelope `ρ n := √(2b)`. **Proved.** Does NOT use `ψ`, critical-radius, or any Lipschitz / Rademacher-integrability machinery — purely bounded-loss. Useful when those inputs are unavailable; rate does not vanish in `n`. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_sharp` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b sharp headline — Foster–Syrgkanis Lemma 29 form (proved).** Under `OSLLocalizedRegime` plus an explicit Lipschitz constant `(L : ℝ) (hL_nonneg) (hF_lip : norm (z↦ℓ z θ g − ℓ z θ₀ g) ≤ L · ‖θ−θ₀‖)`, diameter `Rmax`, the n-dependent critical-radius hypotheses, Rademacher prerequisites, and the **peeling-aware** FS critical-radius lower bound `hδ_dom : ∀ n K, 0 < (split.foldB n).card → Rmax ≤ δ_n · 2^K → b · √(2 log(2(K+1)/δ)/m) ≤ δ_n²`, concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the FS Lemma 29 rate `ρ n := if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then √(2b) else (8L+3) · criticalRadius (ψ (split.foldB n).card)`. **Proved (Codex)** via dyadic peeling + fold-B transport (mirroring `OSL/Modulus.lean:583-606`) + FS-absorption. Axiom-clean. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_singleton` | `theorem` | Singleton smoke-test (Θ_set = {θ₀} ⇒ ρ ≡ 0). **Proved.** |

### 13''''.q `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/LocalizedDRLearner.lean` (Phase-4b end-to-end)

Apply `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized` to the DR-Learner. Verification step 6 of Phase 4b.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DRCriticalRadius S Θ_set eval idx h norm ψ` | `def`, `Prop` | Phase 4b redesigned predicate: `SubRoot ψ ∧ ∀ n, RademacherUpperBound (centred quadratic DR-loss class indexed by idx) norm S.P_Z id n ψ`. The DR centred class is `(φ_h(z) − eval (idx k) z.1)² − (φ_h(z) − S.τ_val z.1)²`. (Deterministic / population — Risk #10.) |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_localized_drLearner` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b success criterion (sharp Foster–Syrgkanis Lemma 29 form, proved).** End-to-end: instantiates `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_sharp` for the DR-Learner OSL system. Takes `(M_Θ, M_Y, M_μ, ε)` boundedness/overlap, `[Countable Θ_set]`, dense `idx`, `DRCriticalRadius`, an explicit Lipschitz constant `(L, hL_nonneg, hF_lip)` for the centred DR-loss class, the diameter `Rmax`, the n-dependent critical-radius facts, the Rademacher prerequisites, and the **peeling-aware** FS lower-bound `hδ_dom : ∀ n K, 0 < |B(n)| → Rmax ≤ δ_n · 2^K → 2·b_loss·√(2 log(2(K+1)/δ)/m) ≤ δ_n²` (stated against `b_loss = (M_Θ + 2·M_μ + 2·(M_Y+M_μ)/ε)²`). Concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the FS Lemma 29 rate `ρ n := if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then √(2b) else (8L+3) · criticalRadius (ψ (split.foldB n).card)`. When chained with `OracleInequality`, gives the Foster–Syrgkanis `O_p(δ_n² + nuisance²)` rate. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_localized_drLearner_bounded` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b end-to-end (uniform-bound fallback variant).** Working chain that calls `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_bounded` with `b := 2·dr_loss_bound`. Concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the *constant* rate `ρ n := √(2 · 2b)` — does NOT use the critical radius. **Proved.** Useful as a low-hypothesis option when the sharp hypotheses (Lipschitz, Rademacher integrability, FS lower bound) are not available. |

### 13''''.r `Estimation/CATE/OSL/Modulus/LocalizedDRLearnerEndToEnd.lean` (Phase-4b localized oracle chain)

Composes the sharp localized DR-Learner modulus with the generic OSL oracle inequality.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `oracle_inequality_localized_drLearner_highProb` | `theorem` | Chains `localEmpProcessModulus_localized_drLearner` with `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM_highProb` to obtain the DR-Learner high-probability oracle inequality with localized rate `ρ n := if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then √(2b) else (8L+3) · criticalRadius (ψ (split.foldB n).card)`. |

## 13gm. `Estimation/GaussMarkov/` — Finite Gauss-Markov / BLUE layer

Reusable finite Gauss-Markov theory: the variance of a linear estimator, spherical/general covariance, the OLS weight via the normal-equations pseudoinverse, and the minimum-variance (BLUE) ordering theorems. Built on Mathlib `Matrix` (`mulVec`/`vecMul`/`dotProduct`, `nonsing_inv`, `PosSemidef`) and `ProbabilityTheory.covariance`/`variance`. Files: `QuadForm.lean` (variance object), `LeastNorm.lean` (algebraic ordering), `OLS.lean` (OLS weight), `Variance.lean` (probability bridge), `BLUE.lean` (variance-form BLUE). Consumed by the imputation event-study efficiency theorem (§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency). No `sorry`/`admit`/`axiom`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar` | `Matrix Obs Obs ℝ → (Obs → ℝ) → ℝ` | Variance of a linear estimator with weight `w` under covariance `Σ`: the quadratic form `w ⬝ᵥ Σ *ᵥ w` (`= wᵀΣw`) | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.SphericalErrors` | `Matrix Obs Obs ℝ → ℝ → Prop` | Spherical (homoskedastic, uncorrelated) covariance: `Σ = σ² • I` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar_spherical` | `SphericalErrors S σ → quadVar S w = σ² * (w ⬝ᵥ w)` | Under spherical errors the variance form reduces to `σ²‖w‖²` | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar_nonneg` | `S.PosSemidef → 0 ≤ quadVar S w` | Variance form of a PSD covariance is nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.colSpan_dotProduct_leftNull` | `z ᵥ* X = 0 → (X *ᵥ g) ⬝ᵥ z = 0` | Column span ⟂ left null space (the orthogonality at the heart of Gauss-Markov) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.gauss_markov_spherical` | `SphericalErrors S σ → wStar = X *ᵥ g → wStar ᵥ* X = c → w ᵥ* X = c → quadVar S wStar ≤ quadVar S w` | **Finite Gauss-Markov, spherical:** among weights with the same unbiasedness image, an OLS weight (in the column span of `X`) minimizes variance (Pythagoras) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.gauss_markov_gls` | `S.PosSemidef → S *ᵥ wStar = X *ᵥ g → wStar ᵥ* X = c → w ᵥ* X = c → quadVar S wStar ≤ quadVar S w` | **Finite Gauss-Markov, general (GLS):** for any PSD covariance, a GLS weight (`Σ wStar` in column span) minimizes variance | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight` | `Matrix Obs Param ℝ → (Param → ℝ) → (Obs → ℝ)` | OLS weight via the normal-equations pseudoinverse: `X (XᵀX)⁻¹ c` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_unbiased` | `IsUnit (Xᵀ * X).det → olsWeight X c ᵥ* X = c` | The OLS weight is unbiased when `XᵀX` is invertible (full column rank) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_mem_colSpan` | `olsWeight X c = X *ᵥ ((Xᵀ * X)⁻¹ *ᵥ c)` | The OLS weight lies in the column span of `X` | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_blue_spherical` | `IsUnit (Xᵀ*X).det → SphericalErrors S σ → w ᵥ* X = c → quadVar S (olsWeight X c) ≤ quadVar S w` | OLS is BLUE (variance-form) under spherical errors | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.covMatrix` | `(Obs → Ω → ℝ) → Measure Ω → Matrix Obs Obs ℝ` | Covariance matrix of a finite random family, `Σ i j = cov(Yᵢ, Yⱼ)` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_linearCombination` | `(∀ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 μ) → Var[∑ i, w i * Y i; μ] = quadVar (covMatrix Y μ) w` | **Probability bridge:** the variance of a random linear estimator equals the covariance quadratic form | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.SphericalFamily` | `(Obs → Ω → ℝ) → Measure Ω → ℝ → Prop` | Random family with spherical errors: each cell variance `σ²`, distinct cells uncorrelated | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.sphericalFamily_covMatrix` | `SphericalFamily Y μ σ → SphericalErrors (covMatrix Y μ) σ` | A spherical family has scalar-identity covariance matrix | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.covMatrix_posSemidef` | `(∀ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 μ) → (covMatrix Y μ).PosSemidef` | A covariance matrix is positive semidefinite | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_spherical` | `(∀ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 μ) → SphericalFamily Y μ σ → wStar = X *ᵥ g → wStar ᵥ* X = c → w ᵥ* X = c → Var[∑ wStar·Y] ≤ Var[∑ w·Y]` | **BLUE (variance form), spherical:** OLS minimizes actual `ProbabilityTheory.variance` in the linear-unbiased class | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_gls` | `(∀ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 μ) → (covMatrix Y μ) *ᵥ wStar = X *ᵥ g → wStar ᵥ* X = c → w ᵥ* X = c → Var[∑ wStar·Y] ≤ Var[∑ w·Y]` | **BLUE (variance form), general:** GLS minimizes variance for the family's own covariance | **Proved** |

---

## 13. `Estimation/ATE/` — Back-door ATE estimation and inference

Specific-system layer instantiating `POBackdoorSystem`. Mirrors
`doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/{aipw_if,plug_in_ate,dml_ate}.tex`. The
concrete `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` in `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean` remains the
production AIPW asymptotic-linearity headline; the abstract framework in
`Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/*` provides the abstractions, and `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`
is **proved** (the θ₀-shift gap was closed by the 2026-05-06 per-η̂ bilinear-remainder
refactor; see §12g.6).

### 13a. `Estimation/ATE/InfluenceFunction.lean` (umbrella)

Single-import aggregator that re-exports the five sub-modules below.
`PlugIn.lean` and `DML.lean` import this file. Refactored from the original
~900-line monolithic file.

#### 13a.1 `Estimation/ATE/Setup.lean`

`BackdoorEstimationSystem` structure, data law, value-space estimand.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BackdoorEstimationSystem P γ` | structure | Extends `POBackdoorSystem` with value-space `μ_val : Bool × γ → ℝ`, `e_val : γ → (0,1)`, and the **observable** compat fields `μ_reg_compat : μ_val d ∘ factualX =ᵐ adjustedCE d` (the ML/regression target — NO assumptions; see `regression_adjustment`), `e_compat : propScore true =ᵐ e_val ∘ factualX`. **The counterfactual reading `μ[Y(d)\|σX] =ᵐ μ_val d ∘ factualX` is no longer a field** — it is the derived lemma `μ_compat` below. |
| `BackdoorEstimationSystem.μ_compat` | `(hA : Assumptions) (d) → μ[Y(d)\|σX] =ᵐ μ_val d ∘ factualX` | **Derived counterfactual compatibility** (was a structure field): `cate_backdoor hA d` composed with `μ_reg_compat`. The counterfactual binding now correctly requires identification, not structural data. **Proved.** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.toBackdoorEstimationSystem` | `(S : POBackdoorSystem) (hov : two-sided overlap a.e.) (hY : Integrable factualY) → BackdoorEstimationSystem P γ` | **Derivability constructor.** Certifies the structure adds no assumptions beyond `POBackdoorSystem` + overlap + integrability: `μ_val := regFn`, `e_val := eLift` clamped into (0,1) off-support; all fields discharged (`μ_reg_compat` from `regression_adjustment`, `e_pos`/`e_lt_one` from the clamp, `e_compat` from overlap). **Proved.** |
| `StrictOverlap S ε` | `Prop` | `0 < ε`, `ε ≤ 1/2`, and `ε ≤ propScore true ω ≤ 1−ε` a.s. |
| `P_X`, `P_Z`, `factualZ` | `def`s | Covariate marginal `μ.map factualX`, joint data law `μ.map (X,A,Y)`, and the data triple. |
| `measurable_factualZ` | `Measurable S.factualZ` | Product-measurability. **Proved.** |
| `θ₀ S` | `def` | Value-space estimand `∫ x, μ_val(1,x) − μ_val(0,x) ∂P_X`. |
| `θ₀_eq_ATE` | `S.θ₀ = S.ATE` | Reduces to `integral_map` + `μ_compat hA` + `condExp_sub` + `integral_condExp`. **Proved.** |

#### 13a.2 `Estimation/ATE/AIPWMoment.lean`

AIPW moment, influence function, nuisance space.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `projX`, `projA`, `projY`, `indA` | `def`s | Projections from `(γ × Bool × ℝ)` and the real-valued indicator of `{a = true}`. |
| `aipwMoment z μ_fn e_fn θ` | `def` | The AIPW moment `m_AIPW` from `def:est-aipw-moment`. |
| `ψ_AIPW S z` | `def` | Influence function at the truth `m_AIPW(η₀, z, θ₀)`. |
| `NuisanceVec γ` | structure | Nuisance pair `(μ_fn, e_fn)` with measurability witnesses. Componentwise `Zero`/`Add`/`Neg`/`Sub`/`SMul ℝ`/`AddCommGroup`/`Module ℝ` instances. **Proved.** |
| `η₀ S` | `def` | The truth as a `NuisanceVec`. |
| `H_ε ε` | `def` | Overlap-bounded realization set `{η : ε ≤ η.e_fn ≤ 1−ε on γ}`, the Lean instantiation of `def:est-aipw-nuisance-space`. |
| `aipwMomentFunctional` | `def` | The AIPW moment as a `NuisanceVec γ → (γ × Bool × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ` family, suitable for `NeymanOrthogonal`. |

#### 13a.3 `Estimation/ATE/MeanZero.lean`

Helpers and the headline mean-zero lemma.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `measurable_ψ_AIPW` | `Measurable S.ψ_AIPW` | **Proved.** |
| `cond_exp_residual_zero` | `μ[indD d · (Y − μ_val d (X)) \| σX] =ᵐ 0` | Reusable backdoor-factorization lemma; cites `cate_backdoor`, `μ_compat`, `condExp_sub`. **Proved.** |
| `theta_zero_factualX_integral` | `S.θ₀ = ∫ ω, (μ_val 1 X(ω) − μ_val 0 X(ω)) ∂P.μ` | One-line `integral_map` pull-back. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_factualZ_integral_zero` | `∫ ω, ψ_AIPW(factualZ ω) ∂P.μ = 0` | Pointwise decomposition into 4 pieces; main + constant cancel, both IPW corrections vanish via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_mean_zero` | `∫ ψ_AIPW dP_Z = 0` | One-line `integral_map` reduction to `aipw_factualZ_integral_zero`. **Proved.** |

#### 13a.4 `Estimation/ATE/ScorePullout.lean`

Shared score pull-out lemmas used by `MeanZero.lean` and `Remainder*.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `e_val_label` | `e_val_label true = e_val`, `e_val_label false = 1 - e_val` | Value-space propensity conversion for labels. **Proved.** |
| `measurable_e_val_label` | `Measurable (S.e_val_label d)` | Measurability of value-space propensity label. **Proved.** |
| `propScore_false_ae` | `propScore false =ᵐ 1 - propScore true` | Linearity of conditional expectation + `indicator_add_indicator_not`. **Proved.** |
| `propScore_eq_e_val_label_ae` | `propScore d =ᵐ e_val_label d (X)` | `σ(X)`-conditional expectation of the treatment indicator. **Proved.** |
| `weighted_residual_integral_zero` | `∫ g(X) · indD d · (Y − μ_val d X) ∂P.μ = 0` for measurable `g` and integrable product | σ(X)-pull-out + `h_residual_ce_zero` + `integral_condExp`. **Proved.** |
| `indicator_to_propScore_integral` | `∫ f(X) · indD d ∂P.μ = ∫ f(X) · e_val_label d (X) ∂P.μ` | Rewrites indicators inside integrals to value-space scores. **Proved.** |

#### 13a.5 `Estimation/ATE/Archived/` — Gâteaux-derivative Neyman witness (archived 2026-05-06)

The textbook Gâteaux-derivative Neyman orthogonality witness for AIPW
(`aipw_neyman` and the supporting `NeymanAux/*` helpers) was archived after
the 2026-05-06 refactor of `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`, which closes the DML
asymptotic-linearity proof through the per-η̂ bilinear-remainder bound
(`aipw_remainder_bound`) directly without going through a directional
derivative.  The archived files are retained for reference; they no longer
participate in the live build.

* `Archived/Neyman.lean` — `aipw_neyman` (Sorry; Gâteaux-form Neyman orthogonality on `H_ε`).
* `Archived/NeymanAux.lean` — umbrella import.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Overlap.lean` — perturbation algebra and overlap bounds.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Score.lean` — assignment-residual pull-out.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/{MuTrueResidual,MuFalseResidual,ETrueResidual,EFalseResidual}.lean` — four directional residual-zero integrals.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Residual.lean` — residual umbrella.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Directional.lean` — `aipwDirDeriv`, `measurable_aipwDirDeriv`, `aipwMoment_diff_quotient_tendsto`, `integral_aipwDirDeriv_eq_zero` (Sorry), `integral_aipwMoment_diff_quotient_tendsto` (Sorry).

#### 13a.6 `Estimation/ATE/FiniteVar.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_finite_var` | `Integrable (ψ_AIPW²) P_Z` | Pushforward to Ω + `MemLp` arithmetic + conditional Jensen on `μ_val(d, X)`. Requires `Integrable (factualY²)` and `Integrable ((YofD d)²)`. **Proved.** |

#### 13a.7 `Estimation/ATE/AIPWScoreL2.lean`

L²(P_Z) continuity of `m_AIPW(η, ·, θ₀)` in `η` on `H_ε`. Consumed by the DML `R₂` empirical-process step.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `K_AIPW ε` | `def` | Lipschitz constant `1 + 2/ε + 2/ε²`. |
| `aipw_score_diff_pointwise_bound` | `∀ᵐ z ∂P_Z, \|m_AIPW(η,z,θ₀) − m_AIPW(η₀,z,θ₀)\| ≤ K_AIPW ε · (Σ_a \|Δμ_a\| + (\|y−μ₁\|+\|y−μ₀\|)·\|Δe\|)` | `P_Z`-a.e. Lipschitz on `H_ε`. Statement is a.e. (not pointwise) so `S.StrictOverlap ε` (`P.μ`-a.s.) suffices: it transfers via `e_compat` and pushforward to `ε ≤ e_val ∘ projX ≤ 1−ε` `P_Z`-a.s., then the algebraic Lipschitz step closes. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `YMuVal_residual_sq` | `def` | `(\|y − μ_val(1,x)\| + \|y − μ_val(0,x)\|)²` — fixed L¹(P_Z) cross-term integrand. |
| `yMuVal_residual_sq_integrable` | `Integrable YMuVal_residual_sq P_Z` | Pushforward `h_y2 + h_yd2` along `factualZ`, plus conditional-Jensen for `μ_val(d,X)² ∈ L²(P_X)`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `aipw_score_diff_isLittleOp_one` | individual rates + `MemLp` of nuisance differences ⇒ `‖m_AIPW(η̂,·,θ₀) − m_AIPW(η₀,·,θ₀)‖_{L²(P_Z)} = o_p(1)` | Uses the a.e. Lipschitz bound, `P_Z.map projX = P_X`, and a truncation argument for the residual-weighted propensity-error cross term. **Proved.** |

### 13b. `Estimation/ATE/PlugIn.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `plugInEstimator` | `def` | `(1/\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i ∈ B(n)} (μ̂(n)(1, X_i) − μ̂(n)(0, X_i))`. |
| `ψ_plugin` | `def` | Plug-in IF `ψ_plugin(z) = μ_val(1, x) − μ_val(0, x) − θ₀`. |
| `plugIn_isAsymLinear` | Anchor (C) | Asymptotic linearity at `θ₀` with IF `ψ_plugin`, **indexed over `split.foldB`**. Hypotheses now include joint measurability `h_mu_meas : ∀ n a, Measurable (fun (p : P.Ω × γ) => μ_hat n p.1 a p.2)`, the new joint fold-A measurability `h_mu_uncurry_foldA : ∀ n a, Measurable[m_A.prod σ_γ] (fun p => μ_hat n p.1 a p.2)` (consumed by the new `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` bridge), and `MemLp` `h_mu_memLp : ∀ n ω a, MemLp (fun x => μ_hat n ω a x) 2 S.P_X` (classical implicit assumptions). All three bullets **proved**: `mean_zero`, `finite_var` inline; `remainder` decomposes into `G + B` (centered fold-B sum + bias) via `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one` plus the `S.P_Z.map projX = S.P_X` bridge. **Modulo the single probability sorry in `IIDCenteredSum.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le` + the pre-existing Markov + DCT sorry in `foldB_centered_sum_truncated_isLittleOp_one`**. |
| `plugIn_tendstoNormal` | `√\|B(n)\| (θ̂ⁿ − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ²_plugin dP_Z)` | "In particular ..." clause of `thm:est-plug-in-ate-al`: composes `plugIn_isAsymLinear` with `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` from `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`. **Proved** modulo the existing AL `sorry`. The `√n`-rate form `√n (θ̂ⁿ − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, σ²/c)` is one further Slutsky scaling step (cf. `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB_sqrt_n`). |

### 13c. `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlEstimator` | `def` | `(1/\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i ∈ B(n)} m_AIPW(η̂(n), Z_i, _)`. |
| `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` | Anchor (B) | Asymptotic linearity at `θ₀` with IF `ψ_AIPW`, **indexed over `split.foldB`**. **Migrated 2026-05-06** to a thin wrapper over the abstract `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear` (Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AIPWInstance.lean): builds `η_hat n ω : NuisanceVec γ` from `(μ_hat n ω, e_hat n ω)`, derives per-η̂_n L²-of-differences from `h_mu_memLp` / `h_e_memLp` (plus inline derivations of `MemLp (S.μ_val a)` via condExp and `MemLp S.e_val` via boundedness), translates the production rate hypotheses into the abstract `ρ₁ = ‖Δμ_T‖₂ + ‖Δμ_F‖₂` / `ρ₂ = ‖Δe‖₂` shape (sum-of-`o_p` arguments), passes the score measurability/integrability bundle, applies the abstract theorem, then transports the conclusion field-by-field via two pointwise equalities: (i) IF equality `−J₀_inv · aipwMomentFunctional S.η₀ z S.θ₀ = S.ψ_AIPW z` (`J₀ = −1` ⇒ `−J₀_inv = 1`); (ii) rescaled-error equality `√\|B(n)\| · (dmlChern − θ₀) = √\|B(n)\| · (dmlEstimator − θ₀)` proved by case-split on `\|B(n)\| = 0` (both sides vanish via `√0 = 0`) vs `\|B(n)\| > 0` (algebraic identity using `aipwMoment(z, μ̂, ê, 0) = aipwMoment(z, μ̂, ê, S.θ₀) + S.θ₀`). The two-step transport correctly handles the empty-fold edge case where the underlying estimators differ pointwise but their rescaled errors agree. Net effect: the original ~615-line proof body (centered fold-B sum + bias decomposition expanded inline) is replaced by ~150 lines of structural translation, with all heavy lifting delegated to the OrthogonalMoments/ abstractions. New hypothesis vs the old version: `h_e_val_pointwise : ∀ x, ε ≤ S.e_val x ∧ S.e_val x ≤ 1 − ε` (needed to construct `aipwGeneralMoment`'s `η₀_mem` field). The `h_L2 : ∀ η ∈ H_ε ε, …` hypothesis was NOT added — it's eliminated by the parallel (2b) refactor of `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`. **Proved** (no sorries). |
| `dml_ATE_tendstoNormal` | `√\|B(n)\| (θ̂ⁿ − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ²_AIPW dP_Z)` | "In particular ..." clause of `thm:est-dml-ate-al`: composes `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` with `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB`. **Proved.** Signature gained one extra hypothesis `h_e_val_pointwise` (forwarded to `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` after the 2026-05-06 migration). The `√n`-rate / `σ²/c` form is the same downstream Slutsky scaling. |

### 13d. `Estimation/ATE/Remainder.lean`

AIPW second-order remainder bound (split into `RemainderIdentity.lean` and
`RemainderBound.lean`).  Combines the algebraic remainder identity with the
overlap-bounded Cauchy–Schwarz step to convert the L²-product rate hypothesis
from `DML.lean` into the `o_p(n^{-1/2})` cross-term that closes `R₁` of the
three-remainder DML decomposition.  After the 2026-05-06 refactor this is the
sole route to Neyman orthogonality on the live AL critical path; the
Gâteaux-derivative witness `aipw_neyman` was archived to
`Estimation/ATE/Archived/Neyman.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_rem_const ε` | `def` | The constant `2 / (ε(1−ε))`, the uniform weight bound on `H_ε`. |
