| `plugin_bias_le_eLpNorm` | `\|∫ (μ̂ − μ_val) dP_X\| ≤ ‖μ̂ − μ_val‖_{L²(P_X)}` | Direct corollary of `abs_integral_le_eLpNorm_two` since `P_X` is a probability measure. **Proved.** (`RemainderIdentity.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_identity` | `∫ aipwMomentFunctional η z θ₀ dP_Z = ∫x ...` | Exact identity rewriting the remainder as cross terms before Cauchy–Schwarz. **Proved.** (`RemainderIdentity.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_bound` | `\|∫ aipwMomentFunctional η z θ₀ dP_Z\| ≤ aipw_rem_const ε · Σ_a ‖Δμ_a‖₂ · ‖Δe‖₂` for `η ∈ H_ε ε` | Algebraic expansion of `aipw_remainder_identity` + weight bound + Cauchy–Schwarz. **Proved.** (`RemainderBound.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_op` | `(η̂(n) ∈ H_ε ε)` ∧ `‖Δμ_a‖₂ · ‖Δe‖₂ = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ⇒ `∫ aipwMomentFunctional η̂ z θ₀ dP_Z = o_p(n^{-1/2})` | Stochastic-order corollary consumed at `DML.lean:152` (the `R₁` cross-term). **Proved.** (`RemainderBound.lean`) |

---

## 13plr. `Estimation/PLR/{Nuisance,Moment,Setup,MeanZero,RemainderBound,DML,ScoreL2,Feasible,JacobianConsistency}.lean` — Partially Linear DML

Instantiates the abstract DML `GeneralMoment` framework (§OrthogonalMoments) with
the Robinson partialling-out score for the partially linear model, built on the PO
causal layer §9g'''. Yields the doubly-robust moment whose product-rate remainder
drives √n-asymptotic normality of the DML estimator of the structural slope `θ`.
The score is a *linear score* with `m_a = −(D−m(X))²`, so the Jacobian
`J₀ = −E[(D−m_val(X))²]` is DGP-dependent (unlike AIPW's `J₀ = −1`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PLRNuisance γ` | structure | Bundled nuisance `(ℓ, m)` of two measurable covariate functions, with `AddCommGroup`/`Module ℝ` instances — the DML nuisance space `H`. |
| `plrResidual η z` | `ℝ` | The treatment residual `d − m(x)` of observation `z=(x,d,y)`. |
| `plrMomentFunctional η z θ` | `ℝ` | The Robinson partialling-out score `(y−ℓ(x)−θ(d−m(x)))·(d−m(x))`. |
| `plrMoment_decomp` | `ψ = m_a·θ + m_b` | Linear-in-parameter decomposition, `m_a = −(d−m)²`, `m_b = (y−ℓ)(d−m)`. |
| `measurable_plrMomentFunctional` | measurability | The score is measurable in the data (the nuisance carries its own measurability). |
| `PLRSystem` | structure (extends model) | Value-space estimation system: a `POPartialLinearModel` plus regression representatives `ℓ_val, m_val`, their compatibility with `E[Y∣σX]`/`E[D∣σX]`, and nondegeneracy `E[(D−m_val(X))²]≠0`. |
| `PLRSystem.P_Z` / `P_X` | `Measure …` | Joint observed-data law `(X,D,Y)` and covariate marginal. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_P_Z` | `∫ f dP_Z = ∫ f∘(X,D,Y) dμ` | Change-of-variables bridge between `P_Z`-level and `μ`-level statements. |
| `PLRSystem.plrGeneralMoment` | `GeneralMoment …` | **The DML moment instance**: score, truth `η₀=(ℓ_val,m_val)`, target `θ₀=θ`, L²(P_X) bilinear seminorms, Jacobian `J₀=−E[(D−m_val(X))²]`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_meanZero` | `MeanZero plrGeneralMoment` | The score has zero population mean at the truth (reduces, via CoV + compat, to `integral_U_resid`). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_remainder_bound` | `\|E[ψ(η,·,θ₀)]\| ≤ (1+\|θ₀\|)·ρ₁·ρ₂` | **Doubly-robust bilinear remainder**: the population bias at an estimated nuisance is the *product* of the two L²(P_X) nuisance errors; the framework's `o_p(n^{-1/2})` product-rate is exactly the textbook "both nuisances at `o_p(n^{-1/4})`." |
| `PLRSystem.plr_finite_var` | `Integrable ψ(η₀,·,θ₀)²` | Finite variance of the influence function, `E[U²·(D−m₀(X))²]<∞`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_isAsymLinear` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator …) θ₀ ψ …` | **Asymptotic linearity** of the one-step partially linear DML estimator: builds mean-zero/finite-var/doubly-robust-remainder from the PLR facts and invokes the proven `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear` engine (measurability + nuisance-rate conditions taken as hypotheses, as in the AIPW headline). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_tendstoNormal` | `√\|B(n)\|·(θ̂ⁿ−θ₀) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ² dP_Z)` | **√n-asymptotic normality** of the one-step DML estimator of the structural slope, via the generic `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` CLT bridge; asymptotic variance `E[U²(D−m₀)²]/E[(D−m₀)²]²`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_score_diff_isLittleOp_one` | `‖ψ(η̂,·,θ₀)−ψ(η₀,·,θ₀)‖_{L²(P_Z)} = o_p(1)` | Score-difference L²-consistency (`ScoreL2.lean`): from bounded truth residuals + a uniform sup-bound on the treatment-regression error + the two L²(P_X) nuisance rates. Discharges the `h_score_diff_rate` hypothesis of `plr_dml_isAsymLinear` (so it need not be assumed). Proof: the purely algebraic 5-term doubly-robust expansion + eLpNorm triangle/L∞·L² bounds. |
| `PLRSystem.plrFeasibleEstimator` | `Pₙ[(Y−ℓ̂)(D−m̂)] / Pₙ[(D−m̂)²]` | The **feasible solved** Robinson DML estimator (`Feasible.lean`) — the ratio actually computed in practice, obtained by solving the empirical orthogonal moment for `θ`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_feasible_tendstoNormal` | `√\|B(n)\|·(θ̂_feas−θ₀) ⇒ N(0, ∫ψ² dP_Z)` | **√n-asymptotic normality of the feasible solved estimator**, with the same limiting variance as the one-step estimator. Reduces to `plr_dml_tendstoNormal` via the asymptotic equivalence `√\|B\|(θ̂_feas−θ̂_os) = (J₀⁻¹−(Pₙmₐ)⁻¹)·(√\|B\|·Pₙψ) = o_p(1)·O_p(1)`, taking the fold-B Jacobian consistency `Pₙmₐ(η̂) →ₚ J₀` as a hypothesis. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_plrMomentA_η₀_eq_J₀` | `∫ mₐ(η₀,·) dP_Z = J₀` | The population integral of the partialling-out moment at the truth equals the Jacobian `J₀=−E[(D−m_val(X))²]` (`JacobianConsistency.lean`). Change of variables + the `mₐ=−(residual)²`/`residSecondMoment` defs. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_plrMomentA_diff_eq` | `∫ (mₐ(η̂)−mₐ(η₀)) dP_Z = −‖Δm‖²₂` | **Bias of the empirical-Jacobian increment**: its population integral is minus the squared L²(P_X) treatment-regression error; the cross term vanishes by orthogonality of the residual (`integral_condExpZero_mul_comp_factualX` + `condExp_resid_sigmaX`). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_jacobian_consistency` | `Pₙmₐ(η̂) →ₚ J₀` | **Fold-B Jacobian consistency** (`JacobianConsistency.lean`): the empirical partialling-out Jacobian at the estimated nuisance, averaged over fold B, converges in probability to its population value `J₀`. This is exactly the `hJ_consist` hypothesis of `plr_dml_feasible_tendstoNormal`, so feasible-estimator normality no longer needs it assumed. Proof: fold-B WLLN on `mₐ(η₀,·)` for the truth average; the bias of the increment is `−‖Δm‖²₂ = o_p(1)` by the L²(P_X) rate; the centered fluctuation is `o_p(1)` from the fold-B centered empirical-process bound renormalized by `(√\|B\|)⁻¹→0`. |

## 13'. `Estimation/ATT/` — Back-door ATT estimation and inference (parallel to §13)

Specific-system layer instantiating `POBackdoorSystem` for the **Average Treatment effect on the Treated** (ATT). Mirrors the ATE pipeline of §13 but uses the Hahn (1998) ATT AIPW form: nuisance is `(μ₀, e)` (no `μ₁`); one-sided overlap (`e ≤ 1 − ε`); rescaled moment `m_AIPW^ATT(η, z, θ) = A·(Y − μ₀(X)) − (1−A)·(e/(1−e))·(Y − μ₀(X)) − A·θ`; Jacobian `J₀ = −π_T` (not `−1`); influence function `ψ_ATT(z) = (1/π_T) · m_AIPW^ATT(η₀, z, θ_ATT)`.

Imports the Phase-1 identification result `PO/ID/Exact/ATT.lean` (§9g').

### 13'a. `Estimation/ATT/InfluenceFunction.lean` (umbrella)

Single-import aggregator that re-exports Setup, AIPWMoment, ScorePullout, MeanZero, FiniteVar.

#### 13'a.1 `Estimation/ATT/Setup.lean` — `TreatedEstimationSystem`, data law, value-space estimand

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TreatedEstimationSystem P γ` | structure | Extends `POBackdoorSystem` with value-space `μ₀_val : γ → ℝ`, `e_val : γ → ℝ`, `e_lt_one`, and the **observable** compat fields `μ₀_reg_compat : μ₀_val ∘ factualX =ᵐ adjustedCE false` (ML/regression target — NO assumptions), `e_compat : propScore true =ᵐ e_val ∘ factualX`. The counterfactual `μ[Y(0)\|σX] =ᵐ μ₀_val ∘ factualX` is the derived lemma `μ₀_compat` below. Only the control-arm μ₀ enters the nuisance. |
| `TreatedEstimationSystem.μ₀_compat` | `(hA : Assumptions) → μ[Y(0)\|σX] =ᵐ μ₀_val ∘ factualX` | **Derived counterfactual compatibility** (was a field): `cate_backdoor hA false` composed with `μ₀_reg_compat`. **Proved.** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.toTreatedEstimationSystem` | `(S : POBackdoorSystem) (hov : two-sided overlap a.e.) (hY : Integrable factualY) → TreatedEstimationSystem P γ` | **Derivability constructor.** `μ₀_val := regFn false`, `e_val := eLift` clamped below 1; fields discharged (`μ₀_reg_compat` from `regression_adjustment false`, `e_lt_one` from clamp, `e_compat` from overlap). **Proved.** |
| `OneSidedOverlap S ε` | `Prop` | `0 < ε ∧ ε ≤ 1/2 ∧ (∀ᵐ ω ∂P.μ, propScore true ω ≤ 1 − ε)`. One-sided variant of `StrictOverlap`. |
| `P_X`, `P_Z`, `factualZ` | `def`s | Same as ATE; covariate marginal, joint data law, data triple. |
| `measurable_factualZ` | `Measurable S.factualZ` | **Proved.** |
| `P_Z_map_projX_eq_P_X` | `S.P_Z.map projX = S.P_X` | **Proved.** |
| `π_val S` | `def := S.toPOBackdoorSystem.propTreated` | Marginal treatment probability `π_T = E[A]`. |
| `θ₀ S` | `def := S.toPOBackdoorSystem.adjustedATT` | Value-space ATT estimand. |
| `θ₀_eq_ATT` | `S.θ₀ = S.toPOBackdoorSystem.ATT` | One-line delegation to Phase-1 `ATT_eq_adjustedATT`. **Proved.** |

#### 13'a.2 `Estimation/ATT/AIPWMoment.lean` — ATT AIPW moment, IF, nuisance

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwMomentATT z μ₀_fn e_fn θ` | `def` | `A·(Y − μ₀(X)) − (1−A)·(e/(1−e))·(Y − μ₀(X)) − A·θ`. Reuses ATE's `projX, projA, projY, indA`. |
| `ψ_ATT S z` | `def := (1/π_val) · aipwMomentATT z μ₀_val e_val 0 − θ₀` | Centered ATT influence function for the population-`π_T` one-shot estimator. |
| `TreatedNuisanceVec γ` | structure | Nuisance pair `(μ₀_fn, e_fn)` with measurability witnesses; componentwise `Zero/Add/Neg/Sub/SMul ℝ/AddCommGroup/Module ℝ` instances. **Proved** (mechanical componentwise). |
| `η₀ S` | `def` | The truth as a `TreatedNuisanceVec`. |
| `H_ε ε` | `def := { η | ∀ x, η.e_fn x ≤ 1 − ε }` | One-sided overlap-bounded realization set. |
| `aipwMomentATTFunctional` | `def` | The ATT AIPW moment as `TreatedNuisanceVec γ → (γ × Bool × ℝ) → ℝ → ℝ`. |
| `measurable_aipwMomentATTFunctional` | `Measurable (fun z => aipwMomentATTFunctional η z θ)` | **Proved.** |

#### 13'a.3 `Estimation/ATT/ScorePullout.lean` — score pull-out helpers (one-sided)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ipwWeight_false` | `def := e_val(x) / (1 − e_val(x))` | Value-space IPW weight for the control arm. |
| `measurable_ipwWeight_false` | `Measurable S.ipwWeight_false` | **Proved.** |
| `propScore_false_ae` | `propScore false =ᵐ 1 − propScore true` | Mirrors ATE counterpart. **Proved.** |
| `propScore_eq_e_val_ae` | `propScore true =ᵐ e_val ∘ factualX` | Direct delegation to `S.e_compat`. **Proved.** |
| `propScore_false_eq_one_minus_e_val_ae` | `propScore false =ᵐ 1 − e_val ∘ factualX` | Combine `propScore_false_ae` + `e_compat`. **Proved.** |
| `residual_false_condExp_zero` | `μ[indD false · (Y − μ₀_val(X)) \| σX] =ᵐ 0` | Mirrors `cond_exp_residual_zero` for `d=false`. Uses `μ₀_compat`, consistency, `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`. **Proved.** |
| `weighted_residual_false_integral_zero` | `∫ g(X)·indD false·(Y − μ₀_val(X)) ∂P.μ = 0` | Mirrors ATE `weighted_residual_integral_zero` for control arm. **Proved.** |
| `indicator_to_propScore_integral` | `∫ f(X)·indD d ∂P.μ = ∫ f(X)·(if d=true then e_val(X) else 1−e_val(X)) ∂P.μ` | Same shape as ATE counterpart. **Proved.** |

#### 13'a.4 `Estimation/ATT/MeanZero.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `measurable_ψ_ATT` | `Measurable S.ψ_ATT` | **Proved.** |
| `measurable_aipwMomentATT_at_θ₀` | `Measurable (fun z => aipwMomentATT z μ₀_val e_val θ₀)` | **Proved.** |
| `aipw_mean_zero_ATT` | `∫ z, aipwMomentATT z μ₀_val e_val θ₀ ∂P_Z = 0` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `0 < π_val`, `Y² ∈ L¹`, `Y(0)² ∈ L¹`, `hIPW` integrability | Reduces to Phase-1 `adjustedATT_eq_aipwForm` via `μ₀_compat + cate_backdoor` ae-rewrite of the σ(X) representatives, then arithmetic cancellation `π_T·θ₀ − θ₀·π_T = 0`. **Proved.** |

#### 13'a.5 `Estimation/ATT/FiniteVar.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_finite_var_ATT` | `Integrable (aipwMomentATT z μ₀_val e_val θ₀)² P_Z` | Pushforward to Ω + `(a+b+c)² ≤ 3(a²+b²+c²)` + each summand bounded via overlap and `Y², Y(0)² ∈ L¹`. **Proved.** |
| `ipw_estimated_integrable` | for any `η` with `0 ≤ e ≤ 1−ε` `P_X`-a.e. and `μ₀ ∈ L²(P_X)`: `Integrable ((1−A)·(e/(1−e))·(Y − μ₀)) P_Z` | **Derives the per-learner `h_IPW_at` gate** from one-sided overlap + L² learners. Weight `(1−A)·e/(1−e)` is `P_Z`-a.e. bounded by `ε⁻¹` (`L^∞`), residual `Y − μ₀ ∈ L²(P_Z)`, product in `L² ⊆ L¹`. Bounds taken **a.e.** (not pointwise). **Proved.** |
| `ipw_truth_integrable` | `Integrable ((1−A)·(propScore/(1−propScore))·(Y − adjustedCE false)) P.μ` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `Y², Y(0)² ∈ L¹` | **Derives the truth-nuisance `hIPW` gate** (consumed by `aipw_mean_zero_ATT` / `att_dml_isAsymLinear`) from overlap + L² outcomes alone — no pointwise bounds. Applies `ipw_estimated_integrable` at `η₀`, transfers `P_Z → P.μ` along `factualZ`, then rewrites to PO representatives via `e_compat` / `μ₀_reg_compat`. **Proved.** |

### 13'b. `Estimation/ATT/Remainder.lean` — second-order remainder (split file)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_rem_const_ATT ε` | `def := 1/ε` | One-sided overlap weight bound (`(1−ê)⁻¹ ≤ 1/ε`). Single product (no Σ over arms). |
| `IsLittleOp.add_eventually_nonneg_rate`, `IsLittleOp.of_abs_le_const_mul` | private helpers | Local copies of the general utilities from ATE/RemainderBound. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_remainder_identity_ATT` | `∫ aipwMomentATTFunctional η z θ₀ ∂P_Z = ∫ x, ((ê − e)/(1−ê)) · (μ̂₀ − μ₀_val) ∂P_X` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `MemLp` of differences, IPW integrability | Hahn (1998) Theorem 1 eq. 7 second-order form. ATT version is one cross-product (no sum over arms). | **One sorry remaining** — the algebraic decomposition into σ(X)-residual zero-means is documented but the final `(ê·(1−e_val) − e_val·(1−ê))/(1−ê) = (ê − e_val)/(1−ê)` recombination step left as `sorry` after 3 codex iterations. (`RemainderIdentity.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_bound_ATT` | `\|∫ aipwMomentATTFunctional η z θ₀ ∂P_Z\| ≤ (1/ε) · ‖μ̂₀ − μ₀_val‖₂ · ‖ê − e_val‖₂` for `η ∈ H_ε ε` | Cauchy–Schwarz on `aipw_remainder_identity_ATT`. **Proved** modulo the identity sorry above. (`RemainderBound.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_op_ATT` | Stochastic-order corollary `o_p(n^{-1/2})` under product rate | **Proved** modulo the identity sorry. (`RemainderBound.lean`) |

### 13'c. `Estimation/ATT/AIPWScoreL2.lean`

L²(P_Z) continuity of `m_AIPW^ATT(η, ·, θ₀)` in `η` on `H_ε`. ATT version drops the d=true arm.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `K_AIPW_ATT ε` | `def := 1 + 2/ε + 2/ε²` | Lipschitz constant. |
| `K_AIPW_ATT_one_le, K_AIPW_ATT_mu_le, K_AIPW_ATT_inv_sq_le` | private monotonicity | **Proved.** |
| `aipw_score_lipschitz_ATT` | `\|m_AIPW^ATT(η,z,θ₀) − m_AIPW^ATT(η₀,z,θ₀)\| ≤ K_ATT(ε)·\|Δμ₀(x)\| + K_ATT(ε)·\|y − μ₀(x)\|·\|Δe(x)\|` | Pointwise Lipschitz on `H_ε`; ATT is two summands (no sum over arms). **Proved.** Hypothesis list extended with `h_e_pointwise : ∀ x, S.e_val x ≤ 1 − ε`. |
| `aipw_score_diff_isLittleOp_one_ATT` | individual rates + `MemLp` ⇒ `‖m_AIPW^ATT(η̂,·,θ₀) − m_AIPW^ATT(η₀,·,θ₀)‖_{L²(P_Z)} = o_p(1)` | **One sorry remaining** — the truncation step for the residual·Δe cross term needs `\|Δe\| ≤ 1`, which the current one-sided `H_ε` (`ê ≤ 1 − ε`, no lower bound) does not directly provide. Documented in a `--` comment. |

### 13'd. `Estimation/ATT/ATTInstance.lean` — abstract `GeneralMoment` instance + headline DML

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `attGeneralMoment` | `def : GeneralMoment …` | Instantiates the abstract framework. `ρ₁ = ‖Δμ₀‖_{L²(P_X)}` (single arm), `ρ₂ = ‖Δe‖_{L²(P_X)}`, `J₀ = −S.π_val`, `J₀_ne_zero` from `0 < S.π_val`. |
| `att_meanZero` | `MeanZero (attGeneralMoment …)` | One-line delegation to `aipw_mean_zero_ATT`. **Proved.** |
| `att_bilinearRem` | `∃ C, BilinearRemainder (attGeneralMoment …) C` | One-line delegation to `aipw_remainder_bound_ATT`; no Σ_a sum to unfold. **Proved.** (Conditional on the RemainderIdentity sorry.) |
| `att_dml_isAsymLinear` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (attGeneralMoment …) sample split η_hat) S.θ₀ (fun z => -J₀_inv · aipwMomentATTFunctional S.η₀ z S.θ₀) sample split.foldB` | Headline composition: `att_meanZero` + `aipw_finite_var_ATT` + per-η̂_n bilinear remainder + `aipw_score_diff_isLittleOp_one_ATT`, plumbed into `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. **Proved.** (Conditional on the AIPWScoreL2 + RemainderIdentity sorries.) |

### 13'e. `Estimation/ATT/DML.lean` — user-facing wrapper

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlEstimator_ATT` | `def := (1/π_val) · (1/\|B(n)\|) Σ_{i ∈ B(n)} aipwMomentATT(Z_i, μ̂₀(n,ω), ê(n,ω), 0)` | Empirical Hahn-form ATT estimator. |
| `ψ_ATT_integral_zero` | `∫ ψ_ATT dP_Z = 0` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `0 < π_T`, `Y², Y(0)² ∈ L¹`, truth IPW integrability | Mean-zero of the centered population-`π_T` IF. Derived from `aipw_mean_zero_ATT` + `∫ A dP_Z = π_T` and `m(η₀,z,0) = m(η₀,z,θ₀) + A·θ₀`. **Proved.** |
| `ψ_ATT_finite_var` | `Integrable (ψ_ATT)² P_Z` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `Y², Y(0)² ∈ L¹` | Finite variance of the centered IF. Derived from `aipw_finite_var_ATT` (moment-at-`θ₀` in `L²`) + boundedness of `A`, via `ψ_ATT = (1/π_T)·(m(η₀,·,θ₀) + θ₀·A) − θ₀ ∈ L²(P_Z)`. **Proved.** |
| `dml_ATT_isAsymLinear` | Asymptotic linearity at `θ₀` with IF `ψ_ATT`, indexed over `split.foldB`, under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, true/estimated propensity bounds, `0 < π_T`, `Y², Y(0)² ∈ L¹`, joint/fold-A measurability, per-ω L² nuisance hypotheses, score integrability, and individual + product L² rates | Wrapper over `att_dml_isAsymLinear`. Builds `η_hat` from `(μ̂₀, ê)`, forwards the abstract score regularity bundle, and transports the Chernozhukov remainder to the population-`π_T` estimator with the centered `ψ_ATT`. The `IsAsymLinear` mean-zero / finite-variance fields are **derived** (via `ψ_ATT_integral_zero` / `ψ_ATT_finite_var`), and the truth / per-learner IPW integrability gates are **derived** (via `ipw_truth_integrable` / `ipw_estimated_integrable`) — none taken as hypotheses. **Proved in `DML.lean`.** |

### Phase-2 status summary

Twelve files written; ten with full proofs, two with one documented sorry each. Two sorries remain in the production chain (RemainderIdentity and AIPWScoreL2 isLittleOp), both stemming from the genuinely simpler statement requirements of the ATT setting that don't directly mirror ATE. Headline mathematical content (mean-zero, finite variance, Lipschitz bound, OR/AIPW remainder identity at the population level, abstract DML asymptotic linearity composition, DML headline transport) is proved except for the documented upstream gaps.

---

## 13''. `Estimation/DTR/` — Sequential AIPW (DTR, n = 2) DML estimation and inference

Dynamic-treatment-regime counterpart of the ATE DML pipeline.  The target is the fixed-regime mean `θ₀ = E[Y(d̄)]` for `d̄ : Fin 2 → δ`; the score is the explicit two-stage sequential AIPW moment with stagewise IPW weights, centered by `θ₀`.  The population remainder is a cross-product identity over the stage-history marginals `P_H₀` and `P_H₁`, with `J₀ = -1` in the abstract `GeneralMoment` instance.

Phase A scope: this module is intentionally specialized to horizon `n = 2`; Phase B generalizes to arbitrary finite horizons.  The treatment space `δ` is discrete enough for equality indicators, via `[MeasurableSingletonClass δ]`.

### 13''a.1 `Estimation/DTR/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DTREstimationSystem P δ γ` | `structure extends PODTRSystem P 2 δ γ` | Adds fixed regime `dbar`, value-space representatives `μ₀_val/e₀_val/μ₁_val/e₁_val`, pointwise propensity bounds in `(0,1)`, and the **observable** regression-compat fields. `μ₀_reg_compat : μ₀_val ∘ factualS 0 =ᵐ innerReg dbar 1` (the outer nested regression `f₁`); `μ₁_reg_compat : μ₁_val ∘ (S₁,D₀,S₀) =ᵐ stageOneReg` (the last-stage regression `f₂`); `e₀_compat`/`e₁_compat` (observable propensities). The counterfactual `μ[Y(dbar)\|σ(histₖ)]` readings are NO LONGER fields. |
| `DTREstimationSystem.μ₀_compat` | `(hA : Assumptions) → μ[Y(dbar)\|σ(hist₀)] =ᵐ μ₀_val ∘ factualS 0` | **Derived** stage-0 counterfactual compat, via `cdtr_backdoor` (`μ[Y(dbar)\|σ(hist₀)] =ᵐ innerReg dbar (n-1)`) + `μ₀_reg_compat`. **Proved.** |
| `DTREstimationSystem.stageOneReg` | `def : P.Ω → ℝ := condExpRatio_{hist₁}(Y·1_{D₁=d̄₁}, 1_{D₁=d̄₁})` | **Observable stage-1 regression** `f₂ = E[Y\|hist₁,D₁=d̄₁]` (the paper CNSS nested-regression base case); the ML/regression target `μ₁_val` represents. |
| `DTREstimationSystem.μ₁_val_comp_eq_stageOneReg` | `μ₁_val ∘ (S₁,D₀,S₀) =ᵐ stageOneReg` | `μ₁_reg_compat` via the `stageOneReg` abbreviation. **Proved.** |
| `DTREstimationSystem.stageOneReg_indD_eq` | `(hA) → stageOneReg · indD dbar 1 =ᵐ indD dbar 1 · μ[Y(dbar)\|σ(hist₁)]` | **Stage-1 `·indD` identification** (regime-local `{D₀=d̄₀}`): the observable `f₂` agrees with the counterfactual conditional on the regime path. Via `cdtr_base` + `stageOneReg = innerReg 0` on `{D₀=d̄₀}`. Foundation for the AIPW-score consumers. **Proved.** |
| `DTREstimationSystem.stageOneReg_memLp` | `(hov : StrictOverlap ε) (h_y2 : Integrable factualY²) → MemLp stageOneReg 2 P.μ` | `f₂ ∈ L²` from strict overlap (denominator `≥ ε`) + factual second moment. Foundation for the L²-membership consumers (replaces the old counterfactual route). **Proved.** |
| `StrictOverlap S ε` | `0 < ε ∧ ε ≤ 1/2 ∧` stage-0 and stage-1 conditional propensities lie in `[ε, 1−ε]` a.e. | Two-stage overlap predicate for the target regime. **Consumer note:** the DTR estimation theorems (`MeanZero`/`ScorePullout`/`FiniteVar`/`DML`/`RemainderIdentity`/`ScoreL2`) now thread `StrictOverlap`/`Integrable factualY²`/`Assumptions` where the old proofs used the (removed) counterfactual compat fields. |
| `PODTRSystem.toDTREstimationSystem` (`Estimation/DTR/Constructor.lean`) | `(S : PODTRSystem P 2 δ γ) (dbar) (two-stage overlap a.e.) → DTREstimationSystem P δ γ` | **DTR derivability constructor.** Builds all four nuisances from Doob–Dynkin lifts — `μ₀_val` from `innerReg dbar 1`, `μ₁_val` from `stageOneReg`, `e₀_val`/`e₁_val` from the stagewise propensity lifts clamped into (0,1) — and discharges all ten structure fields. Certifies `DTREstimationSystem` adds no assumptions beyond a bare DTR system + overlap. Lift machinery: `exists_stage0_lift`/`exists_stage1_lift` (`σ(histₖ) ≤ comap (explicit factual tuple)` + `Measurable.exists_eq_measurable_comp`). **Proved.** |
| `P_H₀`, `P_H₁`, `P_Z` | `Measure (γ 0)`, `Measure (γ 1 × δ × γ 0)`, `Measure (γ 0 × δ × γ 1 × δ × ℝ)` | Stage-history marginals and the factual data law. |
| `factualZ`, `measurable_factualZ` | `Ω → γ 0 × δ × γ 1 × δ × ℝ`; `Measurable S.factualZ` | Observed tuple `(S₀,D₀,S₁,D₁,Y)` and its measurability. |
| `θ₀` | `def := S.toPODTRSystem.dtrEffect S.dbar` | Value-space target estimand `E[Y(dbar)]`. |
| `θ₀_eq_dtrEffect` | `S.θ₀ = S.toPODTRSystem.dtrEffect S.dbar` | Definitional agreement with the PO-level DTR effect; `rfl`. |

### 13''a.2 `Estimation/DTR/SeqDRMoment.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `projS₀`, `projD₀`, `projS₁`, `projD₁`, `projY`, `histH₁` | coordinate projections from `(s₀,d₀,s₁,d₁,y)`; `histH₁ z : γ 1 × δ × γ 0` | Data-tuple projections and cons-ordered stage-1 history. |
| `indEq` | `def (d d' : δ) : ℝ := if d = d' then 1 else 0` | Real equality indicator for discrete treatments. |
| `DTRNuisanceVec₂ δ γ` | structure | Bundles `μ₀_fn/e₀_fn : γ 0 → ℝ` and `μ₁_fn/e₁_fn : γ 1 × δ × γ 0 → ℝ` with measurability witnesses; componentwise algebra instances and extensionality are provided. |
| `seqDRMoment dbar z η θ` | `μ₀ + 1{D₀=d₀}/e₀·(μ₁−μ₀) + 1{D₀=d₀}1{D₁=d₁}/(e₀e₁)·(Y−μ₁) − θ` | Explicit two-stage sequential DR/AIPW moment. |
| `η₀`, `S.seqDRMoment`, `ψ_seqDR` | truth nuisance; system-specialized moment; truth score | Extracts the true nuisance from `S` and defines `ψ_seqDR(z) = S.seqDRMoment z S.η₀ S.θ₀`. |
| `H_ε` | pointwise `ε ≤ e₀,e₁ ≤ 1−ε` on both history spaces | Realization set for overlap-bounded nuisance candidates. |
| `seqDRMomentFunctional`, `measurable_seqDRMomentFunctional` | `DTRNuisanceVec₂ δ γ → Z → ℝ → ℝ`; measurable in `z` | Functional form consumed by the abstract DML layer. **Proved.** |
| `measurable_projS₀`, `measurable_histH₁` | `Measurable (projS₀)`, `Measurable (histH₁)` | Shared projection measurability lemmas. **Proved.** |
| `P_Z_map_projS₀_eq_P_H₀`, `P_Z_map_histH₁_eq_P_H₁` | `P_Z.map projS₀ = P_H₀`, `P_Z.map histH₁ = P_H₁` | Shared pushforward identities used by `RemainderIdentity` and `ScoreL2`. **Proved.** |

### 13''a.3 `Estimation/DTR/ScorePullout.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `weighted_residual_integral_zero_stage0` | weighted integral of `1{D₀=dbar 0}·(μ₁_val(history₁) − μ₀_val(S₀))` is zero | Stage-0 score pull-out. Corrected stage-0 residual uses `μ₁_val(history₁) − μ₀_val(s₀)`. **Proved.** |
| `indicator_to_propScore_integral_stage0` | `∫ f(S₀)·1{D₀=dbar 0} dμ = ∫ f(S₀)·e₀_val(S₀) dμ` | Stage-0 indicator-to-propensity rewrite. **Proved.** |
| `weighted_residual_integral_zero_stage1` | weighted integral of `1{D₀=dbar 0}·1{D₁=dbar 1}·(factualY − μ₁_val(history₁))` is zero | Stage-1 score pull-out with the joint regime indicator. **Proved.** |
| `indicator_to_propScore_integral_stage1` | `∫ f(history₁)·1{D₁=dbar 1} dμ = ∫ f(history₁)·e₁_val(history₁) dμ` | Stage-1 indicator-to-propensity rewrite. **Proved.** |

### 13''b. `Estimation/DTR/RemainderIdentity/Helpers.lean`

Proof helpers for the headline remainder identity; split out to keep `RemainderIdentity.lean` to the headline declaration only.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `indEq_factualD0_eq_indicator`, `indEq_factualD1_eq_indicator` | `indEq (factualD k ω) (dbar k) = dVar k .indicator (dbar k) ω` | Rewrites stage-0/1 equality indicators as `{0,1}`-valued indicator functions. |
| `eta_e0_pos_of_mem_Hε`, `eta_e1_pos_of_mem_Hε` | `0 < η.e₀_fn s₀`, `0 < η.e₁_fn h` | Positivity of nuisance propensities from `H_ε` membership. |
| `indicator_weighted_delta_mu0_integrable` | `Integrable (1{D₀=dbar 0}/ê₀·(μ̂₀−μ₀)) P.μ` | Stage-0 indicator-weighted μ-error integrability via L²·L∞. |
| `indicator_weighted_delta_mu1_stage0_integrable` | `Integrable (1{D₀=dbar 0}/ê₀·(μ̂₁−μ₁)) P.μ` | Stage-0 weight applied to μ₁ error. |
| `indicator_weighted_delta_mu1_stage1_integrable` | `Integrable (1{D₀=dbar 0}·1{D₁=dbar 1}/(ê₀·ê₁)·(μ̂₁−μ₁)) P.μ` | Stage-1 double-indicator-weighted μ₁-error integrability via `ε²`-bounded weight. |
| `split_stage_history_integral` | `∫ z, f₀(projS₀ z)+f₁(histH₁ z) dP_Z = ∫ f₀ dP_H₀ + ∫ f₁ dP_H₁` | Splits a P_Z integral over the two stage-history marginals. |

### 13''b'. `Estimation/DTR/RemainderIdentity.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `seqDR_remainder_identity_stage0` | placeholder stage-0 identity slot, currently `True` | Reserved for independent unrolling of the `P_H₀` cross product. |
| `seqDR_remainder_identity_stage1` | placeholder stage-1 identity slot, currently `True` | Reserved for independent unrolling of the `P_H₁` cross product. |
| `seqDR_remainder_identity` | `∫ m_seqDR(η,z,θ₀) dP_Z = ∫_{P_H₀} (ê₀−e₀)·(1/ê₀)·(μ̂₀−μ₀) + ∫_{P_H₁} 1{D₀=dbar 0}·(ê₁−e₁)·(1/(ê₀·ê₁))·(μ̂₁−μ₁)` | Headline n=2 algebraic remainder identity. Stage-1 RHS includes `indEq h.2.1 (S.dbar 0)` because `P_H₁` is unconditional. Stage weights are `w₀ = 1/η.e₀_fn` and `w₁ = 1/(η.e₀_fn(s₀)·η.e₁_fn(history₁))`. **One documented sorry**: full n=2 algebraic unrolling, the module's second-hardest proof per the plan. |

### 13''c. `Estimation/DTR/RemainderBound.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `seqDR_rem_const ε` | `def := 2 / (ε^2 · (1 − ε))` | Uniform strict-overlap constant for the two-stage IPW weights. |
| `IsLittleOp.add_eventually_nonneg_rate`, `IsLittleOp.of_abs_le_const_mul` | private helpers | Local stochastic-order utilities copied from ATE. **Proved.** |
| `seqDR_remainder_bound` | absolute population remainder ≤ `seqDR_rem_const ε · (‖Δμ₀‖₂+‖Δμ₁‖₂) · (‖Δe₀‖₂+‖Δe₁‖₂)` | Cauchy-Schwarz bound over `P_H₀` and `P_H₁`. **Proved modulo upstream `seqDR_remainder_identity`.** |
| `seqDR_remainder_op` | four product rates imply the population remainder is `o_p(n^{-1/2})` | Stochastic-order corollary using the 00, 11, 01, and 10 cross-products. **Proved modulo upstream identity.** |

| Declaration | Statement | Description |

| `indEq_nonneg`, `indEq_le_one` | `0 ≤ indEq d d' ≤ 1` | Indicator range lemmas. |
| `IsLittleOp.add_one`, `IsLittleOp.of_abs_le_const_mul_one` | composition rules for `o_p(1)` | Add and domination lemmas for stochastic little-o. |
| `eLpNorm_comp_projS₀_eq`, `eLpNorm_comp_histH₁_eq` | `eLpNorm (f ∘ projS₀) 2 P_Z = eLpNorm f 2 P_H₀`, etc. | L² norm transport from P_Z to stage marginals via pushforward. |
| `seqDR_stage0_ratio_bound`, `seqDR_stage1_ratio_bound`, `seqDR_real_bound` | pointwise Lipschitz estimates on `H_ε` | Algebraic bounds for the |Δm_seqDR| estimate. |
| `μ₀_val_memLp`, `μ₁_val_memLp` | `MemLp (μ₀_val ∘ projS₀) 2 P_Z`, etc. | MemLp of true regression values under P_Z. |

| Declaration | Statement | Description |

S.θ₀ S.ψ_seqDR sample split.foldB` | User-facing wrapper over `seqDR_dml_isAsymLinear`, bundling four stagewise hats and translating rates/measurability. **One documented sorry**: needs reusable measurability lemma for `indEq` over `MeasurableSingletonClass δ` histories plus inverse-weight bound normalization for the bundled DTR score. |

### Phase A status summary

Ten files implement the `n = 2` DTR sequential-AIPW DML pipeline.  Three documented sorries remain: `seqDR_remainder_identity`, `seqDR_score_diff_isLittleOp_one`, and `dml_DTR_isAsymLinear`.  All three are large algebraic / infrastructure proofs that mirror the corresponding ATE files at larger stagewise scale; downstream typechecking succeeds because the sorries are inside lemma bodies, not statements.  Phase B (general horizon `n`) is deferred.

---

## 13'''''. `Estimation/NPIV/` — TRAE / NPIV doubly-robust estimation and inference track

Formalizes the non-iterated Tikhonov regularized adversarial estimator (TRAE) of
Bennett et al. (2023) for linear inverse-problem functionals — the canonical
NPIV setup.  Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/trae_inverse_problems.tex`.
**The entire primal one-step Tikhonov track is fully proved (zero sorries).**
Phase A (mixed-bias identity), Phase B (TRAE-DR asymptotic linearity +
asymptotic normality), Phase C-lite + Phase D (operator layer `T` + primal rate
theorem), and Phase E (adjoint `T†`, real-CFC spectral calculus, Tikhonov bias
discharge, and the localized empirical-process event) are all complete.  The
dual TRAE (`def:est-trae-dual`) and its symmetric rate theorem are *not*
formalized — only the `DualSolution` structure the DR score needs is.  See the
per-phase Status section at the end.

### Phase A files

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `InverseProblemSystem Ω μ` | structure | Linear inverse-problem functional system (`def:est-trae-system`).  Carries observation type `𝒲` with projections `xOf : 𝒲 → 𝒳`, `zOf : 𝒲 → 𝒵`; random observation `W : Ω → 𝒲`; candidate sets `Hbar ⊆ (𝒳 → ℝ)`, `Qbar ⊆ (𝒵 → ℝ)` (closure under subtraction; no Hilbert structure in Phase A); observation-level moment maps `m, m_e` with linearity-over-subtraction; per-class measurability + integrability witnesses; primal nuisance `h₀ ∈ Hbar`; primal moment identity `E[m(W; q)] = E[h₀(X) q(Z)]` for `q ∈ Qbar`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.X`, `.Z` | `def`s | Derived random variables `X := xOf ∘ W`, `Z := zOf ∘ W`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.θ₀` | `def` | Scalar target `θ₀ := E[m_e(W; h₀)]`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.phiVal h q w` | `def` | Pointwise pseudo-outcome `m_e(w; h) + m(w; q) − q(zOf w) h(xOf w)`. Reusable for empirical fold averages. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.phi h q ω` | `def` | `phiVal h q (W ω)` — the ω-indexed score. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.Θ h q` | `def` | `Θ(h, q) := E[φ_{h, q}(W)]` (`def:est-trae-dr-functional`). |
| `InverseProblemSystem.DualSolution S q₀` | structure | `q₀ ∈ Qbar` plus the dual moment identity `E[m_e(W; h)] = E[q₀(Z) h(X)]` for `h ∈ Hbar` (`def:est-trae-dual-solution`). |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/MixedBias.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mixed_bias_identity` | `Θ(h, q) − θ₀ = E[(q₀(Z) − q(Z))(h(X) − h₀(X))]` for `h ∈ Hbar, q ∈ Qbar` and a dual solution `q₀` | The **mixed-bias / DR identity** (`prop:est-trae-mixed-bias`).  First NPIV milestone.  Pure integral-linearity proof using `m_e_sub`, the dual identity on direction `h − h₀ ∈ Hbar`, and the primal moment on `q ∈ Qbar`. |
| `Θ_h₀_eq_θ₀` | `Θ(h₀, q) = θ₀` | DR cancellation at the truth on the primal side. Specialization of `mixed_bias_identity` at `h = h₀`. |
| `Θ_q₀_eq_θ₀` | `Θ(h, q₀) = θ₀` | DR cancellation at the truth on the dual side. Specialization at `q = q₀`. |

### Phase B files

#### `Estimation/NPIV/DR/Estimator.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `instMeasurableSpace_𝒲/𝒳/𝒵` | scoped `instance` | Lift `S.inst𝒲/𝒳/𝒵` to typeclass-resolvable instances inside the `DR` namespace so downstream signatures use plain `Measure S.𝒲`, `IIDSample`, etc. without `@`-qualification. |
| `trae_dr_estimator S sample split h_hat q_hat n ω` | `def` | One-shot TRAE-DR estimator (`def:est-trae-dr-estimator`): empirical fold-B mean of `phiVal (h_hat n ω) (q_hat n ω) (sample.Z i ω)`. Nuisances `h_hat, q_hat` are random functions trained (in applications) on fold A. |
| `trae_dr_estimator_eq_avg_phi` | rfl-lemma exposing the fold-B average form | Reusable helper for the asymptotic-linearity decomposition. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/DR/AsymptoticLinear.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ρ₀ S q₀ w` | `def` | Oracle score `φ_{h₀, q₀}(w) − θ₀`. Influence function for the asymptotic-linearity theorem. |
| `mean_zero_of_DualSolution_mu`, `mean_zero_of_DualSolution` | `∫ ρ₀ = 0` | Derive oracle-score centering from the primal/dual moment identities. The `P_W` version transports the structural-law identity along the observation-law bridge `μ.map S.W = P_W` and consumes oracle-score measurability. |
| `TRAEDRRemainderHyps S hq₀ sample split h_hat q_hat` | structure | Bundles (i)–(iv) of `thm:est-trae-dr-al-criterion`: (i) `range` (h_hat / q_hat live in Hbar / Qbar); (ii) `mixed_bias` (`√|B(n)|` × mixed-bias integral is `o_p(1)`); (iii) `ep_remainder` (centered fold-B sum of `φ̂_n − φ_0` is `o_p(1)` after `√|B(n)|` scaling); (iv) `finite_var` (`E[ρ₀²] < ∞` under `P_W`). Mean-zero is derived by `mean_zero_of_DualSolution`, not bundled as a hypothesis. |
| `trae_dr_isAsymLinear` | `IsAsymLinear (trae_dr_estimator …) S.θ₀ (ρ₀ S q₀) sample split.foldB` | **TRAE-DR asymptotic linearity** (`thm:est-trae-dr-al-criterion`): three-line decomposition into oracle-IF main term + conditional bias (closed by `mixed_bias_identity`) + empirical-process remainder (closed by `ep_remainder`). The signature includes the standard observation-law bridge `μ.map S.W = P_W` and oracle-score measurability so the IF centering is derived rather than assumed. |
| `mixed_bias_sufficient` | dominated `bnd n ω` + `√|B(n)|·bnd = o_p(1)` ⇒ `mixed_bias` field | Forward implication abstracting over a deterministic upper bound on the conditional mixed-bias integral; the `min{‖q̂ − q₀‖·‖T(ĥ−h₀)‖, ‖T*(q̂−q₀)‖·‖ĥ−h₀‖}` form will instantiate `bnd` once the operator layer (Phase C) is in place. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/DR/AsymptoticNormal.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `trae_dr_asymp_normal` | `Tendsto_dist (rescaledEstimator …) (gaussianMeasure 0 σ₀²) μ` | **TRAE-DR asymptotic normality** (`thm:est-trae-dr-asymp-normal`): direct composition of `trae_dr_isAsymLinear` with `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` from `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`. Variance is `σ₀² := ∫ ρ₀² dP_W`, indexed at the fold-B rate `√|B(n)|`. |
| `trae_dr_studentized` | `Tendsto_dist (… / σ_hat_n) (gaussianMeasure 0 1) μ` | Studentized convergence under `σ̂_n →_p σ₀`, `σ₀ > 0`, `σ₀² = ∫ ρ₀² dP_W`. Now a thin instantiation of the generic `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian` (`Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`). |
| `trae_dr_wald_coverage` | `Tendsto (μ {ω : |θ̂ − θ₀| ≤ z·σ̂/√|B|})_{n} → (gaussianMeasure 0 1)(Icc -z z)` | Wald asymptotic coverage; specialize at `z = z_{1-α/2}` for the Wald `1 − α` interval. Now a thin instantiation of the generic `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` (`Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`). |

### Phase C-lite + Phase D files (rate theorem track)

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator.lean`

Realises the projected conditional-expectation operator `T = Π_{Q̄} ∘ E[· | σ(Z)]` concretely on the ambient `Lp ℝ 2 μ` (no pushforward boilerplate).  The single population identity `T_inner_eq_integral` is what the rate theorem consumes; `T_add` / `T_sub` are linearity helpers used by the weak-rate triangle step.  `T*` (adjoint) is **not** scaffolded — the simplified rate proof (note lines 250–384) does not need it.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem Ω μ` | `structure ... extends InverseProblemSystem Ω μ` | `InverseProblemSystem` plus the L² realisation: `m_Z` (σ(Z) σ-algebra coarser than `m₀`), `IsFiniteMeasure μ`, closed submodules `Hbar_L2, Qbar_L2 : Submodule ℝ (Lp ℝ 2 μ)`, an `HasOrthogonalProjection` instance for `Qbar_L2`, `MemLp 2`-witnesses for `h ∘ X` and `q ∘ Z`, and coherence (the `Lp` class lies in the closed subspace). |
| `OperatorSystem.hL2 hh`, `.qL2 hq` | `def`s | Lift `h ∈ Hbar` (resp. `q ∈ Qbar`) to its `Lp ℝ 2 μ` class using the `MemLp` witness. |
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStep f` | `def` | Conditional-expectation step `E[· | σ(Z)]` as a self-map of `Lp ℝ 2 μ`. |
| `OperatorSystem.T f` | `def` | The concrete operator `Π_{Q̄} ∘ condExpStep f` (`def:est-trae-system` step iii of the operator triple). |
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStep_sub`, `T_add`, `T_sub` | `lemma`s | ℝ-linearity helpers; `T_sub` is the workhorse for the weak-rate triangle inequality. |
| `OperatorSystem.QbarAEMeasurableMZ` | `def` (predicate) | `q ∘ Z` is `m_Z`-AEStronglyMeasurable for every `q ∈ Qbar`.  Kept as a `def` for backwards compatibility with downstream consumers; **derived** by `qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` from `m_Z_eq + meas_of_Qbar`, so it is not a separate hypothesis on the structure. |
| `OperatorSystem.qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` | `(S : OperatorSystem Ω μ) : S.QbarAEMeasurableMZ` | **Proved** (no extra hypothesis): combines `S.m_Z_eq : m_Z = comap (zOf ∘ W) inst𝒵` with `comap_measurable` and `meas_of_Qbar` to get `m_Z`-strong measurability of `q ∘ zOf ∘ W` for every `q ∈ Qbar`. |
| `OperatorSystem.T_inner_eq_integral` | `⟪T (hL2 hh − hL2 hh'), qL2 hf⟫_{L²(μ)} = ∫ (h(X) − h'(X)) · f(Z) dμ` | **Population identity** — the only fact about `T` the rate proof uses.  Proved via projection invisibility against `Q̄`-elements, `condExpL2` self-adjointness (consuming `qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` internally — no longer takes the measurability hypothesis as an explicit argument), and `Lp` inner-product unfolding. |
| `OperatorSystem.strongNorm`, `.weakNorm` | `def`s | `strongNorm f := ‖f‖`, `weakNorm f := ‖S.T f‖`.  Sugar around the standard `Lp` norm. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/SourceCondition.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SourceCondition S β` | structure | β-source condition at `h₀` (`def:est-trae-source-condition`).  Carries witness `w₀ ∈ Hbar` and the actual spectral identity `S.hL2 S.h₀_mem = Complexification.realCFC S.Tstar_T (fun x => Real.rpow (max x 0) (β/2)) (S.hL2 w₀_mem)` (uses the real-CFC built in `Operator/Complexification.lean`). |
| `TikhonovBiasBound S β λ sc` | structure | Tikhonov bias hypothesis at level `λ` (proof sketch lines 276–303).  Bundles a Tikhonov solution `h*_λ ∈ Hbar` and a constant `C ≥ 0` with three inequalities: `strong_bias` (`‖h*_λ − h₀‖² ≤ C·‖w₀‖·λ^{min β 2}`), `weak_bias` (`‖T(h*_λ − h₀)‖² ≤ C·‖w₀‖·λ^{min(β+1) 2}`), and `strong_convexity` (the population-level inequality `λ‖ĥ − h*_λ‖² + ‖T(ĥ − h*_λ)‖² ≤ ‖T(ĥ − h₀)‖² − ‖T(h*_λ − h₀)‖² + λ(‖ĥ‖² − ‖h*_λ‖²)` for every `ĥ ∈ Hbar`).  Phase E discharges all three from `SourceCondition` via spectral calculus. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/Estimator.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TRAEClasses S` | structure | Candidate class `H ⊆ Hbar` and critic class `F ⊆ Qbar` for the primal TRAE optimisation (`def:est-trae-population-criterion`). Closure / non-emptiness fields included. |
| `innerIntegrand`, `innerObjective`, `supObjective` | `noncomputable def`s | Pointwise integrand `2{m(W;f) − h(X)f(Z)} − f(Z)² + λ h(X)²`, its fold-A empirical average, and the `iSup` over `f ∈ F` (`def:est-trae-primal`). |
| `IsTRAEPrimalEstimator S TC sample split lambda h_hat` | structure | Post-hoc property characterising the primal TRAE: `mem_H : h_hat n ω ∈ TC.H`, an optimality-style inequality `opt`, and a measurability witness.  No `argmin-sup` formalism — the rate theorem only needs the inequality form. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/Rate.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TRAERatePrimalHyps S TC sample split lambda β ζ delta h_hat is_estimator` | structure | Deterministic, paper-facing hypotheses for `thm:est-trae-rate-theorem`: source condition, Tikhonov bias bound, realisability `h*_λ ∈ H`, closedness of the critic class for residuals, mean-square continuity of `m`, almost-sure boundedness, and the paper-scale δ-floor for the fixed confidence level `ζ`, with positive constant `c > 0`: `δ_n ≥ c·√((log log(n+1) + log(1/ζ)) / n)`.  Making `ζ` a parameter avoids forcing one fixed `delta` sequence to satisfy every confidence level at once. |
| `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps S TC sample split lambda β ζ delta h_hat is_estimator` | structure | Extends `TRAERatePrimalHyps` with the packaged **`empirical_process_event`** field — the post-substitution-and-CR form of the localized empirical-process inequality (`SC_RHS ≤ K_ep · (R_b² + δ‖TΔ‖ + δ² + λδ‖Δ‖ + λδ²)`) on a high-probability event of mass `≥ 1 − ζ`.  This is the modular deterministic theorem input, not the paper-facing rate assumption bundle. |
| `combined_metric_algebra` | private real-arithmetic helper | AM-GM absorption: `lambda·x² + y² ≤ K_ep·(R² + δy + δ² + λδx + λδ²) → lambda·x² + y² ≤ 4(K_ep² + K_ep + 1)·(R² + δ²)`. |
| `combined_metric_rate` | `λ·Δ_strong² + Δ_weak² ≤ K · (R_b² + δ_n²)` on `Aζ` | **Combined-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Chains `tikhonov_bias.strong_convexity` with `empirical_process_event` and applies `combined_metric_algebra` for AM-GM absorption. |
| `strong_rate_from_combined` | `‖ĥ − h₀‖² ≤ C_strong · (δ²/λ + ‖w₀‖·λ^{min β 1})` | **Strong-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Triangle inequality `‖a − c‖ ≤ ‖a − b‖ + ‖b − c‖` plus drop of `Δ_weak²` from the combined inequality, plus `weak_bias` (gives `R_b²/λ ≤ C·‖w₀‖·λ^{min β 1}`) and `strong_bias` (absorbed using `λ^{min β 2} ≤ 2 · λ^{min β 1}` for `λ < 2`). |
| `weak_rate_from_combined` | `‖T(ĥ − h₀)‖² ≤ C_weak · (δ² + ‖w₀‖·λ^{min(β+1) 2})` | **Weak-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Triangle inequality on the weak metric using `T_add` / `norm_add_le`, plus drop of `λ·Δ_strong²` from the combined inequality, plus `weak_bias`.  `C_weak := 2K + (2K + 2)·C`. |
| `trae_primal_rate_from_empirical_process` | both rates simultaneously on a single `Aζ` of mass `≥ 1 − ζ` | Abstract TRAE primal rate theorem.  Consumes `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps`, so the empirical-process event is already packaged.  Stitches `combined_metric_rate` → `strong_rate_from_combined` → `weak_rate_from_combined` on a common high-probability event. |
| `trae_primal_rate` | both rates simultaneously on a single `Aζ` of mass `≥ 1 − ζ` | **Paper-facing TRAE primal rate theorem.**  Lives in `Rate.lean` for discoverability.  Consumes deterministic `TRAERatePrimalHyps`, localized regimes, and explicit absorption/small-`n` obligations; internally calls `empirical_process_event_of_absorption` to build `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps`, then invokes `trae_primal_rate_from_empirical_process`. |

### Status

- Phase A: **fully proved** (3/3). `mixed_bias_identity`, `Θ_h₀_eq_θ₀`, `Θ_q₀_eq_θ₀`.
- Phase B: **fully proved** (8/8). `trae_dr_isAsymLinear`, `mixed_bias_sufficient`, `trae_dr_asymp_normal`, `trae_dr_studentized`, `trae_dr_wald_coverage`.
- **Phase C-lite + Phase D**: **fully proved** (5/5).
  - `T_inner_eq_integral` (population identity for `T`): **proved** (Codex via `condExpL2` self-adjointness + projection invisibility + `Lp` inner-product unfolding).
  - `combined_metric_rate`: **proved** (Codex with hand-decomposed AM-GM helper `combined_metric_algebra`).
  - `strong_rate_from_combined`: **proved** (Codex; uses triangle in `Lp ℝ 2 μ` plus `λ^{min β 2} ≤ 2·λ^{min β 1}` case-split).
  - `weak_rate_from_combined`: **proved** (Codex; uses pre-emptively scaffolded `T_sub` / `T_add` helpers in `Operator.lean` for the weak-metric triangle inequality).
  - `trae_primal_rate` (headline): **proved** (Codex stitch).
- **Phase E** — **spectral discharge fully closed**.  Adjoint layer
  fully proved.  The pure-Hilbert-space Tikhonov layer
  (`Operator/Tikhonov.lean`) discharges the existence of the population
  minimiser and population strong convexity via Lax–Milgram on the
  closed subspace `Hbar_L2`.  The real-CFC layer
  (`Operator/Complexification.lean`) is fully closed; it provides the
  spectral API the Tikhonov bias discharge consumes.  `SourceCondition`
  now carries the *actual* real-CFC spectral identity, and
  `SpectralSourceCondition` reduces to two structural fields
  (`beta_nonneg`, `Hbar_L2_eq_top`); the closedness witness and
  nonnegative spectrum facts are derived.  The resolvent identification
  of the Lax–Milgram minimiser (`tikhonovMinimiserL2_eq_resolvent`) and
  the two Tikhonov bias bounds (`strong_bias`, `weak_bias`) are
  **proved** theorems on
  `SpectralSourceCondition` (Codex Phase E2 fill, using
  `Complexification.realCFC_*`).  `tikhonov_bias_from_spectral`
  packages these plus `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` into a
  fully-derived `TikhonovBiasBound`.  Discharge of
  `empirical_process_event` is **fully closed** (no sorries): the
  localized-empirical-process bookkeeping is supplied by the
  `LocalizedRegimeBundle` hypotheses (law / realizability / boundedness /
  diameter / peeling-slack / critical-radius), exactly as the paper
  assumes `δ_n` upper-bounds the critical radii, and the master
  concentration inequality `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp`
  (`Stat/Concentration/UniformDeviationLocalized.lean`) is itself proved.
  See files below.

### Phase E files

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Adjoint.lean` (~110 lines, fully proved)

Bundles the bare-function operator `OperatorSystem.T : Lp ℝ 2 μ → Lp ℝ 2 μ`
as a continuous linear map and exposes the adjoint `T†` and the self-adjoint
composite `T†T`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStepL` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Conditional-expectation step `E[· \| σ(Z)]` as a CLM, via `(lpMeas).subtypeL.comp (condExpL2 ℝ ℝ S.m_Z_le)`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tlin` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Bundled CLM version of `S.T`: `Qbar_L2.starProjection.comp condExpStepL`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tlin_apply` | `S.Tlin f = S.T f` | Coherence: bundled CLM agrees with the bare-function `T`. **Proved (`rfl`).** |
| `OperatorSystem.Tadjoint` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Adjoint `T† := ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint S.Tlin`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tstar_T` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Self-adjoint, PSD composite `T† T = Tadjoint.comp Tlin`. The operator the β-source condition refers to. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tstar_T_isSelfAdjoint` | `IsSelfAdjoint S.Tstar_T` | Self-adjointness of `T† T` via `IsSelfAdjoint.star_mul_self` after identifying adjoint with `star`. **Proved.** |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Tikhonov.lean` (~200 lines, sorry-only scaffold)

Pure-real-Hilbert-space layer for the Tikhonov minimisation problem on
the closed subspace `Hbar_L2`.  Discharges the existence of the
population minimiser and the population strong-convexity inequality via
Mathlib's Lax–Milgram theorem (`IsCoercive.continuousLinearEquivOfBilin`)
— **no continuous functional calculus needed**.  These were previously
baked fields of `SpectralSourceCondition`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovBilin S λ` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] ℝ` | Bilinear form `(u, v) ↦ ⟨T u, T v⟩ + λ ⟨u, v⟩` on the ambient `Lp ℝ 2 μ`. |
| `tikhonovBilin_apply`, `tikhonovBilin_symm` | unfold + symmetry | Pointwise formula and symmetry of the bilinear form. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovBilinSub S λ` | `S.Hbar_L2 →L[ℝ] S.Hbar_L2 →L[ℝ] ℝ` | The bilinear form restricted to the closed subspace `Hbar_L2` (via `Submodule.subtypeL` pre/post-composition).  Argument fed into Lax–Milgram. |
| `tikhonovBilinSub_apply` | unfold | Pointwise formula linking the restricted form back to `tikhonovBilin`. |
| `tikhonovBilinSub_isCoercive` | `IsCoercive (S.tikhonovBilinSub λ)` for `0 < λ` | Coercivity with constant `λ`, since `bilin u u = ‖T u‖² + λ ‖u‖² ≥ λ ‖u‖²`. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovTargetSub S` | `S.Hbar_L2 →L[ℝ] ℝ` | Target functional `v ↦ ⟨T h₀, T v⟩` on `Hbar_L2`, used in the variational identity. |
| `tikhonovTargetSub_apply` | unfold | Pointwise formula for the target functional. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovMinimiserL2 S λ` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Population Tikhonov minimiser at level `λ`, defined for `0 < λ` by Lax–Milgram applied to `tikhonovBilinSub` and `tikhonovTargetSub`; arbitrary value (`0`) for `λ ≤ 0`. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_mem` | `∈ S.Hbar_L2` | The minimiser lies in the closed candidate subspace. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_optimality` | `⟨T h*, T v⟩ + λ⟨h*, v⟩ = ⟨T h₀, T v⟩` for `v ∈ Hbar_L2` | Variational identity (Lax–Milgram statement, restated using the L²-level inner product). |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` | `λ‖ĥ−h*‖² + ‖T(ĥ−h*)‖² ≤ ‖T(ĥ−h₀)‖² − ‖T(h*−h₀)‖² + λ(‖ĥ‖² − ‖h*‖²)` | **Population strong convexity at the minimiser.**  Pure Hilbert-space Taylor-expansion identity at the minimiser; the first-order term cancels via `tikhonovMinimiserL2_optimality`. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Complexification.lean` (~1090 lines, fully proved)

Real continuous functional calculus on `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` via
complexification.  Embeds the real `L²` isometrically into the complex
`L²` (`ιLp` / `reLp` / `imLp`), lifts any real CLM `A` to a complex CLM
`complexLift A` whose action on the real subspace recovers `A`, and
defines `realCFC A f := reLp ∘ cfc (f ∘ Complex.re) (complexLift A) ∘ ιLp`.
Provides the full API the spectral discharge consumes (`realCFC_apply`,
`realCFC_id`, `realCFC_mul`, `realCFC_resolvent_mul_self`,
`realCFC_isSelfAdjoint`, `realCFC_norm_le`).

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/SpectralCalculus.lean` (~1230 lines, fully proved)

Spectral source condition + Tikhonov bias discharge using the real-CFC
API from `Complexification.lean`.  `SourceCondition` (in `../SourceCondition.lean`)
carries the actual spectral identity, so `SpectralSourceCondition`
only adds the structural assumptions the spectral argument needs.
The two bias bounds and the resolvent identification of the Lax–Milgram
minimiser are now theorems on `SpectralSourceCondition`, all proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SpectralSourceCondition S β` | `structure ... extends SourceCondition S β` | Strengthens `SourceCondition` with: `beta_nonneg : 0 ≤ β` and `Hbar_L2_eq_top : S.Hbar_L2 = ⊤` (full-space candidate class).  The closedness witness `Hbar_L2_hasProj` is derived from `Hbar_L2_eq_top`, and `Tstar_T_spectrum_nonneg` is derived from positivity of `T†T`. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.sourceSymbol β` / `continuous_sourceSymbol` | `ℝ → ℝ` / `Continuous` | The continuous extension `x ↦ Real.rpow (max x 0) (β/2)` of `x^{β/2}` to all of ℝ. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.spectralPower sc` | `Lp ℝ 2 μ →L[ℝ] Lp ℝ 2 μ` | Defined as `Complexification.realCFC S.Tstar_T (sourceSymbol β)`; the operator `(T†T)^{β/2}` consumed by the spectral identity. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.biasConst sc` | `ℝ` | Uniform constant `Real.rpow (max 1 (‖S.Tstar_T‖+1)) β` absorbing both the small-β and large-β regimes of the sup-on-spectrum analysis. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_eq_resolvent` | `S.tikhonovMinimiserL2 λ = realCFC S.Tstar_T (x ↦ x/(λ+x)) (S.hL2 S.h₀_mem)` | Identifies the Lax–Milgram minimiser with the resolvent expression `(T†T+λI)⁻¹ T†T h₀`.  Uses `Hbar_L2_eq_top` to lift the variational identity to the full ambient space.  **Proved** (Codex; uses private helpers `realCFC_const_add` and `resolvent_left_inverse` built on `realCFC_resolvent_mul_self`). |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.strong_bias` | `‖h*_λ − h₀‖² ≤ biasConst · ‖w₀‖² · λ^{min β 2}` | Strong-metric Tikhonov bias, derived spectrally from the resolvent identity + `realCFC_norm_le` + sup-on-spectrum lemmas (`residual_symbol_bound_small/_large` private helpers handle β≤2 / β>2 cases).  **Proved** (Codex). |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.weak_bias` | `‖T(h*_λ − h₀)‖² ≤ biasConst · ‖w₀‖² · λ^{min(β+1) 2}` | Weak-metric Tikhonov bias.  Reduces `‖T u‖² = ⟨T†T u, u⟩` (via `Tstar_T`, `adjoint_inner_left`), expresses it as `⟨realCFC A (x · f²) w, w⟩` via `realCFC_mul` + self-adjointness, then bounds via Cauchy–Schwarz + `realCFC_norm_le` with the tight sup `weak_residual_bound` (β≤1 / β>1 case split).  **Proved** (Codex). |
| `TikhonovPullback S β λ sc` | `structure` | Function-level pullback (unchanged):  L² class of the function-level minimiser equals `tikhonovMinimiserL2 S λ`. |
| `tikhonov_bias_from_spectral S β λ λ_pos sc pb` | `TikhonovBiasBound S β λ sc.toSourceCondition` | Packages the spectral `strong_bias` / `weak_bias` theorems plus `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` into a `TikhonovBiasBound` with `C := sc.biasConst · ‖w₀‖`.  **Fully proved** (no sorries). |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/EmpiricalProcessEvent/` — localized EP discharge

Split implementation of the discharge of
`TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps.empirical_process_event` from four localized-regime
instances on `star(H · F), star(m ∘ F), star(F), star(H)`.  The umbrella file
`Primal/EmpiricalProcessEvent.lean` re-exports the final discharge module.

| File | Contents | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Algebra.lean` | `young_cross_envelope` | Deterministic Young / AM-GM envelope converting the localized additive cross terms into the `R² + δ·w + δ² + κ²` shape used by the EP RHS. |
| `Regime.lean` | `LocalizedRegimeBundle`, `LocalizedRegimes` | Bundles one localized deviation regime per abstract function class, including nonnegativity of the abstract localization norm, plus law, realizability, boundedness, closedness, diameter, peeling slack, and interpretation fields connecting the abstract classes to the concrete NPIV losses. |
| `Core.lean` | imports `Regime` | Small compatibility umbrella for the algebra and regime declarations. |
| `LocalizedEventsBase.lean` | `integral_comp_law_W`, `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle` | Shared law-change helper for rewriting integrals under `P_W` back to integrals over `Ω`, plus the NPIV-facing wrapper around `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` for a single `LocalizedRegimeBundle`. The wrapper pulls the product-space sharp event back along the IID sample, uses the bundle's localization-norm nonnegativity, keeps the diameter obligation per concrete index, and rewrites the population mean through `regime.law_W`. |
| `LocalizedEventH.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_H` | Fixed-diameter Ω-side localized deviation event for squared candidate differences, used by the centred regulariser bound. |
| `LocalizedEventHF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_HF`, `localized_omega_event_for_HF_pair`, `localized_omega_event_for_HF_pair_peeled` | Ω-side localized deviation events for the product class `H · F`, including the peeled pair-gap Foster interpretation.  The peeled helper now delegates the dyadic peeling to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle`. |
| `LocalizedEventMF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_mF`, `localized_omega_event_for_mF_pair_peeled` | Ω-side localized deviation events for the moment class `m ∘ F`, including the peeled critic-pair form.  The peeled helper now delegates the dyadic peeling to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle`. |
| `LocalizedEventF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_F`, `localized_omega_event_for_F_pair_peeled` | Ω-side localized deviation events for the squared critic class `F`, including the peeled critic-pair form.  The peeled helper now delegates to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle` and states the leading term at the public `δ_n` scale. |
| `LocalizedEvents.lean` | imports the localized event files | Small compatibility umbrella for all Ω-side localized deviation events. |
| `EPMasterEvent.lean` | `population_closedness_critic_gap_eq`, `empirical_critic_argmax_localized`, `empirical_critic_argmax_localized_from_components`, `ep_master_event_from_localized` | Objective-level EP master event plus the deterministic Foster argmax bridge. `population_closedness_critic_gap_eq` proves the population quadratic-curvature identity at a closedness witness; `empirical_critic_argmax_localized` turns empirical critic optimality plus a one-sided loss-deviation bound into an L² localization bound for the empirical sup-attainer; `empirical_critic_argmax_localized_from_components` packages that bridge from the existing single-critic `m∘F`, `H·F`, and `F²` deviations without adding critic linearity assumptions. `ep_master_event_from_localized` exposes the raw objective-level localized EP modulus before final δ-schedule absorption. |
| `EPPerN.lean` | `ep_pop_inner_at_closedness_witness`, `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations`, `ep_inequality_from_localized` | Per-horizon deterministic EP bridge and public EP inequality. `ep_pop_inner_at_closedness_witness` is the population-side identity `2·E[m·f] − 2·E[h·f] − E[f²] = weak(h - h₀)²` at a closedness witness. `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations` consumes the raw objective-level localized modulus and removes the empirical sup-objective excess using estimator optimality. `ep_inequality_from_localized` extracts the witness payload from `ep_master_event_from_localized` and feeds it through `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations`; `Discharge.lean` then absorbs the explicit rate into the final TRAE population shape. |
| `EPInequality.lean` | imports `EPPerN` | Small compatibility umbrella for the explicit empirical-process inequality. |
| `Regulariser.lean` | `measure_iInter_nat_ge_one_sub_tsum_of_ge`, `centred_regulariser_bound_from_localized` | All-`n` centred empirical regulariser bound obtained from the H-side localized event. |
| `Discharge.lean` | `empirical_process_event_from_localized`, `explicitRate`, `populationShape`, `empirical_process_event_of_absorption` | Combines the EP inequality and regulariser bound, then exposes the `Rate.lean`-shaped empirical-process event conditional on the explicit-rate absorption hypothesis.  The paper-facing theorem using it is `trae_primal_rate` in `Rate.lean`. |

---

## 13W. `Causalean/Panel/Weighted/` — Weighted-Finite-Index Substrate (FWL / WLS Core)

**FWL/WLS-centric framing.** Estimand-characterization papers (Sloczynski,
Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, BJS, DCDH, MTW, Angrist-Imbens, Wooldridge, …)
all express their estimators as `θ̂ = ∑_r ω_r · τ_r`; those `ω_r` are the
implicit FWL / WLS regression weights this substrate makes explicit. The
eight files below build, in order, a finite weighted index (`WeightedSupport
R`), its weighted inner product, the nuisance-subspace projection, the WLS
optimality theorem, the FWL identity, plus reusable indicator-span /
normalized-weight / additive-span subspace constructions. Both `Panel/`
(via `Cells I T := WeightedSupport (I × T)`) and
`Panel/EstimandCharacterization/` (via partition-cell, ordered-IV, response-type,
or cohort-period instantiations of `R`) are thin facades over this layer.

### 13W.a `Weighted/Support.lean`

The substrate: a finite index `R` carrying weights `ω_r ≥ 0` (strictly
positive on the observed subset) summing to 1. Generalizes `Panel/Cells.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport R` | `structure` over `[Fintype R] [DecidableEq R]` | Bundles `observed : Finset R` (nonempty), `weight : R → ℝ`, `weight_pos`, `weight_zero_off`, `weight_sum_one`. |
| `WeightedSupport.weight_nonneg` | `0 ≤ c.weight r` | Pointwise nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight_univ` | `∑ r, c.weight r = ∑ r ∈ c.observed, c.weight r` | Sum over `Finset.univ` reduces to sum over `observed`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight` | `∑ r ∈ c.observed, c.weight r = 1` | Restated normalization. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight_univ_eq_one` | `∑ r, c.weight r = 1` | Normalization over `Finset.univ`. **Proved.** |

### 13W.b `Weighted/InnerProduct.lean`

Weighted inner product `⟨A, B⟩_ω := ∑_{r∈observed} ω_r · A_r · B_r` and its
matrix-valued lift to vector arrays `Fin K → (R → ℝ)`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ip` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) → (R → ℝ) → (R → ℝ) → ℝ` | Scalar weighted inner product `c.ip A B = ∑ r ∈ observed, ω_r · A r · B r`. |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_symm` | `c.ip A B = c.ip B A` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_add_left` / `ip_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in each argument. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_smul_left` / `ip_smul_right` | `c.ip (s • A) B = s · c.ip A B` (and sym.) | Scalar homogeneity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_summand_nonneg` | `∀ r ∈ observed, 0 ≤ ω_r · A r · A r` | Per-summand nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_nonneg` | `0 ≤ c.ip A A` | Positivity (semi-definite). **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_eq_zero_iff` | `c.ip A A = 0 ↔ ∀ r ∈ observed, A r = 0` | Definiteness on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat` | `(Fin K → R → ℝ) → (Fin K → R → ℝ) → Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ℝ` | Entrywise inner product `(c.ipMat A B) j k = c.ip (A j) (B k)`. |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_transpose` | `(c.ipMat A B).transpose = c.ipMat B A` | Transposed symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_add_left` / `ipMat_add_right` | entrywise bilinearity | Additivity in each tuple argument. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_smul_left` / `ipMat_smul_right` | `c.ipMat (s • A) B = s • c.ipMat A B` (and sym.) | Scalar homogeneity entrywise. **Proved.** |

### 13W.c `Weighted/Subspace.lean`

Weighted orthogonal projection onto a nuisance subspace `H ⊆ R → ℝ`, and the
residual maker `M_H := id − P_H`. Existence is via
`Causalean.Mathlib.SemiInnerProjection.exists_orthogonalProjection_of_posSemidef`
(since `c.ip` is only positive-semidefinite, not definite, off the observed
support).

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.weighted_orthogonal_projection_exists` | `∃ P : (R→ℝ) →ₗ[ℝ] (R→ℝ), …` | Existence of the weighted projection onto `H`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) → Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ) → (R → ℝ) →ₗ[ℝ] (R → ℝ)` | Chosen weighted projection `P_H`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_mem` / `proj_orthogonal` | membership / orthogonality | Defining properties of `proj`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_apply_of_mem` | `X ∈ H → proj X = X` on observed | Projection is identity on its image, on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal` | any orthogonal nuisance candidate matches `proj X` on observed | Uniqueness of the projection on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_idem_apply` | `proj (proj X) = proj X` on observed | Idempotence on observed cells. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize` | `Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ) → (R → ℝ) →ₗ[ℝ] (R → ℝ)` | Residual maker `M_H = id − P_H`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.tildeX` | scalar residualization | `X̃ := M_H X`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.tildeXVec` | column-wise residualization | `Fin K → (R → ℝ)` tuple of residualized columns. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_in_orthogonal` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) h = 0` for `h ∈ H` | Residuals are `H`-orthogonal. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_self_of_mem` | `X ∈ H → tildeX X = 0` on observed | Nuisance-space arrays residualize to zero on observed cells. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_idem_apply` | `residualize (residualize X) = residualize X` on observed | Residual maker idempotent on observed cells. **Proved.** |

### 13W.d `Weighted/WLS.lean`

Headline: **weighted projections ARE the WLS solutions.** Characterizes
`proj` / `residualize` as the unique-modulo-off-support solutions to the
weighted least-squares minimization problem.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.proj_eq_argmin` | `∀ h ∈ H, c.ip (X − proj X) (X − proj X) ≤ c.ip (X − h) (X − h)` | The weighted projection minimizes the WLS objective `⟨X − h, X − h⟩_ω` over `h ∈ H`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_orth_iff_argmin` | residual-orthogonality at `p ∈ H` ↔ WLS-optimality at `p` | The WLS first-order condition: orthogonality of `X − p` to all of `H` is equivalent to `p` being the WLS minimizer. **Proved.** |

### 13W.e `Weighted/FWL.lean`

The **headline of the substrate**: the Frisch–Waugh–Lovell identity. This is
the **finite-cell** FWL; the parallel measure-theoretic / L² FWL lives at
`Causalean/Panel/Analysis/Residualization.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.Q_XX` | `WeightedSupport R → (Fin K → R → ℝ) → Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ) → Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ℝ` | Residualized Gram matrix `⟨X̃, X̃⟩_ω`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.rhsVec` | `… → (R → ℝ) → Fin K → ℝ` | Residualized RHS / score vector `⟨X̃, Y⟩_ω`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.thetaHat` | `… → (R → ℝ) → Fin K → ℝ` | FWL coefficient `Q_XX⁻¹ *ᵥ rhsVec`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.RankCondition` | `IsUnit (Q_XX c X H).det` | Nonsingularity of `Q_XX` (finite algebraic form of `Q_XX ≻ 0`). |
| `WeightedSupport.Q_XX_mulVec_thetaHat` | `RankCondition → Q_XX *ᵥ thetaHat = rhsVec` | `thetaHat` solves the residualized normal equations. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.fwl_identity` | long-regression β = residualized-short-regression θ̂ | The joint minimizer `β` of the long WLS objective over `(β, α ∈ H)` equals `thetaHat` of the H-residualized regressors. **Proved.** |

### 13W.f `Weighted/IndicatorSpan.lean`

Finite-cell partition algebra: singleton-cell indicators on a classifier
`G : R → 𝒢`, the linear span they generate, and partition-cell statistics
(cell mass, cell mean). Two-axis variant for cohort+period nuisance classes.
This file ports the **finite-cell** part of the archived
`Panel/CellBridge.lean`; the L² / measure-theoretic part
lives in `Causalean/Panel/Analysis/Residualization.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Weighted.cellIndicator` | `(G : R → 𝒢) → 𝒢 → R → ℝ` | Real-valued singleton-cell indicator `r ↦ if G r = g then 1 else 0`. |
| `Weighted.indicatorSpan` | `(G : R → 𝒢) → Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ)` | Linear span of the singleton indicators `{cellIndicator G g}_{g : 𝒢}`. |
| `Weighted.mem_indicatorSpan_iff` | `h ∈ indicatorSpan G ↔ ∃ c : 𝒢 → ℝ, h = ∑ g, c g • cellIndicator G g` | Characterization of membership. **Proved.** |
| `Weighted.cellIndicator_mem_indicatorSpan` | `cellIndicator G g ∈ indicatorSpan G` | Generators belong to the span. **Proved.** |
| `Weighted.twoAxisIndicatorSpan` | `(G : R → 𝒢) → (T : R → 𝒯) → Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ)` | Two-axis indicator span: sup of `indicatorSpan G` and `indicatorSpan T`. Used for cohort+period nuisance classes (Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham). |
| `Weighted.indicatorSpan_le_twoAxisIndicatorSpan` / `_right` | left and right injection | Each one-axis span sits inside the two-axis span. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMass` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) → (G : R → 𝒢) → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Cell mass `∑_{r ∈ observed, G r = g} ω_r`. |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMass_nonneg` | `0 ≤ c.cellMass G g` | Nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMean` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) → (F : R → ℝ) → (G : R → 𝒢) → 𝒢 → ℝ` | Totalized cell mean of `F` on cell `g`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.jointCellMass` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) → (G : R → 𝒢) → (T : R → 𝒯) → 𝒢 → 𝒯 → ℝ` | Joint cell mass `∑_{r : G r = g, T r = k} ω_r`. |

### 13W.g `Weighted/NormalizedWeights.lean`

Paper-agnostic finite weighted-sum algebra: normalized weights `a_i / ∑ a_j`
and weighted centered covariance / variance identities. Consumed by
estimand-characterization papers when re-expressing a regression coefficient
`θ̂` as `∑_r ω_r τ_r`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NormalizedWeights.normalizedWeight` | `(ι → ℝ) → ι → ℝ` | Generic normalized weight `a_i / ∑ k, a k`. `noncomputable`. |
| `NormalizedWeights.normalizedWeight_nonneg` | `(∀ i, 0 ≤ a i) → 0 < ∑ a → 0 ≤ normalizedWeight a i` | Nonnegativity from nonneg raw weights and positive total mass. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.sum_normalizedWeight_eq_one` | `0 < ∑ a → ∑ i, normalizedWeight a i = 1` | Normalized weights sum to one. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_cov` | `(∑ p = 1) → ∑ p (x − x̄)(y − ȳ) = ½ ∑∑ p p' (x − x')(y − y')` | Weighted centered covariance equals one half the ordered pairwise cross-product average. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_var` | `(∑ p = 1) → ∑ p (x − x̄)² = ½ ∑∑ p p' (x − x')²` | Weighted centered variance equals one half the ordered pairwise squared-gap average. **Proved.** |

### 13W.h `Weighted/AdditiveSpan.lean`

Generic two-axis additive subspace: for a generic `R` with two projections
`f₁ : R → A` and `f₂ : R → B`, the additive span is `{ h | ∃ a b, h r = a (f₁
r) + b (f₂ r) }`. Specialized to `R = I × T`, `f₁ = Prod.fst`, `f₂ =
Prod.snd`, this is the two-way fixed-effect subspace `H_twfe` used in
`Panel/FixedEffect.lean` and across the estimand-characterization layer
(Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, DCDH, BJS, …).

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AdditiveSpan` | `(f₁ : R → A) → (f₂ : R → B) → Submodule ℝ (R → ℝ)` | Generic two-axis additive span. |
| `AdditiveSpan.mem_iff` | `h ∈ AdditiveSpan f₁ f₂ ↔ ∃ a b, ∀ r, h r = a (f₁ r) + b (f₂ r)` | Characterization of membership. **Proved.** |
| `AdditiveSpan.const_mem` | constants lie in the additive span | `∀ c₀ : ℝ, (fun _ => c₀) ∈ AdditiveSpan f₁ f₂`. **Proved.** |
| `AdditiveSpan.finiteDimensional` | `instance [Finite R] : Module.Finite ℝ (AdditiveSpan f₁ f₂)` | Finite-dimensionality. **Proved.** |
| `twoAxisAdditiveSpan` | `(I T : Type*) → Submodule ℝ ((I × T) → ℝ) := AdditiveSpan Prod.fst Prod.snd` | Specialization to `R = I × T`. |
| `IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `(Unit → Time → ℝ) → Prop` | Back-compat membership predicate matching the original `EstimandCharacterization.AdditiveSpan` API. |

### 13W.i `Weighted/OfProbabilityMeasure.lean`

Bridge (audit helper **M1**) connecting the finite weighted-support tower to a
probability measure on a finite measurable space. For a probability measure `μ`
on a `Fintype` `R` with measurable singletons, the point masses
`ω_r := (μ {r}).toReal` form a `WeightedSupport R`, and its weighted inner
product equals the `L²(μ)` integral pairing. Lets the measure-theoretic
cell-bridge layer (`Residualization`/`CellBridge`) and the finite FWL tower be
used interchangeably on a finite probability space.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ofProbabilityMeasure` | `(μ : Measure R) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] → WeightedSupport R` | Weighted support induced by `μ`: weight `r ↦ (μ {r}).toReal`, observed set = positive-mass support. `noncomputable`. **Proved** (all structure fields discharged). |
| `WeightedSupport.ofProbabilityMeasure_weight` | `(ofProbabilityMeasure μ).weight r = (μ {r}).toReal` | Weight unfolds to the point mass. `@[simp]`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_eq_integral` | `(ofProbabilityMeasure μ).ip A B = ∫ r, A r * B r ∂μ` | **M1 bridge**: the induced weighted inner product is the `L²(μ)` integral pairing. **Proved.** |

### 13W.j `Weighted/ScalarFWL.lean`

Scalar (`K = 1`) Frisch–Waugh–Lovell from the weighted **normal equations**
(audit helper **M2**) — the finite weighted-support counterpart of the
measure-theoretic `Causalean.PO.residualizedCoefficient_eq_of_normalEqs`. Bridges
the normal-equation inputs estimand papers produce to the residualized-coefficient
ratio, without going through the joint-minimization `fwl_identity`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ip_tildeX_self` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) X = c.ip (c.tildeX H X) (c.tildeX H X)` | The residualized regressor pairs identically with `X` and its residual (projection part is orthogonal). **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` | `α ∈ H → 0 < ⟨X̃,X̃⟩_ω → ⟨Y−βX−α, X⟩_ω = 0 → (∀ h ∈ H, ⟨Y−βX−α, h⟩_ω = 0) → β = ⟨X̃,Y⟩_ω / ⟨X̃,X̃⟩_ω` | **M2**: scalar FWL from the two weighted normal equations; derives the coefficient ratio (does not assume it). **Proved.** |

---

## 9i0a-FWLInstanceL2. `Panel/FWLInstanceL2.lean` — L²(μ) instance of the abstract FWL

Instantiates the abstract, panel-agnostic FWL development of `Mathlib/FWL.lean`
(§10f) on the genuine inner-product space `Lp ℝ 2 μ`, with a finite-dimensional
nuisance subspace `H`. This is the **measure-theoretic instance** of the canonical
projection layer (see
`doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/INTERFACE_canonical_projection.md`):
the cell-indicator nuisance classes of the conditional-mean estimand papers
(Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, MTW, Słoczyński) are finite-dimensional subspaces of
`Lp ℝ 2 μ`, so the abstract FWL coefficient, residualized normal equations, and
least-squares optimality apply directly. **This file is what makes `Mathlib/FWL.lean`
a live dependency rather than an orphan.** (Re-proving the raw-function
`Causalean.PO.residualizedCoefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` *through* this instance — the
full de-duplication of the measure-theoretic FWL tower — is a documented follow-up
requiring the raw-function ↔ `Lp`-quotient coercion bridge.)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FWLInstanceL2.hasOrthogonalProjection_of_finiteDimensional` | `instance (H : Submodule ℝ (Lp ℝ 2 μ)) [FiniteDimensional ℝ H] : H.HasOrthogonalProjection` | Finite-dim subspaces of `Lp ℝ 2 μ` are complete, hence admit an orthogonal projection (free, via `HasOrthogonalProjection.ofCompleteSpace`). | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.inner_eq_integral` | `inner ℝ f g = ∫ a, f a * g a ∂μ` | L² inner product = population covariance: bridges the abstract FWL inner products to the integrals the papers manipulate. | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.gramResid_mulVec_fwlCoef` | `IsUnit (gramResid H X).det → gramResid H X *ᵥ fwlCoef H X Y = residInnerVec H X Y` | Residualized normal equations on `L²(μ)` (specialization of the abstract lemma). | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.fwlCoef_original_minimizes` | `IsUnit … → h ∈ H → originalObjective … (fwlCoef …) (P_H …) ≤ originalObjective X Y β h` | FWL least-squares optimality on `L²(μ)`. | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.fwlCoef_eq_of_original_minimizer` | minimizer hypothesis `→ β = fwlCoef H X Y` | β-block uniqueness on `L²(μ)`. | **Proved** |

---

## 13sa. `Estimation/MinimaxATE/` — Structure-agnostic ATE optimality (Jin–Syrgkanis 2024)

> **Entry point:** `Estimation/MinimaxATE.lean` (the roll-up beside the folder) imports the
> whole development and carries a docstring index of the headline theorems. Lower bounds:
> `minimax_lower_bound` (base, `ChiSquaredCore.lean`), `…_gen`/`…_var`/`…_continuous`,
> `parametric_lower_bound`. Achievability + capstone: `aipw_minimaxMiss_le`,
> `aipw_attains_minimax_rate` (both `Optimality.lean`).

Causal layer for the **converse** direction of doubly-robust ATE estimation: the
structure-agnostic minimax lower bound showing the DR/AIPW product-bias rate
`√εg·√εm` cannot be beaten (Jin–Syrgkanis 2024, *Structure-agnostic Optimality of
Doubly Robust Learning for Treatment Effect Estimation*, arXiv:2402.14264). Built
on the causal-agnostic `Stat/Minimax/` Le Cam layer (§12w). The paper's
construction makes nuisances piecewise-constant on `M = 2^m` cells of `[0,1]^K`,
so we collapse each cell to a point and use a **finite covariate** `X : Fin M`
(uniform), `D Y : Bool` — all measures finite PMFs.

**Scope of the finite-`X` model (read before citing as "the paper's theorem").**
The paper's covariate is *continuous* (`X` uniform on `[0,1]^K`); only the
nuisances are cell-constant, so within a cell the actual position of `X` is still
observed. Our `X : Fin M` observes *only the cell index* — a strictly more
collapsed object. This loses nothing of the **research content**: the
perturbation construction, the `√εg·√εm` ATE gap, and the Ingster χ² second-moment
bound never touch the within-cell continuum (the within-cell position is
*ancillary* — its conditional law is cell-uniform for every DGP in the class). So
the finite-`X` and continuous-`X`-with-cell-constant-nuisances models are
statistically equivalent for this functional, and a finite-`X` lower bound implies
the continuous-`X` lower bound by a **soft ancillarity reduction** (tensor both
hypotheses with the common within-cell law; the ancillary factor cancels in the
χ²). That reduction is now **formalized** in `ContinuousX.lean` (§13sa.6):
`two_point_lower_bound_continuous` proves the same `1/4` two-point bound
for an arbitrary within-cell ancillary space `(W, ρ)`.

The model covariate is kept as a general finite type `C` (so `Model/Witness/Bump`
read `C`, instantiated to `C = Fin K × Bool` by the explicit construction).

**What the headline matches in the paper, and what it does not.** The paper's
Theorem 1 lower-bounds the **minimax `(1−γ)`-quantile risk** of the *squared* error,
`𝔐ₙ,γ(ℱ) = inf_est sup_{(m,g)∈ℱ} 𝒬_{1−γ}(|est − θ|²)` (eq. for `𝔐ₙ,γ`), for
`γ ∈ (1/2,1)`. A quantile bound `𝔐ₙ,γ ≥ ρ` is *by definition* the statement that
every estimator misses the truth by `√ρ` with probability `≥ 1−γ` on some in-class
DGP. So our `minimax_lower_bound` (`1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss … s`) **is** the
paper's headline theorem at `γ = 3/4`, `ρ = s² ≍ εg·εm` — the *stronger*
(probability/quantile) form, not the weaker expected-risk form. The paper itself
notes (and we formalize in §13sa.7) that the quantile bound *implies* the
expected-`𝔼`-risk bound `sup E[(est−θ)²] ≥ (1−γ)ρ`. On the remaining differences from
the paper's full generality: (i) the **constant general center** `(m₀,g₀,g₁) ∈ (0,1)³`
(the paper's Assumption 2 in the constant-nuisance case, incl. the known-baseline
remark `ĝ(0)=g₀`) **is now formalized** in §13sa.8 (`minimax_lower_bound_gen`),
generalizing the `(m̂,ĝ)≡1/2` headline; the exact `λ`-independent ATE gap survives because
the center is constant *within* each Rademacher pair. (i') A **cell-varying center** —
nuisances that are arbitrary *functions* of the pair index (genuinely non-constant over the
covariate space), bounded away from `{0,1}` — **is now formalized** in §13sa.9
(`minimax_lower_bound_var`), using the new non-uniform `ingster_bound_general`
(§13sa.9) for the per-pair χ² coefficient `Γⱼ`. The earlier note that this "needs a
