Taylor-remainder Fano argument" was **wrong**: the paper keeps the two-point/Le Cam
structure, the first-order bump term cancels (`E[ŵΔ]=0`), and the ATE separation is a
`λ`-independent *lower bound* — packaged abstractly as the **one-sided mixture two-point
lemma** `mixture_two_point_lower_bound` (§13sa.2b). The following are still **not**
formalized: (i'') the *within-pair-varying* center (the two cells of a pair carry different
nuisance values — the only residual of the fully-functional Case-1 construction; needs the
one-sided lemma + the 4-distinct-values-per-pair overlap algebra); (ii) the weight function
is `w ≡ 1` (no `‖w‖∞²`/`‖w‖₂²` factors); (iii) the additive `1/n` parametric-variance
term **is now formalized** (unweighted, `‖w‖₂²=1`) in §13sa.10
(`parametric_lower_bound`); (iv) only Case 1 (`εg ≳ εm`) of the bias construction is
built (Case 2 `εm > εg` would recover `√εgεm` in that regime).

### 13sa.1 `Estimation/MinimaxATE/Model.lean` — finite observed-data model

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Obs M` | `abbrev := Fin M × Bool × Bool` | One observation `(X, D, Y)` with finite covariate. | — |
| `ValidDGP m g` | `structure`, `Prop` | Propensity `m : Fin M → ℝ` and both outcome arms `g : Bool → Fin M → ℝ` take values in `[0,1]`. | — |
| `obsReal m g z` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | Real mass `(1/M)·P(D=d\|x)·P(Y=y\|d,x)` of observation `z`. | — |
| `obsReal_sum` | `∑ z, obsReal m g z = 1` (needs `[NeZero M]`) | Total mass is 1 (sum over `Y`, then `D`, then `X`). **Proved.** | **Proved** |
| `obsReal_nonneg` | `ValidDGP → 0 ≤ obsReal m g z` | Nonnegativity from validity. | **Proved** |
| `obsPMF hv`, `obsLaw hv` | `noncomputable def`, `PMF (Obs M)` / `Measure (Obs M)` | Single-observation law as a PMF / probability measure (via `PMF.ofFintype`). `obsLaw_isProb` instance. | **Proved** |
| `ate g` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ := (1/M) Σ_x (g true x − g false x)` | The ATE functional; depends only on `g`. | — |
| `l2sq a b` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ := (1/M) Σ_x (a x − b x)²` | Squared `L²(P_X)` distance. | — |
| `InClass mhat ghat εg εm m g` | `structure`, `Prop` | Structure-agnostic nuisance class `ℱ`: valid + each outcome arm within `εg` and propensity within `εm` of fixed estimates `(mhat, ghat)`. | — |
| `InClassDGP …` | `def`, subtype | A DGP packaged with its `InClass` proof. | — |
| `productLaw hv n` | `noncomputable def`, `Measure (Fin n → Obs M) := Measure.pi …` | `n`-sample i.i.d. product law; `productLaw_isProb` instance. | **Proved** |
| `nMiss hv n est s` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ` | `(productLaw hv n).real {x \| s ≤ \|est x − ate g\|}`: probability `est` misses the true ATE by `s`. | — |
| `nMSE hv n est` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ := ∫ x, (est x − ate g)² ∂(productLaw hv n)` | Mean-squared error of `est`: the expected-risk functional (the paper's weaker `𝔼`-risk form). | — |
| `nMiss_sq_le_nMSE` | `(hv) (n) (hs : 0 ≤ s) → s²·nMiss hv n est s ≤ nMSE hv n est` (needs `[MeasurableSingletonClass C]`) | **Chebyshev/Markov bridge**: probability-of-miss controls MSE. Quantitative form of "quantile bound ⟹ expected-risk bound". Via `mul_meas_ge_le_integral_of_nonneg`; integrability free on the finite sample space. | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `minimaxMiss mhat ghat εg εm n est s` | `noncomputable def`, `ℝ := ⨆ p : InClassDGP …, nMiss …` | Worst-case-over-class miss probability; a lower bound on it is a minimax lower bound. | — |
| `bddAbove_nMiss_range`, `nMiss_le_minimaxMiss` | bddAbove / `nMiss ≤ minimaxMiss` | Each in-class miss `≤ 1`, so the sup is well-defined and dominates each member. | **Proved** |

### 13sa.2 `Estimation/MinimaxATE/Reduction/Witness.lean` — two-point reduction

Abstract Le Cam reduction (over a general finite covariate `C`). The explicit
witness is now built (§13sa.4); this layer is correct regardless of how it is built.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TwoPointWitness C n mhat ghat εg εm` | `structure` (needs `[Fintype C] [Nonempty C] [MeasurableSpace C]`) | Le Cam witness: separation `s`, TV budget `c`, two `n`-sample laws `Q : Bool → Measure …` (`prob`), true ATEs `θ : Bool → ℝ` with `sep : 2s ≤ \|θ true − θ false\|`, `tvBound : tvDist (Q false) (Q true) ≤ c`, and the realizability field `dominated` (each `Q j`-miss `≤ minimaxMiss`, discharged by a mixture-of-in-class-DGPs construction). | — |
| `twoPointWitness_lower_bound` | `(W) (hest : Measurable est) → (1 − W.c)/2 ≤ minimaxMiss … est W.s` | **Headline.** Le Cam two-point bound (`Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_tvDist_le`) + realizability domination. **Proved, axiom-clean.** | **Proved** |
| `twoPointWitness_quarter` | `(W) (hc : W.c ≤ 1/2) (hest) → 1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss … est W.s` | A witness with TV budget `≤ 1/2` forces every estimator to miss the truth by `s` with prob `≥ 1/4` somewhere in the class. **Proved, axiom-clean.** | **Proved** |

### 13sa.3 `Estimation/MinimaxATE/Reduction/Bump.lean` — `l2sq` / Rademacher-bump algebra

Small reusable algebra for the squared `L²(P_X)` distance, used to verify
class-membership of the perturbed nuisances (a constant-magnitude bump lands
exactly on the budget boundary).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `l2sq_self` | `l2sq a a = 0` | Distance to self is zero. | **Proved** |
| `l2sq_comm` | `l2sq a b = l2sq b a` | Symmetry. | **Proved** |
| `l2sq_nonneg` | `0 ≤ l2sq a b` | Nonnegativity (no `[NeZero M]` needed). | **Proved** |
| `l2sq_bump` | `(∀ x, (σ x)² = 1) ⇒ l2sq (fun x ↦ b x + δ·σ x) b = δ²` (needs `[Nonempty C]`) | **Constant-magnitude (Rademacher) bump**: a `±δ` perturbation has `l2sq = δ²`, so `δ = √ε` lands the perturbation on the boundary of `ℱ(ε,·)`. | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

### 13sa.4 Explicit construction (Jin–Syrgkanis §4.4, Case 1) — `ConstCenterHalf/` (`Construction/Membership/Gap/ExplicitWitness.lean`)

The explicit lower-bound witness, on the **paired-cell covariate** `C = Fin K × Bool`
(pair index × position) with centered estimates `m̂ = ĝ ≡ 1/2` and unweighted ATE.
A Rademacher sign vector `λ : Fin K → Bool` drives the bump
`Δ λ (j,b) = signOf b · signOf (λ j) ∈ {±1}` (`Δ² = 1`, balanced per pair). Every
declaration **sorry-free + axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Δ`, `Δ_sq`, `Δ_mem` | Construction | `Δ λ x = signOf x.2·signOf (λ x.1)`; `Δ² = 1`; `Δ ∈ {±1}` | The Rademacher bump and its sign properties. |
| `mPerturbed`, `gPerturbed` | Construction | `mλ = 1/2 − β·Δ`; `gλ(1) = (1/2+α·Δ)/(1−2β·Δ)`, `gλ(0)=1/2` | The asymmetric (non-linear-in-`λ`) perturbed DGP (eq. 17, `m̂=ĝ=1/2`). |
| `validDGP_perturbed` | Construction | `0≤α, 0≤β, α+2β≤1/2 ⇒ ValidDGP (mPerturbed β λ) (gPerturbed α β λ)` | The perturbed nuisances stay in `[0,1]`. |
| `inClass_perturbed` | Membership | `β²≤εm, (α+β)²/(1−2β)²≤εg ⇒ InClass m̂ ĝ εg εm (mλ) (gλ)` | Class membership: `l2sq(mλ,m̂)=β²` exactly, `l2sq(gλ(1),ĝ(1))≤(α+β)²/(1−2β)²`. |
| `ate_gPerturbed` | Gap | `ate (gPerturbed α β λ) = 2β(α+β)/(1−4β²)` | **Exact ATE — independent of `λ`** (the per-pair sum is sign-invariant). The √(εg·εm) gap. |
| `Qfalse`, `Qtrue`, `signWeight` | ExplicitWitness | `Qfalse = P̂^⊗n`; `Qtrue = (1/2^K)Σ_λ Qλ^⊗n` (uniform `mixture`) | The two `n`-sample data laws (null vs. uniform mixture of perturbations). |
| `explicitWitness` | ExplicitWitness | `… (htv : tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ≤ 1/2) → TwoPointWitness (Fin K×Bool) n m̂ ĝ εg εm` | Assembled witness; `dominated` discharged by `mixtureReal_le` (all `Qλ` in-class, common ATE). |
| `explicit_minimax_lower_bound` | ExplicitWitness | budgets + `htv : tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ≤ 1/2` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss m̂ ĝ εg εm n est (β(α+β)/(1−4β²))` | Conditional form (carries `htv`); `minimax_lower_bound` (§13sa.5) discharges it. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.5 χ² indistinguishability core — `ConstCenterHalf/` (`ChiSqOverlap/Ingster/ChiSquaredCore.lean`)

The Ingster χ² second-moment bound that discharges `htv`, making the lower bound
**unconditional** (only the natural regime hypotheses remain). All sorry-free +
axiom-clean. Builds on the finite χ² formula `finite_one_add_chiSqDiv` (§12w).

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `obsLaw_real_singleton` | ChiSqOverlap | `(obsLaw hv).real {z} = obsReal m g z` | The single-observation PMF point mass (generic `C`). |
| `chiSqOverlap`, `chiSqOverlap_eq` | ChiSqOverlap | `chiSqOverlap α β λ λ' := ∑_z qλ(z)qλ'(z)/p̂(z) = 1 + (2γ/K)·Σ_j signOf(λ j)signOf(λ' j)`, `γ = α²+2αβ+3β²` | **Exact closed form** of the single-obs χ²-overlap (finite algebra; per-pair sign-balanced cancellation). |
| `ingster_bound` | Ingster | `0≤γ, 2γ≤1, 2n²γ² ≤ K·log 2 ⇒ Σ_{λ,λ'} (2^K)⁻²·(1+(2γ/K)Σ_j s_j s'_j)ⁿ ≤ 2` | **The Ingster bound** (standalone real analysis): `(1+x)≤eˣ` + Rademacher independence (factorize to `cosh(nc)^K`) + `cosh y ≤ e^{y²/2}`. |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse` | ChiSquaredCore | `1 + χ²(Qtrue‖Qfalse) = Σ_{λ,λ'} (2^K)⁻²·chiSqOverlap(λ,λ')ⁿ` | **Mixture 2nd-moment identity**: finite χ² formula + product point mass (`pi_real_singleton`) + `Fintype.sum_pow`. |
| `chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse_le_one` | ChiSquaredCore | regime ⇒ `χ²(Qtrue‖Qfalse) ≤ 1` | Combine the identity with `chiSqOverlap_eq` + `ingster_bound`. |
| `tvDist_Qfalse_Qtrue_le_half` | ChiSquaredCore | regime ⇒ `tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ≤ 1/2` | `tvDist ≤ ½√χ² ≤ 1/2` (discharges `htv`). |
| `minimax_lower_bound` | ChiSquaredCore | budgets + `2γ≤1` + `2n²γ² ≤ K·log 2` + `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss m̂ ĝ εg εm n est (β(α+β)/(1−4β²))` | **Headline (unconditional).** No estimator is within `s ≍ √(εg·εm)` of the true ATE w.p. `> 3/4` over the class, in the regime `n²·(εg+εm)² ≲ K`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

Helpers: `absolutelyContinuous_of_singleton_pos` (full support ⇒ AC), `productLaw_real_singleton`, `Qfalse_singleton_ne_zero`, `signWeight_toReal` (`= (2^K)⁻¹`).

### 13sa.6 Continuous covariate — `ContinuousX.lean`

The ancillarity reduction to the paper's continuous-`X` statement. The within-cell
position is modelled by an **arbitrary** probability space `(W, ρ)`; the continuous
`n`-sample laws are the finite-cell laws tensored with `n` ancillary `ρ`-draws.
Tensoring both hypotheses with the common factor leaves the χ² unchanged
(`chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary`, §12w), so the bound transfers verbatim. Sorry-free +
axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ancillaryLaw ρ n`, `QfalseCont`, `QtrueCont` | `Measure.pi (fun _ => ρ)`; `(Qfalse).prod (ancillaryLaw ρ n)`; `(Qtrue …).prod (ancillaryLaw ρ n)` | The `n` within-cell ancillary draws and the continuous null / alternative laws on `(Fin n → Obs (Fin K×Bool)) × (Fin n → W)`. |
| `chiSqDiv_QtrueCont_QfalseCont_le_one` | regime ⇒ `χ²(QtrueCont‖QfalseCont) ≤ 1` | The ancillary factor cancels (`chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary`), reducing to the finite-cell `chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse_le_one`. |
| `tvDist_QfalseCont_QtrueCont_le_half` | regime ⇒ `tvDist QfalseCont QtrueCont ≤ 1/2` | Continuous-`X` indistinguishability. |
| `two_point_lower_bound_continuous` | budgets + regime + `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ max (QtrueCont.real {miss 2s}) (QfalseCont.real {miss 0})` | **Headline (continuous covariate).** Two-point (Le Cam) obstruction on the richer continuous experiment; the ancillary within-cell position cannot help. Bounds the two-hypothesis max-miss, not `minimaxMiss`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.7 Expected-risk (MSE) form — `MSE.lean`

The paper's quantile lower bound (§13sa.5) *implies* the weaker expected-squared-error
bound used by Balakrishnan et al. (`𝔐ₙ,γ ≥ ρ ⟹ minimax `𝔼`-risk `≥ (1−γ)ρ`). This
file makes that implication explicit and **unconditional** via the Chebyshev bridge
`nMiss_sq_le_nMSE` (§13sa.1). The witness DGP is pulled out of the two-point bound
`1/4 ≤ max(…)`: either the null `(m̂,ĝ)` (left branch) or a single Rademacher-perturbed
DGP extracted from the uniform mixture `Qtrue` by `exists_real_ge_mixture` (a finite
average is `≤` its maximal component). Sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `minimax_lower_bound_mse` | budgets + regime + `Measurable est` ⇒ `∃ p : InClassDGP m̂ ĝ εg εm, (β(α+β)/(1−4β²))²/4 ≤ nMSE p.2.valid n est` | **Expected-risk headline.** Every estimator has MSE `≥ s²/4 ≍ εg·εm` on *some* in-class DGP — the product rate is an unbeatable `𝔼`-squared-error floor. The `(1−γ)`-factored (`γ=3/4`) consequence of the quantile bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

(No continuous-covariate MSE form: on the non-finite continuous sample space the
squared error of an arbitrary measurable estimator need not be integrable, so an
unconditional Chebyshev step is unavailable — the continuous result stays in its
native quantile/two-point form, which is already the paper's risk notion.)

### 13sa.8 General constant center — `ConstCenterGeneral/` (`Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound/MSE.lean`, `namespace GenConstr`)

Generalizes the `(m̂,ĝ)≡1/2` development (§13sa.4–7) to an **arbitrary constant
nuisance center** `(m₀,g₀,g₁) ∈ (0,1)³` bounded away from `{0,1}` — the paper's
Assumption 2 in the constant case, including the known-baseline remark. The center
is held constant *within* each Rademacher pair, so the ATE gap stays **exactly
`λ`-independent** (no Taylor/Fano needed) and the abstract `ingster_bound` (§13sa.5)
is reused verbatim with `γ := Γ/2`. The `1/2` headline is the instance
`m₀=g₁=1/2`. All declarations **sorry-free + axiom-clean**; live in `namespace GenConstr`.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GenConstr` | Construction | `structure` (fields `α β m₀ g₀ g₁ : ℝ` + the bounded-away / smallness inequalities `hβg₁ : β<g₁`, `hαg₁ : α≤g₁`, `hgU : g₁+α+β/g₁≤1`, `hmU : m₀(1+β/g₁)≤1`) | Bundles the bump scalars, the constant center, and exactly the inequalities keeping every perturbed probability in `[0,1]`. |
| `mhatG`, `ghatG`, `mPertG`, `gPertG` | Construction | `m̂≡m₀`; `ĝ(1)≡g₁,ĝ(0)≡g₀`; `mλ=m₀(1−(β/g₁)·Δ)`; `gλ(1)=(g₁+α·Δ)/(1−(β/g₁)·Δ)`, `gλ(0)=g₀` | The general-center perturbed DGP (collapses to §13sa.4 at `m₀=g₁=1/2`, `β/g₁=2β`). |
| `denomG_pos`, `validDGP_hatG`, `validDGP_pertG` | Construction | `0<1−(β/g₁)·Δ`; null & perturbed are `ValidDGP` | Positivity of the denominator and `[0,1]`-validity. |
| `gPertG_true_eq` | Gap | `gλ(1,x) = (g₁²+α·g₁·Δ)/(g₁−β·Δ)` | **Cleared treated arm** (clears the inner `β/g₁` fraction); used throughout. |
| `ate_ghatG`, `ate_gPertG` | Gap | `ate ĝ = g₁−g₀`; `ate gλ = (g₁−g₀) + g₁β(α+β)/(g₁²−β²)` (needs `[NeZero K]`) | **Exact ATE — independent of `λ`** (per-pair sum sign-invariant). |
| `ate_gapG` | Gap | `ate gλ − ate ĝ = g₁β(α+β)/(g₁²−β²)` | The `√(εg·εm)` gap (collapses to `2β(α+β)/(1−4β²)` at `g₁=1/2`). |
| `l2sq_mPertG`, `l2sq_gPertG_true_le`, `inClassG` | Membership | `l2sq(mλ,m̂)=(m₀β/g₁)²` exactly; `l2sq(gλ(1),ĝ(1))≤g₁²(α+β)²/(g₁−β)²`; budgets `(m₀β/g₁)²≤εm`, `g₁²(α+β)²/(g₁−β)²≤εg ⇒ InClass m̂ ĝ εg εm mλ gλ` | Class membership. |
| `Γ`, `chiSqOverlapG`, `chiSqOverlap_eqG` | ChiSqOverlap | `Γ = m₀α²/g₁ + m₀(α+β/g₁)²/(1−g₁) + m₀²β²/(g₁²(1−m₀))`; `chiSqOverlapG λ λ' = 1 + (Γ/K)·Σ_j s_j s'_j` | **Exact single-obs χ²-overlap closed form** (linear-`Δ` terms cancel by construction; `Γ=2(α²+2αβ+3β²)` at `m₀=g₁=1/2`). |
| `QfalseG`, `QpertG`, `QtrueG`, `Γ_nonneg` | LowerBound | `P̂^⊗n`; `Qλ^⊗n`; `(1/2^K)Σ_λ Qλ^⊗n`; `0≤Γ` | The two `n`-sample laws and nonnegativity of the overlap coefficient. |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_QtrueG_QfalseG`, `chiSqDiv_QtrueG_QfalseG_le_one`, `tvDist_QfalseG_QtrueG_le_half` | LowerBound | mixture 2nd-moment identity; `Γ≤1`, `2n²(Γ/2)²≤K·log 2 ⇒ χ²≤1`; ⇒ `tvDist≤1/2` | χ² indistinguishability via `ingster_bound` at `γ=Γ/2`. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_gen` | LowerBound | budgets + `Γ≤1` + `2n²(Γ/2)²≤K·log 2` + `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss m̂ ĝ εg εm n est (g₁β(α+β)/(2(g₁²−β²)))` | **Headline (general constant center, unconditional).** No estimator is within `s ≍ √(εg·εm)` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4` over the class, for any constant bounded-away `(m₀,g₀,g₁)`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_mse_gen` | MSE | same hyps ⇒ `∃ p : InClassDGP m̂ ĝ εg εm, (g₁β(α+β)/(2(g₁²−β²)))²/4 ≤ nMSE p.2.valid n est` | **Expected-risk form (general center).** MSE `≥ s²/4` on some in-class DGP; the `(1−γ)`-factored consequence. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9 Cell-varying center — `VaryingCenterCase1/` (`Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`, `namespace VarConstr`) + `Reduction/WitnessMixture.lean` + `VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster.lean` (non-uniform Ingster)

Generalizes §13sa.8 from a *constant* center to a **cell-varying center**: the
nuisance estimates are arbitrary *functions* of the pair index `j : Fin K` (bounded
away from `{0,1}`), genuinely non-constant over the covariate space — the finite-model
analogue of the paper's functional center, for centers constant *within* each
Rademacher pair (the residual within-pair variation is gap (i'') of the §13sa scope
note). Because the center is shared by a pair's two positions, the per-pair ATE gap is
still **exactly `λ`-independent** (no Taylor remainder), but the χ²-overlap coefficient
`Γⱼ` now **varies per pair**, so the bound needs the non-uniform Ingster inequality.
Two reusable pieces of infrastructure were added; everything **sorry-free + axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mixture_two_point_lower_bound` | Reduction/WitnessMixture | null in-class DGP + finite weighted family of in-class alternatives with **per-component** separation `2s ≤ \|ate g₀ − ate(gλ i)\|` + `tvDist(Q₀, mixture) ≤ c` ⇒ `(1−c)/2 ≤ minimaxMiss` | **One-sided mixture two-point lemma.** Sharper than `TwoPointWitness`: the alternatives need not share one ATE — a per-component *lower* bound suffices (the asymmetric testing argument measures each component at its own ATE). The abstract key enabling fully-functional centers. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `ingster_bound_general` | Ingster | `(∀j,0≤d j)`, `Σⱼ d j ≤ 1`, `(n²/2)Σⱼ(d j)² ≤ log 2` ⇒ `Σ_{λ,λ'}(2^K)⁻²(1+Σⱼ d j·s_j s'_j)^n ≤ 2` | **Non-uniform Ingster χ² bound.** Per-pair coefficients `d j` (vs the constant `2γ/K` of `ingster_bound`); factorizes to `∏ⱼ cosh(n·d j)` via `Finset.prod_univ_sum`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `VarConstr` | Construction | `structure` (fields `α β : ℝ`, `m₀ g₀ g₁ : Fin K → ℝ` + per-pair bounded-away/smallness inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and the **cell-varying** center (one bounded-away value per pair). |
| `mhatV`, `ghatV`, `mPertV`, `gPertV`, `validDGP_hatV`, `validDGP_pertV` | Construction | `m̂ x=m₀ x.1`; `ĝ(1,x)=g₁ x.1, ĝ(0,x)=g₀ x.1`; `mλ=m₀ x.1(1−(β/g₁ x.1)Δ)`; `gλ(1,x)=(g₁ x.1+αΔ)/(1−(β/g₁ x.1)Δ)` ; both `ValidDGP` | The cell-varying perturbed DGP, indexed through the pair index `x.1`. |
| `ate_ghatV`, `ate_gPertV`, `ate_gapV`, `ate_gap_nonneg` | Gap | `ate gλ` is a `λ`-independent sum over pairs; `ate gλ − ate ĝ = (card)⁻¹·2β(α+β)·Σⱼ g₁ⱼ/(g₁ⱼ²−β²) ≥ 0` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (sign-invariant within each pair), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPertV_le`, `l2sq_gPertV_true_le`, `inClassV` | Membership | per-pair budgets `(m₀ⱼ·β/g₁ⱼ)²≤εm`, `g₁ⱼ²(α+β)²/(g₁ⱼ−β)²≤εg` (all `j`) ⇒ `InClass m̂ ĝ εg εm mλ gλ` | Class membership via average-of-bounded-terms (per-pair sup bounds are *sufficient*). |
| `ΓV`, `ΓV_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV`, `chiSqOverlap_eqV` | ChiSqOverlap | `Γⱼ = m₀ⱼα²/g₁ⱼ + m₀ⱼ(α+β/g₁ⱼ)²/(1−g₁ⱼ) + m₀ⱼ²β²/(g₁ⱼ²(1−m₀ⱼ))`; `chiSqOverlapV λ λ' = 1 + Σⱼ (Γⱼ/K)·s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair χ²-overlap closed form** (linear-`Δ` terms cancel; coefficient varies per pair → feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j = Γⱼ/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_QtrueV_QfalseV_le_one`, `tvDist_QfalseV_QtrueV_le_half` | LowerBound | `Σⱼ Γⱼ/K ≤ 1`, `(n²/2)Σⱼ(Γⱼ/K)² ≤ log 2` ⇒ `χ²≤1` ⇒ `tvDist≤1/2` | χ² indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var` | LowerBound | per-pair budgets + `Σⱼ Γⱼ/K ≤ 1` + `(n²/2)Σⱼ(Γⱼ/K)² ≤ log 2` + `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss m̂ ĝ εg εm n est (½(ate gλ − ate ĝ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 1 `εg ≳ εm`).** No estimator is within `s` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4`, for a nuisance center varying arbitrarily across pairs. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9b Cell-varying center, Case 2 (`εm > εg`) — `VaryingCenterCase2/Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`

The **symmetric second construction** (Jin–Syrgkanis 2024 eq. (18), `f_n > e_n'`).
`VaryingCenterCase1`'s Case-1 construction attains the product rate `s ≍ √(εg·εm)` only when the
outcome budget dominates (`εg ≳ εm`); this file covers the opposite regime by
**swapping the two nuisances' roles**: the propensity deviation is the large one
(`O(α+β)`, carrying `√εm`) and the outcome deviation the small one (`O(β)`, carrying
`√εg`). In the finite-cell model the construction is `gλ(0,·)=g₀`,
`gλ(1,x)=g₁ⱼ/D`, `mλ=m₀ⱼ(1+αg₁ⱼΔ)D` with `D=1+(β/g₁ⱼ)Δ−αβ`. Although non-linear in
`λ`, the observed masses are polynomial in `Δ` (the `D` cancels in `mλ·gλ(1)`; the
propensity collapses to `mλ=m₀ⱼ(1+κⱼΔ)`, `κⱼ=β/g₁ⱼ+αg₁ⱼ−α²βg₁ⱼ`). Reuses
`VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster`'s `ingster_bound_general`. **All proved, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `VarConstr2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `structure` (fields `α β : ℝ`, `m₀ g₀ g₁ : Fin K → ℝ` + per-pair `hgU`/`hκ`/`hmU` inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and cell-varying center for the propensity-dominant construction; `hgU` forces `D>0` and `gλ(1)≤1`, `hκ` keeps `mλ≥0`, `hmU` keeps `mλ≤1`. |
| `κ`, `D2`, `mhat2`, `ghat2`, `mPert2`, `gPert2`, `mPert2_eq`, `validDGP_hat2`, `validDGP_pert2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `κⱼ=β/g₁ⱼ+αg₁ⱼ−α²βg₁ⱼ`; `D=1+(β/g₁)Δ−αβ`; `mλ=m₀(1+αg₁Δ)D`, `gλ(1)=g₁/D`; `mPert2_eq : mλ=m₀(1+κΔ)`; both `ValidDGP` | The Case-2 perturbed DGP and its affine `Δ`-collapse (via `Δ²=1`), proved valid. |
| `ate_ghat2`, `ate_gPert2`, `ate_gap2`, `ate_gap2_nonneg`, `gPert2_true_eq2`, `clearedDenom_pos`, `denomE_pos` | VaryingCenterCase2/Gap | `ate gλ` is `λ`-independent; `ate gλ − ate ĝ = (card)⁻¹·2β·Σⱼ g₁ⱼ(αg₁ⱼ²(1−αβ)+β)/Eⱼ ≥ 0`, `Eⱼ=g₁ⱼ²(1−αβ)²−β²` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (cleared treated arm `g₁²/(g₁(1−αβ)+βΔ)`; the two-position sum collapses `σ²`), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPert2_le`, `l2sq_gPert2_true_le`, `gPert2_true_sub`, `inClass2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Membership | per-pair budgets `(m₀ⱼκⱼ)²≤εm` (exact, propensity), `β²(αg₁ⱼ+1)²/(1−β/g₁ⱼ−αβ)²≤εg` (worst-case, outcome) ⇒ `InClass m̂ ĝ εg εm mλ gλ` | Class membership in the swapped regime: `mλ−m̂=m₀κΔ` is **exact** (`O(α+β)`→`√εm`), `gλ(1)−ĝ(1)=β(αg₁−Δ)/D` is `O(β)`→`√εg`. |
| `ΓV2`, `ΓV2_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV2`, `obsReal_pert2_eq`, `chiSqOverlap_eq2` | VaryingCenterCase2/ChiSqOverlap | `Γⱼ = m₀ⱼα²g₁ⱼ³ + m₀ⱼ(β/g₁ⱼ+αg₁ⱼ(1−g₁ⱼ)−α²βg₁ⱼ)²/(1−g₁ⱼ) + m₀ⱼ²κⱼ²/(1−m₀ⱼ)`; `chiSqOverlapV2 λ λ' = 1 + Σⱼ(Γⱼ/K)·s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair χ²-overlap closed form** (constant part `=1`, the finite analogue of `E_λ qλ=p̂`; only the `λ·λ'` cross term survives → feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j=Γⱼ/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_Qtrue2_Qfalse2_le_one`, `tvDist_Qfalse2_Qtrue2_le_half` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | `Σⱼ Γⱼ/K ≤ 1`, `(n²/2)Σⱼ(Γⱼ/K)² ≤ log 2` ⇒ `χ²≤1` ⇒ `tvDist≤1/2` | χ² indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var2` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | propensity-dominant per-pair budgets + `Σⱼ Γⱼ/K ≤ 1` + `(n²/2)Σⱼ(Γⱼ/K)² ≤ log 2` + `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss m̂ ĝ εg εm n est (½(ate gλ − ate ĝ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 2 `εm > εg`).** The symmetric construction covering the regime `minimax_lower_bound_var` cannot; together they give the full product rate `√(εg·εm)` in both regimes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.10 Parametric `1/n` term — `Parametric.lean`

The additive `1/n` (parametric-variance) term of the paper's `Ω(εg εm + 1/n)` bound,
via a **separate** two-point construction independent of the Rademacher/Ingster bias
machinery. Both hypotheses are single product laws (no mixture): a constant center
`(m₀,g₀,g₁)` and a constant **outcome shift** `δ` on the treated arm only. The single-
observation χ² has the exact closed form `χ²₁ = m₀δ²/(g₁(1−g₁))`, so tensorizing
(`one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid`) and bounding `(1+χ²₁)^n ≤ e^{nχ²₁} ≤ 2` keeps `tvDist ≤ ½`
once `n·χ²₁ ≤ log 2` — i.e. `δ ≍ 1/√n`. The ATE gap is exactly `δ`. **Axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_obsPert_obsNull` | `1 + chiSqDiv (obsLaw pert) (obsLaw null) = 1 + m₀δ²/(g₁(1−g₁))` | **Exact single-obs χ²** for a constant treated-arm shift `δ` (the `d=0` block cancels; `d=1` block gives `m₀(1+δ²/(g₁(1−g₁)))`). |
| `parametric_lower_bound` | `0≤δ`, `g₁+δ≤1`, `δ²≤εg`, `n·m₀δ²/(g₁(1−g₁)) ≤ log 2`, `Measurable est` ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMiss (fun _=>m₀) (fun d _=>if d then g₁ else g₀) εg εm n est (δ/2)` | **Headline (parametric term).** With `δ ≍ 1/√n` (so `s = δ/2 ≍ 1/√n`, `s² ≍ 1/n`), no estimator is within `s` of the ATE w.p. `>3/4` — the `1/n` minimax floor even with a perfectly-known nuisance class. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.11 Achievability — `AIPWEstimator.lean`

The **matching upper bound**: the fixed-center AIPW estimator attains the `√(εg·εm)` rate.
Because the structure-agnostic class is centered at *fixed* nuisances `(mhat, ghat)` (not
data-estimated), the natural estimator is the plug-in AIPW **sample average**
`θ̂(sample) = (1/n) Σ_i ψ_AIPW(Z_i; mhat, ghat)`. Everything stays in the finite `Obs C` /
`productLaw` world (no potential-outcome lift, no asymptotics), so the pairing with the
`minimaxMiss` converse is direct. This is the finite specialization of the general AIPW DML
asymptotic-normality theorem `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean` (`dml_ATE_tendstoNormal`). **All proved,
axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z` | `def`, `ℝ` | Fixed-center AIPW score `(ĝ₁ x − ĝ₀ x) + D·(Y−ĝ₁ x)/m̂ x − (1−D)·(Y−ĝ₀ x)/(1−m̂ x)` at one observation `z=(x,D,Y)` (`yReal` reads `Y:Bool` as `{0,1}`). |
| `estAIPW mhat ghat n` | `def`, `(Fin n → Obs C) → ℝ` | The estimator: sample average `(n)⁻¹ Σ_i aipwScoreFin mhat ghat (sample i)`. |
| `aipw_pop_mean` | `∫ aipwScoreFin … ∂(obsLaw hv) = Σ_z obsReal m g z · aipwScoreFin …` | Population mean of the score is the finite PMF-weighted sum. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_identity` | `(Σ_z obsReal·score) − ate g = (1/\|C\|) Σ_x [(1−m/m̂)(ĝ₁−g₁) − (1−(1−m)/(1−m̂))(ĝ₀−g₀)]` | **Doubly-robust remainder identity** (the analytic heart): the plug-in bias factors into products of nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `exists_center_overlap` | `(∀x, 0<m̂ x<1) ⇒ ∃ ε>0, ∀x, ε ≤ m̂ x ∧ ε ≤ 1−m̂ x` | Uniform center overlap from the finite `min` (so the score weights `1/m̂`, `1/(1−m̂)` are bounded). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_bound` | `\|bias\| ≤ ε⁻¹·((√l2sq(g₁,ĝ₁)+√l2sq(g₀,ĝ₀))·√l2sq(m,m̂))` | Finite Cauchy–Schwarz over uniform `P_X`: the bias is bounded by the product of `L²` nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `aipwScore_bound` | `\|aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z\| ≤ 1 + 2/ε` | The score is bounded (center valid + `m̂` `ε`-off `{0,1}`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_var_bound` | `variance (estAIPW mhat ghat n) (productLaw hv n) ≤ (1+2/ε)²/n` | Variance of the i.i.d. average (via `variance_sum_pi` + bounded score). **Proved.** |

### 13sa.12 Capstone — `Optimality.lean` (minimax rate is `Θ(√(εg·εm))`, attained by AIPW)

Pairs the converse (`minimax_lower_bound_var`) with the AIPW achievability into the
optimality statement. **All proved, axiom-clean** (`aipw_attains_minimax_rate` uses
only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_mean_eq` | `0<n ⇒ ∫ estAIPW … ∂(productLaw hv n) = Σ_z obsReal·score` | Mean of the i.i.d. average equals the single-obs population mean (via `measurePreserving_eval` + `aipw_pop_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_nMiss_le` | bias `≤ b`, `variance ≤ V`, `b<s` ⇒ `nMiss hv n (estAIPW …) s ≤ V/(s−b)²` | **Bias-centered Chebyshev** miss bound (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`; `MemLp` free on the finite domain via `eLpNorm_lt_top_of_finite`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_inclass_bias_bound` | `InClass mhat ghat εg εm m g ⇒ \|bias\| ≤ ε⁻¹·(2√εg·√εm)` | Uniform-over-class bias bound: `aipw_bias_bound` + `l2sq ≤ εg/εm` via `Real.sqrt` monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_minimaxMiss_le` | `s > ε⁻¹·2√εg√εm ⇒ minimaxMiss mhat ghat εg εm n (estAIPW …) s ≤ ((1+2/ε)²/n)/(s − ε⁻¹·2√εg√εm)²` | **Worst-case AIPW miss bound**, uniform over the class (`ciSup_le` + the three pieces), `→ 0` as `n·εg·εm → ∞`. **Proved.** |
| `MinimaxRateThreshold mhat ghat εg εm n` | `structure` | Reusable "rate attained + unbeatable" vocabulary: a single `estimator` with a converse miss-lower-bound (`probLower` at `sepLower`) and an achievability miss-upper-bound (`missUpper` at `sepUpper`). |
| `aipw_attains_minimax_rate` | `noncomputable def`, `MinimaxRateThreshold P.mhatV P.ghatV εg εm n` | **Capstone.** For the cell-varying construction `P` with the lower-bound budgets/regularity: every estimator misses by `s_P = gap/2 ≍ √(εg·εm)` w.p. `≥ 1/4` (`converse` ← `minimax_lower_bound_var`), while the AIPW estimator's worst-case miss at any `sepUpper > ε⁻¹·2√εg√εm` (also `Θ(√(εg·εm))`) is `≤ ((1+2/ε)²/n)/(sepUpper−…)²`. Hence the minimax ATE rate is `Θ(√(εg·εm))` and DR/AIPW attains it (constant-factor, not constant-sharp). **Axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.C Causal grounding — `Causal/Construction.lean`, `Causal/Bridge.lean`, `Causal/Minimax.lean`

Grounds the observed-data contrast `ate g` in the potential-outcome framework: the minimax lower bound is a bound on the genuine causal ATE `E[Y(1) − Y(0)]`, identified by backdoor adjustment, not merely a regression contrast. `Construction.lean` builds — from a finite DGP `(m, g)` on a finite covariate `C` — the first concrete `POBackdoorSystem` in the library: a backdoor SCM `Un → Xc → A → Y`, `Xc → Y` with latent noise roots `Ea → A`, `Ey → Y` (`A = 1{Ea ≤ m Xc}`, `Y = 1{Ey ≤ g A Xc}`), lifted through `POSystem.ofSCM`. `Bridge.lean` defines the causal estimand and proves it equals the contrast.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dgpSCM` / `dgpPO` / `dgpBackdoor` | the witness gSCM, its `ofSCM` PO system, and the extracted `POBackdoorSystem (dgpPO m g) C` | The concrete finite backdoor construction (treatment `A`, outcome `Y`, covariate `Xc`); `structFun_measurable`/`isProbability_latent` discharged | **Defined** |
| `causalATE m g` | `(dgpBackdoor m g).ATE` | The causal estimand `E[Y(1) − Y(0)]` of the constructed system | **Defined** |
| `dgp_consistency` / `dgp_unconfoundedness` / `dgp_propScore_eq_m` / `dgp_adjustedCE_eq_g` / `dgp_overlap` | the backdoor `Assumptions` fields | Consistency (free from `ofSCM_consistency`); unconfoundedness `A ⟂ (Y(1),Y(0))∣X` (via `ofSCM_condIndepCF_of_dSep` + `condIndepCF_congr_cond`); `P[A=1∣X]=m(X)` and `E[Y∣A=d,X]=g(d,X)` (uniform-coordinate conditional expectations); two-sided overlap from strict `m ∈ (0,1)` | **Proved** |
| `causalATE_eq_ate` | `(hv : ValidDGP m g) (hso : ∀ x, m x ∈ Ioo 0 1) → causalATE m g = ate g` | **Estimand bridge.** The causal ATE equals the observed-data contrast `ate g`, via `BackdoorEstimationSystem.θ₀_eq_ATE` + uniform `P_X`. So every minimax lower bound transfers to `E[Y(1) − Y(0)]`. Axiom-clean (standard axioms + `native_decide` for the graph checks) | **Proved** |
| `minimaxMissCausal mhat ghat εg εm n est s` | `⨆ p : InClassDGP mhat ghat εg εm, (productLaw p.valid n).real {x | s ≤ \|est x − causalATE p.m p.g\|}` | Causal-centered analogue of `minimaxMiss`, with each miss event centered at the concrete backdoor causal ATE instead of the observed-data contrast. | **Defined** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var_causal` | `hm`, `hg`, strict `∀j, P.m₀ j * (1 + P.β / P.g₁ j) < 1`, budgets/regularity ⇒ `1/4 ≤ minimaxMissCausal P.mhatV P.ghatV … gap/2` | Causal version of the cell-varying minimax lower bound. The additional strict-overlap hypothesis keeps the perturbed Var witnesses inside the overlap region, allowing `causalATE_eq_ate` to transfer the observed-data lower bound to `E[Y(1) − Y(0)]`. | **Proved** |

---

## 13eff. `Estimation/Efficiency/` — Hahn (1998) semiparametric efficiency bound

The **third vertex** of the ATE efficiency triangle, joining achievability
(`dml_ATE_isAsymLinear`, §13c) and the minimax lower-bound tower (§13sa): the
*semiparametric efficiency bound* `= Var(efficient influence function)`, the
theorem that the AIPW influence function `ψ_AIPW` (§13a.2) **is** Hahn's efficient
influence function, and the "role of the propensity score" (the bound does not
depend on whether the propensity score is known). Reference: Hahn (1998),
*On the Role of the Propensity Score in Efficient Semiparametric Estimation of
Average Treatment Effects*, Econometrica 66(2):315–331. Plan: `doc/hahn_efficiency_plan.md`.

### 13eff.1 `Estimation/Efficiency/TangentProjection.lean` — abstract Hilbert efficiency machine

Pure Hilbert-space geometry over a real inner-product space `H` with a tangent
subspace `T` (`[T.HasOrthogonalProjection]`). The reusable abstraction every
future estimator-efficiency theorem cites. All **sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsGradient T g ψ` | `Prop := ∀ s ∈ T, ⟪ψ, s⟫_ℝ = ⟪g, s⟫_ℝ` | `ψ` is an influence function / gradient agreeing with reference `g` on every tangent direction. |
| `efficientIF T g` | `noncomputable def := ↑(orthogonalProjection T g)` | The efficient influence function = orthogonal projection of any gradient onto the tangent space. |
| `effBound T g` | `noncomputable def := ‖efficientIF T g‖^2` | The semiparametric efficiency bound. |
| `efficientIF_mem` | `efficientIF T g ∈ T` | The EIF lies in the tangent space. |
| `isGradient_iff_sub_mem_orthogonal` | `IsGradient T g ψ ↔ ψ − g ∈ Tᗮ` | Gradients form the affine coset `g + Tᗮ`. |
| `efficientIF_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g (efficientIF T g)` | The EIF is itself a gradient. |
| `orthogonalProjection_eq_of_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g ψ ⇒ orthogonalProjection T ψ = efficientIF T g` | The projection onto `T` is the same for every gradient. |
| `normSq_gradient_decomp` | `IsGradient T g ψ ⇒ ‖ψ‖² = ‖efficientIF T g‖² + ‖ψ − efficientIF T g‖²` | Pythagoras decomposition along the tangent space. |
| `effBound_le_normSq` | `IsGradient T g ψ ⇒ effBound T g ≤ ‖ψ‖²` | **The efficiency lower bound**: no gradient beats the EIF's norm. |
| `norm_eq_iff_eq_efficientIF` | `IsGradient T g ψ ⇒ (‖ψ‖² = effBound T g ↔ ψ = efficientIF T g)` | Sharpness: equality only at the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_unique` | `IsGradient T g ψ → ψ ∈ T → ψ = efficientIF T g` | A gradient lying in `T` is the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_eq_self_of_mem` | `g ∈ T ⇒ efficientIF T g = g` | A tangent reference is its own EIF. |
| `effBound_eq_of_mem_sub` | `T' ≤ T → g ∈ T' ⇒ efficientIF T' g = g ∧ efficientIF T g = g ∧ effBound T' g = effBound T g` | **Tangent-shrinking** corollary: removing tangent directions orthogonal to `g` leaves the bound unchanged. |

### 13eff.2 `Estimation/Efficiency/Submodel.lean` — regular parametric submodels via exponential tilting

The analytic engine for pathwise derivatives. For a base law `P` and a bounded
mean-zero score `s`, the exponential tilt `P_t = (e^{t·s}/c(t))·P` realizes `s`
as a regular submodel. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tiltNorm P s t` | `noncomputable def := ∫ e^{t·s} dP` | Normalizing constant `c(t)`. |
| `tiltExp P s φ t` | `noncomputable def := (∫ φ·e^{t·s} dP)/tiltNorm` | Tilted expectation `E_{P_t}[φ]`. |
| `tiltMeasure P s t` | `noncomputable def := (ofReal c(t))⁻¹ • P.withDensity (ofReal e^{t·s})` | The tilted probability measure `P_t`. |
| `tiltNorm_zero` | `tiltNorm P s 0 = 1` | At `t=0` the tilt is `P`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tilt_numerator` | `HasDerivAt (fun t => ∫ φ·e^{t·s} dP) (∫ φ·s dP) 0` | Differentiation under the integral sign (Mathlib `hasDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv_le`), bounded score + integrable `φ`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tiltExp` | bounded mean-zero `s`, integrable `φ` ⇒ `HasDerivAt (tiltExp P s φ) (∫ φ·s dP) 0` | **The tilt-derivative lemma**: `d/dt E_{P_t}[φ]|₀ = Cov_P(φ,s) = ∫ φ·s dP`. The analytic core of every pathwise-derivative argument. |
| `tilt_exp_integrable` | `Integrable (e^{t·s}) P` | Bounded score ⇒ density integrable (comparison with constant). |
| `tiltNorm_pos` | `0 < tiltNorm P s t` | Positivity of the normalizer (lower-bounded by `e^{−|t|M}`). |
| `isProbabilityMeasure_tiltMeasure` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (tiltMeasure P s t)` | The tilt has total mass 1 (`ofReal_integral_eq_lintegral_ofReal` + `ENNReal.inv_mul_cancel`). |
| `integral_tiltMeasure` | `∫ h ∂(tiltMeasure P s t) = tiltExp P s h t` | Bridge: expectations under `P_t` are tilted expectations (`integral_withDensity_eq_integral_smul₀`). |

### 13eff.3 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEVariance.lean` — the Hahn variance decomposition

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `hahn_weight_true_Linf`, `hahn_weight_false_Linf` | `MemLp (a/e)`/`((1−a)/(1−e)) ⊤ P.μ` | Overlap ⇒ the IPW weights are bounded (private helpers). |
| `hahn_mu_L2` | `MemLp (μ_val d ∘ X) 2 P.μ` | Conditional-Jensen `L²` bound on the regression (private helper). |
| `aipw_variance_hahn_decomposition` | `∫ ψ_AIPW² dP_Z = ∫ (μ₁−μ₀−τ)² dP_X + ∫ (a/e²)(Y−μ₁)² dP_Z + ∫ ((1−a)/(1−e)²)(Y−μ₀)² dP_Z` | **The Hahn efficiency-bound formula.** `ψ = A+B−C`, `BC=0` (`a∈{0,1}`), the two cross-terms vanish via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`. The last two terms are `E[σ²₁/e]`, `E[σ²₀/(1−e)]`. **Proved.** |
| `dml_ATE_attains_hahn_bound` | `√\|B(n)\|·(θ̂ⁿ − θ₀) ⇒ N(0, V_H)` | AIPW/DML attains the Hahn bound: `dml_ATE_tendstoNormal` with the limit variance rewritten as the 3-term `V_H`. **Proved.** |

### 13eff.4 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATETangent.lean` — ψ_AIPW is the efficient influence function

Instantiates the abstract machine (§13eff.1) in `H = Lp ℝ 2 P_Z`, where the
mean-zero tangent space is `Tfull = (ℝ∙1)ᗮ`. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `propensity_score_residual_integral_zero` | `∫ h(X)·(1_{D=1} − e_val(X)) dμ = 0` | The propensity residual is mean-zero given `σ(X)` (private helper). |
| `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score` | `∫ ψ_AIPW·(α(X)·(indA − e_val(X))) dP_Z = 0` | **Role of the propensity score**: `ψ_AIPW` is orthogonal to every propensity-score direction (B/C terms via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`, A term via the residual helper). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_P_Z` | `IsProbabilityMeasure S.P_Z` | The data law is a probability measure. |
| `aipw_memLp` | `MemLp ψ_AIPW 2 P_Z` | `ψ_AIPW ∈ L²(P_Z)` (from `aipw_finite_var`). |
| `aipwLp`, `oneLp`, `Tfull` | `Lp ℝ 2 P_Z` elements / `(ℝ∙oneLp)ᗮ` | `ψ_AIPW` and constant `1` as `L²` classes; the mean-zero tangent space. |
| `inner_oneLp` | `⟪f, oneLp⟫ = ∫ f dP_Z` | The `L²` inner product against `1` is the mean. |
| `aipwLp_mem_tangent` | `aipwLp ∈ Tfull` | `ψ_AIPW` is mean-zero (`aipw_mean_zero`), so lies in the tangent space. |
| `aipw_is_efficientIF` | `efficientIF Tfull aipwLp = aipwLp` | **ψ_AIPW is its own efficient influence function** (it lies in the tangent space). |
| `effBound_eq_variance`, `effBound_eq_hahn` | `effBound Tfull aipwLp = ∫ ψ_AIPW² dP_Z = V_H` | The efficiency bound equals `Var(ψ_AIPW)` equals the Hahn formula. |
| `efficiency_bound_optimal` | `IsGradient Tfull aipwLp ψ ⇒ effBound Tfull aipwLp ≤ ‖ψ‖²` | **The semiparametric efficiency bound**: every influence function of the ATE has variance `≥ V_H`; `ψ_AIPW` attains it. |
| `effBound_eq_of_known_propensity` | `T' ≤ Tfull → aipwLp ∈ T' ⇒ effBound T' aipwLp = effBound Tfull aipwLp` | **Propensity-invariance** (Hahn's headline): the bound is unchanged when the tangent space shrinks to the propensity-known model (`ψ_AIPW` is still in it by `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score`), so knowing the propensity score does not improve efficiency. |

### 13eff.5 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEEfficientIF.lean` — pathwise ATE efficient influence function

This capstone module connects Hahn's pathwise derivative calculation to the
abstract pathwise-gradient and projection machinery. It records the bridge from
the AIPW covariance formula to the `L²(P_Z)` inner product, identifies the full
mean-zero space as the nonparametric tangent space under the supplied density
hypothesis, and states the conditional Hahn theorem that `ψ_AIPW` is the
canonical efficient influence function for the backdoor ATE.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.ATEEfficientIF -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.inner_aipwLp_eq_integral` | `inner ℝ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) f = ∫ (z : γ × Bool × ℝ), S.ψ_AIPW z * ↑↑f z ∂S.P_Z` | The covariance pairing between the AIPW influence function and any square-integrable score equals the Hilbert-space inner product against the AIPW element in the observed-data square-integrable space. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.score_mem_Tfull` | `m.score ∈ S.Tfull` | Every regular submodel score is mean-zero, so every such score belongs to the full nonparametric tangent space for the observed-data law. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.isTangentSpace_Tfull` | `S.Tfull ≤ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z → Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsTangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z S.Tfull` | The full mean-zero space is a genuine tangent space once the supplied nonparametric-model hypothesis says it is contained in the closed span of regular-submodel scores. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_isPathwiseGradient_ATE` | `(∀ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => ψ (m.path t)) (∫ (z : γ × Bool × ℝ), S.ψ_AIPW z * ↑↑m.score z ∂S.P_Z) 0) → Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ψ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2)` | Hahn's pathwise-derivative identity makes the AIPW score a pathwise gradient of the backdoor ATE functional along every regular submodel. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_is_efficientInfluenceFunction` | `(∀ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => ψ (m.path t)) (∫ (z : γ × Bool × ℝ), S.ψ_AIPW z * ↑↑m.score z ∂S.P_Z) 0) → S.Tfull ≤ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z → Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ψ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) ∧ S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2 ∈ S.Tfull ∧ ∀ (g : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 S.P_Z)), Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ψ g → Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.efficientIF S.Tfull g = S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2` | The AIPW influence function is the efficient influence function for the backdoor average treatment effect in the nonparametric observed-data model. |
<!-- /GEN -->

---

## 14. `Panel/` — Panel regression algebra

> **PARTIALLY ARCHIVED 2026-06-12.** The dead CausalSmith research *question grammar* and its
> population machinery (`Regression/`, `PathLimit/`, `Probability/`, `DGP/`,
> `Baseline/`, `Assignment/`, `Target/`, `Question.lean`, `Bridge.lean`) were moved
> to `archive/Causalean/Panel/` and their API descriptions removed from this file
> (CausalSmith research now proposes free-form questions; no live consumer; `CausalSmith`
> imports nothing from `Causalean.Panel`). See `archive/README.md` and
> `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-panel-substrate-unification-design.md`.

The cell / inner-product / projection / FWL machinery lives in
`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/` (see §13W). The live `Panel/` layer provides the
`R = I × T` instantiation (`Cells I T := WeightedSupport (I × T)`) plus the
panel-specific apparatus: balanced panels (`Cells.balanced`), the two-way
fixed-effect subspace (`H_twfe` via `Weighted.twoAxisAdditiveSpan I T`), the
cell-level potential-outcome layer (`PO/`), the share-weighted two-way panel
(`WeightedTwoWayPanel`, §14c-WTWP), and the TWFE indicator closed forms
(`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`, §14d-IndicatorClosedForms). It is
**algebraic, not estimation-theoretic**: finite-sample statements are identities
conditional on the treatment path.

Live subsections: §14a (`Cells` + `InnerProduct`), §14b (`Subspace` +
`FixedEffect`), §14c (`PO/`), §14c-WTWP (`WeightedTwoWayPanel`),
§14d-IndicatorClosedForms (`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`).

### 14a. `Panel/Cells.lean` + `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

#### 14a.1 `Panel/Cells.lean`

Observed unit-period cells `R ⊆ I × T` with strictly positive weights summing
to 1.  Mirrors Definition 2.1 of the projection note.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells I T` | `structure` (over `[Fintype I] [Fintype T] [DecidableEq I] [DecidableEq T]`) | Bundles `observed : Finset (I × T)` (nonempty), `weight : I × T → ℝ`, `weight_pos`, `weight_zero_off`, `weight_sum_one`. |
| `Cells.weight_nonneg` | `0 ≤ c.weight r` for all `r` | Pointwise nonnegativity (positive on `observed`, zero off it). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight` | `∑ r ∈ c.observed, c.weight r = 1` | Restated normalization. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ` | `∑ r, c.weight r = ∑ r ∈ c.observed, c.weight r` | Sum over `Finset.univ` agrees with sum over `observed`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ_eq_one` | `∑ r, c.weight r = 1` | Normalization restated over `Finset.univ`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.balanced` | `Cells I T` (under `[Nonempty I] [Nonempty T]`) | Balanced panel: `observed = univ`, `weight = 1/(\|I\|·\|T\|)`. `noncomputable`. |
| `Cells.balanced_observed`, `balanced_weight` | `@[simp]` | Definitional unfoldings of `balanced`. |

#### 14a.2 `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

Weighted inner product on scalar arrays and its matrix-valued lift to vector
arrays modeled as `Fin K → ((I × T) → ℝ)`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.ip` | `(c : Cells I T) → (I × T → ℝ) → (I × T → ℝ) → ℝ` | `c.ip A B = ∑ r ∈ c.observed, c.weight r · A r · B r`. |
| `Cells.ip_def` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding of `c.ip A B`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_symm` | `c.ip A B = c.ip B A` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_add_left` / `ip_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in left / right argument. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_smul_left` / `ip_smul_right` | `c.ip (s • A) B = s · c.ip A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_summand_nonneg` | `∀ r ∈ c.observed, 0 ≤ c.weight r * A r * A r` | Per-summand nonnegativity, used by both positivity and definiteness. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_nonneg` | `0 ≤ c.ip A A` | Positivity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_eq_zero_iff` | `c.ip A A = 0 ↔ ∀ r ∈ c.observed, A r = 0` | Definiteness on the support `R`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat` | `(Fin K → I × T → ℝ) → (Fin K → I × T → ℝ) → Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ℝ` | Entrywise inner product `(c.ipMat A B) j k = c.ip (A j) (B k)`. |
| `Cells.ipMat_apply` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_transpose` | `(c.ipMat A B).transpose = c.ipMat B A` | Transposed symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_add_left` / `ipMat_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in each tuple argument (entrywise). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_smul_left` / `ipMat_smul_right` | `c.ipMat (s • A) B = s • c.ipMat A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity entrywise. **Proved.** |

### 14b. `Panel/Subspace.lean` + `Panel/FixedEffect.lean`

Nuisance spaces, weighted residualization, and the two-way fixed-effect
subspace from Definition 2.2.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.V` | `(I T : Type*) → Type _ := (I × T) → ℝ` | Scalar cell-array space used throughout `Panel/`. |
| `Cells.weighted_orthogonal_projection_exists` | `∃ P : V →ₗ[ℝ] V, ...` | Existence of a weighted orthogonal projection onto a subspace. Proved by packaging `c.ip` as a positive-semidefinite bilinear form and applying the generic semidefinite-projection helper. |
| `Cells.proj` | `Cells I T → Submodule ℝ (V I T) → V I T →ₗ[ℝ] V I T` | Chosen weighted projection onto a nuisance subspace. |
| `Cells.proj_mem` / `proj_orthogonal` | membership and orthogonality lemmas | Defining properties of `proj`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.proj_apply_of_mem` / `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal` / `proj_idem_apply` | supportwise projection identities | Projection agrees with members of `H` on observed cells; any orthogonal candidate nuisance component agrees with the chosen projection on observed cells; projection is idempotent there. |
| `Cells.residualize` | `V I T →ₗ[ℝ] V I T` | Residual maker `M_H = id - P_H`. |
| `Cells.tildeX` / `tildeXVec` | scalar and column-wise residualization | Residualized scalar array and `Fin K` tuple of arrays. |
| `Cells.residualize_in_orthogonal` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) h = 0` for `h ∈ H` | Key residual orthogonality. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.residualize_self_of_mem` / `residualize_idem_apply` | supportwise residual identities | Residuals of nuisance-space arrays vanish on observed cells; `M_H` is idempotent on observed cells. |
| `Cells.H_twfe` | `Submodule ℝ (V I T)` | Two-way fixed-effect subspace `{h : h(i,t)=a_i+b_t}`. |
| `Cells.const_mem_H_twfe` | constants are in `H_twfe` | Fixed-effect space contains constant arrays. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_finiteDimensional` | `Module.Finite ℝ (c.H_twfe)` | Finite-dimensionality inherited from the finite ambient cell-array space. |

### 14c. `Panel/PO/` — cell-level potential outcomes

Statement-level scaffolding for the panel potential-outcomes layer.
Mirrors Definitions 2.3–2.4 and Assumption 2.1 of
`CausalSmith/doc/general_projection_carryover_note.tex`.  Treatment paths,
exposure mappings, the bundled `PanelPOSystem`, and the (definitional)
consistency lemma.  Conditional means `m_r^0(D)` and exposure responses
`τ_r(e | D)` are defined via `MeasureTheory.condExp` against the
σ-algebra `σ(D)`.

#### 14c.1 `Panel/PO/TreatmentPath.lean`

Realized treatment paths and finite-memory histories.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TreatmentPath` | `(I T A : Type*) → Type _ := I → T → A` | A unit's treatment trajectory. Mirrors `D = (D_{it})` of Def. 2.3. |
| `TreatmentPath.History` | `[Inhabited A] (p : ℕ) (D : TreatmentPath I (Fin T₀) A) (i : I) (t : Fin T₀) : Fin (p+1) → A` | Length-`p+1` history `(D_{it}, …, D_{i,t-p})` clamped to `default` for out-of-range lags. Implements the `D_{is} = 0` boundary convention. |
| `TreatmentPath.History_zero` | `History p D i t 0 = D i t` | Lag-0 unfolding (contemporaneous treatment). `@[simp]`. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.2 `Panel/PO/Exposure.lean`

Exposure mappings and baseline exposures.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Exposure` | `(I T A : Type*) (E : I × T → Type*) → Type _ := (r : I × T) → TreatmentPath I T A → E r` | Cell-indexed known function `E_r = e_r(D)` of the realized treatment paths. Mirrors Def. 2.3. |
| `BaselineExposure` | `{I T : Type*} (E : I × T → Type*) → Type _ := (r : I × T) → E r` | Baseline `e_r^0 ∈ E_r` for each cell. Mirrors `e_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |

A binary finite-memory builder is intentionally not shipped (awkward dependent-type instantiation); downstream files build the `Exposure` inline against `TreatmentPath.History`.

#### 14c.3 `Panel/PO/CellPO.lean`

Bundled panel potential-outcomes system and its derived objects.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem` | `structure` | Bundles `I, T, A, E, e, e0, Ω, μ, D, Y` together with the typeclass instances (`Fintype`, `DecidableEq`, `MeasurableSpace`, `IsProbabilityMeasure`) and measurability/integrability hypotheses (`D_meas`, `Y_meas`, `Y_integrable`). Mirrors Defs. 2.3–2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedExposure` | `(r : I × T) → Ω → E r := e r (D ω)` | Realized exposure at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY` | `(r : I × T) → Ω → ℝ := Y r (observedExposure r ω) ω` | Realized outcome at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg` | `MeasurableSpace Ω` | σ-algebra `σ(D)` generated by the treatment-path random variable, defined as the supremum over `(i, t)` of the comap of `(fun ω => D ω i t)`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg_le` | `DSigmaAlg ≤ ambient σ-alg` | `σ(D)` is a sub-σ-algebra, proved from the cellwise measurability hypotheses `D_meas` via `measurable_iff_comap_le` and `iSup_le`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.m0` | `(r : I × T) → Ω → ℝ` | Baseline conditional mean `m_r^0(D) := E[Y_r(e_r^0) | σ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `m_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.tau` | `(r : I × T) → E r → Ω → ℝ` | Cell-level exposure response `τ_r(e | D) := E[Y_r(e) − Y_r(e_r^0) | σ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `τ_r(e | D)` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.realizedTau` | `(r : I × T) → Ω → ℝ := tau r (observedExposure r ω) ω` | Realized exposure response `τ_r(E_r | D)`. `noncomputable`. |

#### 14c.4 `Panel/PO/Assumptions/Consistency.lean`

Panel consistency (Assumption 2.1).  Definitional under the structure setup.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY_eq_potentialOutcome` | `observedY r ω = Y r (observedExposure r ω) ω` | Pointwise consistency, by `rfl`. **Proved.** |
| `PanelConsistency` | `(P : PanelPOSystem) → Prop` | Universally-quantified consistency statement: `∀ r ω, observedY r ω = Y r (observedExposure r ω) ω`. Named alias for Assumption 2.1. |
| `panelConsistency_holds` | `PanelConsistency P` | Consistency holds by `rfl` under the structure setup. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.5 `Panel/PO/Mobius.lean`

Boolean-cube inclusion-exclusion for Lemma 3.1.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PO.Mobius.indicator` | `Finset (Fin (p+1)) → Fin (p+1) → Fin 2` | Binary history with ones on the selected subset. |
| `PO.Mobius.indicator_empty` | `indicator ∅ = fun _ => 0` | Empty-subset indicator is the zero history. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.delta` | `((Fin (p+1) → Fin 2) → ℝ) → Finset (Fin (p+1)) → ℝ` | Boolean-cube interaction coefficient `δ_S` by alternating sums. |
| `PO.Mobius.prod_indicator_eq` | product over `S` equals `1` iff `S` is contained in active history support | Converts binary products to subset tests. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.coeff_sum` | alternating coefficient sum is `1` on `A=B`, else `0` | Inclusion-exclusion coefficient identity. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.mobius_expansion` | `τ h = ∑_{nonempty S} δ τ S * ∏_{k∈S} h k` when `τ 0 = 0` | Lemma 3.1 Boolean-cube expansion. **Proved.** |

### 14c-WTWP. `Panel/WeightedTwoWayPanel.lean` — Share-weighted two-way panel (L1 unification target)

Share-weighted generalization of `Panel/UniformTwoWayPanel`
(audit `shared_core.md` item **M5**): two-way demeaning under a general unit pmf
`p` (Goodman-Bacon cohort shares; `p ≡ 1/|Unit|` recovers the uniform panel) with
a uniform period measure. Intended as the single L1 demeaning/FWL layer the
EstimandCharacterization papers instantiate (migration is a separate, review-gated
step).

A **bridge** section connects these concrete closed forms to the generic
`WeightedSupport` FWL substrate (`Panel/Weighted/`, §13W): the panel is the cell
support `R = Unit × Time` with weight `ω_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|`, the `p`-weighted
inner product is `|Time|` times `WeightedSupport.ip`, and `ddot` is the generic
residual maker against the two-axis additive span. The abstract FWL coefficient
lemma `Weighted.WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` is thus reusable through
the bridge, so the former bespoke (and consumer-less)
`finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` and its helper
`finiteResidualizedCoefficient` were removed. (The same-named live helpers in
`UniformTwoWayPanel`, §9i0a-UniformTwoWayPanel, are unaffected.)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WeightedTwoWayPanel.UnitWeights` | `structure { p, pos, sum_one }` | Unit weights as a probability vector (`0 < p i`, `∑ p = 1`) | **Defined** |
| `unitMean` / `timeMean` / `grandMean` | `… → ℝ` | Uniform-time unit mean, `p`-weighted time mean `∑_i p_i V_{it}`, `p`-weighted grand mean | **Defined** |
| `ddot` | `(w) (V) (i) (t) → ℝ` | Double-demeaned residual `V − unitMean − timeMean + grandMean` | **Defined** |
| `inner` | `(w) (V W) → ℝ` | `p`-weighted finite inner product `∑_i ∑_t p_i · V_{it} W_{it}` | **Defined** |
| `IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `abbrev` | Shared additive-class predicate `h_it = a_i + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `ddot_reconstruct` / `sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection` / `unitTimeProjection_additive` | identities | Reconstruction + residual/projection identities | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit` | `(hT : 0 < card Time) → ∑_i ∑_t p_i (ddot·a_i) = 0` | `ddot ⟂` unit-only functions (period sum of `ddot` vanishes per unit) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_time` | `∑_i ∑_t p_i (ddot·b_t) = 0` | `ddot ⟂` time-only functions (`p`-weighted unit sum vanishes per period) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive h → inner w (ddot V) h = 0` | `ddot ⟂` the whole additive class | **Proved** |
| `cellSupport` *(bridge)* | `(w) → WeightedSupport (Unit × Time)` | The panel as a cell-indexed weighted support, weight `ω_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|` (every cell observed); needs `[Nonempty Unit] [Nonempty Time]` | **Defined** |
| `mem_twoAxisAdditiveSpan_iff` *(bridge)* | `h ∈ twoAxisAdditiveSpan ↔ IsUnitTimeAdditive (uncurry h)` | Two-axis additive span membership = the panel additive-class predicate | **Proved** |
| `inner_eq_card_smul_ip` *(bridge)* | `inner w V W = |Time| · (cellSupport w).ip V W` | The `p`-weighted panel inner product is `|Time|` times the generic `WeightedSupport.ip` | **Proved** |
| `ddot_eq_residualize` *(bridge)* | `(cellSupport w).residualize (twoAxisAdditiveSpan) V (i,t) = ddot w V i t` | Concrete↔abstract: `ddot` is the generic residual maker against the two-axis additive span (pointwise, every cell observed) | **Proved** |

### 14d-IndicatorClosedForms. `Panel/FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms.lean` — TWFE residual closed forms

Reusable substrate for `Cells.tildeX c.H_twfe A` when `A` is a structured
indicator (cohort-period, switch-on diagonal, triangular). Bridges the gap
identified in `Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis/Helpers.lean` (L3, L4) and is the
cleanest place to put any future "closed-form residual under TWFE" lemma.
Includes a self-contained `cohortPeriodCells` construction (mirrors
`positiveCohortPeriodCells` from the staggered Q1 attempt; no Q-instance
import).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Cells.tildeX_const` | `c.tildeX c.H_twfe (fun _ => k) r = 0` on observed cells | Constants are absorbed by `H_twfe` (one-line via `residualize_self_of_mem`). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_unit_indicator` | unit-only indicator → 0 | `ind(i = i₀)` is a member of `H_twfe` via `a(i) := ind(i = i₀)`, `b(t) := 0`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_period_indicator` | period-only indicator → 0 | Symmetric: `ind(t = t₀)` ∈ `H_twfe`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_product_indicator_balanced` | `tildeX (1_{S_I}(i)·1_{S_T}(t)) = (ind − |S_I|/|I|)·(ind − |S_T|/|T|)` on observed | Centered-product residual under balanced weights. Proof: candidate `g ∈ H_twfe` plus `H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` plus balanced row/column centered-sum lemmas, then `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.cohortPeriodCells` | `(law : CohortLaw C) → Cells (Fin C) (Fin S)` with weights `π(g)/S` | Generic positive-cohort cells construction. **Defined.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_cell_indicator_cohortPeriod` | cell `ind(g = g₀ ∧ t = t₀)` → four-term factorization | Centered-cell residual under cohort-period weights. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_diagonal_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered diagonal `ind(g.val = t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction `(1/S)·∑_{g.val<S} π(g)`. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus the diagonal-hit Finset reindexing. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_triangular_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered triangular `ind(g.val < t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus finite-sum Fubini for the triangular predicate. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` (private) | unit/period basis test suffices for `H_twfe`-orthogonality | Helper used by the substantive proofs. **Proved**: forward by specialization to unit/period indicators; backward by decomposing arbitrary `h(p) = a(p.1) + b(p.2)` into the `e_{i₀}`/`f_{t₀}` basis via Fintype sum manipulation. |

## 15. `ML/` — Standalone machine-learning spine

A causal-free supervised-learning library: basic regression methods as first-class
objects with their defining-optimization / closed-form-or-structure / population-target
properties. Does **not** import OSL/SCM/PO/Estimation. Design + plan:
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module-design.md`,
`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module.md`.

### 15a. `ML/Core/` — the spine (Phase 0, complete)

Dual-view: a parametric `Predictor` (optimization lives here) and an extensional
`HypothesisClass` (population targets live here), joined by one `Bridge`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `squaredLoss`, `bool01`, `sigmoid`, `softplus`, `logisticScoreLoss` | `Core/Losses.lean` | Pointwise losses + logistic link; with `sigmoid_pos/lt_one`, `softplus_pos`, `squaredLoss_nonneg`. |
| `Predictor` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Parametrized predictor family: admissible `paramSet` + `predict : Θ → X → Y`. |
| `HypothesisClass` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Extensional class: a set of measurable functions `X → Y`. |
| `FeatureMap` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Feature transform `X → (K → ℝ)`; identity gives linear regression, other choices give polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression. |
| `Loss`, `empiricalRisk(P)`, `populationRisk(P)`, `iidEmpiricalRisk`, `HasFinitePopulationRisk` | `Core/Risk.lean` | Risk functionals in both views; `iidEmpiricalRisk` via `Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean`. |
| `IsERMP`, `IsApproxERMP`, `RegularizedERM`, `IsERM`, `IsPopulationRiskMinimizer` | `Core/ERM.lean` | Candidate-wise minimizer predicates via `IsMinOn` (parametric + extensional). |
| `imageClass`, `isERMP_to_extensional`, `populationTarget_pushforward` | `Core/Bridge.lean` | The realized class of a predictor; parametric minimizers push forward to extensional minimizers. |
| `empiricalRiskP_convexOn_of_loss_convex`, `convexOn_comp_inner`, `exists_isMinOn_of_isCompact`, `exists_isMinOn_univ_of_coercive`, `fderiv_eq_zero_of_isMinOn_univ`, `SubgradientAt`, `subgradientAt_zero_iff_isMinOn` | `Core/Convex.lean` | Convex-analysis substrate: convexity of empirical risk, linear-composition convexity, compact/coercive existence, Fermat's rule, subgradient↔minimizer. |
| `populationRisk_eq_of_both_minimizers`, `populationRisk_minimizer_eq_target`, `IsL2Projection`, `square_loss_population_target_of_isL2Projection` | `Core/PopulationTarget.lean` | P3 scaffold + squared-loss keystone: a residual orthogonal to all functions of the covariate (the L²-projection / regression function) minimizes squared population risk. |

### 15b. `ML/Linear/`, `ML/Ridge/` — linear least squares and ridge (Phase 1, complete)

Linear-in-features regression: predictor `x ↦ ∑ₖ βₖ (φ x)ₖ`; the `FeatureMap` makes
polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression instances of the same theorems.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `linearPredict`, `olsObjective`, `ols_is_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq`, `empiricalRisk_squaredLoss_linear` | `Linear/Finite.lean` | OLS objective; P1: a normal-equation solution `XᵀX β̂ = Xᵀy` minimizes SSE; bridge to the spine's `empiricalRisk`. |
| `olsCoef`, `ols_normalEq_of_minimizer`, `olsCoef_normalEq`, `olsCoef_unique` | `Linear/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: minimizer ⇒ normal equations (converse); closed form `(XᵀX)⁻¹Xᵀy` and uniqueness under `IsUnit (XᵀX).det`. |
| `IsPopulationOLS`, `bestLinearPredictor_minimizes_populationRisk` | `Linear/Population.lean` | P3: a coefficient with residual uncorrelated to every feature minimizes squared population risk over the linear class (best linear predictor). |
| `ridgeObjective`, `ridge_is_regularized_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq` | `Ridge/Finite.lean` | Ridge objective `SSE + λ‖β‖²`; P1: a ridge normal-equation solution `(XᵀX+λI)β̂ = Xᵀy` minimizes it (λ ≥ 0). |
| `ridgeCoef`, `ridgeGram_posDef`, `ridgeCoef_normalEq`, `ridgeCoef_unique` | `Ridge/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: for λ>0 the ridge Gram is positive definite ⇒ closed form `(XᵀX+λI)⁻¹Xᵀy`, unique without a full-rank assumption. |
| `populationRidgeObjective`, `IsPopulationRidge`, `populationRidge_minimizes` | `Ridge/Population.lean` | P3: the regularized population normal equations `E[(Y−⟪β,φ⟫)φₖ] = λβₖ` characterize the (shrunk) population ridge minimizer. |

### 15b'. `ML/Ridge/Rate.lean` — ridge root-n estimation rate (learning theory)

The causal-free learning-theory result `ML/Causal/RateAssembly` consumes to discharge the
DML `o_p(n^{-1/4})` nuisance condition: the sample ridge predictor converges to the population
ridge minimizer at the L²(P_X) root-n rate. Built from the i.i.d.-sample second-moment engine
`Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` and the in-probability continuous-mapping /
stochastic-order substrate in `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean` and
`Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `empiricalGram`, `empiricalCross`, `sampleRidgeCoef`, `sampleRidgePredictor`, `populationGram` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The sample ridge estimator over the first `n` i.i.d. points: empirical feature Gram `Ĝₙ`, cross moment `Ĉₙ`, coefficient `(Ĝₙ+λI)⁻¹Ĉₙ`, the induced predictor, and the entrywise population Gram `Gₖₗ = ∫ φₖφₗ`. |
| `empiricalGram_posSemidef`, `sampleRidgeCoef_sub_eq` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | `Ĝₙ` is PSD (average of rank-one `φφᵀ`); the algebraic crux factoring the coefficient error `β̂ₙ−β⋆ = (Ĝₙ+λI)⁻¹(Ĉₙ−Ĝₙβ⋆−λβ⋆)`. |
| `centered_score_mean_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The centered empirical score mean `Ĉₙ−Ĝₙβ⋆−λβ⋆` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` (centered i.i.d. vector mean with square-integrable score coordinates; population mean `λβ⋆` from `IsPopulationRidge`). |
| `sampleRidgeCoef_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The ridge coefficient error `‖β̂ₙ−β⋆‖` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the regularized empirical-Gram inverse is `O_p(1)` (entrywise matrix LLN + inverse continuity at the PosDef limit) times the centered score mean. |
| `continuousAt_matrixInv_entry` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Matrix-topology helper: at any nonsingular finite matrix, each inverse entry `M ↦ (M⁻¹)ₖₗ` is continuous. |
| `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Deterministic: the prediction L²(P_X) error is bounded by a finite constant times the coefficient error, `‖∑ₖ δₖφ·ₖ‖_{L²} ≤ C‖δ‖`. |
| `ridge_achievesL2Rate` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** Under the population ridge normal equation, `λ>0`, the L⁴ feature moment and square-integrable score, the sample ridge predictor attains the root-n L² rate toward the population ridge minimizer. |

### 15c. `ML/Lasso/` — L1-regularized least squares (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `l1penalty`, `lassoObjective`, `softThreshold`, `l1penalty_nonneg`, `convexOn_l1penalty`, `convexOn_olsObjective`, `convexOn_lassoObjective` | `Lasso/Finite.lean` | L1 penalty and lasso objective; convexity of the (non-smooth) objective. |
| `softThreshold_isMinOn` | `Lasso/Optimality.lean` | P2: the soft-threshold `S_λ(a)` is the exact minimizer of the scalar prox objective `(u−a)² + 2λ|u|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity_l1_ball_le` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate (high-dim).** Over `L∞`-bounded features, the L¹-ball linear class has `rademacherComplexity ≤ (X∞·W/√n)·√(2 log 2d)` — the `√(log d / n)` lasso rate. |
| `lasso_erm_excess_rate` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Lasso ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the L¹-ball linear class on the L∞-bounded feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4·(X∞·W/√n)·√(2 log 2d) + 2ε`. Loss is the bare linear functional. |
| `LassoFeat`, `LassoWeight`, `lasso_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Lasso/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Lasso ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(∑ⱼ wⱼxⱼ−y)²` (L∞-bounded features, responses in `[−Yb,Yb]`). Same centered-loss decomposition as the kernel version (quadratic part via the infinite-index contraction + clamped square, cross part `2y·x` as a rescaled lasso linear class), carrying the `√(2 log 2d)` factor: tail bound at threshold `4·((4(X∞W)²+2·Yb·X∞·W)/√n)·√(2 log 2d) + 2ε`. |
| `margin_erm_surrogate_excess_rate` | `Margin/Rate.lean` | **Margin classification surrogate excess-risk rate.** For ANY `L`-Lipschitz surrogate `φ` (hinge, logistic, smoothed 0–1), the margin loss `φ(y·⟪w,x⟩)` over the `W`-ball of linear classifiers (features in `Xb`-ball, labels in `[−Yb,Yb]`). Single contraction: the margin `y·⟪w,x⟩=⟪w,y·x⟩` is a linear class over rescaled features and `φ̃=φ−φ(0)` is `LipschitzAt0`, so `RC ≤ 2L·Yb·Xb·W/√n`; tail bound at threshold `8·L·Yb·Xb·W/√n + 2ε`. |
| `effectiveDimension`, `effectiveDimension_nonneg`, `summable_effectiveDimension_term`, `effectiveDimension_le_trace_div`, `effectiveDimension_le_card`, `effectiveDimension_antitone` | `Kernel/EffectiveDimension.lean` | **Effective dimension** `N(λ)=∑ᵢ μᵢ/(μᵢ+λ)=tr(T(T+λ)⁻¹)` of a trace-class operator (summable nonneg eigenvalues `μ`), the quantity the Caponnetto–De Vito kernel-ridge optimal rate is stated in terms of. Definitional foundation + structural properties: nonneg, summand-summability, the dimension-free bound `N(λ)≤(tr T)/λ`, the finite bound `N(λ)≤#ι`, and antitonicity in `λ`. The downstream optimal-rate theorem additionally needs Hilbert-space operator-concentration (operator Bernstein), a separate substrate — see the file docstring. |

Full subgradient/KKT optimality for general designs and the population L1 target are deferred.

### 15d. `ML/Binary/`, `ML/Surrogate/` — logistic regression and convex ERM (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `logisticEmpRisk`, `convexOn_softplus`, `convexOn_logisticScoreLoss`, `convexOn_logisticEmpRisk`, `continuous_logisticEmpRisk`, `logistic_exists_minimizer_on_compact` | `Binary/Logistic.lean` | P1/P2: logistic log-loss is convex (softplus convex via monotone sigmoid derivative); a minimizer exists on any compact parameter set. |
| `bernoulliCE`, `bernoulliCE_isMinOn` | `Binary/FisherConsistency.lean` | P3: cross-entropy is minimized at the true probability on the interior `(0,1)` (Gibbs inequality). The interior is essential — the closed-interval version is false under `Real.log 0 = 0`. |
| `ProperBinaryLoss`, `properLoss_population_minimizer_recovers_eta` | `Surrogate/GenericERM.lean` | A proper loss is Fisher consistent: the population-risk minimizer recovers the conditional probability `η` (via `integral_mono`). |

### 15d'. `ML/Binary/Rate.lean` — L²-regularized logistic estimation rate (learning theory)

Binary-response analogue of the ridge rate: the `λ‖β‖²` penalty makes the logistic objective
globally `2λ`-strongly convex, so the coefficient error is controlled by a strong-convexity basic
inequality (the role ridge's `λI` played) rather than a closed-form inverse. Reuses the same
centered-score `O_p(n^{-1/2})` machinery and the shared linear-predictor L² bound.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `regLogisticGrad`, `IsPopulationRegLogistic`, `logisticPredictor` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The empirical penalized-logistic gradient `n⁻¹Σ(σ⟨β,φᵢ⟩−yᵢ)φᵢ + 2λβ`, the penalized population first-order condition `E[(σ⟨β⋆,φ⟩−Y)φₖ] + 2λβ⋆ₖ = 0`, and the fitted-probability predictor `x ↦ σ(⟨β,φ(x)⟩)`. |
| `regLogistic_basic_inequality` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Deterministic strong-convexity crux: a sample point solving the empirical FOC `∇ₙ(β̂)=0` satisfies `2λ‖β̂−β⋆‖ ≤ ∑ₖ\|∇ₙ(β⋆)ₖ\|` (gradient strong monotonicity: σ monotone ⇒ logistic part ≥ 0, penalty gives `2λ·id`). |
| `regLogisticGrad_coord_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Each coordinate of the empirical gradient at `β⋆` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` — a centered i.i.d. mean (population mean `0` by `IsPopulationRegLogistic`), via `sampleMean_sub_isBigOp`. |
| `regLogisticCoef_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The coefficient error `‖β̂ₙ−β⋆‖` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the basic inequality times the centered gradient. |
| `regLogistic_achievesL2Rate` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** The fitted probability `σ(⟨β̂ₙ,φ⟩)` attains the root-n L² rate toward `σ(⟨β⋆,φ⟩)`, via the `1/4`-Lipschitz `σ` and `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le`. |

### 15e. `ML/Tree/`, `ML/NeuralNet/`, `ML/Kernel/` — trees, networks, kernels (Phase 3)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `FinitePartitionPredictor`, `eval`, `eval_eqOn_cell` | `Tree/FinitePartitionPredictor.lean` | Piecewise-constant tree predictor; constant on each cell. |
| `RandomForest`, `eval`, `eval_mem_Icc` | `Tree/Forest.lean` | Forest = average of trees; averaging preserves pointwise `[a,b]` bounds. |
| `DenseLayer`, `Activation`, `layerMap`, `evalLayers`, `evalLayers_append`, `evalLayers_lipschitz` | `NeuralNet/Layer.lean`, `NeuralNet/FeedForward.lean` | Feedforward composition class; evaluation respects concatenation; Lipschitz with the product of per-layer constants. (No universal approximation / training.) |
| `IsRKHS` | `Kernel/RKHS.lean` | Abstract reproducing-kernel Hilbert space interface (`f x = ⟪f, kₓ⟫`); Mathlib has no RKHS hierarchy. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_innerBall_le`, `rkhs_ball_empiricalRademacher_le` | `Kernel/RKHSRademacher.lean` | **Intrinsic RKHS-ball Rademacher rate.** The empirical complexity of a Hilbert norm-ball linear class, and hence of an RKHS ball with bounded representers, is bounded by `κ·r/√n`. |
| `gram`, `IsPSDkernel`, `gram_add_lambda_posDef` | `Kernel/Gram.lean` | Kernel Gram matrix; `K + λI ≻ 0` for `λ > 0`. |
| `krrRisk`, `representer_theorem` | `Kernel/Ridge.lean` | P2: any kernel-ridge minimizer lies in the span of the sample representers (orthogonal-decomposition proof). |
| `rademacherComplexity_l2_ball_le` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate.** Over `L²`-bounded features, the `W`-ball linear class (dual/RKHS-ball view of kernel ridge) has `rademacherComplexity ≤ Xb·W/√n`. |
| `kernel_erm_excess_rate` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Kernel ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the closed `W`-ball of linear predictors on the closed `Xb`-ball feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4·Xb·W/√n + 2ε`. Loss is the bare linear functional `⟪w,x⟩`. |
| `clampedSq`, `clampedSq_eq_sq`, `clampedSq_le_sq`, `lipschitzAt0_clampedSq`, `continuous_clampedSq` | `Surrogate/ClampedSquare.lean` | Globally `2c`-Lipschitz surrogate `(clamp_{[-c,c]} t)²` for `t ↦ t²`, agreeing with the square on `[-c,c]`; the Lipschitz function fed to the contraction principle so the (non-Lipschitz) squared loss can be pulled out of the Rademacher complexity on a bounded prediction range. |
| `KFeat`, `KWeight`, `kernel_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Kernel/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Kernel ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(⟪w,x⟩−y)²` (responses in `[−Yb,Yb]`). RC of the centered squared-loss class is bounded by decomposing `⟪w,x⟩²−2y⟪w,x⟩`: quadratic part via the infinite-index Ledoux–Talagrand contraction with the clamped square, cross part `2y⟪w,x⟩=⟪w,2y·x⟩` as a linear class over rescaled features. Tail bound at threshold `4·(4(XbW)²+2·Yb·Xb·W)/√n + 2ε`. |

### 15f. `ML/Causal/` — ML learners as causal nuisance estimators (causal-coupling corner)

The **only** ML files permitted to import the causal layers (`PO`/`Estimation`); the
`Causalean.ML` core stays causal-free. Connects the ML population targets to the
AIPW/DML nuisances via Mathlib `condExp`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `covarSigma`, `covarSigma_le`, `isL2Projection_of_condExp`, `condExp_of_isL2Projection` | `Causal/RegressionBridge.lean` | The conditional mean `E[Y∣X]` (Mathlib `condExp`) **is** an `IsL2Projection`, and conversely — full equivalence linking the ML squared-loss keystone to the regression-function nuisance. |
| `mlOutcomeRegression_ae_eq`, `mlPropensity_ae_eq`, `mlNuisanceVec` | `Causal/Nuisance.lean` | The squared-loss ML target on arm `d` is the outcome regression `E[Y∣X,D=d]`; on the treatment indicator it is the propensity `P(D=1∣X)`; `mlNuisanceVec` packages two ML targets into `Estimation.ATE.NuisanceVec`. |
| `aipw_mlNuisance_meanZero_of_wellSpecified` | `Causal/AIPWExample.lean` | End-to-end: well-specified ML nuisances make the AIPW estimating equation mean-zero at the ATE `θ₀` (composes `mlNuisanceVec` with `Estimation.ATE.aipw_mean_zero`). |
| `AchievesL2Rate`, `isLittleOp_one_of_le_one`, `isLittleOp_mul_quarter`, `isLittleOp_quarter_of_isBigOp_sqrt` | `Core/Rate.lean` | Learning-rate abstraction (estimator attains L²-rate toward its population target) + generic rate algebra: per-nuisance `o_p(n^{-1/4})` rates give DML's individual `o_p(1)` and product `o_p(n^{-1/2})` conditions; a root-n `O_p(n^{-1/2})` rate is `o_p(n^{-1/4})`. (Moved here from `Causal/DMLRate`; causal-free common currency between method rate-proofs and the `Causal` assembly.) |

Note: `ML/Core/PopulationTarget.IsL2Projection` quantifies over **measurable** test functions
(required for the `condExp` bridge); the keystone threads this measurability.

Deferred follow-ups: complexity/generalization bounds, sieve approximation theory, raw-kernel→RKHS
construction, NN universal approximation, RF consistency; and (causal-ML trajectory) an end-to-end
AIPW-ATE example with ML nuisances, then the fast-rate layer discharging DML's `o(n^{-1/4})`
nuisance-rate condition.

## 16. `Mathlib/Probability/DarmoisSkitovich.lean` — Bernstein's theorem (finite-variance Darmois–Skitovich)

Mathlib-staging probability result: the analytic engine behind linear non-Gaussian
causal discovery (LiNGAM, Shimizu et al. 2006). Proves that non-Gaussianity of
disturbances breaks the rotational symmetry the Gaussian case suffers from. Built
entirely on Mathlib's `charFun` infrastructure (`contDiff_charFun`,
`iIndepFun.charFun_map_sum_eq_prod`, `iteratedDeriv_charFun_zero`,
`Measure.ext_of_charFun`, `charFun_gaussianReal`); none of this result is in Mathlib.
Sorry-free; axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bernstein_charFun_funeq` | `f(u+v)·g(u−v) = f u·g u·(f v·g(−v))` for `f = charFun (P.map X)`, `g = charFun (P.map Y)` | Functional equation: joint characteristic function of `(X,Y)` computed two ways, using `X ⟂ Y` and `(X+Y) ⟂ (X−Y)`. Plumbing. **Proved** |
| `charFun_eventually_ne_zero` | `∀ᶠ t in 𝓝 0, charFun μ t ≠ 0` (probability `μ` on `ℝ`) | `charFun` is continuous and `1` at `0`, so nonzero near `0`; this is what makes the logarithmic derivative `(charFun μ)'/charFun μ` well defined. **Proved** |
| `charFun_contDiff_two` | `ContDiff ℝ 2 (charFun (P.map Z))` given `MemLp Z 2 P` | `C²`-regularity of the characteristic function under a finite second moment, via `contDiff_charFun`. **Proved** |
| `gaussianForm_of_funeq` | functional equation + `C²` + value `1` at `0` ⟹ `∃ c`, locally `f t = exp(f'(0)·t + c·t²/2)` and `g t = exp(g'(0)·t + c·t²/2)` (shared `c`) | The hard pure-analysis core (no probability). Log-derivative argument with no `Complex.log`: differentiate the functional equation once in each variable to force the second logarithmic derivative constant, then solve the resulting ODE. **Proved** |
